[D01G] PRELIMINARY TREATMENT OF FIBRES, e.g. FOR SPINNING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The preliminary treatment of fibres for staple yarn spinning.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drafting machines or arrangements -> D01H5/00 - Fibre preparation for papermaking -> D21  **Glossary:** - Fibre: a relatively-short, elongated member of natural or artificial material - Filament: an endless or quasi-endless, elongated member of natural (e.g. silk) or artificial material - Yarn: a unitary assembly of fibres, usually produced by spinning - Thread: an assembly of yarns or filaments, usually produced by twisting - Drawing: stretching and elongating yarns or filaments - Draft: the amount to which yarns or filaments are drawn
[D01G 1/00] Severing continuous filaments or long fibres, e.g. stapling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The transformation of filaments into staple fibres, whereby a distinction is made between processes where fibres are delivered in bulk and processes where fibres are delivered in parallelised forms (e.g. ribbons, tops, slivers).
[D01G 1/02] to form staple fibres not delivered in strand form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and devices where the obtained staple fibres are delivered in randomized orientation or bulk form.  Randomized orientation can for example be found for the cutting of mineral filaments like glass, or guillotine type chopping processes of fibres to shorten them.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Processes and devices where the obtained staple fibres are delivered in parallelised form (e.g. strand, ribbon) -> D01G1/06
[D01G 1/022] by solvents -- The subclass D01G 1/022 covers methods that use solvents to sever continuous filaments or long fibers, such as stapling, as a preliminary treatment before further textile processing. It deals with solvent-based cutting or separation techniques applied to fibers that are otherwise not mechanically cut.
[D01G 1/025] by thermic means, e.g. laser -- The subclass D01G 1/025 covers methods for cutting continuous filaments or long fibres by applying heat, such as using a laser. It is situated within the preliminary treatment of fibres in the textiles field, specifically the severing of fibres before spinning.
[D01G 1/027] by freezing -- This subclass covers a method of cutting or breaking continuous filaments or long fibers by first freezing them, which makes the material brittle and easier to sever. It is used in textile preprocessing such as stapling.
[D01G 1/04] by cutting -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Processes and devices where the cut fibres are delivered in parallelised form (e.g. strand, ribbon) -> D01G1/10
[D01G 1/06] Converting tows to slivers or yarns, e.g. in direct spinning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and devices where the obtained staple fibres are delivered in parallelised form (e.g. strands or ribbons).  Typically, processes like the stretch breaking or converting of synthetic filament tows are found in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Processes and devices where the obtained staple fibres are delivered in randomized orientation (bulk) -> D01G1/02
[D01G 1/08] by stretching or abrading -- This subclass deals with severing continuous filaments or long fibres by applying tension or abrasive action, breaking the fibres to create shorter lengths as part of their preliminary treatment before spinning. It is used in textile processing to convert continuous yarns into staple fibres.
[D01G 1/081] with preferential breaking zones -- D01G 1/081 covers methods for severing continuous filaments or long fibres, such as stapling, by creating zones where breaking occurs preferentially. This technique is used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning, enabling controlled fibre length reduction in textile or flexible-material processing.
[D01G 1/083] obtained by mechanical means, e.g. by squeezing -- The D01G 1/083 subgroup pertains to mechanical methods for severing continuous filaments or long fibres during preliminary fibre treatment for spinning, such as squeezing or other force-based techniques. This falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials and fibre preparation.
[D01G 1/085] obtained by thermic means
[D01G 1/086] by freezing
[D01G 1/088] obtained by electric or electro-static means -- D01G 1/088 covers the severing of continuous filaments or long fibres by means that rely on electric or electro-static phenomena, such as arcs, sparks, plasma or electro-static discharge, used for preliminary fibre treatment before spinning.
[D01G 1/10] by cutting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. the so-called converters for tow (i.e. tow to top).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Processes and devices where the cut fibres are delivered in randomized orientation -> D01G1/04
[D01G 11/00] Disintegrating fibre-containing articles to obtain fibres for re-use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and processes for disintegrating fibre containing articles to obtain fibres for reuse, i.e. recycling.
[D01G 11/02] Opening, unravelling, or teasing ropes or like fibrous strands to obtain fibres for re-use -- This CPC subclass D01G 11/02 addresses the opening, unravelling or teasing of ropes or similar fibrous strands in order to recover individual fibres for reuse, forming part of the preliminary treatment of fibres in textile processing.
[D01G 11/04] Opening rags to obtain fibres for re-use -- This subclass covers processes that open rag-type textile articles to separate the constituent fibres for subsequent reuse, typically by mechanical or chemical means as a preparatory step before spinning. It falls under CPC D01G 11/04, which deals with the preliminary treatment of fibres by disintegrating fibre-containing items to obtain reusable fibres.
[D01G 13/00] Mixing, e.g. blending, fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and processes for mixing, e.g. blending fibres or mixing fibrous and non-fibrous material.
[D01G 15/00] Carding machines or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The technical elements and variants of cards, as well short staple cards (cotton cards) as long staple cards (wool cards).  **Glossary:** - Carding: a mechanical process that breaks up locks and unorganised clumps of fibre and then aligns the individual fibres so that they are more or less parallel with each other. The aim of carding is to perfect the individualisation and cleaning of staple fibres.
[D01G 15/02] Carding machines -- D01G 15/02 relates to carding machines and their accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. The code covers machinery that separates, aligns and cleans fibres by passing them over carding surfaces, forming a continuous web or strand ready for subsequent processes such as drawing or roving. It is part of the textiles hierarchy for preliminary fibre treatment.
[D01G 15/04] with worker and stripper or like rollers operating in association with a main cylinder -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Long staple (e.g. for wool) cards, where the carding action is performed between the workers and the main cylinder.
[D01G 15/06] Garnett machines -- D01G 15/06 covers garnett machines, a type of carding equipment used in the preliminary processing of fibers before spinning. These machines separate, align and clean fibers by passing them over rotating drums fitted with fine teeth or wires, preparing the material for subsequent carding or spinning steps.
[D01G 15/08] with flats or like members or endless card sheets operating in association with a main cylinder -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Short staple (e.g. for cotton) cards, where the carding action is performed between flats and the main cylinder.
[D01G 15/10] with other apparatus, e.g. drafting devices, in integral or closely-associated combination -- The code D01G 15/10 designates carding machines that incorporate other equipment, such as drafting devices, in an integral or closely-associated arrangement for the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. This subclass falls under textiles, specifically the treatment of fibres and carding accessories.
[D01G 15/12] Details
[D01G 15/14] Constructional features of carding elements, e.g. for facilitating attachment of card clothing -- D01G 15/14 covers constructional features of carding elements, such as design aspects that facilitate the attachment of card clothing in carding machines used for preliminary fibre treatment before spinning.
[D01G 15/16] Main cylinders -- D01G 15/16 relates to the main cylinders of carding machines or their accessories, which are central components in the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. These cylinders control fibre opening, cleaning and alignment by providing a specially designed surface that guides fibre flow.
[D01G 15/18] Workers
[D01G 15/20] Feed rollers -- D01G 15/20 designates feed rollers used in carding machines or related accessories for the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. These rollers guide and regulate the flow of fibre webs or slivers through the carding process, ensuring uniform feed to subsequent stages. The code falls under the CPC section for textiles, specifically the subgroup for fibre preparation equipment.
[D01G 15/22] Fancies -- D01G 15/22 - Fancies covers carding machines or accessories specially designed for producing decorative or novelty yarns, including equipment that imparts varied textures, colours or patterns to fibres during the preliminary carding stage.
[D01G 15/24] Flats or like members -- The code D01G 15/24 designates flat or similarly shaped elements that form part of a carding machine or its accessories, such as flat combs, plates or wrapper bars used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. These components are applied to separate, align and clean the fibres.
[D01G 15/26] Arrangements or disposition of carding elements -- This subclass concerns the spatial layout and configuration of carding components such as drums, rollers and wire teeth within a carding machine used for preliminary fibre treatment before spinning. It includes designs for element spacing, orientation and overall arrangement intended to optimise fibre opening, cleaning and alignment.
[D01G 15/28] Supporting arrangements for carding elements -- The D01G 15/28 subclass covers devices that hold, position or tension the carding wires, pins or other working elements in a carding machine or its accessories, ensuring proper alignment and spacing during the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. It includes frames, brackets, sleeves and other support structures that maintain the carding elements in operation.
[D01G 15/30] Bends -- D01G 15/30 covers the bending components of carding machines or their accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. It includes devices that curve, deflect or redirect carding wires, rollers or plates to improve fibre opening, alignment or flow during the carding process. This subclass falls under the broader group for textile fibre preparation and specifically addresses the mechanical shaping of the fibre feed path.
[D01G 15/32] Framework -- D01G 15/32 - Framework designates the supporting structure of carding machines and related accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. It includes the chassis, frame, mounting brackets and other load-bearing components that hold the carding elements in position.
[D01G 15/34] Grids -- The D01G 15/34 subclass refers to grids that are part of carding machines or their accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. These grids are structural elements that help separate, align and transport fibres as they pass through the carding zone.
[D01G 15/36] Driving or speed control arrangements -- The CPC subclass D01G 15/36 covers mechanisms that regulate the drive or speed of carding machines and related accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. It includes systems such as variable-speed drives, electronic controllers, and mechanical speed-adjustment devices that ensure consistent carding performance.
[D01G 15/38] for use during the grinding of card clothing -- This CPC subclass (D01G 15/38) relates to accessories for carding machines that are employed during the grinding of card clothing, the wire or polymer combs that align fibres before spinning. It falls under the preliminary treatment of fibres within the textiles and flexible materials category.
[D01G 15/40] Feeding apparatus -- D01G 15/40 designates feeding apparatus for carding machines, the equipment that introduces and transports fibers into the carding zone during preliminary treatment such as cleaning, opening, and aligning before spinning. The devices ensure continuous, uniform feed of loose or pre-processed fibers.
[D01G 15/42] Feeding from laps -- The D01G 15/42 subclass covers mechanisms that feed fiber laps into a carding machine, ensuring continuous delivery of the lap material to the cylinder or rollers for subsequent carding. It includes devices such as feed rollers, conveyors, or suction systems that pick up the lap and supply it to the carding zone.
[D01G 15/44] Intermediate feeds -- The D01G 15/44 subgroup covers intermediate feed devices used in carding machines or their accessories. These are mechanisms that convey fibers from one point of the carding process to another, ensuring a steady supply to the carding cylinder for effective fiber opening and alignment before spinning.
[D01G 15/46] Doffing or like arrangements for removing fibres from carding elements -- The D01G 15/46 subclass relates to devices that strip or remove fibres that remain on carding elements after carding, such as doffing, brushing or comb-type arrangements that clean the card. These mechanisms are part of carding machines or accessories used in the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning, helping maintain efficiency and product quality in textile processing.
[D01G 15/465] Doffing arrangements for removing fibres using, or cooperating with, pneumatic means -- This subclass covers pneumatic doffing systems in carding machines, using air jets or suction to detach and remove fibres from the carding drum or rollers. The arrangements may be integrated into the machine or added as accessories to improve fibre removal during preliminary treatment.
[D01G 15/48] Stripping-combs -- The D01G 15/48 subclass covers stripping-combs, which are comb-type accessories fitted to carding machines for the removal of short fibers, neps or other contaminants during the preliminary treatment of fibers before spinning. These tools help improve fiber alignment and quality.
[D01G 15/50] Stripping-rollers or like devices -- D01G 15/50 relates to stripping-rollers and similar devices employed in carding machines for the preliminary treatment of fibres. These components remove fibres from the card cylinder, help open and clean the fibre web, and improve the efficiency of spinning preparation.
[D01G 15/52] Web-dividing arrangements -- This subclass covers devices integrated with carding equipment to separate a continuous web of fibres into discrete sections or strips, enabling downstream processing such as winding or cutting. It includes mechanisms that split, slit or otherwise partition the fibre web as part of the preliminary treatment before spinning.
[D01G 15/54] employing doffers specially adapted for web dividing -- The CPC code D01G 15/54 covers carding machines or accessories that use doffers specially adapted for dividing the web of fibres. It falls under the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning, specifically within textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for. This subclass details the use of customized doffer designs to improve web division during the carding process.
[D01G 15/56] employing tapes -- D01G 15/56 covers the use of tapes in carding machines or their accessories during the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. It relates to textile processes where tapes assist in opening, cleaning or aligning fibres in flexible material production.
[D01G 15/58] Sliver or like rubbing apparatus -- A sliver-or-like rubbing apparatus is a machine attached to a carding line that gently rubs the drawn sliver to improve fiber parallelism, remove neps and surface irregularities, and promote uniform thickness before subsequent drawing or spinning processes.
[D01G 15/60] Constructions of rubbing leathers -- The CPC entry D01G 15/60 covers the design and construction of rubbing leathers used in carding machines or their accessories, such as the leather surfaces that contact fibres during preliminary fibre treatment for spinning. It addresses features like shape, mounting, and material composition that affect the effectiveness and durability of the rubbing action.
[D01G 15/62] Slubbing-winding apparatus -- The CPC subclass D01G 15/62 covers equipment used in the slubbing and winding stage of fibre preparation, where fibres are drawn out, slightly twisted and wound onto bobbins before spinning. Devices in this group are specifically designed to handle the continuous feed of sliver and form uniform packets for subsequent processes.
[D01G 15/64] Drafting or twisting apparatus associated with doffing arrangements or with web-dividing apparatus -- D01G 15/64 covers drafting or twisting devices that are integrated with doffing mechanisms or with equipment that splits a fiber web. These apparatuses are used in carding machines or related accessories to control fiber feed and tension during preliminary fiber treatment such as spinning preparation. The classification falls under textile preprocessing of fibers.
[D01G 15/66] with arrangements inserting false twist -- This CPC subclass pertains to carding machines or accessories that incorporate mechanisms for inserting a false twist into fibres during preliminary treatment such as carding, improving fibre alignment before spinning. The false-twist arrangement is integrated into the carding process to control fibre orientation and quality.
[D01G 15/68] with arrangements inserting permanent twist, e.g. spinning -- D01G 15/68 covers carding machines or accessories that incorporate mechanisms to insert a permanent twist into fibers during the preliminary treatment stage, effectively creating a twisted strand before the spinning process. This arrangement enables the production of yarns with built-in twist directly from the carded web.
[D01G 15/70] Arrangements for producing decorative or fancy effects in products -- The D01G 15/70 subclass covers specific arrangements incorporated in carding machines or their accessories that create decorative or fancy effects on the finished textile product. These configurations modify the carding process to produce patterns, textures or visual enhancements during preliminary fibre treatment.
[D01G 15/72] Arrangements for returning waste to be re-carded -- D01G 15/72 covers arrangements that recycle waste fibres back into the carding process. It pertains to carding machines or accessories that return off-cuts, slubs or other waste for re-carding, improving material utilization in textile fibre preparation.
[D01G 15/74] Air draught arrangements -- D01G 15/74 covers air-draught arrangements employed in carding machines or related accessories for the preliminary treatment of fibres. It includes devices that direct a controlled flow of air to assist in fibre opening, cleaning or transport during carding, thereby improving efficiency and product quality.
[D01G 15/76] Stripping or cleaning carding surfaces -- D01G 15/76 concerns methods and apparatus for stripping or cleaning the surfaces of carding machines used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning, covering devices, tools or processes that remove debris, oils or fibres adhering to card clothing, rollers or other carding components to maintain performance.
[D01G 15/763] by suction or blowing  D01G15/785 ,  D01G15/805 ,  D01G15/825  take precedence -- The CPC subclass D01G 15/763 covers carding machines or accessories that operate by suction or blowing to separate and align fibers during preliminary treatment for spinning. It is situated under the broader group for textile fibre preparation and takes precedence over the more specific sub-groups D01G15/785, D01G15/805 and D01G15/825.
[D01G 15/766] Cleaning hand tools -- D01G 15/766 covers hand-operated cleaning tools used in the preliminary treatment of fibres such as carding, including brushes, combs and similar devices that prepare fibres for spinning.
[D01G 15/78] Arrangements for stripping flats -- The code D01G 15/78 covers the specific mechanisms on carding machines that remove flat layers of fibre, known as flats, from the card surface during preliminary fibre treatment. These arrangements improve fibre disentanglement and uniformity before spinning, enhancing the efficiency of the carding process.
[D01G 15/785] by suction or blowing -- The D01G 15/785 subclass covers carding machines or accessories that employ suction or blowing to separate, align, or transport fibers during the preliminary treatment of textile fibres for spinning. It includes designs where air flow is used to move fibres through the carding zone, replace mechanical pick-up, or enhance fibre removal from the card clothing. This method is applied in textile processing equipment to improve fibre handling efficiency.
[D01G 15/80] Arrangements for stripping cylinders or rollers -- This sub-group relates to mechanisms that detach or strip the fibre web from the surface of cylinders or rollers in carding machines and related accessories, such as stripping plates, blades or air-jet devices, employed during the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning.
[D01G 15/805] by suction or blowing -- D01G 15/805 covers carding machines or accessories that use suction or blowing to treat fibers during preliminary processing such as opening and cleaning before spinning. The method directs airflow to separate, align, or transport fibers within the carding zone, improving fiber handling and uniformity.
[D01G 15/82] Arrangements for confining or removing dust, fly or the like -- D01G 15/82 covers devices integrated in carding machines or their accessories that capture or eliminate dust, fly or similar airborne particles generated during preliminary fibre treatment. The arrangements may include dust hoods, suction systems, filters or other confinement mechanisms to keep the work area clean and protect equipment and operators.
[D01G 15/825] by suction or blowing -- The CPC code D01G 15/825 covers carding or related fibre-preparation processes that employ suction or blowing to separate, clean, and align fibres before spinning, typically as part of a carding machine or its accessories. This method uses airflow to transport or disperse fibres, assisting in the preliminary treatment of textiles and flexible materials.
[D01G 15/84] Card clothing -- D01G 15/84 covers the clothing-wire, synthetic or other surfaces-mounted on carding machines that condition, align and separate fibres during the preliminary treatment before spinning, including the design, materials and mounting of the card cloth.
[D01G 15/86] with flexible non-metallic backing -- This CPC subclass D01G 15/86 covers carding machines or accessories that incorporate a flexible non-metallic backing. It falls under preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning within the broader textiles category.
[D01G 15/88] formed from metal sheets or strips -- D01G 15/88 covers carding machines or accessories whose carding elements are formed from metal sheets or strips, used in the preliminary treatment of fibres such as carding before spinning. This subclass specifies that the working parts that engage the fibres are metal-formed rather than wire or polymer, providing increased durability and precision in fibre handling.
[D01G 15/90] Lags, e.g. for jute cards -- Lags are auxiliary wire or metal elements used in carding machines, especially for jute, to guide, space or temporarily hold fibres as they pass through the carding zone. They help control fibre flow and improve the uniformity of the preliminary fibre web before spinning.
[D01G 15/92] Attaching card clothing to carding elements -- The code D01G 15/92 relates to the step of fixing the card clothing - the wire or synthetic surface that carries fibers - onto the carding elements of a carding machine. It includes devices and procedures for securing the clothing to rollers, fingers or other moving parts used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning.
[D01G 15/94] Burr-crushing or removing arrangements -- The D01G 15/94 subclass covers burr-crushing or removal arrangements employed in carding machines or related accessories for the preliminary treatment of fibres. These devices are designed to eliminate or flatten burrs and other surface irregularities on fibres before spinning.
[D01G 15/96] Burr-crushing rollers -- Burr-crushing rollers are specialized components of carding machines that flatten or crush burrs and irregularities on fibres before spinning, improving fibre alignment and reducing breakage. They work by applying pressure through rotating, grooved cylinders to smooth the material.
[D01G 15/98] Morel or like apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Morel cylinder devices, where one cylinder has openings adapted to trap wool fibres entangled with burrs, while a cooperating cylinder will beat the burrs out.
[D01G 17/00] Silk-dressing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A (rare) carding process for individualising silk staple fibres.
[D01G 19/00] Combing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The combing out of short fibres (noils) from a carded fibre mass, in order to retain the longer fibres. The fibres are generally fed to and from the combing machine in sliver form.
[D01G 19/02] with pinned circles, e.g. Noble -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The so-called Noble combing principle where rotating pinned elements act on a passing fibre mass to remove short fibres from it.
[D01G 19/04] with pinned cylinders, e.g. rectilinear -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The combing technique (also called Heilmann combing technique), where combed fibre tufts are drawn out in linear manner from the combing area and are superposed.
[D01G 19/06] Details
[D01G 19/08] Feeding apparatus -- D01G 19/08 is a CPC subclass that covers feeding apparatus for combing machines used in the preliminary treatment of fibres in textile production, providing a continuous supply of material to enable efficient separation and alignment of fibres before spinning.
[D01G 19/10] Construction, mounting, or operating features of combing elements -- The CPC subclass D01G 19/10 relates to the design, attachment and functional aspects of combing elements in machines that preliminarily treat fibres for spinning. It covers features such as the shape, mounting method, drive mechanisms and adjustments of the combs that straighten, align or clean fibres before they enter further textile processes. This subclass sits under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and preliminary treatment of fibres.
[D01G 19/105] Combing cylinders  needle bars  D01H5/14
[D01G 19/12] Devices for laying or holding fibres in combs, e.g. dabbing brushes -- The CPC subclass D01G 19/12 covers devices used to place or retain fibres in combs during preliminary treatment, such as dabbing brushes that distribute fibres evenly before spinning. These tools help improve fibre alignment and consistency in textile processing.
[D01G 19/14] Drawing-off and delivery apparatus -- D01G 19/14 covers drawing-off and delivery apparatus used in textile processing, specifically machines that withdraw yarn or filament from combing or spinning equipment and guide it to subsequent stages such as winding, clearing or further treatment. The devices typically include rollers, guides, take-up mechanisms or other means for continuous handling and delivery of the drawn material.
[D01G 19/16] Nipper mechanisms -- Nipper mechanisms are components of combing machines used in the preliminary treatment of fibers for spinning. They grasp and release the fiber sliver or roving, providing precise feed and separation during the combing process.
[D01G 19/18] Roller, or roller and apron, devices, e.g. operating to draw-off fibres continuously -- The D01G 19/18 subclass covers devices that use a roller alone or a roller together with an apron to continuously draw off fibers during preliminary treatment such as combing. These mechanisms are employed to feed fibers uniformly for subsequent processes like spinning.
[D01G 19/20] operating to draw-off fibres intermittently -- The subclass D01G 19/20 covers equipment or methods that intermittently draw off fibers during preliminary treatment such as combing, preparing them for subsequent spinning. It includes mechanisms that periodically pull fibers from a moving web or sliver rather than a continuous draw-off.
[D01G 19/22] Arrangements for removing, or disposing of, noil or waste -- The CPC subclass D01G 19/22 covers arrangements that remove or dispose of noil and other waste generated by combing machines during the preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning. It includes devices such as suction systems, conveyors, collection bins or other mechanisms that separate short fibres and debris from the main fibre stream to keep the process efficient.
[D01G 19/24] Framework -- D01G 19/24 covers the structural framework of combing machines used in the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning. It addresses the supporting elements, chassis and arrangement that hold combing drums, pins or other working parts in place, ensuring proper alignment and stability during the fibre processing operation.
[D01G 19/26] Driving arrangements -- The subclass D01G 19/26 of the CPC concerns the driving arrangements employed in combing machines for the preliminary treatment of fibres, covering gear, belt, chain, hydraulic, pneumatic or other power-transmission mechanisms that power rollers or pins and control speed and torque.
[D01G 19/28] Air draught or like pneumatic arrangements -- Air draught or like pneumatic arrangements refer to the use of directed air streams or suction devices to transport, separate, or align fibers during the preliminary treatment of textiles, such as combing machines, to prepare the material for spinning.
[D01G 19/30] Heating arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. heated crimping boxes, to increase fibre cohesion.
[D01G 21/00] Combinations of machines, apparatus, or processes, e.g. for continuous processing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Partial or complete processing lines with a combination of fibre preparation machines and process steps; e.g. combed cotton producing line.
[D01G 23/00] Feeding fibres to machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and processes for feeding fibres to machines.
[D01G 23/02] Hoppers -- In the CPC class D01G 23/02, hoppers are defined as containers or devices that receive and feed fibres to spinning or processing machines, ensuring a regulated flow by gravity, vibration or mechanical agitation. They are used in preliminary fibre-treatment steps within textile or flexible-material manufacturing, serving as intermediate storage and feed units.
[D01G 23/04] with means for controlling the feed -- This subclass covers devices that feed fibres to machines while incorporating mechanisms to regulate the feed rate or quantity. It includes systems with sensors, controllers or adjustable openings that provide controlled fibre delivery during preliminary treatment such as carding or spinning. The code D01G 23/04 designates these feed-control features.
[D01G 23/045] by successive weighing; Weighing hoppers -- D01G 23/045 is a CPC subclass for textile processing that deals with feeding fibres to machines by successive weighing. It covers weighing hoppers that deliver measured quantities of fibre to spinning or other treatment equipment, allowing precise control of the feed rate during preliminary fibre preparation.
[D01G 23/06] Arrangements in which a machine or apparatus is regulated in response to changes in the volume or weight of fibres fed, e.g. piano motions -- The subclass covers fibre-feeding arrangements that automatically adjust their operation in response to the instantaneous volume or weight of fibres entering the machine, using sensors, mechanical linkages or cam-type mechanisms such as piano motions to vary speed, feed rate or stroke and maintain a consistent supply.
[D01G 23/08] Air draught or like pneumatic arrangements -- This subclass covers pneumatic systems that create an air flow to transport fibres into processing machines during the preliminary treatment stage before spinning. It includes devices such as air draughts, suction tubes and blow-through arrangements that feed loose fibres from storage or cleaning units to spinning or carding equipment. The focus is on using air currents rather than mechanical conveyors.
[D01G 25/00] Lap-forming devices not integral with machines specified above -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibre packing devices, that wind fibre strands into a compact package (lap) that is easier to transport, e.g.:
[D01G 27/00] Lap- or sliver-winding devices, e.g. for products of cotton scutchers, jute cards, or worsted gill boxes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The re-packaging of laps or slivers into bobbins, in order to accommodate specific format requirements.
[D01G 27/02] with lap-roll or the like loaded to provide firm packages -- The CPC subclass D01G 27/02 covers devices that wind a lap-roll or similar carrier to produce tightly packed fibre strands during preliminary fibre treatment such as spinning preparation. It specifically relates to lap- or sliver-winding machines used with cotton scutchers, jute cards or worsted gill boxes.
[D01G 27/04] with automatic discharge of lap-roll or the like -- A lap- or sliver-winding device that automatically releases the accumulated lap or roll, used in the preliminary treatment of fibres such as cotton, jute or worsted during processes like scutching, carding or gilling. The mechanism speeds handling of the wound material and reduces manual intervention.
[D01G 29/00] Arrangements for lubricating fibres, e.g. in gill boxes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The application of lubricant, e.g. by spraying during fibre preparation.  Lubricant can be sprayed in drafting machines to enable a better fibre gliding or a better penetration of control pins in so-called gill boxes (i.e. fibre control units with pins, placed between drafting rollers in long staple drafting arrangements).
[D01G 3/00] Roughening of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical and wet treatments that affect the surface of natural fibres e.g. sanding of fibres, degreasing of wool fibres.  Typically, techniques that increase surface roughness or permeability (for higher inter-fibre cohesion, higher absorption, higher binding affinity with polymers and the like).
[D01G 31/00] Warning or safety devices, e.g. automatic fault detectors, stop motions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Warning and safety devices, as well as quality monitoring devices applied to spinning preparation machines; e.g. evenness monitors of a card output; sliver breaking detectors etc.  **Glossary:** - Warning: monitoring, as monitoring systems are used to warn in case of value deviation and/or stop the machine.
[D01G 31/003] Detection and removal of impurities -- The CPC entry D01G 31/003 covers devices that detect and remove impurities in the preliminary treatment of textile or flexible material fibres, such as during spinning. It includes safety-related or fault-detecting mechanisms that automatically identify contaminants and stop the process to prevent damage.
[D01G 31/006] On-line measurement and recording of process and product parameters -- D01G 31/006 deals with on-line monitoring and data logging of parameters during preliminary fibre treatment processes, such as tension, temperature, speed or moisture, to detect abnormalities and activate warning or safety actions like automatic stop mechanisms.
[D01G 33/00] Hand tools for treatment of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary devices used by hand to facilitate intervention of fibres; e.g. superposing sliver ends in a comb to create a sliver junction.
[D01G 35/00] Treatment of oakum -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The cleaning and opening of entangled or agglomerated flax or hemp fibres, to obtain a parallelised fibre bundle (e.g. to be used as joint in connecting pipes).  **Glossary:** - Oakum: a preparation of tarred fibre (e.g. hemp or jute) used in shipbuilding, for packing the joints of timbers in wooden vessels and the deck planking of ships, as well as cast iron plumbing applications.
[D01G 5/00] Separating, e.g. sorting, fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibre sorting mechanisms and methods other than silk-dressing machines and combing, e.g. for preparing the natural fibres for a length diagram measurement step.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Silk-dressing machines -> D01G17/00 - Combing machines -> D01G19/00
[D01G 7/00] Breaking or opening fibre bales -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The opening of compressed bales of fibres into workable fibre tufts; e.g. sawtooth grinding of cotton bales.
[D01G 7/02] by means of beater arms -- The CPC subclass D01G 7/02 covers the step of breaking or opening fibre bales by means of beater arms, a technique used in the preliminary treatment of fibres such as cotton before spinning. This process falls under the broader category of textile preparation, specifically the opening of fibres in flexible material manufacturing.
[D01G 7/04] by means of toothed members -- This subclass concerns the preliminary opening or breaking of fibre bales by using toothed members such as combs, toothed rollers or similar tools to separate and loosen the fibres before further processing such as spinning.
[D01G 7/06] Details of apparatus or machines -- The code D01G 7/06 covers the specific structural and functional features of machines that break or open fibre bales as a preliminary step before spinning. It includes descriptions of hammers, rollers, pneumatic or hydraulic devices and the mechanical arrangements that facilitate bale opening.
[D01G 7/08] Arrangements for feeding bales to comminuting elements -- The D01G 7/08 entry concerns devices that feed bales of fibre into crushing or grinding elements for preliminary treatment, such as breaking open or pre-processing fibre bales before spinning. It covers the arrangement of mechanisms that convey the bale to the comminuting zone.
[D01G 7/10] Arrangements for discharging fibres -- The D01G 7/10 class covers devices that convey or release fibres after they have been broken or opened from bales during the preliminary treatment stage of textile production. It includes mechanisms that transport, spread or feed the fibres to subsequent processes such as carding or spinning.
[D01G 7/12] Framework -- The D01G 7/12 subclass covers frameworks that support or hold fibre bales during the breaking or opening process, such as racks, plates, or conveyor-type structures that maintain bale position while fibres are loosened for further treatment. It defines the mechanical arrangement used to keep the material stable during preliminary fibre preparation.
[D01G 7/14] Driving arrangements -- D01G 7/14 covers the mechanical and motorised systems that supply power to equipment for breaking or opening fibre bales, including the types of drives, gearings, and transmission arrangements used to move the breaking tools in preliminary fibre-treatment processes.
[D01G 9/00] Opening or cleaning fibres, e.g. scutching cotton -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The separation of remaining non-fibre elements (e.g. seeds, vegetal contaminants) from fibres in fibre opening and cleaning apparatuses, and the opening i.e. loosening of the fibres, in order to obtain better fibre individualisation for example before carding. Typically, the group refers to blowroom equipment.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The processing of flax stalks in order to extract the flax fibres from the stalk -> D01B1/14 .
[D01G 9/02] by agitation within a moving receptacle -- The subclass D01G 9/02 covers processes for opening or cleaning fibers, such as scutching cotton, by agitating them inside a moving receptacle. It falls under preliminary treatment of fibers for spinning within the broader textiles and flexible-material categories.
[D01G 9/04] by means of beater arms -- The CPC subclass D01G 9/04 relates to the preliminary treatment of fibers, specifically the opening or cleaning of fibers such as cotton by means of beater arms, a process used before spinning. It is situated in the hierarchy under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and preliminary treatment of fibers.
[D01G 9/06] by means of toothed members -- A method for opening or cleaning fibres that employs toothed elements such as combs, rollers or teeth to separate, disentangle or remove impurities from the material before it is spun. This technique is used in preliminary textile treatment processes like scutching cotton.
[D01G 9/08] by means of air draught arrangements -- D01G 9/08 covers the use of air draught arrangements to open or clean fibers during preliminary treatment such as scutching cotton. The method directs a draft of air to separate, fluff or remove impurities from the fiber mass before spinning.
[D01G 9/10] using foraminous cylinders -- The D01G 9/10 subgroup covers processes that employ perforated, screen-like cylinders to open, clean or separate fibres during the preliminary treatment stage before spinning. The foraminous cylinder allows fibres to be fed through its openings, facilitating removal of impurities, alignment and individualisation of the material, such as in scutching or other fibre-opening operations.
[D01G 9/12] Combinations of opening or cleaning machines -- D01G 9/12 covers apparatus that combines two or more fibre-opening or cleaning operations in a single machine, such as integrating scutching, carding or other preliminary treatment steps for cotton, wool or similar fibres. The classification relates to equipment designed to perform multiple opening or cleaning functions simultaneously to streamline fibre preparation before spinning.
[D01G 9/14] Details of machines or apparatus -- D01G 9/14 covers the specific design and components of machines or apparatus used in the opening or cleaning of fibres, such as cotton scutching, within textile processing. It details structural features, mechanisms, and functional elements that enable the preliminary treatment of fibres before spinning.
[D01G 9/16] Feeding arrangements -- D01G 9/16 relates to devices and mechanisms that supply fibers to machines used for opening, cleaning or otherwise preparing them for spinning, such as conveyors, feeders and feeding stations that move fibers from storage to the processing unit in textile manufacturing.
[D01G 9/18] Arrangements for discharging fibres -- The code D01G 9/18 covers devices and systems that remove or discharge processed fibres from machines used in opening or cleaning operations such as scutching of cotton. It includes arrangements that convey, blow or otherwise separate fibres from the treatment zone to collection or subsequent processing.
[D01G 9/20] Framework -- D01G 9/20 - Framework refers to the structural arrangement or supporting system of machinery employed in the opening or cleaning of fibres, such as the frame that holds scutching or other preliminary treatment equipment. It is situated in the textile hierarchy for early fibre processing.
[D01G 9/22] Driving arrangements -- The D01G 9/22 subclass covers the mechanisms that provide power or motion to equipment used in the preliminary treatment of fibres, such as opening, cleaning or scutching of cotton and other textile raw materials. It includes drives, motors, gearing and other arrangements that propel the fibres through the processing device.
[D01G 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- D01G 99/00 refers to any preliminary treatment of fibres for spinning that falls outside the specific groups defined in this subclass, covering miscellaneous preparatory steps such as cleaning, conditioning or other actions applied to textiles or flexible materials before they are spun.
[D01G 99/005] Conditioning of textile fibre during treatment before spinning  conditioning during spinning or twisting  D01H13/30 -- This CPC entry D01G 99/005 belongs to the textiles hierarchy and addresses conditioning of textile fibre during pre-spinning treatments, including conditioning while spinning or twisting, for subject matter not covered by other groups in the subclass.
[D01H] SPINNING OR TWISTING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The spinning or twisting of staple fibre yarns (i.e. comprising fibres of discrete length); e.g. cotton or wool spinning.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Twisting oakum -> D01G35/00 - Crimping or curling of fibres, filaments or yarns -> D02G1/00  **Glossary:** - Fibre: A relatively-short, elongated member of natural or artificial material - Filament: An endless or quasi-endless, elongated member of natural (e.g. silk) or artificial material - Yarn: A unitary assembly of fibres, usually produced by spinning - Thread: An assembly of yarns or filaments, usually produced by twisting - Drawing: Stretching and elongating yarns or filaments - Draft: The amount to which yarns or filaments are drawn
[D01H 1/00] Kinds or types of spinning or twisting machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the spinning processes where twist is inserted into a fibre strand, without interrupting the fibre strand.
[D01H 1/003] Twisting machines in which twist is imparted from the supply packages, e.g. uptwist -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the cases where the supply package provides the twist.
[D01H 1/006] Twisting machines in which twist is imparted at the paying-out and take-up stations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the cases where twist comes from the collecting bobbin, and corresponds for example to ring spinning and similar processes.
[D01H 1/02] ring type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  a spinning process in which a yarn is twisted and wound onto a revolving bobbin surrounded by a ring that moves up-and down, and caries a traveller set in motion by the yarn.
[D01H 1/025] with a condensing device between drafting system and spinning unit -- This subclass covers spinning or twisting machines that incorporate a condensing device located between the drafting system and the spinning unit, where the device compresses or consolidates the drafted fiber strands before they are spun. It pertains to textile equipment that integrates fiber conditioning directly in the feed path.
[D01H 1/04] flyer type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  a spinning process in which a yarn (typically coarse woollen yarn) is twisted by a rotating flyer arm, that winds it onto a bobbin that is free to rotate.
[D01H 1/06] cap type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  a spinning process in which a yarn (typically woollen yarn) is twisted and wound onto a revolving bobbin situated inside a stationary cap.
[D01H 1/08] cup, pot or disc type, in which annular masses of yarn are formed by centrifugal action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  centrifugal spinning, where a yarn mass (or cake) is formed inside or on a rotating element, e.g. a pot, which at the same time imparts twist and collects the yarn.
[D01H 1/10] for imparting multiple twist, e.g. two-for-one twisting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  twisting of several yarns, whereby one spindle turn causes several twist turns in the assembled yarn (obtained e.g. by multiple yarn reversal points in the yarn run path).
[D01H 11/00] Arrangements for confining or removing dust, fly or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning of running surfaces in open-end spinning machines -> D01H4/22
[D01H 11/001] Hand tools used for cleaning the machines -- D01H 11/001 pertains to hand-held devices used to clean textile or spinning equipment, specifically tools that remove dust, lint, flies or similar contaminants from machines. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, spinning or twisting, and arrangements for confining or removing dust, fly or the like.
[D01H 11/003] with a rotary pin -- A D01H 11/003 arrangement for confining or removing dust, fly or similar contaminants in spinning or twisting operations that incorporates a rotary pin to sweep, capture or discharge particles from the work zone, typically used in textile machinery to maintain clean fibers and improve product quality.
[D01H 11/005] with blowing and/or suction devices  in general  A47L7/00 ; in cooperation with thread breakage detecting means  D01H13/1691
[D01H 11/006] travelling along the machines -- This subclass covers devices that travel along spinning or twisting machines to confine or remove dust, fly or similar pollutants. It includes rollers, tracks, carriers or other mechanisms that move over the machine's length while collecting or directing contaminants away from the work area.
[D01H 11/008] with static field means -- This CPC subclass covers textile spinning or twisting equipment that uses electrostatic or static-field devices to attract and capture dust, fly, or similar particles generated during processing. The static field means create an electric charge that draws airborne contaminants onto grounded plates or electrodes, improving cleanliness and product quality. It is a specialised arrangement for removing dust from textile production lines.
[D01H 1/101] in which multiple twist is imparted at the take-up stations -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/101 covers machines for spinning or twisting where several twists are added at the take-up stations. It pertains to textile equipment that imparts multiple twist actions simultaneously as the yarn is wound off, a feature used to control yarn strength and texture. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 1/103] Two-for-one twisting -- The CPC code D01H 1/103, titled Two-for-one twisting, concerns a specific type of spinning or twisting machine that combines two yarns into a single twist in one operation. It is classified under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 1/105] Arrangements using hollow spindles, i.e. the yarns are running through the spindle of the unwound bobbins -- D01H 1/105 refers to spinning or twisting machines that use hollow spindles, enabling yarn to run through the spindle as the bobbin unwinds. The arrangement guides the yarn directly through the spindle for continuous processing in textile production.
[D01H 1/106] Two-for-one twisting -- Two-for-one twisting machines process two yarns simultaneously, twisting them together in a single operation to produce a balanced ply. The equipment typically feeds two individual strands and combines them by rotating a pair of twist elements, allowing efficient production of double-ply yarns or twisted fabrics. This type falls under the CPC subclass for spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 1/108] for two or more supply bobbins one on top of the other -- This CPC subclass (D01H 1/108) pertains to spinning or twisting machines that draw yarn from two or more supply bobbins arranged one on top of the other. It is classified under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and specifically addresses the vertical stacking of bobbins as a feeding arrangement.
[D01H 1/11] Spinning by false-twisting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  spinning techniques like friction spinning or air jet spinning, provided there is no fibre flow interruption.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Increasing the strength of a roving or sliver during drafting -> D01H5/28 - Twisting by false twisting -> D01H7/92 - Increasing the strength of a roving or sliver by false-twisting -> D01H7/92
[D01H 1/115] using pneumatic means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  air-jet spinning by false twist.
[D01H 1/14] Details
[D01H 1/16] Framework -- The D01H 1/16 "Framework" class covers the structural components and overall configuration of machines used for spinning or twisting textile or flexible-material fibers. It pertains to the design of the machine's supporting frame, chassis or skeleton that holds the functional elements together. This falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and types of spinning or twisting equipment.
[D01H 1/162] for ring type -- The CPC code D01H 1/162 designates machines that perform ring-type spinning, a method where fibers are drawn out and twisted into yarn by a rotating ring and traveler. It includes ring frames and related equipment used in textile production.
[D01H 1/164] for flyer type -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/164 covers spinning or twisting machines that employ a flyer system, where a rotating holder (the flyer) carries the roving or yarn and imparts twist as it moves around a spindle. This design is typical of ring-spinning equipment, distinguishing it from other flyer-type mechanisms such as rotor or air-jet machines.
[D01H 1/166] for two-for-one type -- D01H 1/166 designates spinning or twisting machines that operate on a two-for-one principle, processing two separate yarns or fibers simultaneously within a single unit to produce a combined twisted product. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and kinds or types of spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 1/168] Arrangements for the sound-dampening of the machines  in general  G10K11/00 -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/168 relates to general arrangements for sound-dampening of spinning or twisting machines used in textile production. It falls under the hierarchy textiles → textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for → spinning or twisting → kinds or types of spinning or twisting machines, and is shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[D01H 1/18] Supports for supply packages -- D01H 1/18 covers devices that hold or guide yarn or fiber supply packages in spinning or twisting equipment. The class includes reels, spools, bobbins, carriers and similar supports that feed material to the machine while maintaining tension and alignment. These elements are designed for textile processes where a continuous feed of fibers is required.
[D01H 1/183] Overhead suspension devices -- Overhead suspension devices are mechanisms that support and position spinning or twisting machines from above, allowing the equipment to be suspended in a fixed or adjustable manner during operation. They facilitate precise alignment, reduce floor space, and improve safety and maintenance access in textile processing lines.
[D01H 1/186] for supplying from cans -- This subclass covers spinning or twisting machines that are equipped to feed material directly from cans, allowing continuous supply of fibers or yarn during the textile processing operation. It pertains to the specific arrangement or mechanism for dispensing the raw material from canned containers into the machine.
[D01H 1/20] Driving or stopping arrangements -- The entry D01H 1/20 covers the driving and stopping mechanisms used in spinning or twisting machines, including motor drives, clutches, brakes and gear arrangements that start, control or halt the spindle. These systems provide the power transmission and control needed for textile yarn formation.
[D01H 1/22] for rollers -- The CPC code D01H 1/22 classifies spinning or twisting machines that incorporate rollers as part of the process, covering the design, arrangement and operation of rollers used to feed, draw, or twist fibers during textile production. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 1/24] for twisting arrangements, e.g. spindles -- The D01H 1/24 subclass covers machines that provide twisting arrangements for yarn or filament, such as spindles used in textile spinning. It includes devices that impart twist to fibers during processing, detailing the mechanisms and configurations that achieve the desired twist. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting equipment.
[D01H 1/241] driven by belt -- In CPC subclass D01H 1/241 the focus is on spinning or twisting machines whose drive is transmitted by a belt. This includes equipment where a motor powers the spindle or drive shaft through a belt system, a common arrangement in textile production machinery.
[D01H 1/242] driven by toothed wheels -- D01H 1/242 covers spinning or twisting machines that are powered by toothed wheels, describing a drive method that uses gear-like wheels to transmit motion. The entry falls under the textiles subclass for flexible materials, specifically the types of machines used for spinning or twisting.
[D01H 1/243] driven by friction discs -- D01H 1/243 refers to spinning or twisting machines in which the drive is supplied by friction discs that grip the material to be processed, transmitting torque through surface contact. This subclass is used for equipment handling flexible fibers or yarns where friction-disc drives replace gears or belts.
[D01H 1/244] each spindle driven by an electric motor -- The D01H 1/244 subclass covers spinning or twisting machines in which each spindle is powered separately by an electric motor, providing individual drive control for each yarn or thread. This arrangement is used to improve speed, tension regulation and product uniformity in textile production.
[D01H 1/26] with two or more speeds -- This subclass groups spinning or twisting machines that are fitted with two or more selectable speeds, enabling the operator to change the rotational rate during operation. It falls under CPC D01H 1/26, a category of textile equipment dealing with kinds of spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 1/28] for two or more machine elements possessing different characteristics but in operative association -- A subclass of CPC for spinning or twisting machines in which two or more machine elements with differing characteristics operate together, such as combined drafting and twist-forming units or paired rollers of dissimilar size that collaborate to process yarn or filament. This falls under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and specifically the category of spinning or twisting machinery.
[D01H 1/30] with two or more speeds -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/30 pertains to spinning or twisting machines that are capable of operating at two or more speeds, providing variable speed control for processing fibers and yarns. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 13/00] Other common constructional features, details or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary devices of spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 13/005] Service carriages travelling along the machines  characteristics relating to the apparatus supported by the carriage,  see  relevant groups, e.g.  D01H9/005 ,  D01H9/10 ,  D01H13/145 ,  D01H15/00 -- The code D01H 13/005 covers service carriages that move along textile or flexible-material spinning machines, focusing on the design and functional features of the carriage itself and any apparatus it carries, such as tools, spindles or auxiliary units.
[D01H 13/02] Roller arrangements not otherwise provided for -- The D01H 13/02 subclass covers roller configurations used in textile or flexible-material processes that are not specifically defined in other subclasses. It includes any non-standard arrangement of rollers employed for spinning, twisting or related operations, such as unusual positioning, drive mechanisms or auxiliary components.
[D01H 13/04] Guides for slivers, rovings, or yarns -- The code D01H 13/04 covers guides used in the textile spinning process to direct slivers, rovings or yarns as they travel through equipment, ensuring proper alignment and tension. These devices may be fixed or adjustable and are used to prevent snarling, improve feed consistency, and support downstream operations such as drafting, roving or weaving.
[D01H 13/045] Guide tube -- The CPC subclass D01H 13/045 covers guide tubes employed in textile spinning or twisting operations to direct the flow of yarn, filament or rovings during drafting, drawing or winding. These components, typically made of metal or plastic, serve as tubular conduits that control strand path and tension, facilitating consistent fabric formation.
[D01H 1/305] Speed control of the spindles in response to the displacements of the ring rail -- This CPC subclass relates to textile spinning or twisting equipment where the spindle speed is automatically regulated in response to the displacement of the ring rail, helping maintain consistent yarn tension and quality. It is part of the D01H 1/300 series covering speed-control methods for spindles.
[D01H 13/06] Traversing arrangements -- D01H 13/06 concerns traversing arrangements in textile or flexible-material machines, specifically mechanisms that move yarn, filament or similar elements laterally across the work area. It includes guides, rollers, cams or other devices that cause a crosswise or oscillating motion to position the material for subsequent processing steps. This subclass falls under spinning or twisting techniques for textiles.
[D01H 13/08] Twist arresters -- Twist arresters are devices or structural features incorporated in yarns or fibers during spinning or twisting to limit the amount of twist transmitted along the thread, preventing excessive twist buildup that can cause breakage or distortion. They commonly take the form of knots, stops, or other mechanical elements that impede rotation while allowing feed.
[D01H 13/10] Tension devices -- D01H 13/10 covers devices that apply or control tension on yarns, fibers or flexible materials during spinning or twisting operations. It includes mechanisms such as tension rollers, bars, springs or clamps that maintain a constant pull to regulate draft, prevent breakage and ensure uniform construction of the textile product.
[D01H 13/102] Regulating tension by regulating delivery of yarn from supply package  D01H13/108  takes precedence
[D01H 13/104] Regulating tension by devices acting on running yarn and not associated with supply or take-up devices -- D01H 13/104 pertains to devices that control the tension of a running yarn by acting on the yarn itself during processing, distinct from any supply or take-up mechanisms.
[D01H 13/106] for double-twist spindle -- D01H 13/106 deals with double-twist spindles employed in textile spinning or twisting operations. The classification covers spindles that impart two concurrent twists to the yarn, enhancing strength, uniformity or special effects. It is a constructional feature or accessory within the broader category of spinning equipment.
[D01H 13/108] Regulating tension by regulating speed of driving mechanisms of unwinding, paying-out, forwarding, winding or depositing devices, e.g. automatically in response to variations in tension -- The D01H 13/108 subclass covers methods and devices that control the tension of yarn or web material by adjusting the speed of the driving mechanisms in unwinding, paying-out, forwarding, winding or depositing equipment. It includes automatic systems that sense tension variations and modify motor speed to maintain a consistent pull on the material.
[D01H 13/12] Arrangements preventing snarls or inadvertent doubling of yarns -- D01H 13/12 refers to textile or flexible material inventions that provide arrangements or accessories to stop yarns from snarling or unintentionally doubling during spinning or twisting, such as guides, tensioners or other constructional features that keep the yarns separate and prevent tangles.
[D01H 13/14] Warning or safety devices, e.g. automatic fault detectors, stop motions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Warning and safety devices, as well as online measurement arrangements able to provide a warning signal; e.g. yarn breakage detection on ring spinning machine.
[D01H 13/145] set on carriages travelling along the machines; Warning or safety devices pulled along the working unit by a band or the like -- A set of accessories mounted on the carriages that move along a spinning or twisting machine, including warning or safety devices that are drawn with the working unit by a band or similar means. These components are part of the machine's auxiliary constructional features.
[D01H 13/16] responsive to reduction in material tension, failure of supply, or breakage, of material -- D01H 13/16 pertains to textile or flexible material processes that include mechanisms which react to a loss of tension, interruption of material feed, or breakage of the material, adjusting the spinning or twisting operation accordingly. It falls under other common constructional features, details or accessories within the spinning or twisting subgroup.
[D01H 13/1608] where the paying-out and take-up stations are stopped at one and the same time -- It relates to textile spinning or twisting processes where the paying-out and take-up stations are stopped at the same time, a constructional feature used to control filament tension or synchronize start-stop actions. This detail falls under other common constructional features, details or accessories.
[D01H 13/1616] characterised by the detector -- This CPC subclass covers textile or flexible-material articles that are characterised by the inclusion of a detector, i.e., a device that senses or measures a physical, chemical or biological parameter. It includes fabrics, yarns or finished textile products with integrated sensors or transducers for monitoring temperature, pressure, moisture, gases or other stimuli.
[D01H 13/1625] Electro-mechanical actuators -- D01H 13/1625 covers electro-mechanical actuators used in textile or flexible material processes, particularly in spinning or twisting operations. The entry includes devices that transform electrical energy into controlled mechanical motion to drive rollers, guides, or tensioning elements, such as electric motors, piezoelectric or shape-memory components, and their associated control mechanisms.
[D01H 13/1633] Electronic actuators -- This subclass covers electronic actuators employed in the processing of textiles or flexible materials, specifically for spinning or twisting operations and related constructional features or accessories. It includes devices that convert electrical energy into mechanical motion to control yarn tension, speed, feed or other parameters in spinning machinery.
[D01H 13/1641] Capacitor sensing means -- The CPC subclass D01H 13/1641 covers capacitor-sensing means incorporated in textiles or flexible material processes, particularly within spinning or twisting operations. It includes devices or methods that detect changes in capacitance to monitor parameters such as tension, moisture, or deformation of fibers, serving as a constructional accessory rather than a primary textile product.
[D01H 13/165] Photo-electric sensing means -- The D01H 13/165 subclass covers photo-electric sensing devices employed in textile or flexible-material spinning and twisting processes. It includes sensors that detect light interruptions or reflections to monitor yarn position, tension, speed, or other parameters, enabling automatic control of equipment and quality inspection.
[D01H 13/1658] Associated actuators with mutual actuation, e.g. for two or more running yarns -- This CPC subclass covers devices where two or more yarns are driven by linked actuators that operate together, such as paired feed rollers or twin spindles that synchronize motion. It pertains to textile machinery for spinning or twisting that employs mutual actuation of running yarns.
[D01H 13/1666] Lighting or luminous devices making easier the setting of the breakage of yarns -- The D01H 13/1666 class covers lighting or luminous devices that facilitate the detection or setting of yarn breakage in spinning operations, such as illuminated indicators, coloured threads or light-emitting elements integrated into equipment to alert the operator when a yarn snaps.
[D01H 13/1675] Pencil of rays on side of machines -- The D01H 13/1675 subclass covers a side-mounted "pencil of rays" arrangement used on spinning or twisting machines, meaning a series of slender, ray-like elements attached to the machine's side to guide, support or separate fibres or yarns during processing. It falls under the broader category of other common constructional features, details or accessories for textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for.
[D01H 13/1683] Pneumatic sensing means -- D01H 13/1683 covers pneumatic sensing means incorporated in spinning or twisting of textiles or flexible materials, such as air-filled sensors that monitor tension, speed or position of yarns and fibers during processing. These devices are classified under other common constructional features, details or accessories for textile production.
[D01H 13/1691] Thread breakage detector means associated with pneumatic cleaning devices, e.g. suction of broken end of yarn -- The CPC code D01H 13/1691 covers devices that detect yarn breakage and are integrated with pneumatic cleaning systems, such as mechanisms that suction the broken yarn end away from the spinning area. It falls within the textiles hierarchy under spinning or twisting and other constructional accessories.
[D01H 13/18] stopping supply only -- D01H 13/18 covers features or accessories used in spinning or twisting operations that halt the feed of yarn or filament without affecting other parts of the process, such as stoppage devices, clamps or control mechanisms that simply cease supply. It is classified under textiles, specifically other constructional features of flexible materials, and addresses the interruption of material flow in a spinning line.
[D01H 13/181] by stopping supply packages -- The CPC subclass D01H 13/181 covers features that interrupt or halt the delivery of yarn or filament packages during spinning or twisting operations. It includes mechanisms, controls or procedures that stop the supply of a package to a processing line, preventing over-feeding or facilitating changeover.
[D01H 13/182] by raising or lifting of one of the drafting cylinders, e.g. by removing of the loading means -- D01H 13/182 covers textile spinning or twisting machines where a drafting cylinder is raised or lifted, typically by removing its loading means, to adjust the drafting zone. This mechanical variation is classified under other constructional features, details or accessories for spinning or twisting apparatuses.
[D01H 13/183] the yarn moving out of its normal path, e.g. by lateral diverting -- D01H 13/183 pertains to yarn that leaves its intended trajectory during spinning, encompassing mechanisms or methods that laterally divert or otherwise displace the yarn from its normal path, typically to control processing or achieve specific fabric effects.
[D01H 13/185] a plate moving in the nip of drafting or guiding cylinders -- The CPC subclass D01H 13/185 covers devices in textile spinning or twisting where a plate is positioned in the nip formed by drafting or guiding cylinders, serving to shape, regulate or support the yarn or filament as it passes through the cylinder pair.
[D01H 13/186] guiding or drafting cylinders moving by gravity when a yarn breakage occurs -- The code D01H 13/186 refers to guiding or drafting cylinders in textile spinning that are allowed to move by gravity when a yarn breaks, thereby reducing tension and preventing further damage. It covers mechanisms where the cylinder drops or shifts automatically upon breakage as a protective accessory in spinning or twisting processes.
[D01H 13/187] using means stopping the driving of the drafting, guiding cylinders, e.g. friction clutches -- The CPC subgroup D01H 13/187 pertains to textile spinning equipment in which the drive of drafting or guiding cylinders is halted by means such as friction clutches. It covers devices or methods that interrupt cylinder rotation to control yarn feed, forming part of the "other common constructional features, details or accessories" category for spinning or twisting processes.
[D01H 13/188] by cutting or clamping yarns or rovings -- D01H 13/188 covers techniques for processing yarns or rovings during spinning or twisting that involve cutting the material or clamping it to control length, tension or alignment. These methods are used as auxiliary constructional features or accessories in textile production to achieve desired yarn properties.
[D01H 1/32] for complete machines -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/32 refers to complete machines used for spinning or twisting in textile production, encompassing apparatuses that integrate all functional elements to convert fibers into yarn. It is situated within the hierarchy of textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and kinds or types of spinning or twisting machines.
[D01H 13/20] responsive to excessive tension or irregular operation of apparatus -- D01H 13/20 covers accessories or constructional features for spinning or twisting equipment that react to excessive tension or irregular operation, such as tension-release devices, overload protectors or automatic shut-off mechanisms, ensuring safe and continuous textile processing. It falls under the textile-related subclass dealing with other common constructional details for apparatus.
[D01H 13/22] responsive to presence of irregularities in running material -- The subclass covers textile devices or accessories that detect and react to defects, thickness changes or breaks in yarn, filament or fabric while it is being processed, for example by adjusting tension, stopping the machine or issuing an alarm.
[D01H 13/24] responsive to delivery of a measured length of material, completion of winding of a package or filling of a receptacle -- D01H 13/24 covers mechanisms in textile or flexible-material processes that sense when a predetermined length of material has been delivered, a winding is completed or a receptacle is filled, and then trigger a response such as stopping, reversing or adjusting the operation. It includes the sensors, switches and control circuits that provide this automatic termination or control.
[D01H 13/26] Arrangements facilitating the inspection or testing of yarns or the like in connection with spinning or twisting -- The subclass D01H 13/26 covers devices or arrangements built into spinning or twisting machinery that enable continuous inspection or testing of yarns during production, such as sensors, optical systems, or mechanical guides that detect defects, monitor tension, gauge diameter or other properties, thereby supporting quality control in textile manufacturing.
[D01H 13/28] Heating or cooling arrangements -- D01H 13/28 covers heating or cooling arrangements applied to textiles or flexible materials during or after spinning or twisting operations. It includes devices, methods, and accessories that regulate temperature to improve processing, finish, or performance of fabrics. The class addresses any thermal conditioning not otherwise specified in related categories.
[D01H 13/30] Moistening, sizing, oiling, waxing, colouring, or drying yarns or the like as incidental measures during spinning or twisting -- D01H 13/30 refers to the treatment of yarns during the spinning or twisting stage, encompassing incidental processes such as moistening, sizing, oiling, waxing, coloring, or drying that are applied to improve handling, strength or appearance before the yarn is fully formed.
[D01H 13/302] Moistening, e.g. for wet spinning -- D01H 13/302 covers methods and apparatus for applying moisture to fibers or filaments during the spinning process, such as wet spinning where a liquid medium is used to solidify a polymer solution into yarn. The classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification under textiles, specifically spinning or twisting, focusing on moistening steps.
[D01H 13/304] Conditioning during spinning or twisting  for carding or combing  D01G99/005 -- The D01H 13/304 subclass covers conditioning operations performed on fibers during the spinning or twisting stage, specifically when the material has already been carded or combed. It includes treatments such as moisture adjustment, heating, chemical application, or other processes that modify the fiber's physical or chemical properties to improve yarn quality before further textile production.
[D01H 13/306] by applying fluids, e.g. steam or oiling liquids -- D01H 13/306 covers textile or flexible material processes that involve applying fluids such as steam, oiling liquids or other lubricants during spinning or twisting operations, typically to improve yarn formation, reduce friction, or impart desired properties. It falls under the broader group of "other common constructional features, details or accessories" within the textile domain.
[D01H 13/308] by applying solids, e.g. wax -- This subclass covers processes for treating yarns or fabrics by applying solid substances such as wax to impart properties like water repellency, stiffness, or abrasion resistance. It relates to textile spinning or twisting operations where the solid material is applied directly to the thread or fabric as a constructional feature or accessory.
[D01H 13/32] Counting, measuring, recording or registering devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  On-line quality control and measuring devices, like length or evenness measurement.
[D01H 1/34] with two or more speeds -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/34 refers to spinning or twisting machines that provide two or more operating speeds, enabling the user to select different speed settings for processing yarn or thread. This falls under the textiles hierarchy, specifically machines for spinning or twisting flexible materials.
[D01H 1/36] Package-shaping arrangements, e.g. building motions -- The D01H 1/36 subclass covers arrangements that shape the yarn package during spinning or twisting, such as mechanisms that build up motions to form the winding pattern. It relates to specialized package-shaping devices used in textile machines.
[D01H 1/365] for flyer type -- D01H 1/365 covers spinning or twisting machines that employ a flyer mechanism, where the fiber is drawn out and twisted by a rotating flyer attached to a spindle. This sub-group classifies equipment used for flyer-type yarn production within the broader textiles and flexible materials field.
[D01H 1/38] Arrangements for winding reserve lengths of yarn on take-up packages , e.g. transfer tails -- The CPC subclass D01H 1/38 covers apparatus and methods that wind extra or reserve lengths of yarn onto take-up packages, such as transfer tails, to ensure continuous operation when the primary yarn supply is interrupted.
