It includes arrangements for storing and delivering these reserve lengths during spinning or twisting processes, enabling seamless yarn feeding without stopping the machine.
[D01H 1/385] Removing waste reserve lengths from spindles -- The code D01H 1/385 designates machines used in textile spinning or twisting that strip off waste reserve lengths still attached to spindles. It covers equipment that automatically removes these surplus yarn sections after winding, improving efficiency and product quality.
[D01H 1/40] Arrangements for connecting continuously-delivered material to bobbins or the like -- The D01H 1/40 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification concerns devices that join continuously fed yarn, filament or other flexible material to bobbins, spindles or similar take-up units. It includes mechanisms and arrangements that guide, clamp, or otherwise attach the moving material to the winding receptacle during spinning or twisting processes.
[D01H 1/42] Guards or protectors for yarns or threads, e.g. separator plates, anti-ballooning devices -- D01H 1/42 covers devices that shield or separate yarns during spinning or twisting, such as separator plates and anti-ballooning mechanisms, installed on spinning or twisting machines to prevent entanglement or uneven tension.
[D01H 1/422] Separator plates -- D01H 1/422 relates to separator plates incorporated in spinning or twisting machines for textiles, which serve to divide, align or separate individual fibers, yarn strands or tow during drafting, drafting-twisting or winding operations. These plates may be fixed or removable components positioned to improve yarn uniformity and reduce entanglement.
[D01H 1/425] Anti-ballooning rings
[D01H 1/427] Anti-ballooning cylinders, e.g. for two-for-one twist machine  with combined cleaning effect  D01H11/00 -- The code D01H 1/427 covers anti-ballooning cylinders used in spinning or twisting equipment, particularly in two-for-one twist machines, where the cylinder both prevents yarn ballooning and provides a cleaning function. These devices are installed in the machine's twisting zone to maintain yarn tension and remove debris, improving product quality and reducing downtime.
[D01H 15/00] Piecing arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The piecing (repair) of yarn interruptions due to breakage or machine stop on machines.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Piecing arrangements for open-end spinning machines -> D01H4/48 - Piecing of rovings in combination with replacing of completed packages or cans -> D01H9/005
[D01H 15/002] for false-twisting spinning machines -- This classification covers technical features related to false-twisting spinning machines, a type of equipment used in textile production to impart twist to yarns during spinning. It pertains to the piecing arrangements of such machines within the broader category of spinning or twisting processes for textiles or flexible materials.
[D01H 15/004] for centrifugal spinning machines -- The code D01H 15/004 covers the arrangement of piecing mechanisms in centrifugal spinning machines, which are used to produce continuous filaments or yarns by throwing a polymer melt or solution through small orifices while rotating at high speed. It falls under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and the spinning or twisting subgroup, detailing how parts are organized to feed, collect, or combine the spun fibres.
[D01H 15/007] for two-for-one twisting machines -- D01H 15/007 covers machines that perform two-for-one twisting, a process in which two separate yarn strands are simultaneously twisted together to form a single yarn. These devices belong to the spinning and twisting subgroup of textile technologies and are classified under flexible-material textiles.
[D01H 15/013] Carriages travelling along the machines -- D01H 15/013 relates to the design and operation of carriages that travel along textile machines during spinning or twisting processes. It covers carriage-based mechanisms that support, feed, or guide the workpiece while moving longitudinally on the machine, enabling precise positioning and continuous operation.
[D01H 17/00] Hand tools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand tools for spinning and twisting, e.g. yarn threaders, portable yarn splicers, hand tools for applying travellers to rings etc.
[D01H 17/02] Arrangements for storing ring travellers -- The CPC subclass D01H 17/02 covers devices that provide storage or holding arrangements for ring travellers used in spinning or twisting of yarns. It includes mechanisms that keep the ring travellers in place when not engaged in the drafting or winding stages of textile production.
[D01H 2700/00] Spinning or twisting machines -- D01H 2700/00 pertains to machines that spin or twist fibers into yarn, encompassing both continuous and intermittent equipment that draws, stretches, or combines filaments. It is classified under textiles, specifically textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for, in the spinning or twisting subclass.
[D01H 2700/01] Preparatory spinning machines -- D01H 2700/01 refers to preparatory spinning machines, equipment that conditions fibers before the main spinning stage by drawing, roving or otherwise drafting the material to ready it for subsequent twisting.
[D01H 2700/20] Spinning mules -- D01H 2700/20 covers spinning mules, a class of spinning or twisting machines that draw out and twist fibers into yarn in a continuous process, a historically important technology in the textile industry.
[D01H 2700/202] Carriages or their movement -- D01H 2700/202 covers the carriages and their movement in spinning or twisting machines. It addresses devices that support, guide, or transport bobbins, spindles or other workpieces during the spinning or twisting process, including the drive and positioning mechanisms.
[D01H 2700/205] Spindles or spindle control in spinning mules -- The CPC subclass D01H 2700/205 pertains to spindles and spindle-control systems employed in spinning mules, machines that draw and twist fibers into yarn. It includes designs for spindle drive, speed regulation and synchronization with the mule's carriage movement.
[D01H 2700/207] Yarn delivery rollers -- Yarn delivery rollers are components of spinning or twisting machines that guide and feed yarn from a source to subsequent processing sections, maintaining consistent tension and alignment. They typically consist of paired cylinders that rotate synchronously to pull the yarn forward while preventing snarls or breakage. This function supports smooth material flow in textile production.
[D01H 2700/21] Piecing or cleaning in spinning mules -- This CPC subclass covers processes for repairing or removing defects in spinning mules, such as piecing broken yarns or cleaning machine components during operation. It is used to classify patents concerning maintenance actions on spinning mule equipment.
[D01H 2700/22] Winding devices for spinning mules -- The CPC subclass D01H 2700/22 defines winding devices used with spinning mules, i.e., the mechanisms that collect the yarn produced by the mule and guide it onto bobbins, spools or other receiving supports. This falls under the broader category of machines for spinning or twisting textiles.
[D01H 2700/24] Spinning or twisting machines of different kinds -- The code D01H 2700/24 pertains to spinning or twisting machines of different kinds, covering equipment that performs various spinning or twisting operations on textile fibers, including machines that combine distinct mechanisms or can be interchanged to produce yarn by different methods.
[D01H 2700/242] Spinning or twisting devices wherein twist is created during winding -- A spinning or twisting device that generates the twist while the yarn is being wound, integrating the twisting action directly into the winding process to produce twisted yarns in a single operation. This type of equipment is used in textile manufacturing where twist is created during winding rather than in a separate step.
[D01H 2700/245] Conception or fabrication of drafting cylinders -- The entry covers the design, development and manufacturing of drafting cylinders used in spinning or twisting machines, where the cylinders create a controlled draft to attenuate yarn or filament bundles. It includes concepts, material selection, geometry, surface treatment and assembly methods that affect the draft performance and fabric quality.
[D01H 2700/247] Guilding means for veil or sliver on drafting systems -- A guilding means for veil or sliver on drafting systems is a device that guides the thin web of fibers (veil) or individual strands (sliver) as they pass through the drafting arrangement of a spinning or twisting machine, ensuring correct alignment and spacing before further processing. This equipment is used to control fiber flow and improve uniformity in textile production.
[D01H 3/00] Spinning or twisting machines in which the product is wound-up intermittently, e.g. mules -- **Definition:** This place covers:  a discontinuous spinning process in which the drafting and twisting stage is performed separately from the subsequent winding stage; e.g. spinning mule.
[D01H 3/02] Details
[D01H 3/04] Carriages -- The D01H 3/04 class covers the carriage mechanisms of intermittent winding machines such as spinning mules. These carriages support, guide and move the yarn package or bobbin during the winding cycle, enabling the yarn to be taken up and released in a controlled, step-by-step manner. It is a subcategory of spinning or twisting machines where the product is wound up intermittently.
[D01H 3/06] Carriages -- Carriages are the moving supports that hold and guide the spindle or bobbin in intermittent winding machines such as ring or mule spinning frames, allowing the yarn to be drawn and wound in a stepwise fashion. They are part of the carriage-spindle assembly that synchronizes feed and take-up motions.
[D01H 3/08] Drawing-out or taking-in motions -- The D01H 3/08 subclass covers mechanisms that provide drawing-out or taking-in motions in spinning or twisting machines where the yarn is wound intermittently, such as mule-type equipment. It includes the control of yarn tension and feed during the alternating draw-out and winding phases.
[D01H 3/10] Moving-creel arrangements, e.g. for twiners -- D01H 3/10 refers to moving-creel arrangements in spinning or twisting machines where the product is wound intermittently, such as mule-type equipment and twiners. The subclass deals with mechanisms that convey and position multiple creels to feed yarn continuously during intermittent winding.
[D01H 3/12] Package-shaping motions -- D01H 3/12 relates to package-shaping motions in spinning or twisting machines that wind the product intermittently, such as mule frames. It covers the movements used to form or adjust the shape of the wound package-bobbin, spool or similar-during the pause-and-draw cycles of the winding process.
[D01H 3/14] Roller-driving arrangements -- The D01H 3/14 subclass covers roller-driving arrangements used in spinning or twisting machines where the yarn or filament is wound up intermittently, such as in mule-type equipment. It addresses the mechanical means of transmitting power to the rollers that advance, tension or guide the material during the winding cycle.
[D01H 3/16] Spindle-driving arrangements -- D01H 3/16 covers spindle-driving arrangements for spinning or twisting machines that wind the product intermittently, such as mule-type equipment. It resides in the CPC hierarchy under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 3/18] Tin rollers -- The CPC subclass D01H 3/18 covers tin rollers employed in intermittent winding spinning machines such as mules. These rollers are typically mounted in the winding section to guide, tension and smooth the yarn as it is periodically drawn off the spindle, improving handling and preventing breakage during the intermittent feed cycle.
[D01H 3/20] Spindle-driving arrangements during drawing-out or backing-off -- The code D01H 3/20 concerns spindle-driving arrangements used in intermittent winding machines such as mules, specifically how the spindle is powered while the yarn is drawn out or backed off. It addresses mechanisms that control the spindle speed and torque during these phases of the spinning or twisting process.
[D01H 3/22] Spindle-driving arrangements during taking-in -- The subclass D01H 3/22 covers the drive mechanisms that power the spindle of intermittent winding spinning machines such as mules during the taking-in phase when the fiber bundle is fed onto the spindle. It includes arrangements for transmitting motion from motors or gearing to the spindle to control speed and torque while the material is being drawn in.
[D01H 3/24] Quadrant motions -- The D01H 3/24 subclass covers quadrant motions used in spinning or twisting machines where the yarn or thread is intermittently wound, such as in mule-type equipment. It pertains to the specific path or movement of the product during winding that follows a quarter-circle trajectory, distinguishing it from other winding motions in textile processing.
[D01H 3/26] Driving or stopping arrangements not otherwise provided for -- D01H 3/26 relates to the control mechanisms of intermittent winding machines, such as textile mules, that start, stop, or otherwise regulate the winding drum when the product is wound up in a stop-and-go manner. These driving or stopping arrangements are not covered by other more specific subclasses and fall under the broader category of spinning or twisting of textiles or flexible materials.
[D01H 4/00] Open-end spinning machines or arrangements for imparting twist to independently moving fibres separated from slivers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the spinning processes where twist is inserted into a fibre strand, whereby an interruption of the fibre strand takes place in order to insert twist; e.g. the fibres are taken out from the strand and blown into a rotor that turns at high speed.
[D01H 4/02] imparting twist by a fluid, e.g. air vortex -- **Definition:** This place covers:  open-end air jet spinning.
[D01H 4/04] imparting twist by contact of fibres with a running surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  open-end friction spinning.
[D01H 4/06] co-operating with suction means -- This subclass covers open-end spinning machines that employ suction devices to draw or hold fibres that have been separated from slivers, assisting fibre handling and twist formation. The suction may be used to feed fibres into the twist zone, maintain flow, or remove waste. It belongs to the textiles group, specifically spinning or twisting, open-end spinning machines for imparting twist to independently moving fibres.
[D01H 4/08] Rotor spinning, i.e. the running surface being provided by a rotor -- D01H 4/08 refers to rotor spinning, where the running surface is a rotor that imparts twist to independently moving fibres separated from slivers, as used in open-end spinning machines. This subclass lies under textiles → textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for → spinning or twisting.
[D01H 4/10] Rotors -- Rotors are the rotating components of open-end spinning machines that impart twist to individually moving fibres separated from slivers, gathering them into a yarn. They may consist of one or several discs or rings, driven mechanically or by a pneumatic flow, and their shape and speed govern yarn quality and production efficiency.
[D01H 4/12] Rotor bearings -- D01H 4/12 covers rotor bearings used in open-end spinning machines or devices that impart twist to independently moving fibres separated from slivers. The bearing supports the rotating component that drives the rotor, ensuring stable operation and precise fibre handling. It falls within the CPC group for textiles, specifically spinning or twisting technology.
[D01H 4/14] Rotor driven by an electric motor -- The D01H 4/14 sub-class covers open-end spinning machines or fibre-twisting arrangements in which the rotor that imparts twist to independently moving fibres is driven directly by an electric motor. It specifies motor-driven rotors for producing yarn without the need for external mechanical drive shafts.
[D01H 4/16] Friction spinning, i.e. the running surface being provided by a pair of closely spaced friction drums, e.g. at least one suction drum -- D01H 4/16 covers open-end spinning arrangements that impart twist by means of friction between a pair of closely spaced drums, one of which may be a suction drum. The running surface is created by the friction drums rather than conventional rollers, providing a method of friction spinning.
[D01H 4/18] Friction drums, e.g. arrangement of suction holes -- D01H 4/18 covers friction drums employed in open-end spinning machines for imparting twist to independently moving fibres, specifically drums that incorporate arrangements of suction holes to aid fibre handling and control.
[D01H 4/20] Drum bearings
[D01H 4/22] Cleaning of running surfaces -- The code D01H 4/22 relates to cleaning of the running surfaces in open-end spinning machines or devices that twist independently moving fibres separated from slivers. It covers methods, apparatuses or operations for removing debris, deposits or foreign matter from the parts that contact the fibres during the twisting process.
[D01H 4/24] in rotor spinning -- D01H 4/24 covers rotor-spinning methods used in open-end spinning machines, where twist is imparted to independent fibres that have been separated from the sliver. The technique involves a rotating rotor that twists the fibres as they are fed, producing yarn without a traditional spindle.
[D01H 4/26] in friction spinning -- This CPC subclass covers the use of friction spinning, a method where fibres are twisted together by frictional forces rather than conventional mechanical drafting, typically employed in open-end or rotor spinning machines to produce yarn from independently moving fibres.
[D01H 4/28] using electrostatic fields -- This subclass covers the use of electrostatic fields in open-end spinning machines or in devices that impart twist to individually moving fibers that have been separated from slivers, enabling or improving the twisting process.
[D01H 4/30] Arrangements for separating slivers into fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the individualisation of fibres that are to be fed into the spinning region, e.g. by little opening rollers.
[D01H 4/32] using opening rollers -- This subclass concerns open-end spinning machines or twist-imparting arrangements that handle fibres already separated from slivers, specifically the use of opening rollers to feed, spread or pre-treat the independently moving fibres before twist is applied. It falls under CPC code D01H 4/32.
[D01H 4/34] using air-jet streams -- D01H 4/34 covers the use of air-jet streams to form yarn in open-end spinning machines or in devices that twist independently moving fibres separated from slivers. The classification pertains to textile processes where pneumatic action replaces traditional mechanical drafting.
[D01H 4/36] with means for taking away impurities -- The D01H 4/36 subclass concerns open-end spinning or twist-imparting machines that include means for removing impurities from the fibre stream, such as suction filters, air-flow devices or cleaning stations that extract dust, short fibres or other contaminants while the fibres are independently moving and being twisted.
[D01H 4/38] Channels for feeding fibres to the yarn forming region -- The code D01H 4/38 covers the conduit systems that deliver individual fibres from the supply to the yarn-forming zone in open-end spinning equipment. It includes designs for channels that guide fibres while they are separated from slivers and twisted into yarn.
[D01H 4/40] Removing running yarn from the yarn forming region, e.g. using tubes -- D01H 4/40 covers the removal of the running yarn from the yarn-forming zone of an open-end spinning machine or a device that twists independently moving fibres, typically by directing the yarn into tubes or similar conduits to separate it from the forming area.
[D01H 4/42] Control of driving or stopping -- This subclass covers mechanisms that start, regulate, or halt the drive of open-end spinning machines or twist-imparting devices for individually moving fibers separated from slivers. It includes controls for speed, torque, and stop functions to ensure consistent yarn formation.
[D01H 4/44] in rotor spinning -- D01H 4/44 covers open-end or rotor spinning machines that twist independently moving fibres, separated from slivers, to produce yarn. The classification relates to textile processes where twist is imparted by a rotary system rather than traditional spindle methods. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 4/46] in friction spinning -- Friction spinning creates yarn by using frictional contact between moving fibers and a working surface to impart twist, rather than a spindle, as the fibers are drawn from a feed and combined into a continuous strand. The process is applied in open-end spinning machines that treat independently moving fibers separated from slivers. This method falls under the CPC subclass D01H 4/46 for textile spinning or twisting techniques.
[D01H 4/48] Piecing arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the piecing in an open-end spinning machine, after a machine stop or spinning incident.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Piecing arrangements not in conjunction with open-end spinning -> D01H15/00  **Glossary:** - Piecing: joining of yarn ends.
[D01H 4/50] for rotor spinning -- This subclass concerns open-end (rotor) spinning machines that twist fibers which have been separated from slivers, describing the rotor arrangement and related components that effect rotor spinning. It sits within textiles → textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for → spinning or twisting.
[D01H 4/52] for friction spinning -- A friction-spinning device or method that imparts twist to independently moving fibres in an open-end spinning process, typically using a high-speed rotating element to generate frictional forces that produce yarn without the need for a traditional spindle. This technique falls under the textile category for open-end spinning machines.
[D01H 5/00] Drafting machines or arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drafting machines as stand alone, as well as drafting arrangements in spinning machines. Slivers are drafted into slivers, rovings or fibre strands by the action of progressively accelerating drafting cylinders.
[D01H 5/005] Arrangements for feeding or conveying the slivers to the drafting machine -- The CPC subclass D01H 5/005 covers devices that transport the continuous sliver from the rollers to the drafting section of a spinning line. It includes feeders, conveyors and guiding systems that ensure uniform delivery of the fiber bundle before it is drawn out by the drafting machine.
[D01H 5/02] Gill boxes or other drafting machines employing fallers or like pinned bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drafting machines having fibre control arrangements placed between the drafting cylinders; said control elements are pinned bars. The machines themselves are called gill boxes, and are used in long staple (i.e. wool, flax) spinning preparation.
[D01H 5/04] with pinned bars actuated by screw members -- The CPC entry D01H 5/04 covers drafting devices used in spinning or twisting where the drafting bars are mounted on pins and driven by screw mechanisms. The pins support the bars while the screws convert rotary motion into linear movement to regulate the feed of fibers. This arrangement is a specific type of drafting machine within the broader textile class.
[D01H 5/06] Intersecting gill boxes -- D01H 5/06 covers drafting arrangements that use intersecting gill boxes to regulate the flow of fibers during spinning. The intersecting gill boxes are positioned so that the fiber stream passes through multiple, crossing openings, improving draft uniformity and tension control in the preparation of yarns.
[D01H 5/08] with bars connected by links, chains, or the like -- D01H 5/08 covers drafting machines or arrangements used in spinning or twisting of textiles where the guiding bars are linked together by chains, links or similar connectors. The design enables coordinated movement of multiple bars to control tension or alignment during fiber processing.
[D01H 5/10] with pinned bars unconnected with each other but actuated through pressure of one against another -- The subclass D01H 5/10 covers drafting devices for yarn or thread where the drafting bars are separate pins that are not mechanically linked; they are driven by the frictional pressure that one bar exerts on an adjacent bar during operation. This arrangement is used in spinning or twisting equipment to control the feed of fibers.
[D01H 5/12] Details
[D01H 5/14] Pinned bars -- The code D01H 5/14 covers the use of pinned bars in drafting mechanisms for yarn or filament production. Pinned bars are rigid elements with pins that separate, guide or space fibers during spinning or twisting, improving uniformity and preventing entanglement. This subclass falls under textile spinning and drafting machines.
[D01H 5/16] Framework -- This subclass covers the supporting structure or frame of drafting machines or arrangements used in textile spinning or twisting. It includes chassis, supporting members, mounting brackets and other framework elements that hold rollers, guides and tensioning devices in place.
[D01H 5/18] Drafting machines or arrangements without fallers or like pinned bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  drafting machines with other or no fibre control means between the drafting rollers, e.g. cotton preparation drafting machines or drafting arrangements on spinning machines.
[D01H 5/20] in which fibres are controlled by contact with stationary or reciprocating surfaces -- D01H 5/20 covers spinning or twisting methods where the fibres are regulated through contact with stationary or moving surfaces, such as rollers, belts or plates, that adjust fibre alignment, tension or feed rate. This sub-group pertains to textile processes that employ surface contact to control fibre behavior during formation.
[D01H 5/22] in which fibres are controlled by rollers only -- D01H 5/22 covers drafting machines or arrangements in spinning or twisting where the fibres are regulated only by rollers, with no additional guiding or gripping elements. It pertains to textile or flexible-material processes that rely solely on roller control to manage fibre feed.
[D01H 5/24] with porcupines or like pinned rotary members -- D01H 5/24 covers spinning or twisting operations that use rotary members equipped with porcupine-type or similarly pinned protrusions to grip and manipulate fibers. The design of these pin-laden rotors helps control fiber feed and tension during textile production.
[D01H 5/26] in which fibres are controlled by one or more endless aprons -- The subclass D01H 5/26 covers drafting devices for spinning in which the fibres are guided and regulated by one or more endless aprons that continuously loop around the machine, controlling fibre tension and alignment during the drafting process. This arrangement is used to ensure uniform feed of fibres in textile production.
[D01H 5/28] in which fibres are controlled by inserting twist during drafting -- This subclass covers drafting devices where the fibre bundle is twisted as it is drawn out, allowing the amount of twist to regulate fibre tension and alignment during the spinning process. The method is used to achieve controlled yarn properties in textile manufacturing.
[D01H 5/30] incorporating arrangements for severing continuous filaments, e.g. in direct spinning -- D01H 5/30 covers spinning or twisting processes that incorporate means for cutting continuous filaments during the operation, such as in direct-spinning systems where the fiber is drawn, formed and severed in a single line. The subclass addresses apparatus and arrangements that integrate the severing step into the spinning machine.
[D01H 5/32] Regulating or varying draft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  means used to regulate the draft in predefined patterns or in response to irregularity detection.
[D01H 5/34] by manual adjustments -- D01H 5/34 concerns drafting machines or arrangements used in the spinning or twisting of textiles where the draft is set or altered by manual adjustments. It includes devices that let an operator change tension, speed or fibre draw distance by hand rather than by automatic control.
[D01H 5/36] according to a pre-arranged pattern, e.g. to produce slubs -- A drafting machine or arrangement that varies the feed of fibers in a predetermined sequence to create intentional thickness irregularities, or slubs, in the yarn during spinning. This system controls the twist and draw of the yarn according to a pre-arranged pattern to produce the desired textured effect.
[D01H 5/38] in response to irregularities in material -- This subclass covers drafting machines or arrangements that automatically adjust the draw-off of fibers to compensate for irregularities in the material during spinning or twisting. It includes mechanisms that sense changes in yarn thickness, tension or elasticity and modify feed rates or roller gaps to maintain a uniform draft. The purpose is to improve consistency of the resulting textile product.
[D01H 5/385] employing hydraulic or pneumatic time-delay devices -- The subgroup D01H 5/385 covers drafting machines or arrangements for spinning or twisting that employ hydraulic or pneumatic time-delay devices to control the feed of fibers, providing a delayed actuation that regulates tension or feed rate during the drafting process.
[D01H 5/40] employing mechanical time-delay devices -- A drafting machine or arrangement for spinning or twisting textiles that incorporates a mechanical time-delay device, which delays the transmission of motion to control yarn feed, twist timing or other process steps, thereby improving synchronization and product quality.
[D01H 5/42] employing electrical time-delay devices -- This subclass concerns drafting machines or arrangements used in spinning that incorporate electrical time-delay devices to regulate fiber or yarn feed, providing delayed actuation for precise control of tension and draw.
[D01H 5/44] Adjusting drafting elements, e.g. altering ratch -- **Definition:** This place covers:  adjustment means to change distances between rollers or roller pairs.
[D01H 5/46] Loading arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  means to exert a load or pressure on a roller or rollers, to ensure an adequate nipping of fibres.
[D01H 5/48] using weights -- The CPC subclass D01H 5/48 covers drafting machines or arrangements employed in spinning or twisting processes that use weights to regulate the draw-off of fibers, control tension, or maintain a consistent draft during yarn production. It pertains specifically to weight-based mechanisms incorporated in textile drafting equipment.
[D01H 5/50] using springs -- D01H 5/50 covers spinning or twisting apparatus that incorporate springs as functional elements. The springs may be used to regulate tension, provide return force, or assist feed and take-up mechanisms in machines that handle yarn or other flexible fibrous materials. This subclass is part of the broader group dealing with textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for.
[D01H 5/505] for top roller arms -- D01H 5/505 pertains to drafting machines or arrangements used in spinning or twisting processes that are specifically designed for top roller arms, falling under the textiles-or-flexible-materials category of the CPC. This subclass covers mechanisms that adjust or control the position of the upper rollers to regulate yarn draft during production.
[D01H 5/52] using fluid pressure -- This subclass covers textile drafting machines or arrangements that use fluid pressure-hydraulic, pneumatic or other-to control the tension and draw-down of fibres during spinning or twisting. It includes devices in which fluid power drives rollers, clamps or guides to achieve the desired draft of the material.
[D01H 5/525] for top roller arms -- D01H 5/525 covers mechanisms that support or adjust the top roller arms of drafting machines used in spinning or twisting of fibers. These arms hold the upper rollers in place, allowing precise control of roller tension and position to regulate fibre feed during the drafting process.
[D01H 5/54] using magnetic arrangements -- The subclass D01H 5/54 covers the use of magnetic arrangements in drafting machines or related setups for spinning or twisting of textile fibres. It includes methods where magnetic fields, magnets or magnetic actuators control yarn tension, spacing or feed rate during the drafting process, providing a non-contact means of regulation.
[D01H 5/56] Supports for drafting elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The structures and frames that hold the drafting arrangement(s) in spinning machines.
[D01H 5/565] Top roller arms -- D01H 5/565 designates the top roller arms of drafting machines used in the spinning or twisting of textiles or flexible materials. The arms support and position the upper rollers that control fibre feed and tension during draft formation.
[D01H 5/58] Arrangements for traversing drafting elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means to realize a lateral movement of drafting rollers, e.g. to reduce local wearing of rollers.
[D01H 5/60] Arrangements maintaining drafting elements free of fibre accumulations -- The entry concerns devices or methods incorporated in drafting machines used for spinning that keep the drafting elements-such as rollers, plates or belts-free of fibre buildup. It covers arrangements that use cleaning actions, airflow, vibration or special geometry to prevent accumulation, thereby maintaining uniform draft and reducing defects.
[D01H 5/62] Non-rotary cleaning pads or plates -- D01H 5/62 covers non-rotary cleaning pads or plates employed in textile or flexible-material processes, such as spinning or twisting, where cleaning is achieved by stationary or reciprocating contact rather than rotating action. These items may be made of woven, non-woven or composite materials and are designed to remove debris, fibers or contaminants from machinery, rollers or workpieces without the use of a rotating brush or disc.
[D01H 5/625] in cooperation with suction or blowing means -- The CPC entry D01H 5/625 refers to drafting machines or arrangements used in spinning or twisting of textiles that employ suction or blowing means, such as vacuum or air-jet devices, to advance, hold or control the fibers during the drafting process.
[D01H 5/64] Rollers or aprons with cleaning surfaces -- The CPC subclass D01H 5/64 covers rollers or aprons equipped with cleaning surfaces that are used in drafting or spinning equipment to remove fibers, debris or lubricants from the material as it passes through the machine. These cleaning elements help maintain consistent thread tension and product quality.
[D01H 5/645] in cooperation with suction or blowing means -- The entry D01H 5/645 covers drafting machines or arrangements that incorporate suction or blowing devices to assist the drawing out of fibers. By applying airflow, the system controls fiber tension and alignment during the spinning or twisting process, improving draft consistency and product quality. This feature is part of the broader category of textile spinning technologies.
[D01H 5/66] Suction devices -- Suction devices used in drafting machines or arrangements for textile processing, particularly for controlling the feed and tension of fibers or yarns during spinning or twisting operations. This subclass falls under D01H 5/66 within the CPC hierarchy for textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 5/68] Suction end-catchers -- The D01H 5/68 subclass covers suction end-catchers used in spinning or twisting processes for textiles and other flexible materials. These devices employ a vacuum or suction mechanism to capture the end of a yarn, filament or thread as it exits the drafting zone, aiding continuous operation and preventing breakage.
[D01H 5/70] Constructional features of drafting elements -- D01H 5/70 pertains to the structural design of drafting elements within drafting machines or arrangements used in the spinning or twisting of textiles and flexible materials. It covers the geometry, configuration and material aspects of rollers, belts or other components that control strand draw.
[D01H 5/72] Fibre-condensing guides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Guides that are placed in or after the drafting zone, to control and narrow the path followed by the fibres.  **Glossary:** - Compact spinning: Spinning by using of a fibre condensing guide after the drafting zone of a ring spinning machine
[D01H 5/74] Rollers -- Rollers are cylindrical components incorporated in drafting machines or arrangements for spinning and twisting textile fibers, guiding and feeding the material while applying controlled tension and alignment as it moves through the drafting zone. They may be smooth, grooved or adjustable to regulate fiber speed and positioning.
[D01H 5/76] Loose-boss assemblies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  roller assemblies that lie in a loose manner on the fibre strands in the drafting zone of a spinning machine, thereby exerting mild fibre control.
[D01H 5/78] with flutes or other integral surface characteristics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  rollers with structured surfaces, e.g. lower metallic drafting rollers with helical grooves in a drafting machine.
[D01H 5/80] with covers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  roller with a generally soft coat like rubber, e.g. upper drafting rollers of a spinning machine.
[D01H 5/82] Arrangements for coupling roller sections -- The subclass covers the various ways roller sections are joined together in drafting-machine used for spinning or twisting fibers. It includes gear, belt, shaft, clamp or other mechanical couplings that transmit motion and tension between adjacent rollers to ensure consistent drafting of the yarn.
[D01H 5/84] Porcupines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  needled rollers, used to control fibres in a drafting zone.
[D01H 5/86] Aprons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  aprons and arrangement with aprons to control fibres in a drafting zone.
[D01H 5/88] Cradles -- Cradles are supporting structures that hold and guide drafting devices-such as rollers, pins or guides-used to control the tension and alignment of yarns during the drafting stage of spinning or twisting processes. They provide a stable, adjustable base that maintains the precise spacing and position of drafting elements, ensuring consistent fiber feed and uniform product quality.
[D01H 7/00] Spinning or twisting arrangements -- D01H 7/00 covers devices and mechanisms designed to spin or twist fibers, yarns or filaments during textile manufacture. It includes arrangements such as drafting units, twist rollers, spindle drives and related control systems that impart the required twist or draw to produce yarns of specific properties. The code is applied to inventions involving the configuration, combination or improvement of these spinning and twisting elements.
[D01H 7/02] for imparting permanent twist -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/02 covers textile processes that create a permanent twist in yarns or filaments. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, specifically the category of spinning or twisting arrangements, and includes methods and devices that impart a lasting helical form to the material.
[D01H 7/04] Spindles -- D01H 7/04 designates spindles, the rotating devices that hold and drive yarn or fiber during the spinning or twisting of textiles. It covers all forms of spindle construction and arrangement used to impart twist, speed control, or tension to the material, and falls under the CPC hierarchy for textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting processes.
[D01H 7/041] Spindles with sliding contact bearings  D01H7/045  takes precedence
[D01H 7/042] Spindles with rolling contact bearings  D01H7/045  takes precedence -- This subclass designates spindles equipped with rolling-contact bearings used in textile spinning or twisting arrangements. It falls under the broader category of textiles, specifically the flexible-material segment for spinning or twisting. The code D01H 7/042 is subordinate to the related class D01H 7/045, which has precedence.
[D01H 7/044] Spindles with fluid bearings -- Spindles equipped with fluid bearings are rotating shafts used in textile spinning or twisting machines where a thin film of oil or other fluid supports the spindle, reducing friction and wear while providing precise speed control and smoother operation. This design improves yarn quality and allows higher spindle speeds compared to plain bearings, making it suitable for high-performance textile production.
[D01H 7/045] Spindles provided with flexible mounting elements for damping vibration or noise, or for avoiding or reducing out-of-balance forces due to rotation  in general  F16F15/00 -- The CPC code D01H 7/045 falls under textiles, specifically spinning or twisting, and covers spindles that incorporate flexible mounting elements to damp vibration or noise and to reduce out-of-balance forces during rotation. It pertains to devices used in textile spinning machinery to improve stability and acoustic performance.
[D01H 7/047] with springs -- The code D01H 7/047 covers spinning or twisting mechanisms that incorporate springs, where the spring element is used to apply tension, maintain spacing or assist in the formation of yarns and other flexible textile products.
[D01H 7/048] with means using plastic deformation of members -- This subclass concerns spinning or twisting arrangements that create yarns or threads by plastically deforming the material, for example by rolling, drawing or compressing the members through dies or similar tools. The primary means of forming the strand is the deformation of the members rather than conventional mechanical twisting.
[D01H 7/06] Stationary spindles with package-holding sleeves -- Stationary spindles equipped with sleeves that hold yarn packages enable continuous unwinding and feeding of yarn during spinning or twisting processes. The design keeps the spindle fixed while the package rotates within the sleeve, improving stability and control.
[D01H 7/08] Mounting arrangements -- The D01H 7/08 subclass concerns mounting arrangements for spinning or twisting processes, covering devices that secure spindles, bobbins, or other rotating elements to textile machinery. It includes clamps, brackets, holders and supporting structures engineered to position and retain these components during operation.
[D01H 7/10] Spindle supports -- D01H 7/10 classifies spindle supports, the components that hold, guide or brace the spindle in textile spinning or twisting equipment. These supports ensure proper alignment and stability of the spindle while processing yarns or flexible fibres, contributing to consistent thread formation.
[D01H 7/12] Bolsters -- Bolsters are short, loosely spun rods or bundles of fibers that are incorporated alongside or within a yarn core during spinning or twisting to provide bulk, support, or enhanced elasticity, often improving the yarn's heat resistance or structural stability.
[D01H 7/14] Holding-down arrangements -- Holding-down arrangements are devices or mechanisms used in spinning or twisting processes to secure yarns, fibers or filaments in place, preventing unwanted movement and maintaining proper tension. They may include clamps, rollers, pins or magnetic fixtures that keep the material stable during drafting, winding or twist insertion, thereby improving product consistency and process control.
[D01H 7/16] Arrangements for coupling bobbins or like to spindles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  arrangements for coupling bobbins or like to spindles of spinning machines.
[D01H 7/18] Arrangements on spindles for suppressing yarn balloons -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/18 covers devices mounted on spinning spindles that prevent the formation of yarn balloons, such as air-or fluid jets, guides, or mechanical barriers that keep the yarn close to the spindle and control its take-up. These arrangements improve yarn handling and reduce defects during the spinning or twisting process.
[D01H 7/20] Lubricating arrangements -- D01H 7/20 covers lubricating arrangements used in spinning or twisting processes for textiles or flexible materials. It includes devices or methods that apply lubricants to fibers or yarns to reduce friction and improve draft during spinning, covering both continuous and intermittent lubrication systems.
[D01H 7/22] Braking arrangements -- A braking arrangement in the context of spinning or twisting refers to any device or mechanism that slows, stops, or controls the rotation or unwinding of yarn, filament or rope during the process. It includes friction brakes, clutch systems, electromagnetic or hydraulic brakes, and related control features designed to maintain tension, prevent over-speed, and ensure consistent product quality.
[D01H 7/2208] using mechanical means -- The CPC code D01H 7/2208 classifies apparatus, devices and processes that spin or twist fibers by mechanical means, such as gears, belts, rollers or other moving components that impart rotation to the material. It covers mechanical arrangements used in yarn or thread production, including machines that convert raw fibers into continuous strands through physically driven twisting or spinning actions.
[D01H 7/2216] with one or two manually actuated shoe-brakes acting on a part of the whorl -- D01H 7/2216 covers spinning or twisting devices that include one or two manually actuated shoe-brakes that press against a portion of the whorl. The brakes are operated by hand to regulate the rotation of the whorl, providing precise speed or tension control during the yarn-forming process. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials, and spinning or twisting arrangements.
[D01H 7/2225] the braking means surrounding nearly the whole periphery of the whorl -- This subclass covers devices that provide a braking mechanism encircling almost the entire circumference of the whorl in spinning or twisting equipment. The brake applies friction around the outer edge of the rotating disc to regulate its speed or stop motion, and may be a fixed or adjustable band, ring or sleeve that contacts the whorl during operation.
[D01H 7/2233] by suppressing the driving means, e.g. by declutching -- The CPC entry D01H 7/2233 concerns spinning or twisting processes in which the driving mechanism is intentionally suppressed, for example by declutching the drive. It covers arrangements that disengage or pause the power transmission to control yarn feed, tension or winding without stopping the entire machine.
[D01H 7/2241] the belt being moved off the driven whorl -- The entry details a mechanism for disengaging a belt from the driven whorl in textile spinning or twisting equipment, allowing the belt to be moved off the whorl to control drive transmission, tension or to enable a change of speed or direction.
[D01H 7/225] and the spindle being braked simultaneously -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/225 concerns spinning or twisting arrangements in which the spindle is braked at the same time as the yarn is being twisted, typically to control tension or speed during the process. It falls under textiles, specifically flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and addresses synchronized braking mechanisms within spindle-driven spinning machines.
[D01H 7/2258] the pivoted spindle being pulled off the belt -- This CPC subclass deals with textile spinning or twisting machines in which a spindle mounted on a pivot is pulled off a moving belt - a feature that enables yarn removal, spindle replacement or maintenance without stopping the belt.
[D01H 7/2266] and braked simultaneously -- D01H 7/2266 classifies spinning or twisting arrangements that incorporate a braking mechanism operating at the same time as the twist is imparted, such as spindles or twisting devices where simultaneous rotation and controlled deceleration are provided to regulate yarn tension.
[D01H 7/2275] using hydraulically or pneumatically operated brakes -- The code D01H 7/2275 pertains to textile spinning or twisting apparatus in which speed or tension is regulated by hydraulic or pneumatic brakes, allowing precise control of the rotation during processing. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting arrangements.
[D01H 7/2283] using electromagnetically operated brakes -- The code D01H 7/2283 covers spinning or twisting arrangements that employ electromagnetically operated brakes to control the rotation of spindles, rollers or other moving parts. These brakes use magnetic fields to apply and release braking force, allowing precise regulation of tension, speed or winding in textile or flexible-material production processes.
[D01H 7/2291] characterised by the control of braking means, e.g. operated by a yarn break-detector or tension device -- A subclass of spinning or twisting arrangements in which the brake is automatically controlled, usually by a yarn break-detector or a tension-sensing device that adjusts the braking action when a break or tension change is sensed. This entry falls under textiles, flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and relates specifically to the regulation of braking means in yarn processing.
[D01H 7/24] Flyer or like arrangements -- D01H 7/24 covers flyer or similar devices used in spinning or twisting processes for textiles and flexible materials. It pertains to arrangements that introduce fibers into a drafting zone, often using a rotating or vibrating flyer to aid in fiber opening and alignment before twist is applied. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, spinning or twisting, and textile or flexible material preparations.
[D01H 7/26] Flyer constructions -- Flyer constructions refer to the specific designs and arrangements of flyer components that guide, tension, or collect fibers during spinning or twisting operations, particularly in open-end or rotor spinning processes. The classification covers the structural features, mounting methods, and functional adaptations of these flyers that affect yarn formation and handling.
[D01H 7/28] arranged to guide material over exterior of legs -- This subclass of CPC D01H 7/28 covers arrangements that guide yarn or other fibers over the exterior of the legs of a spinning or twisting device, using external guides, rollers, shuttles or similar elements to control material flow during the twisting process.
[D01H 7/30] with guide channels formed in legs, e.g. slubbing flyers -- The subclass D01H 7/30 covers spinning or twisting devices where guide channels are integrally formed in the legs of the equipment, such as slubbing flyers, to direct material flow and improve fibre alignment during drafting. It is a specific arrangement within textile processing equipment.
[D01H 7/32] with pressing devices -- This subclass covers spinning or twisting arrangements that incorporate pressing devices such as rollers, plates or cams to flatten, compact or shape the yarn or filament while it is being formed. The pressing action may occur before, during or after twisting to improve uniformity, control cross-section or impart special mechanical properties, and is used in the production of specialised textile yarns.
[D01H 7/34] with haul pulleys or like arrangements -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/34 falls under the textiles section and covers spinning or twisting operations that incorporate haul pulleys or comparable arrangements to draw and guide the fiber bundle through the twisting zone. It specifies the use of such pulley-type devices in the spinning process.
[D01H 7/36] with traversing devices -- This subclass covers spinning or twisting arrangements that incorporate traversing devices, such as rollers, guides or conveyors that move the fiber or yarn back and forth across the processing zone. The traversing action improves strand uniformity, tension control or twist distribution during spinning operations.
[D01H 7/38] Ring flyers -- Ring flyers are rotating components attached to the ring of a ring-spinning frame that guide the yarn from the drafting zone to the traveller and impart twist while the spindle rotates. They transfer the spindle-generated twist to the yarn and help maintain tension and uniformity during the spinning or twisting process.
[D01H 7/40] Flyer supports, e.g. rails -- D01H 7/40 covers flyer supports such as rails used in spinning or twisting machines to guide and stabilise the flyer on the spindle during yarn formation. These components help maintain proper tension and orientation of the filament as it is produced.
[D01H 7/42] Arrangements coupling flyers to spindles -- This CPC subgroup (D01H 7/42) concerns the mechanical arrangements that join the flyer, which guides or draws out fibers, to the spindle in textile spinning or twisting machines. It details the various coupling methods that transmit rotation between these two components to ensure synchronized operation.
[D01H 7/44] Drag arrangements for bobbins or flyers -- The D01H 7/44 entry covers drag mechanisms fitted to bobbins or flyers in spinning and twisting equipment. These devices create controlled resistance to regulate the unwinding speed and tension of yarn, ensuring consistent feed and product quality during textile production.
[D01H 7/46] Devices attached to, or integral with, flyers for temporarily increasing twist in material passing to them -- The classification D01H 7/46 covers devices that are mounted on or built into the flyer of a spinning system and that temporarily add twist to the yarn or other filament as it passes through. Such mechanisms are employed to boost twist for short periods, for example during start-up or specific processing steps, without permanently altering the machine's geometry.
[D01H 7/48] Eyes or like guiding arrangements -- D01H 7/48 covers eyes, eyelets or similar guiding elements that direct yarn, thread or filament through spinning or twisting machines, helping to maintain alignment and tension. These components are used in textile processes to guide material along the path of spinning or twisting arrangements.
[D01H 7/50] Interrelated flyer and bobbin drive mechanisms, e.g. winding-on motions for cotton-roving frames -- The D01H 7/50 subclass covers mechanisms that jointly control the flyer and bobbin drives, enabling coordinated winding-on motions on cotton-roving frames during spinning or twisting operations. These interrelated drive systems are designed to improve yarn take-up and tension control in textile production.
[D01H 7/52] Ring-and-traveller arrangements -- Ring-and-traveller arrangements are devices that transmit twist from a driven spindle to a yarn guide or bobbin via a rotating ring and a small travelling contact that moves around the ring's circumference. The configuration ensures continuous, controlled delivery of twist during spinning or twisting operations.
[D01H 7/54] with fixed rings -- The code D01H 7/54 designates spinning or twisting arrangements that employ fixed rings to transmit torque to the fibers, as found in conventional ring-spinning frames where the ring remains stationary while a traveler moves around it, enabling controlled twist insertion during yarn formation.
[D01H 7/56] with freely-rotatable rings -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/56 covers spinning or twisting devices that incorporate freely-rotatable rings as part of the arrangement. Such rings rotate independently of the spindle or traveller to guide and tension the yarn during production, a characteristic of conventional ring-spinning frames.
[D01H 7/565] with fluid bearings -- The subclass D01H 7/565 covers spinning or twisting machines that use fluid bearings to support rotating shafts or other moving parts. Fluid bearings rely on a thin film of liquid or gas, providing low friction and allowing high-speed operation in textile equipment.
[D01H 7/58] with driven rings -- D01H 7/58 covers spinning or twisting arrangements that incorporate driven rings to rotate the workpiece or feed material. It is a subdivision of the textile field, specifically within textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for, under the broader category of spinning or twisting.
[D01H 7/585] by fluid driving means -- D01H 7/585 covers spinning or twisting arrangements that are powered by fluid driving means. It includes mechanisms where jets of air, gas or liquid impart rotational motion to spindles or twist elements to form yarns or threads. These systems are used in textile production where the driving force is supplied by a fluid rather than mechanical gears or shafts.
[D01H 7/60] Rings or travellers -- D01H 7/60 refers to rings or travellers employed in spinning or twisting apparatuses for textiles, encompassing the components that guide, support or transmit motion to the yarn during processing. This sub-class falls under the CPC hierarchy of Textiles → Textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for → Spinning or twisting arrangements.
[D01H 7/602] Rings -- The D01H 7/602 entry classifies rings that are part of spinning or twisting arrangements for textiles or flexible materials. It covers devices such as tension rings, guide rings or ring rollers that support, tension, direct or otherwise manage yarn or filament during the spinning or twisting process.
[D01H 7/604] Travellers -- The D01H 7/604 entry covers travellers, the small detachable components that slide on a spindle or bobbin to guide, tension or transport yarn during spinning or twisting operations. It includes the design, mounting and functional variations of these moving parts used in textile or flexible-material spinning equipment.
[D01H 7/606] Driving means for travellers -- In CPC class D01H 7/606, the focus is on the driving mechanisms that rotate the traveller in spinning or twisting equipment. It covers devices such as gears, belts, electromagnetic or pneumatic drives, and their control systems that supply the power needed to impart twist to the yarn.
[D01H 7/608] Cleaning means for travellers -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/608 covers portable cleaning devices or methods designed to maintain textiles while being used by travelers, such as collapsible brushes, lint rollers, or compact cleaning systems integrated into garments. It falls under spinning or twisting arrangements within the broader textiles category.
[D01H 7/62] Arrangements providing lubricant for travellers -- Arrangements that supply lubricant to the traveler - the moving part that guides and tensions yarn or filament during spinning or twisting. These devices deliver oil, grease or similar substances to the traveler to lower friction, reduce wear and improve the efficiency of the spinning process.
[D01H 7/64] Ring supports, e.g. ring rails -- The entry pertains to devices that support or guide the ring component of a spinning or twisting apparatus, such as rails, brackets or other holders that keep the ring correctly positioned during operation, thereby maintaining stability and consistent tension on the yarn as it passes through the ring.
[D01H 7/66] Cap arrangements -- Cap arrangements are components that cover or seal the end of a spindle, bobbin or other rotating element in spinning or twisting machines, helping to retain fibers and control tension during processing. They may be removable or fixed caps designed to protect the yarn and facilitate winding.
[D01H 7/68] Cap constructions -- The D01H 7/68 class covers devices and methods that form caps on yarns or threads during or after spinning or twisting, including techniques for sealing, knotting, or attaching protective caps to the ends of filaments. It addresses cap-construction arrangements used to finish or modify spun or twisted fibers.
[D01H 7/70] Arrangements for supporting caps on spindles -- D01H 7/70 covers mechanisms that hold or secure caps on the ends of spindles during spinning or twisting processes. These arrangements keep the cap in place while the spindle rotates, facilitating continuous operation and preventing cap loss.
[D01H 7/72] Bobbin-supporting arrangements, e.g. bobbin rails -- Covers mechanisms that support, guide or retain the bobbin within spinning or twisting equipment, such as rails, shafts, frames or other structures that keep the bobbin correctly positioned and aligned during operation.
[D01H 7/74] Cup or like arrangements -- D01H 7/74 covers cup or similar arrangements used in spinning or twisting processes for textiles or flexible materials, where the device forms a cup-shaped chamber or guide to hold, align, or draft fibers during production, facilitating controlled yarn formation. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, spinning or twisting arrangements.
[D01H 7/76] Rotary discs -- Rotary discs are rotating components employed in textile spinning or twisting arrangements to impart twist to fibers or yarns. They may carry filaments, pins or surface features that grip and rotate the material, facilitating continuous yarn formation in processes such as open-end or ring spinning.
[D01H 7/78] Constructions of cups, e.g. spinning boxes -- D01H 7/78 pertains to the design and structure of cups or similar containers used in spinning processes, commonly referred to as spinning boxes. These constructions support and guide the yarn or filament as it is twisted, helping to control tension and feed during yarn formation in textile or flexible-material manufacturing.
[D01H 7/80] adapted to collect wet yarns -- This subclass covers spinning or twisting equipment that includes mechanisms designed to gather yarn while it is still wet, allowing the yarn to be collected directly from the wet state for further processing or handling.
[D01H 7/82] Casings or guards for rotary cups or the like -- Casings or guards for rotary cups and similar components are protective structures that encircle the spinning or twisting elements in textile machines, shielding them from damage, containing fiber spillage and improving safety. The devices are fitted to the rotating parts of the spindle system to maintain proper operation and prevent material loss during the processing of flexible textile materials.
[D01H 7/84] Spindles or yarn carriers for co-operation with rotary cups -- The CPC code D01H 7/84 covers spindles or yarn carriers designed to cooperate with rotary cups in spinning or twisting operations, falling under the textiles hierarchy for spinning or twisting arrangements of flexible materials.
[D01H 7/86] Multiple-twist arrangements, e.g. two-for-one twisting devices -- D01H 7/86 refers to multiple-twist arrangements in textile spinning, such as two-for-one twisting devices that twist two threads together in a single operation. The subclass falls under spinning or twisting arrangements for flexible textile materials.
[D01H 7/862] Arrangements for holding the bobbin in a fixed position -- The code D01H 7/862 covers devices that secure a bobbin in a stationary position during textile spinning or twisting operations. It includes clamps, holders, and other mechanisms that keep the bobbin fixed while yarn is drawn off or fed, ensuring consistent tension and alignment. This subclass falls under arrangements for holding the bobbin in a fixed position.
[D01H 7/864] Coupling devices between the fixed and the rotative parts -- D01H 7/864 covers coupling devices that link the fixed and rotating parts of spinning or twisting equipment, such as clutches, gear couplings or other mechanisms that transmit torque while permitting relative motion.
[D01H 7/866] Means to facilitate the unwinding of yarn -- This subclass covers devices or mechanisms that aid in the controlled release and separation of yarn from a bobbin or spindle during or after spinning. It includes guides, tensioners, or other apparatus designed to prevent snarling and ensure smooth unwinding.
[D01H 7/868] Yarn guiding means, e.g. guiding tubes -- The D01H 7/868 subclass covers devices used in spinning or twisting processes that guide yarn, such as guiding tubes or other mechanisms that direct the thread path. These yarn-guiding means help maintain tension, alignment and quality during production of textiles or flexible materials. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and spinning or twisting.
[D01H 7/88] Hollow-spindle arrangements -- Hollow-spindle arrangements refer to devices in which the spindle used for spinning or twisting yarn contains a central cavity or channel. The hollow core can guide fibers, deliver lubricants, or allow the passage of air or other fluids during the spinning process, improving yarn quality or enabling specialized treatment. This subcategory falls under spinning or twisting arrangements within textile flexible materials.
[D01H 7/90] Arrangements with two or more twisting devices in combination -- D01H 7/90 covers textile spinning or twisting arrangements that employ two or more twisting devices working together. The code is used for inventions where multiple twists are produced in combination, such as twin-spindle or multi-twister machines, to achieve specific yarn structures or processing advantages.
[D01H 7/92] for imparting transient twist -- A process or device that temporarily twists fibers or yarns during spinning to modify their characteristics, such as tension, draft or strength, before the twist is released. This transient twist aids in handling, orientation or loft of the material and is removed in a later stage.
[D01H 7/923] by means of rotating devices -- The CPC subclass D01H 7/923 relates to spinning or twisting arrangements that employ rotating devices such as spindles, rollers or rotary tools to impart twist to fibers or filaments. It is nested under textiles, then textiles or flexible materials not otherwise provided for, and finally spinning or twisting.
[D01H 7/926] by means of traversing devices -- This CPC subclass deals with spinning or twisting methods that employ traversing devices, such as rollers, guides or carriers that move the yarn or fiber back and forth across the spinning zone to control tension and twist distribution. The devices are arranged to shift the material transversely during the spinning operation.
[D01H 9/00] Common features or details of, or accessories for, spinning or twisting machines of various kinds or types -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The logistical aspects taking place on empty or full bobbins and receptacles on spinning machines.
[D01H 9/001] Bobbin-taking arrangements -- This subclass concerns the devices and accessories that remove, handle or position bobbins in spinning or twisting machines, including mechanisms for taking up finished bobbins, feeding them to subsequent stages or transporting them within the equipment. It covers various designs for bobbin removal, positioning and conveyance incorporated in textile machinery.
[D01H 9/003] Graspers operating under the action of a fluid -- The CPC subclass D01H 9/003 relates to graspers that are driven by a fluid, such as air or hydraulic pressure, and are used as accessories for spinning or twisting machines to handle yarn, fibers or other flexible materials during processing.
[D01H 9/005] for removing empty packages or cans and replacing by completed (full) packages or cans at paying-out stations; also combined with piecing of the roving -- The CPC subclass D01H 9/005 covers devices that automatically withdraw empty spools, packages or cans from a spinning or twisting machine and replace them with filled ones at the paying-out station, often integrating a roving-piecing function. It pertains to accessories that keep continuous production by managing empty and full containers on the line.
[D01H 9/006] for two-for-one twist type machines -- A subclass of textile spinning and twisting machinery covering devices that produce two strands of yarn from a single feed by a two-for-one twist method, including the specific components and accessories used with such twist-type machines.
[D01H 9/008] for cans -- D01H 9/008 covers accessories or details of spinning or twisting machines that are adapted for use with cans, such as containers, handling or feeding devices that support yarn or filament during processing. It falls within the textiles hierarchy for equipment that spins or twists material.
[D01H 9/02] for removing completed take-up packages and replacing by bobbins, cores, or receptacles at take-up stations -- This subclass deals with mechanisms that detach finished take-up packages from a spinning or twisting machine and insert new bobbins, cores or other receptacles at the take-up station, enabling continuous operation. It covers devices such as automatic changers, grabs or guides used in textile production equipment.
[D01H 9/04] Doffing arrangements integral with spinning or twisting machines -- D01H 9/04 covers devices that are built into spinning or twisting equipment to automatically remove the finished yarn or thread from the machine, such as integral take-off rollers, guides or stripping mechanisms. The arrangements are part of the machine rather than separate accessories, facilitating continuous operation of textile processing.
[D01H 9/043] for cap type machines -- The CPC subclass D01H 9/043 relates to accessories for cap-type spinning or twisting machines in the textile field, covering devices that assist with feeding, guiding, tensioning or collecting yarn on cap-type spindles used in various spinning or twisting operations.
[D01H 9/046] for flyer type machines -- This subclass covers parts, features, or accessories designed for flyer-type spinning or twisting machines, which use a rotating flyer to draw out and twist fibers into yarn. It includes elements such as flyers, rings, guides, and related mechanisms that are specific to this machine configuration.
[D01H 9/06] Removing yarn from centrifugal cups on to yarn carriers -- The D01H 9/06 subgroup covers devices or methods that detach yarn from the centrifugal cup of a spinning or twisting machine and place it onto yarn carriers such as bobbins, spools or ribbons. It relates to accessories for spinning equipment that manage the transfer of freshly spun yarn to subsequent handling or winding stages.
[D01H 9/08] Doffing arrangements independent of spinning or twisting machines -- Doffing arrangements independent of spinning or twisting machines are devices or methods for removing spun yarn or filament from the machine without being integral to the machine itself, including auxiliary take-off frames, rollers or conveyors that operate separately.
[D01H 9/10] Doffing carriages -- Doffing carriages are accessories for spinning or twisting machines that hold and automatically remove depleted bobbins, cones or spindles after winding is finished, allowing quick replacement with fresh supplies and improving production efficiency. They are mounted on or integrated with the machine's carriage system.
[D01H 9/12] Manual cop-tube applying apparatus -- The CPC subclass D01H 9/12 covers a manually operated device for applying cop-tubes to fibers during spinning or twisting processes. It pertains to accessories that attach a protective or guiding tube to the fiber strand in textile machines, enabling manual placement or adjustment of the tube rather than automatic application.
[D01H 9/14] for preparing machines for doffing of yarns -- This subclass covers apparatus attached to spinning or twisting equipment that removes yarn from the machine after winding, i.e., doffing devices. It includes mechanisms for preparing machines for yarn removal such as doffer rollers, suction systems and related accessories.
[D01H 9/16] Yarn-severing arrangements -- This classification covers devices and mechanisms attached to or integrated with spinning or twisting machines that cut yarn to a desired length, including scissors, knives, rotary cutters and related control systems, and any accessories that facilitate yarn breakage or trimming.
[D01H 9/18] for supplying bobbins, cores, receptacles, or completed packages to, or transporting from, paying-out or take-up stations -- The code D01H 9/18 covers devices that supply bobbins, cores, receptacles or finished packages to, or transport them from, the pay-out or take-up stations of spinning or twisting machines, serving as accessories for these operations.
[D01H 9/182] Overhead conveying devices -- D01H 9/182 relates to overhead conveying devices that serve as accessories for spinning or twisting machines in the textile sector. These devices transport materials, tools or components above the equipment, typically using rollers, belts or chains to move items between workstations.
[D01H 9/185] Transporting cans -- This subclass deals with devices and arrangements that move cans-containers used for fibers or yarn-through or between parts of spinning or twisting machinery. It includes conveyors, elevators and other handling accessories that transport the cans within the machine line.
[D01H 9/187] on individual supports, e.g. pallets -- The subclass covers features or accessories that provide individual support units, such as pallets, for machines used in spinning or twisting of fibers. It includes designs, arrangements or mechanisms that hold, transport or position yarn packages, bobbins or spindles on separate supports within the textile production line.
[D02] YARNS -- In this class, the following terms are used with the meanings indicated: "fibre" means a relatively-short, elongated member of natural or artificial material; "filament" means an endless or quasi-endless, elongated member of natural or artificial material; "yarn" means a unitary assembly of fibres, usually produced by spinning; "thread" means an assembly of yarns or filaments, usually produced by twisting.
[D02G] CRIMPING OR CURLING FIBRES, FILAMENTS, THREADS, OR YARNS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The post treatment of fibres and filaments (usually synthetic) to give them a structure more like natural fibres i.e. crimped or curled to make them easier to work with during further processing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Yarns or threads, e.g. fancy yarns; Processes or apparatus for the productionthereof -> D02G3/00 - Unwinding, paying-out, forwarding, winding or coilingfilamentary material not intimately associated with spinning or twisting -> B65H - Cores, formers, supports or holders for coiled or wound material, e.g. bobbins -> B65H - Mechanical methods or apparatus in the manufacture of artificial filaments, threads, fibres, bristles, or ribbons -> D01D - Chemical features in the manufacture of artificial filaments, threads, fibres, bristles, or ribbons; apparatus specially adapted for the manufacture of carbon filaments -> D01F - Twisting oakum -> D01G35/00 - Spinning or twisting -> D01H - Making chenille -> D03D , D04D3/00 - General aspects of chemical treatment -> D06M - Testing yarns, rovings, slivers, fibres, or fibre webs -> G01  **Glossary:** - Fibre: a relatively-short, elongated member of natural or artificial material. - Filament: an endless or quasi-endless, elongated member of natural or artificial material. - Yarn: a unitary assembly of fibres, usually produced by spinning. - Thread: an assembly of yarns or filaments, usually produced by twisting. - False twisting: synthetic filaments are twisted, heat set while twisted and then untwisted to form a crimp - Texturing or texturizing: curling and crimping - Stuffer box: a confined space where yarns or filaments are compressed and deformed
[D02G 1/00] Producing crimped or curled fibres, filaments, yarns, or threads, giving them latent characteristics -- This CPC subclass D02G 1/00 covers processes for producing crimped or curled fibers, filaments, yarns, or threads that impart latent characteristics such as elasticity, bulk, or improved handling. It includes mechanical or chemical methods that create permanent or temporary crimp or curl, allowing the material to regain shape after deformation.
[D02G 1/002] by knitting, weaving or tufting, fixing and then unravelling -- This subclass deals with producing crimped or curled fibres, filaments, yarns or threads by first forming them into a fabric or tuft - such as through knitting, weaving or tufting - then fixing the structure and subsequently unravelling it to obtain the crimped product. The process imparts latent characteristics useful for later processing or end-use performance.
[D02G 1/004] by heating fibres, filaments, yarns or threads so as to create a temperature gradient across their diameter, thereby imparting them latent asymmetrical shrinkage properties -- This CPC subclass D02G 1/004 concerns a method of treating fibers, filaments, yarns or threads by heating them so that a temperature gradient is established across their diameter. The gradient creates latent asymmetrical shrinkage, allowing the material to later crimp or curl when activated. The process imparts stored deformation characteristics to the textile.
[D02G 1/006] by impinging the yarn against an uneven surface and thereby deforming it -- D02G 1/006 covers a method of crimping yarn by striking it against a textured or uneven surface so that the yarn is deformed, creating permanent or latent curl. This technique is used to impart bulk, elasticity or texture to yarns in textile manufacturing.
[D02G 1/008] with provision for imparting irregular effects to the yarn -- D02G 1/008 covers processes for crimping or curling yarns where the method includes a step to create irregular or uneven effects, such as varying pitch or amplitude, to give the yarn latent properties for later finishing. It belongs to the textile subclass dealing with yarn crimping.
[D02G 1/02] by twisting, fixing the twist and backtwisting, i.e. by imparting false twist -- D02G 1/02 deals with producing crimped or curled yarns by applying a twist, securing it, and then back-twisting, a process known as imparting false twist. The technique creates latent crimp that can be revealed later during processing.
[D02G 1/0206] by false-twisting -- The code D02G 1/0206 covers the technique of false-twisting to impart a crimp or curl to fibers, filaments, yarns or threads, creating latent properties that can be developed later. False-twisting involves twisting the material, heat-setting, then untwisting, which fixes the desired deformation.
[D02G 1/0213] after drawing the yarn on the same machine -- The D02G 1/0213 entry covers a method where yarn is first drawn and then crimped or curled on the same machine, producing crimped yarn that retains latent characteristics for later processing. This integrated step falls within the textile hierarchy of yarns and the specific subcategory of crimping or curling fibres, filaments, threads or yarns.
