[D04B 1/28] gloves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[D04B 13/00] Circular knitting machines with fixed spring or bearded needles, e.g. loop-wheel machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Circular weft knitting machines with concurrent stitch formation in a row of stitches. Example:
[D04B 13/02] with horizontal needles -- This subgroup relates to circular knitting machines that use fixed spring or bearded needles arranged horizontally, such as loop-wheel machines, where the needles are oriented parallel to the machine axis. It specifies machines where the horizontal needle configuration is a defining feature.
[D04B 15/00] Details of, or auxiliary devices incorporated in, weft knitting machines, restricted to machines of this kind -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All details on devices for weft knitting machines not provided for in one of the sub-groups, e.g. frames or servicing aids.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details or auxiliary devices incorporated in warp knitting machines -> D04B27/00 .
[D04B 15/02] Loop-transfer points -- The CPC subclass D04B 15/02 pertains to auxiliary devices built into weft knitting machines, specifically the mechanisms that define loop-transfer points where yarn loops are guided from one needle to the next. It falls under the hierarchy Textiles → Braising → Knitting and covers details of these transfer components.
[D04B 15/04] for straight-bar knitting machines -- This CPC subclass concerns the structure and auxiliary devices incorporated in weft knitting machines, limited to straight-bar (or straight-needle) knitting machines. It includes mechanisms such as feeders, tensioning units, or other components that support or enhance the knitting process.
[D04B 15/06] Sinkers -- The D04B 15/06 subclass covers sinkers, which are auxiliary devices or details incorporated in weft knitting machines to control yarn placement and tension during the knitting process, limited to machines of this specific type.
[D04B 15/08] Needle latch openers -- The D04B 15/08 class covers auxiliary devices for weft knitting machines that open the needle latch, a mechanism that secures the needle during operation. These needle latch openers facilitate needle release and positioning, improving machine efficiency and stitch quality in weft knitting processes.
[D04B 15/10] Needle beds -- The CPC subclass D04B 15/10 covers needle beds, which are components of weft knitting machines that hold and guide the knitting needles, including details of their design and any auxiliary devices integrated into the machine. It falls under the Textiles > Braiding > Knitting hierarchy.
[D04B 15/12] Shogging devices therefor -- D04B 15/12 pertains to auxiliary devices installed in weft knitting machines, specifically shogging mechanisms that shift the yarn carriage laterally during operation. The entry covers the design and function of these shogging devices for improved fabric formation.
[D04B 15/14] Needle cylinders -- D04B 15/14 pertains to the components of weft knitting machines, specifically needle cylinders and any auxiliary devices that are built into or attached to them. The classification covers details of these cylinders in machines used for weft knitting, without extending to other knitting types.
[D04B 15/16] Driving devices for reciprocatory action -- The code D04B 15/16 covers driving mechanisms that produce reciprocal motion in weft knitting machines, including auxiliary devices integrated into the machine to control back-and-forth movement of needles or other functional parts. It is used for classifying inventions that improve or modify the reciprocating action of such knitting equipment.
[D04B 15/18] Dials -- The CPC entry D04B 15/18 pertains to dials used as auxiliary devices on weft knitting machines. It covers the design, arrangement and operation of rotary controls that adjust tension, stitch length or other machine parameters during the knitting process. The classification falls under the hierarchy textiles-braiding-knitting.
[D04B 15/20] Needle bars -- The classification D04B 15/20 covers needle bars, which are auxiliary components of weft knitting machines. It pertains to devices incorporated in these machines to guide, position or replace needles during the knitting process, and falls under the hierarchy of Textiles → Braiding → Knitting.
[D04B 15/22] Driving devices therefor
[D04B 15/24] Sinker heads -- This CPC subclass D04B 15/24 covers auxiliary devices incorporated in weft knitting machines, specifically sinker heads that guide or control the weft yarn during knitting. It falls under the hierarchy textiles, then braiding, then knitting, and relates to the mechanical components that assist the knitting process.
[D04B 15/26] Slurcocks -- D04B 15/26 - Slurcocks covers auxiliary devices installed in weft knitting machines that regulate or stop the passage of yarn, such as clamps or valves used to control thread supply during operation. The classification is part of the CPC textile section, specifically the knitting subgroup.
[D04B 15/28] Needle pressers -- This CPC subgroup covers auxiliary devices fitted to weft-knitting machines, specifically needle pressers that act on the needles during the weaving process. The pressers improve stitch formation, regulate needle tension and aid in fabric quality control.
[D04B 15/30] Driving devices for thread-carrier rods -- The CPC subclass D04B 15/30 relates to mechanisms that drive the thread-carrier rods in weft knitting machines, such as cams, gears, belts or electromagnetic actuators, enabling controlled movement of yarn across the needle bed. These auxiliary devices are specific to weft knitting equipment and support the formation of knitted fabrics.
[D04B 15/32] Cam systems or assemblies for operating knitting instruments -- D04B 15/32 relates to auxiliary devices for weft knitting machines, specifically cam mechanisms or assemblies that control knitting needles or other knitting instruments. The entry covers the design and integration of cam systems used to drive or coordinate the motion of these instruments during the knitting process.
[D04B 15/322] in circular knitting machines with needle cylinder and dial  cam systems specially provided for dials  D04B15/34 -- This subclass relates to auxiliary devices incorporated in weft knitting machines, specifically circular knitting machines that use a needle cylinder together with dial-cam systems and include special provisions for the dials.
[D04B 15/325] in circular knitting machines with two opposed needle cylinders -- This CPC subclass (D04B 15/325) concerns auxiliary devices or details incorporated in weft knitting machines, limited to circular knitting machines that use two opposed needle cylinders. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → BRAIDING → KNITTING and addresses specialized features of such machines.
[D04B 15/327] for stitch-length regulation -- This classification covers auxiliary devices incorporated in weft-knitting machines that control the length of each stitch. It pertains to mechanisms such as length regulators, sensors, or adjustable cam units that keep stitch length consistent during operation. Belongs to textile braid/knitting hierarchy.
[D04B 15/34] for dials -- This subclass covers auxiliary devices such as dials that are integrated into weft knitting machines, focusing on control or adjustment mechanisms specific to those machines and situated within the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting.
[D04B 15/36] for flat-bed knitting machines -- The CPC code D04B 15/36 covers flat-bed weft knitting machines, specifically detailing auxiliary devices and components built into those machines. It falls within the hierarchy of Textiles > Braiding > Knitting and concerns the functional features of flat-bed knitting equipment.
[D04B 15/362] with two needle beds in V-formation -- The D04B 15/362 subclass defines auxiliary components or structural details for weft knitting machines that employ two needle beds set in a V-shaped arrangement. It falls under the textile knitting hierarchy and addresses devices integrated into this specific machine configuration.
[D04B 15/365] with provision for loop transfer from one needle bed to the other -- This subclass details auxiliary devices fitted to weft knitting machines that allow a loop to be transferred from one needle bed to another, enabling inter-bed stitch formation. It applies only to machines of this type within the knitting segment of textile technology.
[D04B 15/367] with two opposed needle beds -- The subclass D04B 15/367 covers auxiliary features or detailed components built into weft knitting machines that have two opposed needle beds, such as devices for controlling yarn feed, tension, or stitch formation specific to this twin-bed arrangement. It pertains only to machines configured with the two beds operating in opposition.
[D04B 15/38] Devices for supplying, feeding, or guiding threads to needles -- The entry D04B 15/38 covers auxiliary devices built into weft knitting machines that manage the delivery of yarn to the needles. It includes mechanisms for supplying, feeding, or guiding threads during the knitting process, specifically for machines that form weft-knitted fabrics.
[D04B 15/40] Holders or supports for thread packages -- D04B 15/40 pertains to holders or supports for thread packages that form part of weft knitting machines, including any auxiliary devices integrated into the machine to manage yarn supply.
[D04B 15/42] Frames for assemblies of two or more reels -- This subclass covers frames that support assemblies of two or more yarn reels in weft-knitting machines, serving as auxiliary devices that organize and feed yarn to the knitting zone. The frames are integral to machines that incorporate such reel arrangements for continuous yarn supply.
[D04B 15/44] Tensioning devices for individual threads -- The classification covers devices that apply tension to each yarn individually within weft knitting machines, enabling precise control of thread feed and stitch formation. Such tensioning mechanisms are auxiliary components that improve fabric uniformity and prevent yarn breakage during the knitting process.
[D04B 15/46] for elastic threads -- D04B 15/46 covers auxiliary devices integrated in weft-knitting machines that handle elastic threads, such as feeders, tensioners or guides that enable the incorporation of stretch yarn into the fabric.
[D04B 15/48] Thread-feeding devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All positive thread feeding devices actively forwarding thread from the storage to a knitting head and thread feeding devices not covered by one of the sub-groups.
[D04B 15/482] comprising a rotatable or stationary intermediate storage drum from which the thread is axially and intermittently pulled off; Devices which can be switched between positive feed and intermittent feed -- D04B 15/482 covers auxiliary devices for weft-knitting machines that incorporate a rotatable or stationary intermediate storage drum from which yarn is drawn axially and intermittently, and that can be switched between a positive feed mode and an intermittent feed mode. This sub-class falls under TEXTILES → BRAIDING → KNITTING.
[D04B 15/484] Yarn braking means acting on the drum -- This subclass covers devices that control yarn flow on the drum of weft knitting machines, providing braking action to regulate tension and feed rate. It is used as an auxiliary feature in weft knitting machines within the broader categories of textiles, braiding, and knitting.
[D04B 15/486] Monitoring reserve quantity -- The entry D04B 15/486 pertains to auxiliary devices incorporated in weft-knitting machines that monitor the amount of reserve yarn available. It covers systems that detect, display or control the remaining yarn supply to ensure continuous knitting operation. This classification falls under the textiles hierarchy of braiding and knitting.
[D04B 15/488] in co-operation with stitch-length-regulating mechanism -- This subclass pertains to auxiliary components built into weft knitting machines that operate together with a stitch-length-regulating mechanism. It covers details of such devices that adjust or control stitch length during knitting.
[D04B 15/50] for elastic threads -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary devices and detailed features incorporated in weft knitting machines that are specifically designed for elastic threads. It includes mechanisms for feeding, tensioning, or otherwise handling stretchable yarns within the machine, and is applied to patents that improve knitting of elastic materials.
[D04B 15/52] for straight-bar knitting machines -- This subclass relates to the design, construction or auxiliary components of straight-bar weft knitting machines, covering features such as feeding, tension-control, drive or other devices that are incorporated into or added onto these specific machines.
[D04B 15/54] Thread guides -- D04B 15/54 in the CPC classifies the thread-guide mechanisms that direct yarn in weft knitting machines, covering auxiliary devices incorporated into these machines. It is nested under the hierarchy textiles - braiding - knitting and pertains to details of, or devices incorporated in, weft knitting equipment.
[D04B 15/56] for flat-bed knitting machines -- The code D04B 15/56 covers auxiliary components and detailed features built into or added to weft knitting machines designed for flat-bed operation. It includes devices such as tension regulators, feeding mechanisms and other support equipment that enhance the performance of flat-bed weft knitters.
[D04B 15/565] Associated thread-clamping or thread-severing devices -- D04B 15/565 covers auxiliary devices integrated into weft knitting machines that clamp or sever the weft thread, specifically designed for machines used in knitting operations. It falls under the hierarchy of TEXTILES → BRAIDING → KNITTING, focusing on equipment that directly controls thread handling during the knitting process.
[D04B 15/58] for circular knitting machines -- The CPC entry D04B 15/58 covers auxiliary devices and detailed components that are incorporated into weft knitting machines of the circular type. It pertains specifically to mechanisms such as feed rollers, tensioners, and control elements that support the operation of circular knitting equipment. This classification falls under the broader textile group for knitting and the more specific sub-group for braiding.
[D04B 15/60] with thread-clamping or -severing devices -- The CPC entry D04B 15/60 refers to auxiliary devices built into weft-knitting machines, specifically mechanisms that clamp or cut the thread during operation. It covers any thread-clamping or thread-severing features integrated in such machines, falling under the broader textile, braiding and knitting categories.
[D04B 15/61] arranged within needle circle -- D04B 15/61 covers auxiliary components or structural details positioned inside the needle circle of weft knitting machines. The code applies specifically to devices integrated into the machine's knitting zone, such as guides, feeders or tensioning elements that operate within the area bounded by the needles.
[D04B 15/62] with thread knotters -- The D04B 15/62 subclass covers weft-knitting machines that incorporate thread-knotting devices, which automatically form knots in the weft yarn as it is fed into the machine. These auxiliary mechanisms are integral to the machine's construction and enable seamless linking of yarn sections during knitting.
[D04B 15/64] for straight-bar knitting machines -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary components built into weft knitting machines, specifically straight-bar (straight-needle) machines, such as feed devices, tensioning mechanisms or other accessories that aid the knitting process. It resides under TEXTILES → BRAIDING → KNITTING.
[D04B 15/66] Devices for determining or controlling patterns -- The subclass D04B 15/66 covers auxiliary devices fitted to weft knitting machines that detect or regulate stitch patterns, allowing precise control of the fabric design. It includes sensors, electronic or mechanical controllers, and related mechanisms that monitor yarn placement and adjust pattern formation during knitting.
[D04B 15/665] Driving-gear for programme or pattern devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Driving-gear including electronic control means, e.g. microcomputers for driving the knitting machine.
[D04B 15/68] characterised by the knitting instruments used -- D04B 15/68 covers auxiliary devices or specific details of weft knitting machines that are defined by the type of knitting instruments employed. The classification applies only to machines of this particular kind and includes features such as guides, tensioning elements, or other components that assist the weft-knitting process.
[D04B 15/70] in flat-bed knitting machines -- The CPC code D04B 15/70 covers specific details and auxiliary devices of weft knitting machines that are of the flat-bed type. [D04B 15/74] Pattern drums -- The code D04B 15/74 covers pattern drums, which are auxiliary components fitted to weft-knitting machines to generate repeatable stitch designs. It includes the construction, mounting and operation of these drums, which control yarn feed and pattern selection during the knitting process.
[D04B 15/76] Pattern wheels -- The entry D04B 15/76 refers to pattern wheels, auxiliary devices incorporated in weft-knitting machines that control stitch formation or fabric patterning. [D04B 15/78] Electrical devices -- The CPC subclass D04B 15/78 comprises electrical devices that are details or auxiliary components built into weft knitting machines, limited to that specific type of machine. [D04B 15/80] characterised by the thread guides used -- The subclass D04B 15/80 covers auxiliary equipment or detailed components of weft knitting machines that are defined by the specific thread-guide arrangements employed. [D04B 23/00] Flat warp knitting machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flat warp knitting machines with one or two needle bars, e.g. tricot or raschel machines. Example:
[D04B 23/02] with two sets of needles -- Flat warp knitting machines that employ two separate needle sets, allowing simultaneous knitting of two yarn courses to create fabrics with distinct front and back structures or complex patterns such as rib, interlock or double-face textiles. This configuration enables higher productivity and more versatile stitch formations compared with single-needle arrangements.
[D04B 23/04] with independently-movable knitting needles -- The CPC subclass D04B 23/04 covers flat warp knitting machines that feature independently movable knitting needles. It falls under the textile field, specifically in the braiding and knitting sections, and relates to machines that can alter needle position for complex pattern formation.
[D04B 23/06] for producing fabrics consisting of, or incorporating, elastic threads -- The CPC subclass D04B 23/06 covers flat warp-knitting machines that produce fabrics containing or made from elastic yarns. It includes techniques for introducing stretch fibres during the knitting process to create flexible, stretchable textile structures. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of textiles → braiding → knitting → flat warp knitting machines.
[D04B 23/08] with provision for incorporating pile threads -- D04B 23/08 denotes flat warp-knitting machines equipped with a system for adding pile yarns while the fabric is formed. The subclass covers any provision-such as separate feed rollers, needles or guide mechanisms-that enables the creation of piled surfaces like terry or plush directly on the loom.
[D04B 23/10] for knitting through thread, fleece, or fabric layers, or around elongated core material -- This subclass covers techniques where a flat warp knitting machine forms stitches by passing yarn through an existing thread, fleece or fabric layer, or by encircling a continuous elongated core such as a tape or filament, enabling the production of reinforced fabrics, composite structures, and garments with integrated cores.
[D04B 23/12] with provision for incorporating unlooped wefts extending from selvedge to selvedge -- D04B 23/12 relates to flat warp knitting machines equipped with a means for introducing unlooped wefts that extend continuously from selvedge to selvedge, enabling the formation of integrated supplemental yarns or functional inserts within the knitted fabric. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Textiles → Braiding → Knitting.
[D04B 23/14] with provision for incorporating small auxiliary elements, e.g. for decorative purposes -- The D04B 23/14 subclass covers flat warp knitting machines that are designed to incorporate small auxiliary elements, such as decorative inserts, into the fabric during knitting. It relates to textile equipment enabling integrated embellishment of knits without separate post-processing steps.
[D04B 23/16] specially adapted for producing fabrics, or article blanks, of particular form or configuration -- Flat warp knitting machines specially adapted to produce fabrics or article blanks that have a particular shape or configuration, such as pre-shaped panels, three-dimensional structures or custom-cut forms, enabling the creation of items with defined geometry directly from the knitting process.
[D04B 23/18] with provision for narrowing or widening -- Flat warp knitting machines equipped with a mechanism that can adjust the stitch width, enabling the fabric to be narrowed or widened during production. This feature allows variable gauge knitting, providing flexibility in shaping the textile while maintaining continuous warp knitting operation.
[D04B 23/20] for producing stocking blanks -- The D04B 23/20 subclass refers to flat warp knitting machines specifically designed to produce stocking blanks, i.e., unfinished tubular fabric pieces that form the basis of stockings. It falls under the textiles hierarchy of braiding and knitting, focusing on equipment that creates the raw knitted sections before they are shaped and finished into final hosiery.
[D04B 23/22] with special thread-guiding means -- This subclass covers flat warp knitting machines equipped with special thread-guiding means, such as guides, rollers or other devices that control the path of warp yarns during knitting to improve stitch formation, tension control or fabric quality. It falls under the CPC hierarchy textiles-braiding-knitting-flat warp knitting machines.
[D04B 23/24] with cut needle presser arrangements to produce patterns -- D04B 23/24 covers flat warp knitting machines that use cut-needle presser mechanisms to create patterned fabric structures. The cut-needle presser arrangement allows individual needles to be raised or lowered during knitting, enabling the formation of intricate designs and varying stitch densities within the same fabric.
[D04B 25/00] Warp knitting machines not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Warp knitting machines specially adapted for knitting articles of particular structure or configuration, e.g. meat casings, ornamental articles etc. Example:
[D04B 25/02] Tubular machines -- D04B 25/02 classifies warp-knitting machines designed specifically to produce tubular fabrics without a need for separate shaping steps. These tubular machines form continuous tube-shaped textiles used in garments, medical tubing, and technical applications, and are not covered by other warp-knitting categories.
[D04B 25/04] Milanese machines -- Milanese machines are a subgroup of warp-knitting equipment designed for producing fabrics by interlacing warp yarns on two needle beds that operate in parallel. They are used mainly for flat or tubular textile structures such as ribbons, elastic bands and technical webbing, and are not covered by any other more specific subclass.
[D04B 25/06] Galloon crocheting machines -- D04B 25/06 covers machines designed to produce galloon-narrow, ornamental braid-by a crocheting process. These devices belong to the warp-knitting family and automate the formation of decorative textile strips used in apparel trim, upholstery and similar applications.
[D04B 25/08] for producing pile fabrics -- This CPC subgroup pertains to warp-knitting machines that are specially configured to produce pile fabrics, creating a raised surface of loops or tufts for a textured finish. It sits within the hierarchy of textiles, braiding and knitting, focusing on equipment not otherwise covered elsewhere.
[D04B 25/10] for producing patterned fabrics -- The CPC subclass D04B 25/10 covers warp-knitting machines that are not otherwise specified and are designed for producing patterned fabrics. It falls under the hierarchy textiles → braiding → knitting and relates to equipment that creates decorative or structured patterns during the warp-knitting process.
[D04B 25/12] with independently-movable weft-thread guides controlled by Jacquard mechanisms -- The D04B 25/12 subclass covers warp knitting machines that feature independently movable weft-thread guides operated by Jacquard control mechanisms. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles → braiding → knitting → warp knitting machines not otherwise provided for, and specifies the integration of Jacquard-driven guide movement for complex stitch patterns.
[D04B 25/14] specially adapted for producing articles of particular configuration -- This subclass covers warp-knitting machines that are specially adapted or equipped to produce textile articles having a particular configuration, such as specific shapes, patterns or structural features not addressed by standard warp-knitting equipment.
[D04B 27/00] Details of, or auxiliary devices incorporated in, warp knitting machines, restricted to machines of this kind -- **Definition:** This place covers:  all details on devices for warp knitting machines not provided for in one of the sub-groups, e.g. frames or servicing aids.
[D04B 27/02] Warp-thread guides -- This subclass relates to warp knitting machines and covers the design, function, or auxiliary devices that guide the warp threads through the machine. It includes mechanisms that position, tension, or direct warp yarns during knitting, limited to warp-thread guide components.
[D04B 27/04] Sinkers -- The D04B 27/04 entry covers sinkers, which are auxiliary components built into warp knitting machines to control fabric tension and yarn positioning during knitting. It relates specifically to details of such devices for warp knitting equipment.
[D04B 27/06] Needle bars -- D04B 27/06 covers needle bars and related auxiliary devices that are part of warp-knitting machines, focusing on the mechanisms that guide and control needles during the knitting process. The code falls under textiles, braiding, knitting.
[D04B 27/08] Driving devices therefor -- This subclass covers mechanisms that provide power transmission or motion control for warp-knitting machines, including gears, shafts and other drive components that move the warp threads during knitting.
[D04B 27/10] Devices for supplying, feeding, or guiding threads to needles -- The CPC subclass D04B 27/10 relates to devices that supply, feed or guide threads to the needles of warp-knitting machines, including any auxiliary mechanisms built into such machines. It is situated within the hierarchy textiles → braiding → knitting and addresses details of thread handling equipment specific to warp knitting.
[D04B 27/12] Tensioning devices for individual threads -- The D04B 27/12 subclass concerns tensioning devices that act on individual warp threads in warp-knitting machines, detailing mechanisms that regulate thread tension during operation. It is located under textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 27/14] Thread tensioning rod arrangements -- The D04B 27/14 entry pertains to auxiliary devices in warp-knitting machines, specifically arrangements of rods that provide thread tensioning during operation. It classifies inventions that improve thread handling in knitting processes within the broader textiles-braiding-knitting hierarchy.
[D04B 27/16] Warp beams -- The entry D04B 27/16 pertains to warp beams and any auxiliary devices incorporated in warp knitting machines, covering their construction, adjustment, and feeding functions. It falls within the hierarchy textiles - braiding - knitting and addresses details specific to machines of this type.
[D04B 27/18] Warp beam braking devices for thread tensioning -- This subclass covers auxiliary mechanisms integrated in warp knitting machines that control thread tension by braking the warp beam. It includes devices that adjust or regulate the braking force on the warp beam to maintain consistent tension during knitting.
[D04B 27/20] Warp beam driving devices -- D04B 27/20 designates warp-beam driving devices for warp knitting machines, covering details of, or auxiliary devices incorporated in, the beam drive system and limited to machines of this kind.
[D04B 27/22] electrically controlled -- The subclass D04B 27/22 covers electrically controlled auxiliary devices incorporated in warp-knitting machines, such as sensors, actuators, electronic control units or other electrically driven mechanisms that regulate the operation of these machines. It pertains specifically to warp-knitting equipment within the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting.
[D04B 27/24] Thread guide bar assemblies -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary devices incorporated in warp knitting machines, specifically thread guide bar assemblies that direct yarns during knitting. It falls under the hierarchy textiles - braiding - knitting and is used to improve yarn handling and fabric formation.
[D04B 27/26] Shogging devices therefor -- The CPC subclass D04B 27/26 relates to auxiliary or detail devices fitted to warp-knitting machines that provide shogging, i.e., lateral movement control of the warp. It covers mechanisms integrated into such machines for adjusting fabric formation, and is nested under textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 27/28] with arrangements to reduce the number of members of pattern chains -- This CPC subclass covers warp-knitting machines that incorporate mechanisms to shorten pattern chains by reducing the number of yarn carriers or needles involved, simplifying the stitch pattern. It relates to auxiliary devices built into the machine to achieve this reduction.
[D04B 27/30] with driving-gear comprising force-multiplication devices -- This subclass relates to warp-knitting machines that include auxiliary devices where the driving gear incorporates force-multiplication mechanisms, such as gear reducers, hydraulic drives or similar devices, to increase torque to the knitting needles.
[D04B 27/32] with independently-movable thread guides controlled by Jacquard mechanisms -- This subclass covers warp-knitting machines equipped with auxiliary devices that have independently movable thread guides controlled by Jacquaar d mechanisms, enabling individual yarn selection during knitting. It pertains to detailed features of such warp-knitting equipment.
[D04B 27/34] Take-up or draw-off devices for knitted products -- It covers mechanisms that retrieve or release knitted fabric from warp knitting machines, such as rollers, clamps or belts that control fabric tension and guide the material as it is formed. These devices are auxiliary components of warp knitting equipment that ensure smooth take-up or draw-off of the product.
[D04B 27/36] with temples -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary devices incorporated in warp-knitting machines, specifically the inclusion of temples that guide or support the warp threads during operation. It pertains to machines designed for warp knitting where such temples are integral to the machine's structure or function.
[D04B 3/00] Hand tools or implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand knitting methods using knitting needles for forming stitches, and devices for effecting such knitting methods.

[D04B 31/00] Crocheting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Crocheting methods using crocheting needles for drawing stitches. Example:
[D04B 31/02] Crocheted strips or threads -- The CPC entry D04B 31/02 covers crocheted strips or continuous threads produced by crochet techniques, a subset of textile manufacturing under knitting and braiding. It addresses the creation of elongated, flexible elements that can be used as yarns, decorative bands, or components in woven or knitted fabrics.
[D04B 33/00] Crocheting tools or apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for effecting crocheting methods. Example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Galloon crocheting machines -> D04B25/06
[D04B 35/00] Details of, or auxiliary devices incorporated in, knitting machines, not otherwise provided for -- D04B 35/00 covers the specific components and auxiliary devices that are integrated into knitting machines when they are not covered by any other more detailed subclass. It includes features such as specialized feeders, tensioning devices, guide mechanisms or other add-on parts that improve or modify the knitting process but are not otherwise classified.
[D04B 35/02] Knitting tools or instruments not provided for in group -- The CPC subclass D04B 35/02 covers auxiliary devices and specific tools for knitting machines that are not covered elsewhere in the classification. It includes details of custom or specialized equipment integrated into knitting apparatus to perform functions not otherwise described. The entry falls under textiles, specifically the knitting category.
[D04B 35/04] Latch needles -- Latch needles are auxiliary components used in knitting machines to control loop formation and release. They feature a movable latch that opens and closes around the needle tip, allowing precise handling of yarn and facilitating complex stitch patterns not covered by standard machine elements.
[D04B 35/06] Sliding-tongue needles -- D04B 35/06 (Sliding-tongue needles) pertains to details of or auxiliary devices incorporated in knitting machines that are not otherwise specified, focusing on the sliding-tongue needle mechanism used to control loop formation during knitting. It falls under the broader hierarchy of textiles, braiding, and knitting.
[D04B 35/08] Spring or bearded needles -- The code D04B 35/08 covers spring or bearded needles used as auxiliary components in knitting machines. These needles provide self-actuating movement or grip to improve stitch formation and are classified under textiles - braiding - knitting.
[D04B 35/10] Indicating, warning, or safety devices, e.g. stop motions -- The entry D04B 35/10 covers auxiliary devices built into knitting machines that provide indication, warning or safety functions, such as mechanisms that stop motion of the machine. These components are not otherwise specified in the classification and relate to textile knitting equipment.
[D04B 35/12] responsive to thread consumption -- The D04B 35/12 subclass covers features of knitting machines that monitor or react to the amount of thread being used. It includes auxiliary devices or mechanisms that adjust operation based on thread consumption, when such details are not addressed elsewhere in the classification.
[D04B 35/14] responsive to thread breakage -- D04B 35/14 covers auxiliary devices incorporated in knitting machines that detect and react to yarn breakage, such as break-detection sensors or automatic shut-off mechanisms, ensuring continuous operation and preventing fabric defects.
[D04B 35/16] with detectors associated with a series of threads -- D04B 35/16 covers knitting-machine accessories that incorporate sensors or detectors linked to a series of yarns, enabling monitoring or control of thread behavior during operation. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, braiding, and knitting and concerns auxiliary devices not otherwise specified.
[D04B 35/18] responsive to breakage, misplacement, or malfunctioning of knitting instruments -- The entry covers mechanisms in knitting machines that sense or correct broken, displaced, or defective needles or other knitting tools, automatically adjusting operation or signalling a fault, and is not addressed by other more specific sub-entries.
[D04B 35/20] responsive to defects, e.g. holes, in knitted products -- Devices or methods built into knitting machines that detect fabric defects such as holes and automatically respond, for example by repairing, reinforcing, or adjusting the stitch pattern to correct the flaw during production. This auxiliary equipment is specific to textile knitting processes and is not covered by other classifications.
[D04B 35/22] Devices for preparatory treatment of threads -- D04B 35/22 covers auxiliary devices incorporated in knitting machines that perform preparatory treatment of yarns before knitting, such as tensioning, cleaning, lubricating or arranging threads. These devices are not otherwise specified in other subclasses of the knitting section.
[D04B 35/24] by moistening or lubricating -- The subclass D04B 35/24 concerns knitting-machine provisions that moisten or lubricate the knitting process, such as spray nozzles, oiling systems or other moisture-applying devices integrated into the machine. These features aid yarn feeding, tension control and stitch formation and are not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[D04B 35/26] by heating -- The D04B 35/26 subclass concerns knitting machines that incorporate heating elements or devices to aid the knitting process. It includes auxiliary heating means such as heated needles, rollers, or zones that apply temperature to yarns or fabrics for shaping, setting, or processing synthetic fibers, where the heating function is not covered by other more specific subclasses.
[D04B 35/28] Devices for lubricating machine parts -- This subclass covers devices that apply lubrication to moving parts of knitting machines, including mechanisms and auxiliary systems for delivering oil or other lubricants to machine components that are not otherwise specified elsewhere in the classification.
[D04B 35/30] Devices for controlling temperature of machine parts -- These are auxiliary temperature-control devices incorporated in knitting machines, such as heaters, coolers or thermostatic elements, designed to regulate the temperature of machine parts during operation. The devices are used to maintain optimal conditions for yarn handling and machine performance.
[D04B 35/32] Devices for removing lint or fluff -- Devices incorporated in knitting machines that remove lint, fluff or other loose fibres from the fabric or yarn path, typically using air jets, brushes, suction or vibrating elements. These auxiliary mechanisms help maintain fabric quality and prevent defects during the knitting process.
[D04B 35/34] Devices for cutting knitted fabrics -- Devices for cutting knitted fabrics are auxiliary components integrated into knitting machines that separate finished material from the machine's needles or rollers. These cutting tools improve production efficiency by providing precise, on-machine removal of the knit without manual handling.
[D04B 35/36] Devices for printing, coating, or napping knitted fabrics -- D04B 35/36 covers auxiliary devices incorporated in knitting machines that apply printing, coating or napping treatments to knitted fabrics. It belongs to the textile hierarchy under braiding and knitting, specifying equipment that performs surface finishing during the knitting process rather than as a separate post-knitting operation.
[D04B 37/00] Auxiliary apparatus or devices for use with knitting machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ancillaries for use with but separate from knitting machines, e.g. design systems or knitting tool containers.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices exclusively incorporated in weft knitting machines -> D04B15/00 - Devices exclusively incorporated in warp knitting machines -> D04B27/00 - Details of, or auxiliary devices incorporated in, knitting machines not limited to specific types of knitting machines or not otherwise provided for -> D04B35/00
[D04B 37/02] with weft knitting machines -- The CPC subclass D04B 37/02 covers auxiliary apparatus or devices that are employed with weft knitting machines. It includes items such as feeders, tension-control units, take-up mechanisms and other accessories that support or enhance the operation of machines that produce fabrics by weft knitting.
[D04B 37/04] for inserting or adjusting pattern pins or like elements in pattern drums or wheels -- This classification covers devices used on knitting machines to insert or adjust pattern pins or similar elements in pattern drums or wheels. It falls under the CPC code D04B 37/04, a subcategory of auxiliary apparatus for knitting within the textile, braiding and knitting hierarchy.
[D04B 37/06] with warp knitting machines -- D04B 37/06 relates to auxiliary apparatus or devices specially designed for use with warp-knitting machines, covering tools, attachments or mechanisms that assist, control, or enhance the operation of these knitting machines. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[D04B 39/00] Knitting processes, apparatus or machines not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Knitted articles, knitting processes and apparatus for making such articles not or not fully covered by weft or warp knitting technology, e.g. Kemafil articles or needleless knitting machines. Examples:
[D04B 39/02] with work carrier in screw form -- D04B 39/02 designates knitting processes, equipment or machines that use a screw-type work carrier to move yarn through the knitting zone. The screw form feeds the yarn and controls stitch formation, distinguishing it from other carrier mechanisms. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting.
[D04B 39/04] adapted for combined weft and warp knitting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[D04B 39/06] adapted for combined knitting and weaving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[D04B 39/08] Sewing machines modified for knitting -- D04B 39/08 refers to sewing machines that have been altered to perform knitting operations. The entry is placed under textiles → braiding → knitting and covers knitting processes, apparatus or machines not otherwise provided for, specifically those where a standard sewing machine is modified to produce knitted fabric.
[D04B 5/00] Knitting apparatus or machines without needles for domestic use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Knitting tools forming stitches without needle movements. Example:
[D04B 7/00] Flat-bed knitting machines with independently-movable needles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flat weft knitting machines with sequential stitch formation in a row of stitches.
[D04B 7/02] with one set of needles -- This subclass covers flat-bed knitting machines in which each needle can move independently but the machine uses only a single set of needles rather than multiple overlapping rows. It relates to textile production where a lone needle bed performs all knitting actions.
[D04B 7/025] with stitch-length regulation  cam systems for stitch-length regulation  D04B15/327
[D04B 7/04] with two sets of needles -- This subclass covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently and are arranged in two separate sets, enabling simultaneous formation of interlaced stitches. It falls under the knitting group within textile braiding.
[D04B 7/045] with stitch-length regulation  cam systems for stitch-length regulation  D04B15/327
[D04B 7/06] for purl work or Links-Links loop formation -- D04B 7/06 covers flat-bed knitting machines in which each needle can move independently to produce purl stitches or linked loop structures, enabling the formation of links-links loops. This provision is used for creating fabrics with distinctive textured patterns on flat beds.
[D04B 7/08] for domestic use -- D04B 7/08 designates flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently and are intended for domestic use, such as home-based sewing, hobby knitting or small-scale garment production. The code falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES ► BRAIDING ► KNITTING ► Flat-bed knitting machines with independently-movable needles.
[D04B 7/10] with provision for narrowing or widening to produce fully-fashioned goods -- The CPC subclass D04B 7/10 is positioned under Textiles > Braiding > Knitting and relates to flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently to narrow or widen the stitch formation. This capability enables the production of fully-fashioned goods directly on the machine.
[D04B 7/12] with provision for incorporating pile threads -- Flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently and that feature a system for adding pile yarns during the knitting cycle, allowing the production of fabrics with raised loops or pile constructions. This subclass falls under knitting in the broader textile and braiding categories.
[D04B 7/14] with provision for incorporating internal threads in laid-in fabrics -- D04B 7/14 covers flat-bed knitting machines equipped with a mechanism that can introduce additional internal threads into fabrics as they are laid in, enabling complex structures such as in-garments or decorative yarns. The provision allows the needles to handle extra yarns while forming the knitted material. This sub-class falls under textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 7/16] for producing fabrics consisting of, or incorporating, elastic threads -- D04B 7/16 refers to flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently and are used to produce fabrics that contain or are made from elastic threads, enabling stretch properties in the final material. This subclass falls under textiles-braiding-knitting and addresses the specific method of incorporating elastic yarns during the knitting process.
[D04B 7/18] incorporated as weft or inlaid threads -- D04B 7/18 covers flat-bed knitting machines whose independently movable needles can incorporate additional weft or inlaid threads during the knitting cycle, enabling the creation of fabrics with supplemental yarns for patterning, reinforcement or functional effects.
[D04B 7/20] with provision for changing the fabric construction, e.g. from plain to rib-loop fabric -- The D04B 7/20 subclass covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently and that incorporate a mechanism to alter the fabric structure during operation, allowing switches such as from a plain stitch to a rib-loop configuration. It pertains to textile equipment that can dynamically change knit patterns without stopping the machine.
[D04B 7/22] with special provision for commencing goods, e.g. with non-run edges -- A flat-bed knitting machine in which the needles move independently and includes a specific mechanism for starting the fabric, such as handling non-run edges or other special provisions that initiate the knitting of a new piece of material.
[D04B 7/24] for producing patterned fabrics -- D04B 7/24 covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently to create patterned fabrics, detailing mechanisms and control methods that enable varied stitch designs on a single fabric width. It falls under the knitting branch of textile technology, focusing on pattern generation rather than uniform structures.
[D04B 7/26] with colour patterns -- CPC D04B 7/26 covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently and that are able to create colour patterns in the fabric. The subclass falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES > BRAIDING > KNITTING and pertains to equipment for multicolour knitting operations.
[D04B 7/28] with stitch patterns -- Flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently to create specific stitch patterns, allowing varied textures and designs within a single fabric. This subclass covers machines that program or manually select these patterns during operation for advanced textile applications.
[D04B 7/30] specially adapted for knitting goods of particular configuration -- D04B 7/30 covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles move independently and are specially adapted to produce knitted articles of particular shapes or configurations, such as three-dimensional or uniquely contoured fabrics, within the broader textile and knitting classification.
[D04B 7/32] tubular goods -- D04B 7/32 covers flat-bed knitting machines whose needles can move independently to produce tubular goods, such as seamless sleeves, socks or hose, directly from yarn without a separate shaping step. The classification falls under textiles-braiding-knitting and focuses on the machine architecture that enables continuous, round fabric formation.
[D04B 7/34] gloves -- The code D04B 7/34 covers the production of gloves on flat-bed knitting machines where each needle moves independently, allowing shaped hand coverings to be knitted directly from yarn. It falls within the textiles-braiding-knitting hierarchy and is used by the EPO and USPTO to index glove-manufacturing patents.
[D04B 9/00] Circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  circular weft knitting machines with sequential stitch formation in a row of stitches. Example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - circular warp knitting machines -> D04B25/02 , D04B25/04
[D04B 9/02] with one set of needles -- D04B 9/02 covers circular knitting machines that employ a single set of independently movable needles to form loops, allowing each needle to be raised, lowered or shifted individually. The class is used for machines producing tubular or flat fabrics without multiple needle beds.
[D04B 9/025] with stitch-length regulation  cam systems for stitch-length regulation  D04B15/327
[D04B 9/04] with spring or bearded needles -- The D04B 9/04 subclass pertains to circular knitting machines whose needles are independently movable and feature either spring-loaded or bearded tips, allowing each needle to be raised and lowered separately during operation. This design is used to produce knitted structures on a rotating drum and falls under the hierarchy of textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 9/06] with needle cylinder and dial for ribbed goods -- This subclass covers circular knitting machines that use a needle cylinder and a dial to create ribbed fabrics, producing the alternating knit-and-purl stitches characteristic of ribs. The machines feature independently movable needles, enabling the formation of raised and recessed rib patterns.
[D04B 9/065] with stitch-length regulation  cam systems for stitch-length regulation  D04B15/327 -- Circular knitting machines that use independently-movable needles and incorporate cam mechanisms to control stitch length. The cams adjust needle movement during each cycle, allowing precise regulation of stitch size across the fabric. This technology is classified under D04B 9/065 in the CPC.
[D04B 9/08] for interlock goods -- D04B 9/08 covers circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles that are configured to produce interlock fabrics, a double-knit structure where two sets of loops intermesh to form a stable, reversible textile.
[D04B 9/10] with two needle cylinders for purl work or for Links-Links loop formation -- This CPC subclass D04B 9/10 covers circular knitting machines that use two needle cylinders to produce purl stitches or to form Links-Links loops, a technique that creates specialized texture and elasticity in knitted fabrics. It is situated under the hierarchy textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 9/12] with provision for incorporating pile threads -- Circular knitting machines whose needles can move independently and include a mechanism for feeding pile yarns during knitting, enabling the production of piled fabrics such as terry or looped textures. The classification covers the arrangement of the needle drive, yarn delivery system and controls that allow pile yarn incorporation within the circular knitting process.
[D04B 9/14] with provision for incorporating loose fibres, e.g. in high-pile fabrics -- D04B 9/14 relates to circular knitting machines that include a mechanism for adding loose fibres during knitting, enabling the creation of high-pile fabrics such as carpets or shaggy textiles. The provision typically involves feeder systems or auxiliary needle arrangements that incorporate the fibres into the stitch structure.
[D04B 9/16] with provision for incorporating internal threads in laid-in fabrics -- The D04B 9/16 entry covers circular knitting machines equipped with a system that inserts internal threads into the fabric as it is produced, creating laid-in yarns within the knit structure. This feature enables the creation of reinforced or decorative elements without stopping the machine, and falls under the hierarchy of textiles → braiding → knitting.
[D04B 9/18] with provision for splicing by incorporating reinforcing threads -- Circular knitting machines that include a mechanism for splicing by inserting reinforcing threads, allowing the yarn to be joined or strengthened during knitting. This feature is used to repair breaks or add strength without stopping the machine, fitting within the textile-braiding-knitting hierarchy.
[D04B 9/20] with provision for narrowing or widening -- D04B 9/20 covers circular knitting machines whose needles can move independently and include a mechanism that allows the knitted fabric to be narrowed or widened during operation. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of textiles, braiding, and knitting, and pertains to equipment that adjusts stitch width on the fly.
[D04B 9/22] with provision for changing the fabric construction, e.g. from plain to rib-loop fabric -- D04B 9/22 covers circular knitting machines whose independently-movable needles can be reconfigured on-the-fly to switch the fabric structure, for example from a plain stitch to a rib or looped construction, allowing different textures to be produced without stopping the machine.
[D04B 9/24] with special provision for commencing goods, e.g. with non-run edges -- D04B 9/24 covers circular knitting machines whose needles move independently and include a special mechanism for starting a fabric piece without a run-edge, such as a non-run edge starter. The provision enables the machine to commence knitting a new garment or product directly, improving edge quality and reducing material waste. This sub-class falls under textiles → braiding → knitting → circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles.
[D04B 9/26] for producing patterned fabrics -- D04B 9/26 covers circular knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently to create patterned fabrics. The classification applies to technologies that control needle selection and timing during knitting, enabling complex designs such as motifs, gradients, or textured effects within a continuous tubular fabric.
[D04B 9/28] with colour patterns -- D04B 9/28 covers circular knitting machines whose independently-movable needles create fabrics with colour patterns, including variegated or multicoloured designs. It addresses the mechanisms and control methods for introducing and coordinating colour changes during the knitting process.
[D04B 9/30] by striping -- D04B 9/30 classifies circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles that produce patterned fabrics by striping, i.e., creating alternating colored or texture bands as the yarn is deposited. This subcategory falls under the broader hierarchy of textiles, braiding and knitting.
[D04B 9/32] by wrap striping -- The subclass D04B 9/32 relates to circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles that form patterns by wrap striping, a method where a strip of yarn is wrapped around each needle to produce coloured or textured stripes on the knitted tube. This technique is used to create decorative bands or functional features in the resulting fabric.
[D04B 9/34] by plating -- D04B 9/34 covers circular knitting machines that have independently-movable needles and are designed to produce plated fabrics, where two yarns are interlaced so that each side of the knitted sheet shows a different color or texture. This subclass focuses on the plating technique applied by the machine's needle control system.
[D04B 9/36] Intarsia work obtained by reciprocatory action -- **Glossary:** - Intarsia: is a knitting technique used to create patterns with multiple colours. There is only one active colour on any given stitch.
[D04B 9/38] with stitch patterns -- This subclass concerns circular knitting machines whose needles are independently movable and can be programmed to produce specific stitch patterns, falling under the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting. It addresses the technical features that enable varied pattern formation on a continuously rotating knitting cylinder.
[D04B 9/40] with provision for transfer of knitted goods from one machine to another -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Circular knitting machines with the provision for transfer of knitted goods from one machine to another whereby the other machine may form part of the knitting machine but performs a special function, e.g. toe closing, not performed on the knitting head.
[D04B 9/42] specially adapted for producing goods of particular configuration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  knitting machines modified for performing particular knitting processes, e.g. knitting of one-piece panty hose.
[D04B 9/44] elongated tubular articles of small diameter, e.g. coverings for cables -- This CPC subclass D04B 9/44 pertains to elongated tubular articles of small diameter, such as protective coverings for cables, produced by circular knitting machines with independently-movable needles. It falls within the hierarchy of textiles, braiding and knitting, addressing the manufacture of narrow, tube-like knitted items.
[D04B 9/46] stockings, or portions thereof -- D04B 9/46 covers knitting technology specifically for producing stockings or parts of stockings using circular knitting machines whose needles can move independently. The classification addresses the design, arrangement and operation of such machines to create hosiery items, focusing on the mechanisms that shape and form the leg-covering fabric. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, braiding and knitting in the cooperative patent system.
[D04B 9/48] non-run stockings -- The CPC code D04B 9/48 covers knitting methods for producing non-run stockings, i.e., stockings that are not made as a continuous length, using circular knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently.
[D04B 9/50] micromesh stockings
[D04B 9/52] surgical stockings -- A surgical stocking is a hosiery item produced on circular knitting machines whose needles can be moved independently, allowing precise control of stitch formation and shaping suited to medical compression requirements. This subclass covers the specific machine features and knitting methods used to manufacture such therapeutic garments.
[D04B 9/54] welts, e.g. double or turned welts -- A D04B 9/54 circular knitting machine feature that creates welts - raised or recessed edges - on knitted fabric, such as double-welts or turned-welts formed by independent needle movements. It falls under textiles → braiding → knitting, covering machinery for producing decorative or structural edge profiles.
[D04B 9/56] heel or toe portions -- The D04B 9/56 subclass covers circular knitting machines equipped with mechanisms for forming the heel or toe sections of garments, such as socks, during the knitting process. It includes features that shape, join or otherwise treat these end portions separately from the main tubular fabric.
[D04B 9/58] gloves -- The D04B 9/58 subclass refers to gloves manufactured on circular knitting machines that have independently-movable needles, allowing the creation of varied stitch structures and precise shaping of each finger and hand portion. It is situated within the textile hierarchy under braiding and knitting, specifically for circular knitting machines with needle independence.
[D04C] BRAIDING OR MANUFACTURE OF LACE, INCLUDING BOBBIN-NET OR CARBONISED LACE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Planar fabric or elongate bulk structure braiding machines. These aspects are covered by the main groups D04C3/00 and D04C5/00  Circular braiding machines producing filled or hollow braids. These aspects are covered by the group D04C3/40  Details of devices incorporated in braiding machines. These aspects are covered by the groups D04C3/14 - D04C3/38 and D04C3/42 - D04C3/46  Auxiliary devices for use with braiding machines, e.g. mandrels. These aspects are covered by the group D04C3/48  Hand braiding or lacing equipment. These aspects are covered by the main group D04C7/00  Braids or laces made by hand or machines, multiple layer fabrics including at least two inter-braided layers. These aspects are covered by the main group D04C1/00  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Laminates comprising at least one braided layer whereby the layers are not inter-braided (i.e. not connected by braiding) -> B32B5/00
[D04C 1/00] Braid or lace, e.g. pillow-lace -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Braiding processes or fabrics and bulk structures with specific details on materials and/or braid structure  Braids and braiding processes not otherwise provided for  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tubular fabrics or endless fabrics (i.e. circular woven products) -> D03D3/02 or D03D3/04
[D04C 1/02] made from particular materials -- The D04C 1/02 subclass covers braids, laces, or pillow-laces that are distinguished by the particular material from which they are formed, such as synthetic fibers, metal wires, or blended yarns. It includes processes or products where the choice of material is a defining technical feature of the braid or lace.
[D04C 1/04] Carbonised or like lace -- D04C 1/04 covers carbonised or comparable lace, a form of braid or lace produced by methods such as bobbin-net or carbonising, used for decorative items like pillow-lace. It falls under the textiles hierarchy within the braid and lace manufacturing sector of the CPC.
[D04C 1/06] Braid or lace serving particular purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Braids having a braided configuration specifically adapted for the intended purpose.
[D04C 1/08] Tulle fabrics -- Tulle fabrics are extremely light, open-work net textiles made by fine braiding or knitting, often used for veils, gowns, and decorative elements such as pillow-lace. They fall under CPC D04C 1/08, which covers braid or lace types in the broader textile and braiding hierarchy.
[D04C 1/10] Pattern tulle fabrics -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Knitted lace -> D04B1/104 , D04B21/12  **Glossary:** - Pattern tulle fabrics: openwork braids with a predominantly non-uniform arrangement of apertures or distinct yarns.
[D04C 1/12] Cords, lines, or tows -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All solid elongated braids in the form of cords, lines or tows having a small but full-cross section, independent from their purpose.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hollow braids or solid bulk braids, e.g. beams -> D04C1/06
[D04C 3/00] Braiding or lacing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Braiding or lacing machines for open work, hollow or bulk-braiding and auxiliary devices.
[D04C 3/02] with spool carriers guided by track plates or by bobbin heads exclusively -- A subclass of braiding or lace-making machines in which the spool carriers are guided solely by track plates or by the heads of the bobbins, providing precise, linear movement of the yarn carriers during the braiding process. This arrangement is used to control the path of the threads and produce consistent braid structures.
[D04C 3/04] with spool carriers guided and reciprocating in non-endless paths -- This CPC subclass D04C 3/04 covers braiding or lacing machines where the spool carriers are guided and move back and forth along non-continuous paths rather than rotating endlessly. It falls under textiles, braiding, specifically braiding or manufacture of lace, including bobbin-net or carbonised lace.
[D04C 3/06] with spool carriers moving always in the same direction in endless paths -- This subclass covers braiding or lacing machines whose spool carriers travel continuously in one direction along a closed-loop path. The design ensures the spools are fed without reversing, enabling uninterrupted production of braid or lace structures.
[D04C 3/08] with means for superimposing threads or braids -- Braiding or lacing machines that incorporate a device for laying one or more threads or pre-formed braids on top of each other during the braiding operation, enabling the production of multilayered cords, composite lace or reinforced structures. This feature allows superimposition of material strands to create complex, durable textile forms.
[D04C 3/10] with means for forming edge loops, ears, or eyes -- A braiding or lacing machine equipped with a device that creates closed loops, extensions or eye-shaped openings at the fabric edge, such as loops for handles, lacing eyelets, or decorative ears, during the braiding or lace-making process.
[D04C 3/12] with means for introducing core threads -- This subgroup of CPC D04C 3/12 pertains to braiding or lacing machines equipped with devices that feed a core-thread through the braid, allowing the core to be incorporated during the braiding or lacing process, commonly used for reinforced fabrics or decorative lace.
[D04C 3/14] Spool carriers -- Spool carriers are devices integrated into braiding or lace-making machines that hold and rotate spools of yarn, thread or filament, supplying the material to the braiding zone. They ensure continuous feed, allow quick spool changes, and are positioned to align with the machine's bobbin or carrier system.
[D04C 3/16] for horizontal spools -- The CPC subclass D04C 3/16 covers braiding or lacing machines that use horizontal spools to hold the working yarns. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → BRAIDING → BRAIDING OR MANUFACTURE OF LACE, INCLUDING BOBBIN-NET OR CARBONISED LACE. This code specifies equipment where the bobbins are arranged on a horizontal axis for producing braids or lace.
[D04C 3/18] for vertical spools -- The CPC subclass D04C 3/18 covers braiding or lacing machines that are designed to operate with vertical spools, typically used in the production of lace, bobbin-net, or carbonised lace. It falls under the textile braiding hierarchy, specifically for equipment handling vertical spool configurations.
[D04C 3/20] Arrangement of bobbin heads and guides or track plates in the machine -- The D04C 3/20 entry covers the specific layout of bobbin heads, guides and track plates inside braiding or lace-making machines. It details how these components are arranged to control the path of the yarn or filament during the formation of braids, bobbin-net or carbonised lace. This classification helps identify patents focused on machine architecture that optimises yarn handling and pattern creation.
[D04C 3/22] Guides or track plates -- Guides or track plates are components that direct the movement of cords, threads or yarns within braiding or lacing machines, ensuring the correct path and tension during the formation of braided structures or lace. They are typically metal or polymer plates with slots or grooves that cooperate with the machine's moving elements.
[D04C 3/24] Devices for controlling spool carriers to obtain patterns, e.g. devices on guides or track plates -- This subclass deals with mechanisms that steer spool carriers in braiding or lace-making machines to produce defined patterns, such as guide rollers, track plates or other positioning devices. By controlling the path of the spools, these devices enable the creation of specific braid or lace designs.
[D04C 3/26] by stopping bobbin heads -- The subclass D04C 3/26 relates to braiding or lace-making machines that incorporate a means for stopping the bobbin heads. It covers mechanisms that halt the rotation or movement of the bobbins to control tension, pattern formation or yarn placement during the production of bobbin-net or carbonised lace.
[D04C 3/28] by stopping only the spool carrier -- D04C 3/28 covers braiding or lace-making machines where the stopping mechanism acts only on the spool carrier, allowing the rest of the device to continue moving. This approach halts thread supply without interrupting the motion of the braiding or lacing elements.
[D04C 3/30] by controlling switches of guides or track plates -- D04C 3/30 covers braiding or lacing machines that adjust the path of the yarns by means of switches that move guides or track plates. The classification is used for equipment where the switching action determines the braid pattern or lace formation.
[D04C 3/32] Pattern input -- The D04C 3/32 subclass relates to braiding or lacing machines that receive a defined pattern to guide the formation of the braid or lace. It covers the means of inputting, storing, or transmitting pattern data-such as punched cards, electronic files, or manual codes-that determine the interlacing sequence during operation.
[D04C 3/34] Beater, or beat-up, mechanisms -- Beater or beat-up mechanisms are components in braiding or lace-making machines that repeatedly strike or compress the work to consolidate yarns, improve density, and maintain fabric tension during production. They are used in equipment for braid, bobbin-net or carbonised lace manufacturing to ensure uniform texture and stability of the finished product.
[D04C 3/36] Frames -- This subclass covers frames that support braiding or lacing machines used in textile manufacturing, including structures that hold bobbin-net or carbonised lace apparatus. It relates to the mechanical framework providing rigidity and alignment for the braid or lace formation process.
[D04C 3/38] Driving-gear -- D04C 3/38 pertains to the driving-gear mechanisms employed in braiding or lacing machines used for producing textiles such as braids, bobbin-net lace or carbonised lace. The classification falls under the broader categories of textiles, braiding, and the manufacture of lace, and it covers gear sets that transmit power to the working elements of these specialized machines.
[D04C 3/40] for making tubular braids by circulating strand supplies around braiding centre at equal distances -- This subclass covers braiding or lacing machines that create tubular braids by circulating multiple strand supplies around a central braiding point at equal distances, forming a continuous tube. It applies to textile products such as lace, bobbin-net or carbonised lace.
[D04C 3/42] with means for forming sheds by controlling guides for individual threads -- The subclass D04C 3/42 covers braiding or lace-making machines that create sheds by individually controlling the guides that steer each thread, allowing precise separation and pattern formation. It is a specialized category within textile braiding equipment for producing braid or lace, including bobbin-net and carbonised lace.
[D04C 3/44] with means for forming sheds by subsequently diverting various threads using the same guiding means -- This subclass covers braiding or lace-making machines that create the required sheds by laterally diverting different threads with the same set of guiding elements, allowing a single guiding system to form multiple openings for interlacing yarns. It falls under textile braiding equipment for lace, bobbin-net or carbonised lace production.
[D04C 3/46] with thread carriers supported on rolls -- This subclass covers braiding or lacing machines where the thread carriers are mounted on rolls, allowing continuous feeding of yarns for the production of lace, bobbin-net or carbonised lace. The arrangement is used to guide multiple threads through the braiding zone and is classified under D04C 3/46.
[D04C 3/48] Auxiliary devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for use with braiding machines, e.g. take-up devices, hoops or mandrels.
[D04C 5/00] Twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines -- D04C5/00 covers machines that produce twist or bobbin-net lace, a type of delicate lace made by interlacing threads through twisting actions or bobbin-net techniques. These devices are used in textile manufacturing to create lace patterns, including bobbin-net and carbonised lace.
[D04C 5/02] Net looms for tulle fabrics -- The D04C 5/02 subclass concerns net looms specifically designed for producing tulle and other fine open-work net fabrics. It includes machines that form the delicate mesh structure of bobbin-net or carbonised lace by interlacing warp and weft threads in a regular, airy pattern. These looms are used in the textile industry for making lightweight, sheer fabrics employed in garments, accessories and decorative applications.
[D04C 5/04] Net or lace curtain machines -- D04C 5/04 refers to machines that produce net or lace curtains, specifically twist or bobbin-net lace-making equipment used in the textile sector for braiding and lace manufacture. The code classifies apparatus designed to create lace and net fabrics for curtain applications.
[D04C 5/06] Machines for making twist-lace fabrics -- D04C 5/06 covers machines designed specifically for producing twist-lace or bobbin-net fabrics, a type of lace made by interlacing twisted threads on a bobbin. These machines automate the braiding and lace-making process, enabling high-speed manufacture of fine, patterned textile sheets.
[D04C 5/08] Bobbin carriages -- The D04C 5/08 classification covers carriage mechanisms that hold and move bobbins in lace-making machines, such as bobbin-net or carbonised lace equipment. It pertains to components that guide the bobbin's position and motion during the twisting or braiding process, enabling the production of patterned lace.
[D04C 5/10] Bobbin-carriage guides -- D04C 5/10 covers mechanisms that guide the carriage holding bobbins in machines that produce bobbin-net or carbonised lace. These guides control the movement and spacing of bobbins to achieve accurate pattern formation and uniform tension during the lace-making process.
[D04C 5/12] Drives for advancing bobbin carriages -- D04C 5/12 covers the drive mechanisms that move the bobbin carriage in twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for textiles, braiding, and the manufacture of lace including bobbin-net and carbonised lace.
[D04C 5/14] Pattern input -- The D04C 5/14 class covers the means for supplying pattern information to twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines. It includes devices, controls, or software that translate design data into the mechanical actions that produce lace patterns.
[D04C 5/16] Warp thread feeding or guiding devices -- The D04C 5/16 subclass covers mechanisms that feed or guide the warp threads in bobbin-net or carbonised lace-making machines. It includes devices that control thread tension, position and movement to ensure consistent lace formation during the braiding or weaving process. These solutions are used in textile equipment for producing fine lace structures.
[D04C 5/18] Guide bars -- Guide bars are rods installed in bobbin-net or carbonised lace-making machines that separate and steer individual yarns as they are twisted and interlaced, maintaining proper spacing and tension to produce the desired lace pattern.
[D04C 5/20] Jacks or pickers -- D04C 5/20 covers jacks or pickers used in the production of twist or bobbin-net lace on braid-making or lace-manufacturing machines. These devices assist in handling or feeding threads during lace formation.
[D04C 5/22] Delivery devices -- The D04C 5/22 subclass defines delivery devices used in twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines, encompassing mechanisms that feed, transport or supply yarn, bobbins or other material to the lace-manufacturing apparatus. It falls under the braid and lace manufacturing group within the textiles section of the CPC.
[D04C 5/24] Driving-gear -- D04C 5/24 covers the driving-gear assemblies that transmit motion to the working parts of twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines, enabling synchronized operation of looms, bobbins and related mechanisms. The classification includes gears, gear trains and coupling devices used to control lace production in textile braiding processes.
[D04C 5/26] Auxiliary devices -- Auxiliary devices are specialised tools or add-ons that support twist or bobbin-net lace-making machines, helping to control thread tension, guide patterns or improve stitching efficiency. They are used in the manufacture of braid-type or bobbin-net lace, including carbonised lace, to enhance machine performance and product quality.
[D04C 7/00] Tools or apparatus for braiding or lacing by hand -- D04C 7/00 pertains to hand-operated tools and apparatus used to braid or lace fabrics, including devices such as braiding frames, hand lacing implements and similar equipment that enable manual production of lace, braids or bobbin-net structures.
[D04D] TRIMMINGS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  tuft strings and elongated pile articles serving other than decorative purposes.  These aspect are covered by D04D3/00 .  textile decorative or ornamental articles of rope or ribbon shape with decorative properties imparted during textile formation  flat or three dimensional articles made up from such materials  textile forming processes other that weaving, knitting, crocheting or braiding providing these articles  machines for making these articles  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fittings or trimmings for hats, e.g. hat bands -> A42C5/00 - Making jewellery or other personal adornments -> A44C27/00 - Products obtained by processes producing special ornamental surface effects -> B44C1/00 - Special ornamental bodies and processes of making same -> B44C5/00 - Flexible elongated elements, e.g. straps, for bundling or supporting articles -> B65D63/10 - Decorative textiles by mechanical or chemical treatment -> D06Q  **Glossary:** - Trimmings: are decorative or ornamental articles made wholly or partly of textile or analogous material, or strips of textile or analogous material, intended to be used for decorative or ornamental purposes, not otherwise provided for.
[D04D 1/00] Ropes or like decorative or ornamental elongated trimmings made from filamentary material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  elongated textile decorative articles.
[D04D 1/02] by twisting strands around centrally arranged cores -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative ropes wrapped for aesthetic purposes.
[D04D 1/04] by threading or stringing pearls or beads on filamentary material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  articles comprising beads or pearls incorporated during the textile forming process, e.g. stringing of beads.
[D04D 11/00] Ribbon-threading apparatus or devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  tools and machines for making decorative rope or ribbon articles.
[D04D 3/00] Chenille trimmings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  elongated articles including transversely oriented fibrous material, e.g. pile yarns, not strictly limited to a decorative purpose.
