[D04D 5/00] Fringes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  ribbons including transversely oriented non pile-forming material, e.g. strips, bands or cords.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Chenille fringes -> D04D3/00 - Tasselled fringes -> D04D7/08
[D04D 7/00] Decorative or ornamental textile articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative textile articles, processes of making same and devices for performing said processes.
[D04D 7/02] Flat articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  flat structures assembled from elongated textile decorative articles, e.g. beaded strings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fabrics with embellishments attached after the fabric forming process, e.g. by sewing or gluing -> A41D27/08 - Fabrics with embellishments made by altering locally the surface material -> B44C1/005 - Fabrics with embellishments incorporated by weaving -> D03D - Fabrics with embellishments incorporated by knitting -> D04B - Fabrics with embellishments applied by embroiding -> D05C17/00 - Making patterns or designs on fabrics -> D06C23/00
[D04D 7/04] Three-dimensional [3D] articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  three dimensional decorative structures composed of one or more elongated textile articles.
[D04D 7/06] Ball-like tufts, e.g. pompons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Decorative articles with pile fibres, filaments, tapes or yarns arranged in an approximately spherical three-dimensional configuration.
[D04D 7/08] Tassels or tasselled fringes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative rope or strip bundles combined at one end or united at an intermediate portion.
[D04D 7/10] Decorative bow structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  three dimensional adornments including bunching tapes.
[D04D 7/105] made by means of a drawstring or drawstrip -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative bow structures formed or shaped by pulling one or more of the constituents.
[D04D 9/00] Ribbons, tapes, welts, bands, beadings, or other decorative or ornamental strips, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative or ornamental textile strips not provided for in one of the subclasses, and textile processes of making same.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Laminated tapes -> B32B - Flexible elongated elements, e.g. straps, for bundling or supporting articles -> B65D63/00 - Adhesive tapes -> C09J7/20 - Woven curtain heading tapes -> D03D1/06 - Woven tapes -> D03D3/005 - Knitted or crocheted tapes -> D04B1/22 , D04B21/20 - Braided tapes -> D04C1/00
[D04D 9/02] made by lengthwise folding of fabric strip -- **Definition:** This place covers:  longitudinally folded or pleated decorative tapes.
[D04D 9/04] built-up from several strips or elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  assembled decorative tapes.
[D04D 9/06] made by working plastics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  decorative foil strips.
[D04G] MAKING NETS BY KNOTTING OF FILAMENTARY MATERIAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Textile articles including knots to secure members, e.g. beads or tufts, or to connect threads for forming nets machines and methods for making said articles by knotting.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Knotters for binding harvested produce -> A01D59/04 - Wire network -> B21F27/00 - B21F33/00 - Tying articles by knotting -> B65B13/26 - Weavers' tools, e.g. knot-tying tools -> D03J3/00
[D04G 1/00] Making nets by knotting of filamentary material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Knotted nets, methods of hand knotting same and tools for performing said methods.
[D04G 1/02] in machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of net knotting machines; machines not limited to knotting particular types of netting; ancillary equipment for use with knotting machines.
[D04G 1/04] using a single thread -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nets machine-knotted from a single thread, processes for making same and apparatus specifically designed for such processes.
[D04G 1/06] using a single thread and a series of threads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nets machine-knotted from a single thread and a series of threads, processes for making same and apparatus specifically designed for such processes.
[D04G 1/08] using two series of threads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nets machine-knotted from two series of threads, processes for making same and apparatus specifically designed for such processes.
[D04G 3/00] Making knotted carpets or tapestries -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of making knotted carpets or tapestries and devices for use in such methods not provided for in one of the subgroups, e.g. tools for preparing tufts
[D04G 3/02] by hand -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand tools for knotting tufts into carpet canvas.
[D04G 3/04] in machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for knotting tufts into carpet backings
[D04G 5/00] Knotting not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Knotting not provided for in D04G1/00 or D04G3/00 , e.g. forming knots along a cord; tying rope ends or Macramé work tools and devices for use in such knotting methods
[D04H] MAKING TEXTILE FABRICS, e.g. FROM FIBRES OR FILAMENTARY MATERIAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Non-woven fabrics having an intermediate or external layer of a different kind, e.g. of woven fabric -> B23B - Weaving -> D03 - Knitting -> D04B - Braiding or lace making -> D04C - Net-making or making knotted carpets -> D04G - Sewing -> D05B - Tufting -> D05C - Finishing non-woven fabrics -> D06  **Glossary:** - Carded thermal bonded and resin bonded staples: fabrics formed by processing textile staple fibers over a card and bonding by resin or thermal means. - Air laid: fabrics formed by air laying and bonding pulp or staple fibers. - Wet laid: fabrics that contain long (longer than pulp) fibers and are made by papermaking techniques. - Needle punched staples: fabrics formed by processing staple fibers over a card or other web forming device and entangling them by penetrating the fabric with multiple barbed needles. - Spun laced or hydro entangled: fabrics formed by carding, air laying or other web forming techniques and consolidated by hydraulic needling. - Spun bonded: fabrics formed by in-line melt extrusion spinning of filaments of conventional textile denier. - Melt blown: fabrics formed by in-line melt extrusion spinning of very fine fiber diameter, less than one denier. - Non-woven fabrics: fabrics formed wholly or partly of textile material by processes comprising operations other than the weaving, knitting, braiding, lacing, or knotting of yarns, threads, or filaments for which provision is made in other subclasses of Section D . This expression includes felts, cotton-wool, and wadding. - Composite fibres: Conjugated fibres that are manufactured by composite spinning of two or more kinds of immiscible resins, of which the resins form such phase structures as side-by-side, sheath-core, sea-island, wood grain pattern, multi-layered, radial or mosaic in the cross section surface vertical to the fibre axis. - Mixed fibres: Mixture of two or more kinds of fibres made of different substances. - Fleece: Non-woven - Pile: Upright loop or nap raised from the surface of fabrics. - Welding: bonding of fibres or filaments using heat and/or pressure or solvent bonding, i.e. autogenously bonding
[D04H 1/00] Kinds or types of non-woven fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed from fibres or filaments which have been specifically cut to a length less than continuous.
[D04H 1/005] Making three-dimensional articles by consolidation  D04H1/22  takes precedence -- This group covers methods that form three-dimensional textile articles by consolidating fibers or filaments into a solid shape, such as molding, pressing, or bonding, without weaving or knitting. It is a subcategory of D04H dealing with non-woven fabrics and falls under the broader headings of textiles, braiding, and making textile fabrics.
[D04H 1/02] Cotton wool -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics of short fibers are composed of cotton-wool or wadding (could be useful to look at carding D01G ) and are used to make cosmetic pads, absorbent pads, quilting materials etc.  **Glossary:** - Wadding: A heap of voluminous and low-density fibre for use as fillers in furniture or quilting.
[D04H 1/04] from fleeces or layers composed of fibres having existing or potential cohesive properties, e.g. natural fibres, prestretched or fibrillated artificial fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are composed of fibers having existing or potential cohesive properties and are consolidated by mechanical means such as natural fibers like wool or fibrillated artificial fibers. The fibers are crimped or crinkly and will entangle easily with each other (could be useful to look at felting apparatus D04H17/00 ).
[D04H 1/06] by treatment to produce shrinking, swelling, crimping or curling of fibres -- D04H 1/06 is a CPC subgroup covering processes that treat fibres to cause dimensional changes such as shrinking, swelling, crimping or curling, thereby creating or modifying non-woven fabrics. It falls under textile braiding and the manufacture of fabrics from fibres or filamentary material.
[D04H 1/067] Regenerated cellulose series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers of regenerated cellulose.
[D04H 1/073] Acrylonitrile series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers from the acrylonitrile series.
[D04H 1/08] and hardened by felting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, hardened by a felting technique including also felts and products made from felts.
[D04H 1/09] Silk -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, hardened by a felting technique including also felts and products made from felts and the fibers are made of silk.
[D04H 1/10] Felts made from mixtures of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of felts made from a mixture of different types of fibres.
[D04H 11/00] Non-woven pile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven pile fabrics - the pile is formed from upstanding fibers. (Layered products forming non-woven pile fabrics are classified in B32B ; woven pile fabrics D03D ; tufting D05C , e.g. D05C15/04 ; made by knotting D04G3/00 ; by knitting D04B1/00 , D04B21/00 )
[D04H 11/04] formed by zig-zag folding of a fleece or layer of staple fibres, filaments, or yarns, strengthened or consolidated at the folds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven pile fabrics formed by zigzag folding of a fleece or layer of staple fibers, filaments, or yarns, strengthened or consolidated at the folds.
[D04H 11/08] formed by creation of a pile on at least one surface of a non-woven fabric without addition of pile-forming material, e.g. by needling, by differential shrinking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven pile fabrics formed by the creation of a pile on at least one surface of a non-woven fabric without the addition of pile-forming material, e.g. by needling, by differential shrinking (needling machines D04H18/00 )
[D04H 1/12] and incorporating artificial organic fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of felts made from a mixture of different types of fibres and incorporate artificial organic fibres.
[D04H 1/14] and incorporating inorganic fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of felts made from a mixture of different types of fibers and incorporate inorganic fibers.
[D04H 1/16] Laminated felts in which the separate layers are united by a felting process -- D04H 1/16 covers laminated felt structures where two or more individual felt layers are joined together by a felting action rather than by adhesives or mechanical bonding. The process creates a single non-woven fabric panel with combined thickness and properties, used in applications such as insulation, sound-absorption or reinforcement.
[D04H 1/20] Felts incorporating inserts or attachments, e.g. for ornamental purposes -- The CPC subclass D04H 1/20 covers felts that contain added elements such as decorative inserts, fasteners or other attachments integrated into the non-woven fabric structure. It includes methods and products where these embedded components provide ornamental, functional or reinforcement purposes within the felt material.
[D04H 1/22] Three-dimensional [3D] articles formed by felting processes -- D04H 1/22 covers three-dimensional textile items produced through felting, where fibers are interlocked to create shaped, non-woven structures such as molded felt pads, automotive components or architectural panels. The category falls under textiles, braiding and the making of fabrics from fibers or filamentary material, specifically addressing kinds of non-woven fabrics.
[D04H 1/24] Covers felted on to three-dimensional [3D] articles -- D04H 1/24 pertains to the production of non-woven textiles where a felted layer is applied to three-dimensional items such as molded parts, garments or industrial components, creating a protective or decorative covering.
[D04H 1/26] Wood pulp -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics of short fibers having existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties are fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made of wood pulp.
[D04H 1/28] Regenerated cellulose series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics of short fibers having existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties are fleeces and webs or layers of these, made of regenerated cellulose series.
[D04H 1/30] Collagen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics of short fibers having existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties are fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made of fibers of collagen.
[D04H 13/00] Other non-woven fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other non-woven fabrics not accounted for in the previous groups.
[D04H 13/001] Making non-woven fabrics from staple fibres, filaments or yarns, bonded to at least one web-like material, e.g. woven, knitted non-woven fabric, paper, leather, during consolidation -- The CPC subclass D04H 13/001 covers processes for producing non-woven fabrics in which staple fibres, filaments or yarns are placed onto or into another web-like material such as a woven or knitted fabric, paper or leather and then bonded together during a consolidation step, yielding a composite textile structure.
[D04H 13/002] characterised by the disposition or nature of their elements -- This CPC subclass concerns non-woven textile fabrics produced by braiding processes where the defining feature is the way the individual fibers, filaments or other elements are arranged or their intrinsic nature. It includes fabrics whose performance derives from the disposition, orientation or material type of the constituent elements rather than from interlacing loops.
[D04H 13/003] strengthened or consolidated by mechanical methods  needling machines  D04H18/00 ; knitting  D04B , sewing  D05B -- D04H 13/003 covers processes that mechanically strengthen or consolidate textile fabrics, especially non-woven materials, by using needling, tufting or similar mechanical actions. It falls within the broader textile braiding and fabric-making categories and is linked to related techniques such as knitting (D04B) and sewing (D05B).
[D04H 13/005] by needling -- D04H 13/005 covers the production of non-woven textile fabrics by needling, a process where barbed needles mechanically entangle fibers to bond a web into a cohesive sheet. This method is used to create various needled fabrics from loose fibers or filaments.
[D04H 13/006] strengthened or consolidated by the use of bonding agents in solid or liquid form -- The code covers methods for producing non-woven textile fabrics that are reinforced or unified by applying bonding agents in solid or liquid form, such as adhesives, binders or resin sprays. The process includes spreading, impregnating or curing the agents to consolidate fibres or filaments, resulting in a stronger, more stable fabric.
[D04H 13/007] strengthened or consolidated by welding together the various components -- The D04H 13/007 code covers methods for strengthening or consolidating non-woven textile fabrics by welding the individual components together, typically using heat, ultrasonic energy or adhesive bonding to fuse fibers or filaments and improve the material's integrity.
[D04H 13/008] Glass fibre products; Complete installations for making them -- This class covers glass-fibre items and the complete plant or equipment used to manufacture them, including processes and apparatus for producing glass-fibre mats, ropes, or other non-woven forms. It falls under D04H 13/008 in the CPC, a sub-category of textile braiding and fabric making.
[D04H 13/02] Production of non-woven fabrics by partial defibrillation of oriented thermoplastics films -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other non-woven fabrics produced by partial defibrillation of oriented thermoplastics films.
[D04H 1/32] Synthetic pulp -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics of short fibers having existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties are fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made of synthetic pulp.  **Glossary:** - Synthetic pulp: typically comprised of a thermoplastic polymer such as polyolefins or some polyamides and having a structure resembling wood pulp. That is, the synthetic pulp has a microfibrillar structure comprised of microfibrils exhibiting a high surface area as contrasted with the smooth, rod-like morphology of synthetic short staple fibers
[D04H 1/40] from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties.
[D04H 1/407] containing absorbing substances, e.g. activated carbon -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties containing absorbing substances, e.g. activated carbon.  **Glossary:** - Absorbing substances: water-absorbing agents such as water-absorbing polymer, deodorant or humidity absorbing agents such as activated carbon
[D04H 1/413] containing granules other than absorbent substances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties containing granules other than absorbent substances.  **Glossary:** - Granules: for example, those having antibiotic properties or granular substances with flame retardant properties.
[D04H 1/42] characterised by the use of certain kinds of fibres insofar as this use has no preponderant influence on the consolidation of the fleece -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces, webs or layers of fleeces or webs composed of fibres without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties characterised by the fibres having particular fibre features, e.g. chemical composition, fineness or shape, regardless of the method of manufacturing the fleeces, webs or layers.
[D04H 1/4209] Inorganic fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are inorganic fibers.
[D04H 1/4218] Glass fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs or layers of these are inorganic glass fibers.
[D04H 1/4226] characterised by the apparatus for manufacturing the glass fleece -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are inorganic glass fibers and are characterized by the apparatus for manufacturing the glass fleece.
[D04H 1/4234] Metal fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs or layers of these are inorganic metal fibres.
[D04H 1/4242] Carbon fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are inorganic carbon fibres.  **Glossary:** - Carbon fibres: a kind of inorganic fibre. Polyacrylonitrile (PAN) -based, pitch-based or rayon-based Carbon fibers are included.
[D04H 1/425] Cellulose series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are fibers belonging to the cellulose series egg. cotton, kapok, linen, jute, flax, ramie, sisal, hemp, kenaf, industrial hemp, rattan, vine fibers, fique, banana and agave.
[D04H 1/4258] Regenerated cellulose series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are fibers belonging to the regenerated cellulose series egg. rayon, acetate, triacetate, bamboo fibers, rayon, lyocell, Tencel, Modal and Viscose.
[D04H 1/4266] Natural fibres not provided for in group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are natural fibers not provided for in group D04H1/425 e.g. wool, silk (and other protein fibers).
[D04H 1/4274] Rags -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics which are composed of the fibres obtained and reused by cutting or fibreizing wastes or garnets, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or like relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties insofar as this use has no preponderant influence on the consolidation of the fleece. Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from rags; Fabric scraps.
[D04H 1/4282] Addition polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from addition polymers. (e.g. PVC)
[D04H 1/4291] Olefin series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from addition polymers from the olefin series.
[D04H 1/43] Acrylonitrile series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from addition polymers from the acrylonitrile series.
[D04H 1/4309] Polyvinyl alcohol -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from addition polymers of polyvinyl alcohol.
[D04H 1/4318] Fluorine series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from addition polymers from the fluorine series.
[D04H 1/4326] Condensation or reaction polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made from fibers made from condensation or reaction polymers.
[D04H 1/4334] Polyamides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  These condensation polymers being polyamides.
[D04H 1/4342] Aromatic polyamides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Condensation polymers being aromatic polyamides.
[D04H 1/435] Polyesters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Condensation polymers being polyesters.
[D04H 1/4358] Polyurethanes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reaction polymers being polyurethanes.
[D04H 1/4366] Phenol series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reaction polymers belonging to the phenol series.
[D04H 1/4374] using different kinds of webs, e.g. by layering webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics which are composed of mixture of different kind of webs, including multi-layered non-woven fabrics in which layers are made continuously and each layer is composed of different kind of fibres, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or like relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties insofar as this use has no preponderant influence on the consolidation of the fleece. Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, made using different kinds of webs, e.g. by layering webs.
[D04H 1/4382] Stretched reticular film fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[D04H 1/43825] Composite fibres -- Composite fibres are hybrid yarns that combine two or more distinct fiber or filament components-such as natural, synthetic, or inorganic strands-into a single filament to impart combined mechanical, thermal or functional properties, and are used in the production of non-woven textile fabrics.
[D04H 1/43828] sheath-core -- Sheath-core refers to a composite textile structure in which a core material is encased by a sheath of a different fiber, typically produced through braiding or other non-woven techniques to combine properties such as strength, flexibility, and protective performance.
[D04H 1/4383] sea-island -- Sea-island is a type of non-woven fabric produced by extruding a polymer filament that contains discrete core (island) strands surrounded by a different polymer matrix (sea); the surrounding polymer is later dissolved or removed, leaving a web of ultra-fine fibers. This technique falls under the making of textile fabrics from filaments in CPC class D04H 1/4383.
[D04H 1/43832] side-by-side -- This subclass of the CPC covers side-by-side non-woven fabrics, a type of textile made by arranging two or more layers of fibers or filaments side by side during production. It falls under D04H 1/43832 within the broader categories of textiles, braiding, and making textile fabrics.
[D04H 1/43835] Mixed fibres, e.g. at least two chemically different fibres or fibre blends -- The entry D04H 1/43835 covers the production of non-woven fabrics that incorporate mixed fibres, meaning the material contains at least two chemically different fibres or blends of fibres. It pertains to processes for making textile fabrics from such combined fibre types.
[D04H 1/43838] Ultrafine fibres, e.g. microfibres -- The subclass D04H 1/43838 pertains to the use of ultrafine fibres, such as microfibres, in the production of non-woven textile fabrics. It includes methods for handling or forming fibres whose diameters are in the micrometre range, which provide high surface area and fine texture for applications like filtration, hygiene products and technical textiles.
[D04H 1/4391] characterised by the shape of the fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics characterised by the shape of the fibres used to form the non-woven fabric. These fibres generally have configurations that deviate from a smooth round fibre shape. For example, the fibres can have modified external cross-sections, e.g. noncircular cross-sections, modified internal cross-sections, e.g. circular or noncircular hollow fibre cores and noncircular internal fibre cores, or the fibres can have nonlinear configurations such as crimped fibres on which specific crimps are given.  Examples of fibres having modified external cross-sections:  Examples of fibres having modified internal cross-sections:  Examples of fibres having hollow internal cross-sections:  The non-woven fabrics are formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or the like or relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties insofar as this use has no preponderant influence on the consolidation of the fleece. Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are made from fibres which are characterized by their shape.
[D04H 1/43912] fibres with noncircular cross-sections -- The subclass D04H 1/43912 covers fibers whose cross-section is not circular, such as oval, rectangular, trilobal or other shaped profiles, used in processes for making textile fabrics, including non-woven materials. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles → braiding → making textile fabrics from fibres or filamentary material, focusing on the specific geometry of the fiber that influences fabric properties and handling.
[D04H 1/43914] hollow fibres -- The CPC class D04H 1/43914 covers hollow fibres, which are tubular filaments with an internal cavity used in making non-woven textile fabrics. These fibres can be produced from polymers or glass and serve functions such as thermal insulation, acoustic damping or filtration, and may be incorporated by braiding or other forming methods.
[D04H 1/43916] microcellular fibres, e.g. porous or foamed fibres -- The CPC entry D04H 1/43916 falls under textile braiding processes and concerns the production of microcellular fibres, which are porous or foamed fibres containing internal cavities that give them a low density and high surface area. These fibres are used to create non-woven fabrics with improved insulation, acoustic damping or lightweight characteristics. The classification captures methods for forming, treating or assembling such foamed or porous filamentary materials.
[D04H 1/43918] nonlinear fibres, e.g. crimped or coiled fibres -- The entry concerns non-linear fibres such as crimped or coiled strands that are employed in the production of non-woven textile structures. It addresses the handling, processing and formation of these shaped fibres to impart elasticity, bulk or special mechanical characteristics to the resulting fabric.
[D04H 1/44] the fleeces or layers being consolidated by mechanical means, e.g. by rolling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties and are consolidated by mechanical means, e.g. by rolling.
[D04H 1/45] by forming intermeshing loops or stitches from some of the fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by forming intermeshing loops or stitches from some of the fibers. Fibers are pulled out from the structure of the web and then pushed back into it to bind the web. No additional yarn material is added to the web. (Is not related to knitting D04B ; or sewing D05B )
[D04H 1/46] by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers. The needling is done by stabbing or punching with barbed needles which pull out and push in the fibers of the web many consecutive times. (Needling machines D04H18/00 )  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forming intermeshing loops or stitches from some of the fibres -> D04H1/45
[D04H 1/465] Hydraulic needling -- Hydraulic needling is a technique for forming non-woven fabrics in which high-pressure water jets are directed at the fiber web to interlock the fibres, providing mechanical bonding similar to needle-punching but without physical needles. It is used to produce various kinds of non-woven textiles, enhancing strength, flexibility and surface texture.
[D04H 1/48] in combination with at least one other method of consolidation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers in combination with at least one other preceding or subsequent method of consolidation.
[D04H 1/482] in combination with shrinkage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers in combination with shrinkage.
[D04H 1/485] in combination with weld-bonding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers in combination with weld-bonding.
[D04H 1/488] in combination with bonding agents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers in combination with bonding agents.
[D04H 1/49] entanglement by fluid jet in combination with another consolidation means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers in combination with entanglement by fluid jet, e.g. by hydro entangling/ hydro needling/ spun lace methods with water jets).
[D04H 1/492] by fluid jet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are mechanically consolidated only by fluid jet (usually this is with water e.g. by hydro entangling/ hydro needling/ spun lace methods or can be gas/air).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Entanglement by fluid jet in combination with another consolidation means -> D04H1/49
[D04H 1/495] for formation of patterns, e.g. drilling or rearrangement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated only by fluid jet for the formation of patterns, e.g. drilling or rearrangement of the fibers.
[D04H 1/498] entanglement of layered webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Multi-layered non-woven fabrics which are entangled by needling or like operations, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or like relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties. Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, mechanically consolidated by needling or like operations to cause entanglement of fibers. Specifically for the entanglement of layered webs.
[D04H 1/50] by treatment to produce shrinking, swelling, crimping or curling of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties and are consolidated by mechanical means by using a treatment to produce shrinking, swelling, crimping, or curling of fibers.
[D04H 1/52] by applying or inserting filamentary binding elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, composed of fibers without existing or potential mechanical cohesive properties and are consolidated by mechanical means by applying or inserting filamentary binding elements, e.g. threads or yarns are used as an extra stitching element. (Knitting D04B ; Sewing D05B )
[D04H 1/54] by welding together the fibres, e.g. by partially melting or dissolving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, are composed of fibers having existing or potential cohesive properties and are consolidated by welding together the fibers, e.g. by partially melting or dissolving (if in combination with needling then D04H1/48 ).
[D04H 1/5405] at spaced points or locations -- D04H 1/5405 covers non-woven textiles in which the fibers are bonded at spaced points or locations, rather than throughout the whole sheet. It applies to methods that create discrete joins-such as mechanical, thermal or chemical bonding-to produce fabrics used in braided or other textile structures.
[D04H 1/541] Composite fibres, e.g. sheath-core, sea-island or side-by-side -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these are welded together and are composite fibers e.g. sheath-core, sea-island or side-by-side; Mixed fibers.  **Glossary:** - Mixed fibres: Mixed fibres refers to the case when different kinds of fibres exist and one of them melts or softens first
[D04H 1/5412] sheath-core -- It covers methods and apparatus for producing non-woven fabrics with a sheath-core construction, where a core filament or fibre is encased by a surrounding sheath material, typically formed by braiding or other joining techniques.
[D04H 1/5414] side-by-side -- D04H 1/5414 - side-by-side designates a non-woven fabric made by laying two or more streams of fibers or filaments adjacent to each other during formation, resulting in a material with parallel, distinct zones or layers.
[D04H 1/5416] sea-island -- The D04H 1/5416 entry covers non-woven textile fabrics produced by the sea-island method, in which fine "island" filaments are spun within a surrounding "sea" polymer matrix that is later removed to leave a web of micro-fibres. This technique creates lightweight, high-surface-area fabrics used in technical and medical applications.
[D04H 1/5418] Mixed fibres, e.g. at least two chemically different fibres or fibre blends
[D04H 1/542] Adhesive fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these welded together and are adhesive fibers i.e thermoplastic.
[D04H 1/544] Olefin series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers belong to the olefin series.
[D04H 1/545] Polyvinyl alcohol -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers are polyvinyl alcohol.
[D04H 1/546] Polyvinyl acetate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers are polyvinyl acetate.
[D04H 1/548] Acrylonitrile series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers belong to the acrylonitrile series.
[D04H 1/549] Polyamides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers are polyamides.
[D04H 1/55] Polyesters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers are polyesters.
[D04H 1/551] Resins thereof not provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the adhesive fibers are resins not provided for in groups D04H1/544 - D04H1/55 .
[D04H 1/552] by applying solvents or auxiliary agents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, welded together by applying solvents or auxiliary agents which promote welding by dissolving the fibers.
[D04H 1/554] by radio-frequency heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, welded together by radio-frequency heating.
[D04H 1/555] by ultrasonic heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, welded together by ultrasonic heating.
[D04H 1/556] by infrared heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, welded together by infrared heating.
[D04H 1/558] in combination with mechanical or physical treatments other than embossing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs, or layers of these, welded together in combination with mechanical or physical treatments other than embossing.
[D04H 1/559] the fibres being within layered webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Multi-layered non-woven fabrics using bonding fibres in which layers are made continuously, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or like relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties. Fleeces and webs welded together the fibers being within layered webs.
[D04H 1/56] in association with fibre formation, e.g. immediately following extrusion of staple fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are welded together in association with fiber formation, e.g. immediately following extrusion of staple fibers.
[D04H 1/565] by melt-blowing -- Melt-blowing is a process for producing non-woven fabrics where molten polymer is extruded through fine nozzles and attenuated by high-velocity heated air, forming a web of microfibers that are subsequently bonded. The resulting material is lightweight, porous and used in filtration, insulation and disposable products.
[D04H 1/58] by applying, incorporating or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents, e.g. adhesives -- This subclass covers processes for producing non-woven fabrics, especially braided textiles, by using chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents such as adhesives to join fibres or filaments. The method may involve applying the bonding agent to the material, incorporating it during fabric formation, or activating it after placement to create a cohesive fabric.
[D04H 1/587] characterised by the bonding agents used -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents characterized by the bonding agents used.
[D04H 1/593] to layered webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Multi-layered non-woven fabrics using chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents in which layers are made continuously, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of staple fibres or like relatively short fibres and formed from fleeces or layers composed of fibres without existing or potential cohesive properties. Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents in layered webs.
[D04H 1/60] the bonding agent being applied in dry state, e.g. thermo-activatable agents in solid or molten state, and heat being applied subsequently -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in a dry state, e.g. thermo-activatable agents in solid or molten state, and heat being applied subsequently.
[D04H 1/62] at spaced points or locations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in a dry state at spaced points or locations.
[D04H 1/64] the bonding agent being applied in wet state, e.g. chemical agents in dispersions or solutions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in wet state, e.g. chemical agents in dispersions or solutions
[D04H 1/641] characterised by the chemical composition of the bonding agent -- D04H 1/641 covers non-woven textile fabrics whose defining feature is the chemical composition of the bonding agent used to bind fibers or filaments together. It includes processes that rely on specific adhesives, binders or polymeric resins to create the fabric structure, distinguishing these materials by their bonding chemistry rather than mechanical interlacing.
[D04H 1/642] characterised by the impregnation or treatment-process or apparatus -- D04H 1/642 covers non-woven textile fabrics whose defining feature is the application of an impregnation or treatment step, or the apparatus used for that step, such as the introduction of binders, resins, chemicals or surface-modifying agents during manufacture. It includes methods and equipment for treating the fabric to impart desired physical or chemical properties.
[D04H 1/643] characterised by a combination of the chemical composition and the impregnation or treatment process or apparatus -- This subclass covers non-woven fabrics whose properties arise from a specific chemical composition together with a particular impregnation, treatment step, or the equipment used to apply it. It includes processes that combine material formulation with treatment methods to produce the final fabric.
[D04H 1/645] Impregnation followed by a solidification process -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in wet state i.e impregnation and is then followed by a solidification process.
[D04H 1/65] using mixed or composite fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in wet state i.e impregnation and is then followed by a solidification process and using mixed or composite fibres.
[D04H 1/655] characterised by the apparatus for applying bonding agents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in a wet state characterised by the apparatus for applying bonding agents.
[D04H 1/66] at spaced points or locations -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The bonding agent being applied in the form of foam -> D04H1/68
[D04H 1/68] the bonding agent being applied in the form of foam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the fleeces and webs are consolidated by applying, incorporating, or activating chemical or thermoplastic bonding agents the bonding agent being applied in a wet state in the form of foam.
[D04H 1/70] characterised by the method of forming fleeces or layers, e.g. reorientation of fibres -- The CPC subclass D04H 1/70 covers processes for producing non-woven fabrics by forming fleeces or layered structures, typically involving the re-orientation of fibres to create a cohesive sheet. It includes techniques such as needling, carding, or other mechanical actions that align fibres into a fleece or layered textile.
[D04H 17/00] Felting apparatus -- D04H 17/00 covers machines that convert loose fibres into felted fabric by compacting, agitating or vibrating the material. The apparatus typically includes rollers, drums or vibrating plates that mat the fibres into a coherent sheet.
[D04H 17/10] for felting between rollers, e.g. heated rollers -- The subclass D04H 17/10 represents felting apparatus that processes material by passing it between rollers, frequently heated, to consolidate fibers into a fabric. It is situated within the CPC hierarchy for textiles, under braiding and the making of textile fabrics from fibres or filamentary material.
[D04H 17/12] Multi-roller apparatus -- A multi-roller apparatus is a machine that uses several rotating rollers to manipulate fibres or filaments while forming textile fabrics, especially for felting or braiding processes. The coordinated motion of the rollers compresses, interlocks and shapes the material into a cohesive fabric, allowing continuous production of woven, knitted or felted structures.A multi-roller apparatus is a machine that uses several rotating rollers to manipulate fibres or filaments while forming textile fabrics, especially for felting or braiding processes. The coordinated motion of the rollers compresses, interlocks and shapes the material into a cohesive fabric, allowing continuous production of woven, knitted or felted structures.
[D04H 1/72] the fibres being randomly arranged -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, causing the fibres to be randomly arranged i.e. isotropic fleeces.
[D04H 1/724] forming webs during fibre formation, e.g. flash-spinning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, where  the fibres are randomly arranged during fibre formation, e.g. flash-spinning.
[D04H 1/728] by electro-spinning -- The CPC subclass D04H 1/728, under textiles-braiding-making textile fabrics, covers the production of non-woven fabrics by electro-spinning, a process that creates fine fibers from polymer solutions or melts using an electric field.
[D04H 1/732] by fluid current, e.g. air-lay -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, where the fibers are randomly arranged by fluid current, e.g. air-lay
[D04H 1/736] characterised by the apparatus for arranging fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, where the fibers are randomly arranged and characterized by the apparatus for randomly arranging the fibres.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - By electro-spinning -> D04H1/728 - By fluid current -> D04H1/732
[D04H 1/74] the fibres being orientated, e.g. in parallel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, where the fibers are orientated, e.g. in parallel as in anisotropic fleeces.
[D04H 1/76] otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for forming non-woven fleeces or webs, or layers of these, where the fibers are orientated otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way
[D04H 18/00] Needling machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needling machines e.g. needle punching machines.
[D04H 18/02] with needles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needling machines with barbed needles.
[D04H 18/04] with water jets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needling machines using water jets e.g. spunlace machines.
[D04H 3/00] Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length. The term continuous filament is used.
[D04H 3/002] Inorganic yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length i.e continuous filament.
[D04H 3/004] Glass yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous inorganic filament is made of glass.
[D04H 3/005] Synthetic yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is synthetic.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Regenerated cellulose series -> D04H3/013
[D04H 3/007] Addition polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is synthetic made from addition polymers e.g. olefin series, acrylonitrile series, polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), fluorine series, PVC.
[D04H 3/009] Condensation or reaction polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is synthetic formed from condensation or reaction polymers e.g. PLA, Ppolyacetals, polyamides.
[D04H 3/011] Polyesters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is formed from a condensation polymer specifically from polyesters.
[D04H 3/013] Regenerated cellulose series -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is made from the regenerated cellulose series e.g. rayon, acetate, triacetate, bamboo fibres, rayon, lyocell, Tencel, Modal and Viscose.
[D04H 3/015] Natural yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is made natural yarns or filaments e.g. silk.
[D04H 3/016] characterised by the fineness -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the web of continuous filament is characterized by its fineness.
[D04H 3/018] characterised by the shape -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics characterised by the shape of the yarns or like filamentary material used to form the non-woven fabric. For example, the filamentary material can have modified external cross-sections, e.g. noncircular cross-sections, modified internal cross-sections, e.g. circular or noncircular hollow fibre cores and noncircular internal fibre cores, or the yarns or like filamentary material can have nonlinear configurations such as crimped yarns or like filamentary material on which specific crimps are given.  Examples of filamentary materials having modified external cross-sections:  Examples of filamentary materials having modified internal cross-sections:  Examples of filamentary materials having hollow internal cross-sections:  The non-woven fabrics are formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length and where a web of continuous filaments is characterised by the shape of the filaments.
[D04H 3/02] characterised by the method of forming fleeces or layers, e.g. reorientation of yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the non-woven fabric formed of continuous filament is characterized by the method of forming fleeces, webs or layers of these e.g. reorientation of yarns or filaments (lap-forming devices D01G25/00 ; paper web-making by wet methods D21F , D21H ).
[D04H 3/03] at random -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of randomly laid continuous filaments.
[D04H 3/033] reorientation immediately after yarn or filament formation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics to which the means for changing the orientation of yarns or like filamentary material are given after the formation of yarns or like filamentary material, and which are also included in the scope of the upper group, namely, formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length. Where the method forms webs or layers of webs and lays the continuous filaments at random and reorientation takes place immediately after yarn or filament formation.
[D04H 3/037] reorientation by liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs by randomly laying continuous filaments and reorientation is achieved by liquid.
[D04H 3/04] in rectilinear paths, e.g. crossing at right angles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid in rectilinear paths, e.g. crossing at right angles.
[D04H 3/045] for net manufacturing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid in rectilinear paths, crossing at right angles for net manufacturing.
[D04H 3/05] in another pattern, e.g. zig-zag, sinusoidal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid in another pattern, e.g. zigzag, sinusoidal, and wavy.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Characterised by the method of forming fleeces or layers in rectilinear paths -> D04H3/04
[D04H 3/07] otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid in otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way.
[D04H 3/073] Hollow cylinder shaped -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid otherwise than in a plane so that they are hollow and cylindrically shaped.
[D04H 3/077] Stick, rod or solid cylinder shaped -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Where the method forms webs or layers of webs of continuous filaments laid otherwise than in a plane so that the web is formed as a stick, rod or a solid cylinder.
[D04H 3/08] characterised by the method of strengthening or consolidating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length i.e. continuous filaments characterized by the method of strengthening or consolidating the web.
[D04H 3/10] with bonds between yarns or filaments made mechanically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds made mechanically.
[D04H 3/102] by needling -- D04H 3/102 covers the manufacture of non-woven fabrics that consist mainly of yarns or long filamentary material by using needling, a mechanical technique where barbed needles repeatedly strike the web to interlock the fibers and create a coherent sheet. This method falls under textile making within the broader braiding and fabric-formation categories.
[D04H 3/105] by needling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made mechanically (needling machines D04H18/00 ; knitting D04B ; sewing D05B ).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Needling machines -> D04H18/00
[D04H 3/11] by fluid jet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by fluid jet.
[D04H 3/115] by applying or inserting filamentary binding elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by applying or inserting filamentary binding elements i.e. by stitching.
[D04H 3/12] with filaments or yarns secured together by chemical or thermo-activatable bonding agents, e.g. adhesives, applied or incorporated in liquid or solid form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by filaments or yarns secured together by chemical or thermo-activatable bonding agents, e.g. adhesives, applied or incorporated in liquid or solid form.
[D04H 3/14] with bonds between thermoplastic yarns or filaments produced by welding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by filaments or yarns secured together by bonds between thermoplastic yarns or filaments produced by welding.
[D04H 3/147] Composite yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by filaments or yarns secured together by bonds between thermoplastic yarns or filaments produced by welding using composite yarns or filaments i.e. bicomponent, multicomponent filaments.
[D04H 3/153] Mixed yarns or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by filaments or yarns secured together by bonds between thermoplastic yarns or filaments produced by welding using mixed yarns or filaments i.e. where some of the filaments are thermoplast and some are not.
[D04H 3/16] with bonds between thermoplastic filaments produced in association with filament formation, e.g. immediately following extrusion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed wholly or mainly of continuous filaments with bonds between yarns or filaments made by filaments or yarns secured together by bonds between thermoplastic yarns or filaments produced in association with filament formation, e.g. immediately following extrusion i.e. melt spinning.
[D04H 3/163] at spaced points or locations -- The CPC entry D04H 3/163 relates to manufacturing non-woven textile fabrics made largely of yarns or filamentary material that are secured at spaced points or locations, a technique often used in braided or lattice-like structures. It covers processes for arranging and fixing the fibers to create a fabric where the bonding occurs intermittently rather than continuously.
[D04H 3/166] the filaments being flash-spun -- D04H 3/166 covers non-woven fabrics in which the constituent filaments are produced by flash-spinning, a method that extrudes a polymer solution into a high-temperature gas stream, causing rapid solvent removal and instantaneous fiber formation. This technique is used for making fabrics of substantial filament length, often for high-strength or specialized applications.
[D04H 5/00] Non woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively short fibres and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length
[D04H 5/02] strengthened or consolidated by mechanical methods, e.g. needling -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Needling machines -> D04H18/00
[D04H 5/03] by fluid jet -- D04H 5/03 covers the production of non-woven fabrics by using a fluid jet to entangle or bind short fibers and longer yarns or filaments. The method involves directing high-velocity air or water jets onto the web to create mechanical interlocking without need for adhesives. It falls under the textile braiding and fabric-making subclass of the CPC.
[D04H 5/04] strengthened or consolidated by applying or incorporating chemical or thermo-activatable bonding agents in solid or liquid form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length and strengthened or consolidated by applying or incorporating chemical or thermo-activatable bonding agents in solid or liquid form.
[D04H 5/06] strengthened or consolidated by welding-together thermoplastic fibres, filaments, or yarns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length and strengthened or consolidated by welding-together thermoplastic fibers, filaments, or yarns.
[D04H 5/08] characterised by the method of forming fleeces or layers, e.g. reorientation of fibres or yarns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length characterized by the method of forming fleeces or layers, e.g. reorientation of fibers or yarns (lap-forming devices D01G25/00 ; paper web-making by wet methods D21F , D21H ).
[D04H 5/10] otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length characterized by the method of forming fleeces or layers otherwise than in a plane, e.g. in a tubular way.
[D04H 5/12] Glass fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-woven fabrics formed of mixtures of relatively-short fibers and yarns or like filamentary material of substantial length using glass fibers.
[D05] SEWING -- D05 covers the methods, machines and accessories used for joining textile materials, including stitching, embroidery and related operations. It encompasses industrial and domestic sewing machines, needles, threads, and specialized devices or techniques for fabric attachment and repair.
[D05B] SEWING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing apparatus or machines  Sewing processes dependent on their use  The thread seams produced thereby  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of garments (e.g. stitches) or of their making -> A41D27/00 - Sewing tables (as furniture) -> A47B29/00 - Permanently attaching together sheets, quires or signatures by stitching with filamentary material, e.g. textile thread (i.e. sewing in bookbinding) -> B42B2/00 - Sewing machines modified for knitting -> D04B39/08  **Glossary:** - Sewing: Uniting or fastening by stitches.
[D05B 1/00] General types of sewing apparatus or machines without mechanism for lateral movement of the needle or the work or both -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing apparatus or machines wherein neither the needle nor the work moves laterally.
[D05B 1/02] for making single-thread seams -- The CPC subclass D05B 1/02 covers sewing machines that do not provide lateral movement of the needle or the fabric and are designed to create single-thread seams. It falls under the hierarchy of Textiles → Sewing → General types of sewing apparatus or machines without mechanism for lateral movement of the needle or the work or both. This classification is used for devices that join material using only one thread per stitch.
[D05B 1/04] Running-stitch seams -- **Glossary:** - A running-stitch: Basic stitch done through one or more layers, worked by passing the needle in and out the workpiece
[D05B 1/06] Single chain-stitch seams -- **Glossary:** - Single chain stitch seams: Seams forming a chain on the upper surface of the workpiece.
[D05B 1/08] for making multi-thread seams -- The D05B 1/08 subclass covers sewing apparatus designed to produce multi-thread seams, where the machine forms the seam by interlocking two or more threads but lacks any mechanism that moves the needle or the work laterally during stitching. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing, specifically addressing general types of sewing machines without lateral movement features.
[D05B 1/10] Double chain-stitch seams -- The D05B 1/10 subclass covers sewing apparatus that form double-chain-stitch seams, producing two interlocking rows of looped stitches for decorative, elastic or reinforcing purposes. It includes machines that create this stitch without any lateral movement of the needle or the workpiece.
[D05B 11/00] Machines for sewing quilts or mattresses -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Control of workpiece-holding frames, or of sewing machines, in order to obtain particular seam configuration -> D05B21/00  **Glossary:** - Quilt: Coverlet of two or more layers of cloth filled with padding held in place by stitched designs - Mattress: Fabric case filled with resilient material (as cotton, hair, feathers, foam rubber, or an arrangement of coiled springs), used either alone as a bed or on a bedstead
[D05B 11/005] for sewing the edges of mattresses -- The D05B 11/005 entry classifies machines specifically designed to stitch the edges of mattresses, such as quilting or binding equipment that secures the perimeter fabric. It falls under the textile and sewing hierarchy, covering devices used in the final assembly of mattress coverings.
[D05B 1/12] Lock-stitch seams -- **Glossary:** - Lock-stitch seams: Seams using two threads, an upper and a lower, which lock together in the hole in the workpiece they pass through
[D05B 1/14] Combined or alternative chain-stitch and lock-stitch seams -- D05B 1/14 covers sewing machines that form seams using a combination of chain-stitch and lock-stitch techniques or can switch between them, without providing lateral movement of the needle or the workpiece. This classification falls under the textiles-sewing group for general sewing apparatus lacking side-to-side motion.
[D05B 1/16] Pseudo-lock-stitch seams in which the thread loops do not positively interlock -- A pseudo-lock-stitch seam is a sewing construction where the thread forms loops that resemble a lock-stitch but the loops are not mechanically interlocked, providing a weaker joint. This category covers such seam types used in sewing machines that lack lateral needle or work movement. It falls under the broader classification of sewing apparatus without lateral motion mechanisms.
[D05B 1/18] Seams for protecting or securing edges -- The D05B 1/18 subclass defines seams that are designed to protect or secure the edges of textile or sewn products. It includes edge-reinforcing stitches applied with sewing machines that lack lateral movement of the needle or the material.
[D05B 1/20] Overedge seams -- **Glossary:** - Overedge seams: Seams that are typically used for sewing together and edge-finishing two fabric layers in one step
[D05B 1/22] combined with joining or securing seams -- A sewing apparatus in which the needle and workpiece remain fixed laterally while the machine incorporates a mechanism that simultaneously joins or secures the seam, such as stitching combined with an adhesive, ultrasonic bonding, or other seam-locking techniques. This subclass covers devices that integrate seam-joining functions without lateral movement of the needle or material.
[D05B 1/24] for making blind-stitch seams -- **Glossary:** - Blind stitch seams: Seams that are typically used for attaching lining to a garment where the stitches should not show on either side.
[D05B 1/26] for making fluid-tight seams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or machines for making non-welded fluid tight seams by sewing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sewing machines for concurrently making thread and welded seams -> D05B17/00 .
[D05B 13/00] Machines for sewing sacks -- The D05B 13/00 class covers machines specifically designed to sew industrial sacks, including automated equipment that forms, joins, and seals the fabric panels of bag-type containers. It includes mechanisms for handling bulk materials, closing the tops of sacks, and integrating features such as reinforced seams or closures. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing machinery.
[D05B 13/02] for closing filled bags or sacks -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines for sewing sacks combined with bag- or sack-filling apparatus -> B65B , B65B51/07 - Machines for sewing sacks combined with bag- or sack-filling apparatus -> B65B
[D05B 15/00] Machines for sewing leather goods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines specifically designed for sewing leather goods, i.e. suitcases, bags, and shoes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines for making pseudo-lock-stitch seams -> D05B1/16 - Seat coverings for vehicles by sewing, stitching or threading -> B60N2/5883 - Manufacturing methods specially adapted for seat coverings -> B60N2/5891
[D05B 15/005] Corner sewing of suitcases or bags -- The code D05B 15/005 covers the sewing of corners on suitcases, bags and similar luggage, describing the stitching methods and machines that create reinforced corner seams for leather or textile goods. It falls under the CPC subgroup for sewing of leather articles, specifically the corner-sewing operation.
[D05B 15/02] Shoe sewing machines -- D05B 15/02 designates sewing machines specifically engineered for stitching leather shoes and other footwear. It falls under the CPC hierarchy that moves from the broad textile category through general sewing equipment to machines used in leather-goods production. This code is used by both the EPO and USPTO to classify patents for shoe-making sewing machinery.
[D05B 15/025] for sewing moccasin-type seams -- D05B 15/025 covers machines designed to sew moccasin-type seams in leather goods. It falls within the subclass of sewing machines for leather products, specifying the stitching method used for moccasin seams.
[D05B 15/04] for lock-stitch work -- The D05B 15/04 subclass covers machines specifically designed for sewing leather goods that employ a lock-stitch mechanism. It includes equipment used to join leather pieces in the production of items such as bags, belts, and upholstery by forming interlocking threads that provide a strong, durable seam.
[D05B 15/06] Welt sewing machines -- Welt sewing machines are specialized equipment used to stitch leather or heavy fabrics to create a welt - a narrow strip of material that covers raw edges, commonly seen on belts, bags and upholstery. The machines automate the stitching of the visible welt on the finished product. They are classified under CPC D05B 15/06 for machines for sewing leather goods.
[D05B 15/08] for lock-stitch work -- D05B 15/08 designates machines for sewing leather goods that perform lock-stitch operations. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of TEXTILES → SEWING → SEWING, specifically covering equipment used to produce lock-stitched seams in leather products.
[D05B 15/10] Lining sewing machines -- D05B 15/10 classifies machines built to stitch linings onto garments or leather products, integrating feeding and stitching actions that join the inner layer with the outer material. It is a subcategory of sewing machines for leather goods that focuses specifically on attaching lining material.
[D05B 17/00] Sewing machines for concurrently making thread and welded seams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for concurrently making thread and welded seams using i.e. heat, pressure, ultrasonic means, infrared means, laser or fusible materials.  **Glossary:** - Welding: Uniting by heating and allowing the products to flow together, i.e. by compressing
[D05B 19/00] Program-controlled sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Program-controlled sewing machines, i.e. machines using computer means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Program-controlled sewing machines with devices for automatically controlling movement of work-carrier relative to stitch-forming mechanism in order to obtain particular configuration of seam -> D05B21/00 - Program-controlled devices for stopping drive when sewing tools have reached a predetermined position -> D05B69/22
[D05B 19/003] Zig-zag sewing machines with electric or electronic programming -- This CPC subclass covers zig-zag sewing machines that incorporate electric or electronic control for stitch patterns, feed and other functions. It includes machines where the zig-zag operation is governed by programmable electronics rather than purely mechanical cams. Used to categorize patents on advanced domestic and industrial stitching equipment.
[D05B 19/006] Control knobs or display means  D05B19/085 ,  D05B19/105  take precedence -- D05B 19/006 covers the arrangement of control knobs, switches and visual display elements on program-controlled sewing machines, allowing the operator to select stitch patterns and parameters. Sub-groups D05B19/085 and D05B19/105 provide more specific details and therefore take precedence over this broader entry.
[D05B 19/02] Sewing machines having electronic memory or microprocessor control unit -- The CPC subclass D05B 19/02 refers to sewing machines that incorporate electronic memory or a microprocessor control unit, allowing the machine to operate under programmed control. This entry sits within the hierarchy of textiles → sewing → sewing → program-controlled sewing machines.
[D05B 19/04] characterised by memory aspects -- The D05B 19/04 class covers program-controlled sewing machines that incorporate memory functions, such as storing stitch patterns, machine settings or operational sequences for later retrieval. It includes devices that can recall and reuse programmed data to control sewing actions automatically.
[D05B 19/06] Physical exchange of memory -- The CPC subclass D05B 19/06 relates to the physical exchange of memory devices in program-controlled sewing machines, covering removable memory modules, cartridges or cards that store stitching patterns, settings or control data. It includes the mechanisms, slots and interfaces that allow users to insert, remove or replace such memory to transfer or update the machine's programmed functions.
[D05B 19/08] Arrangements for inputting stitch or pattern data to memory -- The entry pertains to systems that transfer stitch designs or pattern data into a sewing machine's memory, including keyboards, touch screens, card readers or software interfaces, allowing the machine to automatically reproduce the stored stitches.
[D05B 19/085] Physical layout of switches or displays; Switches co-operating with the display -- The CPC subclass D05B 19/085 covers the arrangement of user-interface elements on program-controlled sewing machines, specifically how switches and visual indicators are positioned and linked so that the switches operate in coordination with the display. It addresses the physical layout that enables the operator to control and monitor machine functions.
[D05B 19/10] Arrangements for selecting combinations of stitch or pattern data from memory -- The subclass D05B 19/10 covers arrangements that retrieve stitch or pattern data stored in a machine's memory and combine it for selection during operation, enabling the user to choose and switch between different stitch sequences or designs. It applies to program-controlled sewing machines within the broader textile and sewing classification.
[D05B 19/105] Physical layout of switches or displays; Switches co-operating with the display -- The D05B 19/105 subclass deals with the design and arrangement of user-interface components on program-controlled sewing equipment, covering how switches, buttons or other controls are positioned relative to electronic displays and how they interact to convey and modify machine settings.
[D05B 19/12] characterised by control of operation of machine -- This CPC subclass covers sewing machines whose functions are directed by programmed logic or electronic control, such as microprocessor-driven speed, stitch selection and timing. It includes machines where the operation is automatically adjusted by software commands rather than manual settings.
[D05B 19/14] Control of needle movement, e.g. varying amplitude or period of needle movement -- The class covers systems that vary the needle's motion in a sewing machine, such as adjusting stroke length, speed, timing or frequency to achieve different stitch characteristics. It includes electronic or program-controlled mechanisms that modulate the amplitude, period or phase of needle movement for precise stitching control.
[D05B 19/16] Control of workpiece movement, e.g. modulation of travel of feed dog -- The CPC entry D05B 19/16 covers the control systems that regulate the movement of the workpiece in program-controlled sewing machines, such as varying the feed dog's travel speed, direction or timing. It includes mechanisms that modulate fabric advance to synchronize with needle actions, enabling precise stitching patterns and adaptive feed control.
[D05B 21/00] Sewing machines with devices for automatically controlling movement of work-carrier relative to stitch-forming mechanism in order to obtain particular configuration of seam, e.g. program-controlled for sewing collars or for attaching pockets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing machines with devices for automatically controlling movement of work-carrier relative to stitch-forming mechanism in order to obtain particular configuration of seam, e.g. program-controlled for sewing collars, for attaching pockets, bar tacking.  **Glossary:** - Bar tacking or bartacking: Specialized sewing stitch designed to provide tensile strength to the product it is used on, for reinforcing areas of stress on a garment, such as pocket openings.
[D05B 21/002] consisting of lateral displacement of the workpieces by a feed-dog or a fluted presser foot -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. machines with accessory devices controlled by the presser foot, with fluted means having grooves to improve friction.
[D05B 21/005] to obtain spiral seams  fixed or adjustable pin holding the work for spiral stitching  D05B35/107
[D05B 21/007] to obtain circular or elliptical seams  fixed or adjustable pin holding the work for circular stitching  D05B35/107
[D05B 23/00] Sewing apparatus or machines not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. sewing machines for sewing straw hats, straw cases, wisps of straw, canvas shoes, for hemming hats, ribbing corsets, binding or uniting carpets, assembling parts of knitted panties or closing toe parts of stockings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sewing apparatus or machines for making zip closures -> A44B19/00 .
[D05B 23/001] Straw hat sewing machines -- A D05B 23/001 classification covers sewing machines specifically engineered for assembling straw hats. These devices are intended to handle the delicate, stiff material of hat brims and crowns, providing stitch formation suited to the unique construction methods of millinery. The code falls within the broader categories of textiles, sewing, and sewing apparatus not otherwise provided for.
