[D05B 23/002] Hat hemming sewing machines -- D05B 23/002 covers sewing machines specifically designed for hemming hats, including devices that form, attach, and finish the brim and crown edges of headwear. These machines fall under the textiles-sewing hierarchy and are classified as sewing apparatus not otherwise provided for in the CPC system.
[D05B 23/003] Sewing machines for straw cases, wisps of straw or canvas shoes -- D05B 23/003 pertains to sewing machines specially designed for stitching straw cases, bundles of straw, or canvas shoes. These machines are adapted to handle the lightweight, flexible nature of such materials and are not covered by other sewing-apparatus categories.
[D05B 23/004] Sewing machines for ribbing corsets -- D05B 23/004 refers to sewing machines specially designed for creating ribbed structures on corsets. These machines incorporate mechanisms that form and stitch the decorative ribbing characteristic of corset panels, distinguishing them from general-purpose sewing equipment. The subclass covers any apparatus dedicated to ribbing production in corsetry.
[D05B 23/005] Sewing machines specially adapted for binding or uniting carpets -- This CPC subclass D05B 23/005 covers sewing machines that are specifically designed to bind or join carpet pieces. Such machines are used in textile manufacturing for carpet assembly, providing mechanisms tailored to handle thick, heavy material and create durable seams.
[D05B 23/006] Sewing machines for making cylindrical articles -- D05B 23/006 covers sewing machines specifically engineered to produce cylindrical articles such as tubes, sleeves, or hose-like products. These machines typically employ circular stitching or rotary mechanisms to join fabric edges into a continuous tube shape, differing from standard flat-bed or overlock equipment.
[D05B 23/007] Sewing units for assembling parts of knitted panties or closing the stocking toe part -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing units for assembling parts of knitted panties or closing the stocking toe part, after the knitting operation has been processed.
[D05B 23/008] Line closers, i.e. sewing units for forming the body portion of the panty hose -- The CPC subclass D05B 23/008 refers to line-closing devices, specifically sewing units used to join the body portion of pantyhose. It covers apparatus designed to form the continuous seam that creates the main tube of the hosiery, rather than general sewing machines.
[D05B 23/009] Toe closers -- The entry D05B 23/009 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers specialized sewing machine attachments or mechanisms designed to close or seal the toe area of a shoe, boot or similar item during stitching. These toe closers guide the material around the toe and secure the seam, improving fit and speed in footwear manufacturing.
[D05B 25/00] Sewing units consisting of combinations of several sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing units consisting of combinations of several sewing machines which can be mounted in series or in parallel, incorporating e.g. conveying devices and devices not incorporated in the sewing machines for supplying and removing the work.
[D05B 27/00] Sewing-machine elements for feeding, positioning, handling, or treating the work -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Work-feeding means forming part of the sewing machine, e.g. feed dogs, rotary circular feed members, sewing needles, used in order to move the work through the sewing machine.  **Glossary:** - Feed dogs: A part of a sewing machine that feeds the work under the needle.
[D05B 27/02] with feed dogs having horizontal and vertical movements -- The CPC subclass D05B 27/02 covers sewing-machine feed devices in which the feed dogs move both horizontally and vertically to advance fabric. This dual-direction motion enables precise positioning and handling of the workpiece during stitching.
[D05B 27/04] arranged above the workpieces -- This subclass covers sewing-machine components that are positioned above the workpieces, such as presser foot assemblies, guide rails or feed devices that operate over the fabric while it is being stitched, providing support, guidance or treatment from a superior location.
[D05B 27/06] arranged above and below the workpieces -- The classification D05B 27/06 covers sewing-machine components that are situated both above and below the fabric or workpiece, such as presser feet, feed rollers, or guides that hold, move, or treat the material from both sides during stitching. This sub-category falls under sewing-machine elements for feeding, positioning, handling, or treating the work within the broader textiles hierarchy.
[D05B 27/08] with differential feed motions -- This CPC subclass D05B 27/08 relates to sewing-machine components that feed the workpiece with differential motions, where parts of the feed mechanism move at different speeds or directions to control positioning, tension, or stitching patterns during operation.
[D05B 27/10] with rotary circular feed members -- This subclass covers sewing-machine elements that feed the fabric using rotary circular feed members such as rotating discs or rollers that move the material in a circular path to position it for stitching. These devices are used to control the advance of the workpiece during stitching operations.
[D05B 27/12] rotating continuously -- The CPC subclass D05B 27/12 refers to sewing-machine components that rotate continuously to feed, position, handle or treat the workpiece. It includes mechanisms such as continuously rotating feed rollers, needles or other rotating elements that move fabric through the machine during stitching.
[D05B 27/14] rotating discontinuously -- The D05B 27/14 subclass includes sewing-machine components that move in a stepped or intermittent fashion rather than continuously, such as feed rollers, needles or other elements that rotate discontinuously to advance, position, or treat the workpiece during stitching. This covers mechanisms that provide periodic motion for fabric handling within a sewing machine.
[D05B 27/16] with differential feed motions -- The D05B 27/16 subclass covers sewing-machine elements that provide differential feed motions, i.e., mechanisms that adjust the relative feed speed of the workpiece and needle to prevent stretching or puckering. It falls within the broader category of sewing-machine components for feeding, positioning, handling, or treating the material being sewn.
[D05B 27/18] Feed cups
[D05B 27/185] Guides or supports for the work presented to the feed cups, e.g. uncurling guides -- This subclass covers devices that guide or support fabric as it enters a sewing-machine's feed cups, such as mechanisms that straighten or uncurl the material before stitching, ensuring correct positioning during feeding. It falls under sewing-machine elements for handling the work.
[D05B 27/20] constituted by sewing needles -- D05B 27/20 covers sewing-machine elements whose primary constituent is a sewing needle, used to feed, position, handle or treat the workpiece during stitching. It includes designs where the needle itself forms the functional component of mechanisms such as feed or treatment devices.
[D05B 27/22] with means for setting length of stitch -- The subclass D05B 27/22 covers sewing-machine components that provide a means for setting the length of each stitch, such as adjustable selectors, cams or levers that control feed or needle movement. These mechanisms ensure consistent stitch length during sewing operations.
[D05B 27/24] Feed-dog lifting and lowering devices -- This subclass concerns mechanisms that raise or lower the feed dogs of a sewing machine to control fabric movement. It includes adjustable or selectable devices that modify the feed-dog position during operation, enabling different stitch lengths or specialized handling of material.
[D05B 27/26] in machines for sewing leather -- D05B 27/26 relates to sewing-machine elements that feed, position, handle or treat leather during stitching. It covers mechanisms such as feeding devices, guides, presser foot adaptations and other components specially configured for leather work.
[D05B 29/00] Pressers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  pressers and presser feet resting on the work and pressing it down upon the table of the sewing machine.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Presser feet for feeding the work -> D05B27/04
[D05B 29/02] Presser-control devices -- Presser-control devices are mechanisms that adjust and regulate the pressure applied by a sewing machine's presser foot onto the fabric. The classification includes springs, levers, pneumatic or hydraulic systems, and other components that allow the operator to set the optimal pressing force for different materials and stitching operations.
[D05B 29/04] Pressers in machines for sewing leather -- The CPC class D05B 29/04 covers components called pressers that are installed in sewing machines specifically designed for leather work. These pressers apply pressure to the material during stitching, helping to feed the thick, stiff leather through the machine and produce consistent seams. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing equipment.
[D05B 29/06] Presser feet -- Presser feet are the interchangeable attachments mounted on the pressurized plate of a sewing machine to hold and guide fabric during stitching, enabling tasks such as gathering, hemming, quilting, or applying accessories. They are classified under D05B 29/06 within the CPC hierarchy for textiles, sewing and pressers.
[D05B 29/08] comprising relatively-movable parts -- The CPC subclass D05B 29/08 refers to sewing pressers that include relatively-movable components, such as hinged, swinging or pivoting jaws and plates that adjust during the stitching process. It covers machines where the pressing element can shift position to accommodate fabric thickness, guide movement, or facilitate stitching of irregular shapes, enhancing the flexibility and control of the sewing operation.
[D05B 29/10] with rollers -- The CPC subclass D05B 29/10 covers devices that press fabric using rollers as part of a sewing operation. It includes roller-type presses, roller feeders, and other mechanisms where rotating cylinders apply pressure to a textile during stitching or finishing. This subclass falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → SEWING → Pressers.
[D05B 29/12] Presser-foot attachment -- The CPC subclass D05B 29/12 covers devices that attach a presser foot to a sewing machine, enabling the foot to exert pressure on fabric while stitching. It includes mechanisms, brackets or couplings designed to secure the foot in place and allow easy removal or adjustment during sewing operations.
[D05B 3/00] Special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus or machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing apparatus or machines with mechanism for lateral movement of the needle or the work or both, e.g. for bar-tacking, i.e. forming series of stitches used for reinforcing areas of stress on a garment, such as pocket openings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sewing machines characterised by the program control for lateral movement of the needle or the work or both -> D05B19/00 - Sewing machines characterised by devices for automatically controlling movement of work-carrier relative to stitch-forming mechanism in order to obtain particular configuration of seam -> D05B21/00
[D05B 3/02] with mechanisms for needle-bar movement -- The D05B 3/02 subclass covers sewing machines that incorporate mechanisms for moving the needle bar, such as cam-driven, pneumatic or electronic actuation, allowing variable needle motion during operation. These features are found in special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus designed to produce particular stitch types or improve stitching accuracy.
[D05B 3/025] with a pair of needles mounted on the needle-bar -- D05B 3/025 covers automatic or special-purpose sewing machines that have two needles mounted on a single needle bar. The paired needles operate together to form double-needle stitches such as parallel rows or reinforced seams, commonly used in industrial garment and textile production.
[D05B 3/04] with mechanisms for work feed -- D05B 3/04 covers special-purpose or automatic sewing machines that incorporate mechanisms to advance the material being sewn. It includes devices such as feed rollers, hooks, loopers or other work-feed systems that move the fabric automatically during the stitching cycle, integrating material transport with the sewing operation.
[D05B 3/06] for sewing buttonholes -- D05B 3/06 covers machines that form buttonholes while sewing, integrating cutting and stitching in a single operation, and is used in special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus.
[D05B 3/08] for buttonholes with eyelet ends -- D05B 3/08 covers automatic or special-purpose sewing machines designed to create buttonholes that terminate in an eyelet. The apparatus forms the surrounding stitch and cuts or reinforces the opening to produce a reinforced, rounded eyelet suitable for threading. It is classified under textiles, sewing, special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus.
[D05B 3/10] for making piped openings -- **Glossary:** - Piped openings: are openings e.g. formed for making pockets in a garment. The machines for making piped openings may have two needles
[D05B 31/00] Workpiece holders or hold-downs in machines for sewing leather -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Workpiece holders or hold-downs in machines for sewing leather, e.g. balls, shoes.
[D05B 31/02] Welt guides -- Welt guides are devices attached to sewing machines that direct the welt or edge of a workpiece during stitching, ensuring correct alignment and tension especially in leather sewing. They form part of the workpiece holder or hold-down group in the CPC class D05B 31/02.
[D05B 3/12] for fastening articles by sewing -- The D05B 3/12 subclass pertains to sewing equipment or devices whose primary purpose is to fasten articles by stitching, covering mechanisms that join fabrics, leather, or similar materials through sewn seams. It includes both manual and automatic apparatuses designed specifically for the fastening function.
[D05B 3/14] perforated or press buttons -- D05B 3/14 refers to perforated or press buttons that are applied by special-purpose or automatic sewing machines. The classification includes fastening elements that are punched or pressed into fabric during the sewing process, serving decorative or functional closure purposes.
[D05B 3/16] shank buttons -- D05B 3/16 pertains to shank buttons, fasteners that have a raised stem which is sewn onto fabric, typically using specialized or automatic sewing machines. The code covers the design, manufacture and attachment methods for these textile fasteners.
[D05B 3/18] hooks or eyelets -- This subclass covers sewing machines or devices that incorporate hooks or eyelets for fastening, such as those that automatically form or attach them to fabrics. It includes mechanisms that produce, handle, or secure eyelets and hooks during sewing operations, typically in specialized or automatic equipment.
[D05B 3/20] labels -- D05B 3/20 covers devices that attach or produce labels by sewing, including machines designed to apply or affix pre-made labels to fabrics or garments. It includes automated or special-purpose equipment that integrates labeling into the sewing process.
[D05B 3/22] Article-, e.g. button-, feed mechanisms therefor -- The code D05B 3/22 refers to mechanisms that advance individual articles-such as buttons, studs or similar items-into a sewing machine during operation. It covers feed devices integrated in special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus that position each article for stitching.
[D05B 3/24] formed by general-purpose sewing machines modified by attachments, e.g. by detachable devices -- This subclass covers sewing operations where a standard, general-purpose sewing machine is adapted with auxiliary or detachable attachments to achieve special functions. The modifications may include add-on devices that alter stitch formation, feed mechanisms, or material handling without requiring a dedicated special-purpose machine. It is used to classify inventions that improve or extend the capabilities of ordinary sewing machines through removable accessories.
[D05B 3/243] for embroidering, e.g. monograms -- The D05B 3/243 subclass covers special-purpose or automatic sewing machines designed to create embroidery, such as monograms. It includes mechanisms that precisely deposit thread in decorative patterns on fabric, allowing automated production of embroidered lettering or motifs.
[D05B 3/246] for darning -- D05B 3/246 falls under the CPC group for special-purpose or automatic sewing apparatus within textiles and sewing. It designates machines or attachments specifically engineered to perform darning, the repair of fabric by interlacing thread to fill holes or worn areas.
[D05B 33/00] Devices incorporated in sewing machines for supplying or removing the work -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices incorporated in sewing machines for supplying or removing the work, e.g. conveying devices, feeding workpieces separated from piles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Work-feeding means, e.g. feed dogs, rotary circular feed members -> D05B27/00
[D05B 33/003] by equal steps of adjustable length, e.g. for automatically positioning successive buttonhole locations -- The CPC subclass D05B 33/003 covers mechanisms that move the workpiece in a sewing machine by equal, adjustable steps, typically to set successive buttonhole positions automatically. Such devices control the feed distance so each buttonhole is placed at a precise, repeatable interval without manual measurement.
[D05B 33/006] Feeding workpieces separated from piles, e.g. unstacking -- D05B 33/006 covers mechanisms attached to sewing machines that separate individual fabric pieces from a stacked pile and feed them one by one into the stitching area, enabling automatic unstacking of workpieces.
[D05B 33/02] and connected, for synchronous operation, with the work-feeding devices of the sewing machine
[D05B 35/00] Work-feeding or -handling elements not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Work-feeding or work-handling elements for specific applications, e.g. for facilitating seaming, hem-turning elements, for attaching bands, ribbons, strips, for binding, ruching, gathering, casing, filling lace, pleating devices, cuttlers, curlers, edge guides, indicators for positioning work.
[D05B 35/02] for facilitating seaming -- **Glossary:** - Hem: Border of a cloth article doubled back and stitched down.
[D05B 35/04] with movable tools -- D05B 35/04 covers sewing apparatus where the work-feeding or handling elements are not otherwise specified and incorporate movable tools, such as clamps, guides or conveyors that can be repositioned during operation to advance, align or secure the material being sewn. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing, focusing on mechanisms that actively manipulate the workpiece while stitching.
[D05B 35/06] for attaching bands, ribbons, strips, or tapes or for binding -- This subclass covers devices, methods, or components used to attach or bind bands, ribbons, strips or tapes in textile or sewing applications, including fixtures, fasteners, clamps, or other work-feeding or handling elements not covered elsewhere.
[D05B 35/062] with hem-turning  D05B35/066  takes precedence
[D05B 35/064] for attaching slide fasteners  connection of the interlocking member to the stringer tape  A44B19/40 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Connection of separate, or one piece, interlocking members to stringer tapes and reinforcing such connections, e.g. by stitching -> A44B19/40
[D05B 35/066] for attaching small textile pieces, e.g. labels, belt loops -- D05B 35/066 classifies sewing apparatus or work-feeding elements specifically designed to attach small textile items such as labels, belt loops or similar pieces to a fabric. It falls under the broader category of work-handling elements not otherwise provided for in sewing operations.
[D05B 35/068] for attaching belt loops -- This subgroup covers devices or attachments used to fasten belt loops to garments during sewing, such as clamps, guides or other work-feeding elements that secure the loops while the fabric is handled. It falls under the broader category of handling elements not otherwise provided for in textile sewing operations.
[D05B 35/08] for ruching, gathering, casing, or filling lace, ribbons, or bindings -- **Glossary:** - Ruching lace: Forming a pleat of lace for trimming a work - Ruffler: Attachment on a sewing machine used for making frills - Crimper: A mechanical device consisting of a cylindrical tube around which the work is wound to curl it
[D05B 35/085] for making folds transversely to the sewing direction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for making folds transversely to the sewing direction, e.g. rufflers.
[D05B 35/10] Edge guides -- Edge guides are components that control the position of a fabric edge as it moves through a sewing or handling system, ensuring consistent alignment and preventing drift. They are used in textile equipment where specific work-feeding or handling elements are not otherwise defined.
[D05B 35/102] Edge guide control systems with edge sensors -- This subclass pertains to sewing equipment that employs edge sensors to monitor and adjust the fabric's trajectory, providing automatic edge-guide control to improve positioning accuracy and reduce material waste. It falls under work-feeding or handling elements for textiles, specifically within the sewing domain.
[D05B 35/105] Guiding while stretching the workpiece, e.g. by means of weighted clips -- The subclass deals with mechanisms that simultaneously stretch and guide a workpiece during sewing, employing elements such as weighted clips to keep the material taut and correctly positioned. It is part of the textile work-feeding or handling category for items not otherwise provided for.
[D05B 35/107] Fixed or adjustable pin holding the work, e.g. for circular or spiral stitching -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sewing machines with devices for automatically controlling movement of work-carrier relative to stitch-forming mechanism in order to obtain spiral seams -> D05B21/005
[D05B 35/12] Indicators for positioning work, e.g. with graduated scales -- This subclass covers devices that give visual or tactile cues to help locate or align material during sewing operations, such as graduated scales, markers or other positioning indicators attached to feeding or handling elements not otherwise specified.
[D05B 37/00] Devices incorporated in sewing machines for slitting, grooving, or cutting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices incorporated in sewing machines for slitting, grooving or cutting the work.  **Glossary:** - Slitting: Forming a long and narrow cut - Grooving: Forming a long narrow channel
[D05B 37/02] Slitting or grooving devices -- D05B 37/02 pertains to devices built into sewing machines that create narrow cuts or channels in fabric, such as slitting or grooving tools. These mechanisms are used to trim, shape, or prepare textile material during stitching.
[D05B 37/04] Cutting devices -- D05B 37/04 covers devices incorporated in sewing machines that perform cutting functions such as slitting, grooving or trimming the fabric during stitching. It includes mechanisms like blades, knives or rotary cutters integrated into the machine to shape or separate material, and falls under the textiles-sewing hierarchy.
[D05B 37/06] with oscillating tools -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cutting devices with oscillating tools, i.e. tools swinging back and forth with a steady, uninterrupted rhythm, not just moving up and down.
[D05B 37/063] in synchronism with the movement of the needle bar or the work-feeding means -- D05B 37/063 covers sewing machine accessories that cut, slit or groove fabric in timing with the needle bar or the work-feeding means, ensuring that the cuts occur precisely as the needle moves or the material is advanced.
[D05B 37/066] Pinking -- **Glossary:** - Pinking: is forming a perforated pattern.
[D05B 37/08] with rotatable tools -- The D05B 37/08 subclass covers sewing-machine accessories that employ rotatable tools-such as rotary knives, scissors or cutters-to perform slitting, grooving or cutting operations on fabrics during sewing. It pertains to devices integrated into the machine for these rotary cutting functions.
[D05B 37/085] Pinking -- **Glossary:** - Pinking: is forming a perforated pattern.
[D05B 37/10] with heated tools -- D05B 37/10 covers sewing-machine devices that perform slitting, grooving or cutting with heated tools. The heated element melts or softens the material to enable precise cuts, especially useful for synthetic fabrics or specialty threads.
[D05B 39/00] Workpiece carriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Workpiece carriers, e.g. for cap, support structures, sewing frames.
[D05B 39/005] Quilting frames -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Quilting frames in sewing machines.
[D05B 41/00] Work-collecting devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Work-collecting devices, e.g. worktables, stack support assemblies.
[D05B 43/00] Sewing-machine elements for supplying, handling, or treating thread material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spool-pin assemblies incorporated in sewing machines for holding spools.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Applications of bobbin-winding or -changing devices -> D05B59/00  **Glossary:** - Spool pin: is a pin on which the spool of thread is seated.
[D05B 45/00] Applications of measuring devices for determining the length of threads used in sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applications of measuring devices for determining the length of threads used in sewing machines, e.g. in order to cut the thread after performing sewing, to control the speed, to determine the thread reserve.
[D05B 47/00] Needle-thread tensioning devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needle thread tensioning devices and applications of tensometers, i.e. devices controlling the needle thread in order to avoid a defective stitching or a broken thread.
[D05B 47/02] Manually-controlled tensioning devices -- Manually-controlled tensioning devices are mechanisms that allow the operator to adjust the thread tension on a needle during sewing. By using a knob, lever or similar control, the device changes the force applied to the thread to achieve balanced stitches and prevent breakage. These devices are commonly found in sewing machines and specialized hand-sewing tools.
[D05B 47/04] Automatically-controlled tensioning devices -- D05B 47/04 covers automatically-controlled tensioning devices used in sewing machines to regulate thread tension. These devices employ feedback mechanisms or sensors that continuously adjust tension, ensuring consistent stitch quality in textile production.
[D05B 47/06] Applications of tensometers -- D05B 47/06 covers the use of tensometers-devices that measure or control thread tension-in sewing and textile processes. It includes applications where such tension-measuring instruments are integrated into sewing machines, fixtures or related equipment to monitor or adjust thread pull during stitching.
[D05B 49/00] Take-up devices, e.g. levers, for the needle thread -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Take-up devices, e.g. levers, for the needle thread, in order to provide enough thread for the loop and preventing knotted wads of thread.
[D05B 49/02] operated by cams or linkages -- The CPC subclass D05B 49/02 pertains to take-up devices for the needle thread that are actuated by cams or linkage mechanisms, such as levers used in sewing machines to control thread tension and retrieval. It is situated within the textile sewing hierarchy of the CPC.
[D05B 49/04] rotary -- Rotary take-up devices are lever mechanisms that rotate to capture and wind the needle thread during stitching. They are used in sewing machines to pull the thread off the bobbin and guide it onto the fabric, providing a continuous thread supply.
[D05B 49/06] for machines for sewing leather -- D05B 49/06 relates to take-up devices such as levers that control the needle thread in machines built for sewing leather, focusing on mechanisms that manage thread tension and positioning during leather stitching. It falls under the hierarchy textiles → sewing → sewing.
[D05B 5/00] Sewing machines for temporarily connecting articles, e.g. pairs of socks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sewing machines for temporarily connecting articles, e.g. for joining knitted articles.  **Glossary:** - Overlocking machine: Type of machine often used for temporarily connecting articles
[D05B 51/00] Applications of needle-thread guards -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applications of needle-thread guards and thread-break detectors, i.e. devices for guiding the needle thread and for detecting the absence of the thread, respectively.  **Glossary:** - Needle-thread guard: Mechanical element preventing skipping stitches
[D05B 53/00] Thread- or cord-laying mechanisms -- **Glossary:** - Thread laying mechanism: A device used to guide the thread in order to manage the stitches when a smooth and flat finish is desired.
[D05B 55/00] Needle holders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needle holders and needle bars, e.g. devices for fastening needles to needle bars, needle guides, needle-bar drives.
[D05B 55/02] Devices for fastening needles to needle bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for fastening needles to needle bars for all kinds of needles.
[D05B 55/04] Devices for inserting needles -- Devices for inserting needles are tools that hold, guide or feed a needle so it can be introduced into fabric with precision, commonly found in hand-sewing aids and sewing-machine mechanisms. They reduce manual effort and improve stitch accuracy by mechanically positioning the needle during the sewing process.
[D05B 55/06] Needle guides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needle guides and needle protectors, exclusively designed to guide and protect the needle, not to guide with regard to the needle or protect from the needle.
[D05B 55/08] Driving arrangements for curved needles -- The D05B 55/08 subclass covers the specific mechanisms that move or power curved needles in sewing equipment, detailing drive systems such as cams, levers, or electronic actuators that control needle motion. It focuses on the engineering of these arrangements rather than the needle itself or the stitching pattern. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing, particularly needle holders.
[D05B 55/10] Needle bars for multiple-needle sewing machines -- The CPC code D05B 55/10 covers needle bars designed for multiple-needle sewing machines. These components support and guide several needles simultaneously, enabling high-speed, parallel stitching in industrial textile production. The classification falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing equipment.
[D05B 55/12] with provision for varying the distance between the needles -- This subclass covers needle holder devices that allow the user to adjust the spacing between two or more needles during sewing. The mechanism includes adjustable arms, slides or similar elements that can be set to different distances, enabling variable stitch width or pattern changes.
[D05B 55/14] Needle-bar drives -- Needle-bar drives are mechanisms that convert rotary motion into the up-and-down motion of the sewing needle, allowing precise control of needle penetration during stitching. They are integral components of needle-holder assemblies in both industrial and domestic sewing machines, providing the power transmission needed for consistent stitch formation.
[D05B 55/16] with provision for disengaging individual needle bars -- This CPC subclass pertains to sewing-machine needle holders that incorporate a mechanism for disengaging individual needle bars, enabling selective activation, easy maintenance or replacement of specific needles while the others remain engaged.
[D05B 57/00] Loop takers, e.g. loopers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Loop takers and loopers, i.e. mechanisms placed underneath the workpiece for grabbing the loop of thread before the needle pulls up, and wrapping it around either another piece of thread or another loop in the same piece of thread.
[D05B 57/02] for chain-stitch sewing machines, e.g. oscillating -- The D05B 57/02 subclass covers loop-taker mechanisms used in chain-stitch sewing machines, specifically those that create the stitch by taking a loop of thread. It includes designs such as oscillating loopers that move back-and-forth to form the chain stitch.
[D05B 57/04] rotary -- The CPC subclass D05B57/04, titled rotary, falls under textiles and sewing, specifically loop takers such as loopers. It covers rotary mechanisms used in loop-taking devices for sewing machines.
[D05B 57/06] for overedge-stitch sewing machines -- This CPC subclass covers overedge-stitch sewing machines, which form a stitch that folds over the raw edge of fabric to prevent fraying. Such machines are used in garment and textile production for finishing seams and edge trims. They belong to the D05B 57/06 hierarchy under textiles and sewing.
[D05B 57/08] for lock-stitch sewing machines -- The CPC subclass D05B 57/08 covers mechanisms for loop takers, also called loopers, that are used in lock-stitch sewing machines to form the lower thread loop during stitching. This subclass falls under the broader textile and sewing categories and specifically addresses the components that catch and guide the lower thread to create a secure lock stitch.
[D05B 57/10] Shuttles -- Shuttles are components used in sewing machines to form loops of thread, commonly in lockstitch mechanisms. They move back and forth, carrying the lower thread to interlock with the upper thread, enabling the creation of sturdy stitches in woven fabrics. This element falls under the CPC subclass for loop takers within textile sewing equipment.
[D05B 57/12] oscillating -- The D05B 57/12 subclass covers oscillating loop-taker mechanisms used in sewing machines, where the loop-forming element moves back and forth to capture and release thread loops during stitch formation. This includes designs that rely on reciprocating motion rather than continuous rotation to create the loop needed for a stitch.
[D05B 57/14] with rotary hooks -- D05B 57/14 refers to sewing machine mechanisms that use rotary hooks as loop-takers, allowing the bobbin thread to be caught and formed into a stitch. It is classified under textile sewing equipment for loop-taking devices.
[D05B 57/143] Vertical axis type -- The CPC subclass D05B 57/143 covers loop-taking devices, such as loopers, that operate with a vertical axis configuration. It pertains to sewing machines where the loop-forming mechanism moves up and down rather than rotating horizontally, facilitating the creation of stitches in textile fabrication. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing equipment.
[D05B 57/146] Inclined axis type -- The D05B 57/146 subclass covers loop-taking devices-commonly called loopers-that are mounted on an inclined axis relative to the fabric path. These mechanisms are used in sewing machines to pull thread loops through the material, a technique employed especially in specialized textile stitching operations. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[D05B 57/16] with bobbin casings guided in tracks -- The code D05B 57/16 refers to sewing mechanisms in which the bobbin casing is confined to and moves along guiding tracks. It covers machines that control the bobbin's path by fixed channels, ensuring precise stitching positions and consistent thread tension. This sub-category falls under textile sewing equipment.
[D05B 57/18] with bobbin casings held by removable caps -- D05B 57/18 covers sewing machine mechanisms that enclose the bobbin in a casing secured by removable caps, allowing the bobbin to be inserted and taken out without disassembling the machine. It applies to loop-taking devices such as loopers where the cap-type closure simplifies bobbin handling and maintenance.
[D05B 57/20] with bobbin casings held by magnetic forces -- The subclass D05B 57/20 refers to sewing machines whose loop-taking or looping mechanisms use magnetic forces to hold the bobbin casing in place. Instead of a mechanical latch, a permanent magnet or electromagnet secures the bobbin case to the needle plate or shuttle housing, allowing rapid attachment and release during stitching.
[D05B 57/22] with spool-size bobbin for lower thread -- D05B 57/22 refers to sewing machine technologies that employ loop-taking (looper) mechanisms and use a spool-size bobbin to feed the lower thread. The classification covers designs where the bobbin is as large as a spool, enabling continuous thread supply for under-thread stitching.
[D05B 57/24] with lower-thread packages mounted for controlled sliding movements -- D05B 57/24 relates to sewing machines with loop takers or loopers that have lower-thread packages mounted so they can slide in a controlled way. The sliding mechanism lets the bobbin thread be positioned accurately during stitch formation, enhancing stitch consistency and machine flexibility.
[D05B 57/26] Bobbin holders or casings -- D05B 57/26 relates to bobbin holders or casings employed in sewing machines, especially within loop-taker mechanisms that form stitch loops. It covers devices that secure and protect the bobbin while guiding thread feeding during sewing operations.
[D05B 57/265] for looptakers with vertical axis -- The CPC subclass D05B 57/265 covers sewing machines that employ a loop taker (also called a looper) whose rotating axis is oriented vertically relative to the fabric. This design is used to form the lower loop of a lockstitch or similar stitch in textile construction.
[D05B 57/28] Applications of bobbins for storing the lower thread -- D05B 57/28 deals with bobbins that store the lower thread in sewing machines, especially for loop takers such as loopers. The code covers designs, materials and methods for holding and feeding the bobbin thread during stitching operations.
[D05B 57/30] Driving-gear for loop takers -- D05B 57/30 relates to the driving gear that powers loop takers, also known as loopers, in sewing machines. The gear transfers motion to the loop taker mechanism, enabling it to catch and form thread loops for stitch formation. This falls within the textiles-sewing subclass for sewing machine drive components.
[D05B 57/32] in chain-stitch sewing machines -- D05B 57/32 relates to the loop-taking mechanisms in chain-stitch sewing machines, describing designs that form the loop of thread that creates the chain stitch. It includes various loopers, hook systems and associated drive elements that capture and transfer the thread during stitching.
[D05B 57/34] in overedge-stitch sewing machines -- This CPC subclass defines loop-taking mechanisms, such as loopers, that are incorporated in overedge-stitch sewing machines. It covers designs that form the overedge stitch while simultaneously capturing the thread loop to create a secure, finished edge on textile materials.
[D05B 57/36] in lock-stitch sewing machines -- D05B 57/36 classifies lock-stitch sewing machines equipped with loop takers, commonly called loopers, which form the lower thread loop of the stitch. This subgroup falls under the textile hierarchy of sewing and loop-taker mechanisms.
[D05B 57/38] Shuttle drives -- The CPC subclass D05B 57/38 pertains to shuttle-drive mechanisms used in sewing machines, specifically those that move the loop taker or looper to form stitch loops. It covers motorised or mechanically powered arrangements that transmit motion to the shuttle component.
[D05B 59/00] Applications of bobbin-winding or -changing devices -- D05B 59/00 covers devices for winding or changing bobbins in sewing machines and related textile equipment. It includes mechanisms that automatically replace or reposition bobbins to facilitate continuous stitching, such as automatic bobbin changers and winding stations. The classification falls under the textile sewing subgroup.
[D05B 59/02] Devices for determining or indicating the length of thread still on the bobbin -- The D05B 59/02 subclass includes any device that measures or visually indicates the amount of thread remaining on a bobbin in a sewing machine, such as mechanical counters, optical sensors, or electronic displays. These tools help operators monitor thread supply to avoid interruptions or breakage during stitching.
[D05B 59/04] Devices for changing the bobbin -- D05B 59/04 covers devices used to replace or exchange the bobbin in a sewing machine, typically mechanisms that automate the removal of a spent bobbin and insertion of a fresh one. These bobbin-changing tools are employed in domestic and industrial sewing equipment to improve efficiency and reduce downtime.
[D05B 61/00] Loop holders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Loop holders, loop spreaders and stitch-forming fingers, i.e. devices retaining the loop of the needle thread underneath the workpiece until the following operation.
[D05B 63/00] Devices associated with the loop-taker thread, e.g. for tensioning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices associated with the loop-taker thread, e.g. for tensioning, monitoring.
[D05B 63/02] Loop-taker thread take-up levers -- The CPC subclass D05B 63/02 deals with levers that take up the loop-taker thread in sewing machines, controlling its tension and positioning during stitching. These devices are part of the loop-taker mechanism that feeds thread to the needle, ensuring consistent stitch formation.
[D05B 63/04] Loop-taker thread guards -- Loop-taker thread guards are devices that protect the thread used for taking loops in sewing machines, especially where tensioning is applied. They prevent snagging, breakage or interference with the thread as it moves through the loop-taker mechanism. This subclass falls under textile sewing equipment for controlling thread motion.
[D05B 65/00] Devices for severing the needle or lower thread -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for severing the needle or lower thread, e.g. knife, heated tool, and for disposing of the severed thread end. Cutting devices inside or associated with the thread disposal device are covered by D05B65/06 .
[D05B 65/003] the thread being engaged manually against the cutting edge of a fixedly arranged knife -- The CPC subclass D05B 65/003 covers sewing devices in which the operator manually pulls the thread against a fixed knife to cut it. It describes mechanisms that engage the thread directly on a stationary cutting edge for severing after stitching, as in many lockstitch and overlock machines.
[D05B 65/006] with heated tools -- A D05B 65/006 device is a sewing tool that cuts the needle or lower thread using heat rather than a mechanical blade. The heated element melts or vaporises the material, providing a clean severance especially useful for synthetic fabrics or delicate seams.
[D05B 65/02] controlled by the sewing mechanisms -- D05B 65/02 covers devices that cut the needle or lower thread and are operated by the sewing machine's own mechanisms rather than by separate hand-held tools. It applies to automatic or semi-automatic severing functions integrated into sewing equipment, typically for finishing stitches.
[D05B 65/04] controlled by the workpieces -- D05B 65/04 pertains to sewing apparatuses in which the cutting of the needle or lower thread is actuated or regulated by the workpiece itself, such as fabric-mounted sensors or mechanical features that trigger the severing device. This sub-class falls under devices for severing the needle or lower thread within the broader textile sewing category.
[D05B 65/06] and for disposing of the severed thread end -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for severing the needle or lower thread and for disposing of the severed end, only if the cutting device is inside or associated with the thread disposal device, e.g. catching or wiping devices for the severed thread.
[D05B 67/00] Devices incorporated in sewing machines for lubricating, waxing, or colouring the threads -- D05B 67/00 concerns built-in devices for applying lubricants, waxes or colourants to thread during sewing, such as oilers, wax dispensers or dye applicators integrated into a sewing machine. This subcategory falls under TEXTILES → SEWING → SEWING.
[D05B 69/00] Driving-gear, control, lubricating, or cooling devices for sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Driving gear and control devices defined by the structure, e.g. mechanical, fluid, electrical, or by the application, e.g. changing speed, stopping drive, or by details.
[D05B 69/02] Mechanical drives -- D05B 69/02 covers the mechanical drives that transmit power to sewing machine components such as the needle, feed or stitch-forming mechanisms. It includes gears, belts, shafts, pulleys and motor-driven assemblies that provide the motion required for stitching operations in both domestic and industrial machines.
[D05B 69/04] Manual drives -- The D05B 69/04 class covers manually operated drive mechanisms for sewing machines, such as hand cranks, foot treadles or other manual actuators that transmit motion to the needle and feed system. It pertains to devices that provide the power source without electric or pneumatic assistance, fitting within the broader category of driving-gear, control, lubricating or cooling devices for sewing equipment.
[D05B 69/06] Pedal drives -- The CPC class D05B 69/06 covers pedal-actuated drive mechanisms for sewing machines, including the gears, control systems and lubrication or cooling components that transmit the operator's foot pressure into machine motion. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing, specifically for driving-gear and related devices that power a sewing machine.
[D05B 69/08] Fluid drives, e.g. pneumatic -- D05B 69/08 covers sewing-machine drive mechanisms that use fluid power, particularly pneumatic or hydraulic systems, to transmit motion to the needle, feed, or other moving parts. It includes designs for compressors, air-reservoirs, valves and control devices that generate, regulate or distribute the driving fluid within the machine.
[D05B 69/10] Electrical or electromagnetic drives -- The CPC subclass D05B 69/10 covers sewing-machine drive systems that are powered by electrical or electromagnetic means, such as electric motors, solenoids or other electromechanical actuation devices, used to rotate the needle bar, feed mechanism or related gear. It includes the electrical components and control circuitry that replace conventional mechanical drives in textile-sewing equipment.
[D05B 69/12] using rotary electric motors -- D05B 69/12 covers the use of rotary electric motors to drive, control, lubricate or cool sewing-machine mechanisms. It is classified under Textiles > Sewing > Sewing, focusing on motor-powered gear that operates the machine's movement and auxiliary functions.
[D05B 69/125] Arrangement of clutch-brake construction -- D05B 69/125 covers the specific layout of clutch-brake mechanisms within the drive, control, lubrication or cooling systems of sewing machines. It classifies how these components are arranged to engage or disengage the machine's motion, ensuring smooth operation and safety during stitching. This entry falls under the broader textile-sewing hierarchy for sewing-machine gear and devices.
[D05B 69/14] Devices for changing speed or for reversing direction of rotation -- The D05B 69/14 subclass covers mechanisms that alter the speed or reverse the direction of a sewing-machine drive. It includes variable-speed gearboxes, electronic speed controllers, reversing gears and similar devices that adjust rotation to control stitch formation. These devices are part of the broader group of driving-gear, control, lubricating or cooling systems for sewing machines.
[D05B 69/16] mechanical -- The D05B 69/16 subclass covers mechanical components and arrangements that drive, control, lubricate or cool sewing machines, including gears, shafts, levers, bearings and related devices that power or regulate the needle and feed mechanisms.
[D05B 69/18] electric -- D05B 69/18 is a CPC subclass under Textiles → Sewing → Sewing that covers electric components used as driving-gear, control, lubricating or cooling devices for sewing machines. It includes electrically powered mechanisms that regulate machine speed, provide vibration damping, dispense oil or coolant, and otherwise assist the machine's operation.
[D05B 69/20] Control devices responsive to the number of stitches made -- This subclass covers devices that monitor stitch count and adjust machine operation accordingly, such as automatic stop or speed control based on stitches made. They are used in sewing machines to improve precision, reduce waste, or automate functions tied to stitch count.
[D05B 69/22] Devices for stopping drive when sewing tools have reached a predetermined position -- A device that interrupts the drive of a sewing machine once the needle or other sewing tool arrives at a set position, preventing further motion. It is used to achieve precise stitching lengths or to stop operation automatically when a predetermined point is reached. This control mechanism belongs to the class of driving-gear, control, lubricating, or cooling devices for sewing machines.
[D05B 69/24] Applications of devices for indicating or ascertaining sewing-tool position -- The CPC subclass D05B 69/24 covers devices that indicate or determine the position of sewing tools, such as needles or presser feet, during operation. It includes visual or electronic indicators, sensors, and mechanisms that monitor tool location to improve stitch quality and machine control.
[D05B 69/26] with automatic means to reduce speed of drive, e.g. in one or more steps -- The CPC subclass D05B 69/26 pertains to sewing machines that incorporate automatic means for reducing the drive speed, such as stepped speed-control mechanisms. These devices adjust the stitching rate without manual intervention, improving stitch quality or adapting to different materials.
[D05B 69/28] Applications of servo devices for tool-positioning purposes -- D05B 69/28 designates inventions that use servo mechanisms to control the positioning of sewing-machine tools, such as needles or presser feet, to achieve precise movement during stitching operations. It falls under the textiles-sewing group for driving-gear, control, lubricating, or cooling devices for sewing machines.
[D05B 69/30] Details -- The CPC subclass D05B 69/30 covers specific details of the driving-gear, control, lubricating or cooling mechanisms used in sewing machines. It includes design elements such as gears, belts, lubrication systems, cooling fins and related components that affect the machine's operation. This subclass falls under the textiles-sewing hierarchy, focusing on the mechanical parts that power and regulate stitching.
[D05B 69/32] Vibration-minimising devices -- The CPC subclass D05B 69/32 addresses vibration-minimising devices for sewing machines, specifically mechanisms that dampen or counteract oscillations in the driving-gear, control, lubricating or cooling systems, thereby improving stitch quality and reducing wear.
[D05B 69/34] Hand-wheel clutches -- D05B 69/34 covers hand-wheel clutches that are part of the driving, control, lubricating or cooling mechanisms of sewing machines. These devices transmit torque from a hand-operated wheel to the machine's drive train, allowing the operator to start, stop or vary speed manually. They are classified under textiles, sewing, sewing equipment.
[D05B 69/36] Devices for stopping drive when abnormal conditions occur, e.g. thread breakage -- This CPC subclass defines mechanisms that interrupt the drive of a sewing machine when an abnormal condition such as a thread break occurs. The devices belong to the control and safety systems for sewing-machine gear, ensuring the machine stops to prevent damage or poor stitching.
[D05B 7/00] Linking machines, e.g. for joining knitted fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  linking machines, e.g. for joining knitted fabrics, e.g. overlocking machines.  **Glossary:** - Linking: Coupling or connecting by or as if by a link
[D05B 71/00] Lubricating or cooling devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lbricating or cooling devices, e.g. for loop-taker, needle, taking the workpiece into consideration.
[D05B 71/02] Loop-taker lubricating devices -- Loop-taker lubricating devices are mechanisms that apply a thin film of lubricant to the loop-taker component of a sewing machine's looper system. By reducing friction during the catching and releasing of the thread loop, they improve stitch formation, lower wear on the loop-taker and enhance overall sewing efficiency.
[D05B 71/04] Needle cooling devices -- D05B 71/04 covers devices designed to cool or lubricate sewing needles during textile work, reducing heat buildup and friction that can degrade needle performance. Such needle cooling devices are used in sewing machines and other equipment to improve stitch quality and extend needle life.
[D05B 73/00] Casings, supports, covers, or accessories for sewing machines -- The CPC subclass D05B 73/00 pertains to casings, supports, covers and other accessories specifically made for sewing machines, such as protective housings, mounting brackets, decorative shields and functional add-ons that aid operation or safeguard the equipment.
[D05B 73/005] Doors or covers for accessing inner parts of the machine; Security devices therefor -- This CPC subgroup covers components that protect the interior of a sewing machine, including doors, covers, and associated locking or security mechanisms that prevent unauthorized access to internal parts. It is used for classifying inventions related to protective enclosures and safety features on sewing machines.
[D05B 73/02] Upper casings -- D05B 73/02 covers upper casings, the protective or decorative housings that surround the top part of a sewing machine, including covers, supports and accessories that enclose the working area, guide mechanisms or thread path above the needle plate. These elements help shield components, reduce noise and improve ergonomics.
[D05B 73/04] Lower casings -- D05B 73/04 refers to lower casings for sewing machines, the components that enclose and protect the underside of the machine, providing structural support and covering moving parts. These casings are accessories used in textile and sewing equipment to improve durability and safety.
[D05B 73/06] for free-arm sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fee-arm sewing machines, i.e. machines having an arm holding bobbin, shuttle and feed dogs.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for converting free-arm sewing machines into flat-bed machines -> D05B73/10 .  **Glossary:** - A free-arm sewing machine: has a shape allowing the fabric to slip around the arm.
[D05B 73/08] for column-type sewing machines -- D05B 73/08 refers to accessories for column-type sewing machines, including casings, supports, covers and other components that hold or protect the machine and aid fabric handling. This subclass falls under the textiles-sewing hierarchy and specifically addresses equipment designed for vertical, column-style industrial sewing units.
[D05B 73/10] Devices for converting free-arm sewing machines into flat-bed machines -- The D05B 73/10 subclass covers mechanical adapters that transform a free-arm sewing machine into a flat-bed configuration. These devices provide a rigid work surface and mounting points, allowing the machine to handle larger or flatter fabrics and accessories that would otherwise be impractical on a free-arm setup.
[D05B 73/12] Slides -- Slides are small, movable components attached to sewing-machine accessories that guide, adjust or support the motion of fabric, needles or other parts during stitching. They help control tension, positioning or alignment, improving stitch quality and machine handling in various sewing operations.
[D05B 75/00] Frames, stands, tables, or other furniture adapted to carry sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  frames, stands, tables, or the like adapted to carry sewing machines, e.g. for drop-head sewing machines, with noise-suppressing devices, and arrangements for mounting sewing-machine casings to frames, stands or tables.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Furniture aspects of frames, stands, tables or the like adapted to carry sewing machines -> A47B - Chairs -> A47C
[D05B 75/02] for drop-head sewing machines -- **Glossary:** - A drop-head sewing machine: machine e.g. foldable, with multi-position adjustment.
[D05B 75/04] with noise-suppressing devices -- D05B 75/04 covers frames, stands, tables or other furniture designed to hold sewing machines that incorporate noise-suppressing features, such as acoustic insulation, dampening panels or vibration-absorbing components, to reduce the sound generated during operation.
[D05B 75/06] Arrangements, e.g. hinges, for mounting sewing-machine casings to frames, stands, or tables -- The D05B 75/06 subclass covers mechanical arrangements, such as hinges or other fastening devices, used to attach sewing-machine casings to supporting structures like frames, stands or tables. It pertains to the design of mounting systems that allow the machine to be securely positioned while permitting convenient removal or adjustment.
[D05B 77/00] Covers, or portable enclosures, for sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Covers, or portable enclosures, for sewing machines, e.g. case cover, bed extension table, shield assembly.
[D05B 79/00] Incorporations or adaptations of lighting equipment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Incorporations or adaptations of lighting equipment in sewing machines, e.g. lights, magnifying glasses.
[D05B 81/00] Sewing machines incorporating devices serving purposes other than sewing, e.g. for blowing air, for grinding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  sewing machines incorporating devices serving purposes other than sewing, e.g. for blowing air, for grinding, for discharging material, vacuum boxes, brushes.
[D05B 83/00] Guards or like devices for preventing injury to operator -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Guards or like devices for preventing injury to operator, e.g. preventing fingers to be pierced by the needle.
[D05B 85/00] Needles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needles, e.g. hooked needles, curved needles, flexible needles, hollow needles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Surgical needles, e.g. for sewing body parts -> A61B17/06
[D05B 85/003] with two tips and an eye in the middle -- A sewing needle that has sharp points at both ends and a centrally positioned eye for the thread, allowing stitching to be done in either direction without re-inserting the needle. This design is used for hand-sewing applications where rapid, reversible stitching is needed.
[D05B 85/006] Hooked needles -- Hooked needles are hand tools with a curved or hooked tip used to pull yarn or thread through loops in textile work such as crochet and hand knitting. They belong to the sewing subclass dealing with needles that have a hook shape for forming stitches.
[D05B 85/02] with slotted eyes -- This CPC subclass D05B 85/02 covers sewing needles whose eye is formed as a slot rather than a round hole, allowing easier thread insertion or guiding. It includes designs where the slotted eye improves feeding of thread in hand or machine sewing.
[D05B 85/04] Spring or bearded needles -- The CPC subclass D05B 85/04 refers to sewing needles that incorporate a spring mechanism or a bearded tip to aid fabric penetration and stitch formation. These needles are designed to flex or retain thread more effectively during sewing operations, improving control and reducing breakage.
[D05B 85/06] Curved needles -- D05B 85/06 - Curved needles are sewing needles whose shaft is bent to form a curvature, allowing stitching in confined or hard-to-reach areas such as leather, denim or upholstery. The subclass covers the shape, tip geometry and specific applications of these curved tools within textile and garment manufacturing.
[D05B 85/08] Flexible needles -- D05B 85/08 pertains to flexible needles used in textile and sewing processes, designed to bend or curve for stitching in confined or difficult-to-reach areas, allowing better handling of specialty fabrics and reducing damage during sewing.
[D05B 85/10] Hollow needles -- Hollow needles are sewing tools featuring an internal channel that permits thread, yarn or other filament to be drawn through the needle during stitching. They are used in textile processes such as embroidery, tufting and specialized stitching where material must be fed from the needle tip into the workpiece.
[D05B 85/12] Coated needles -- Coated needles are sewing needles whose shank, tip or other surfaces are treated with a layer of material such as polymer, metal, wax or lubricants to improve durability, reduce friction, or give specific functional properties during stitching. They are used in textile manufacturing where high speed or delicate fabrics require reduced wear.
[D05B 85/14] Latch needles -- Latch needles are specialized sewing needles with a bent shank and a catch mechanism that grips fabric or metal loops, allowing the needle to form or engage a latch or hook during stitching. They are used in textile manufacturing for attaching fasteners and securing seams where a retractable lock is needed.
[D05B 87/00] Needle- threading devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Needle- or looper-threading devices, i.e. devices for positioning the thread in the needle eye or in the looper, e.g. with mechanical means.
[D05B 87/02] with mechanical means for moving thread through needle eye -- D05B 87/02 covers needle-threading devices that employ mechanical components to move thread through the eye of a needle. Examples include hand-held or machine-integrated threaders, automatic threading mechanisms and devices that guide or pull the thread into the needle using levers, rollers or other moving parts. These tools facilitate needle preparation in textile and sewing applications.
[D05B 87/04] with optical devices to assist threading -- The subclass D05B 87/04 covers needle-threading devices that incorporate optical aids such as magnifying lenses, light sources, cameras or visual displays to help the user see the needle eye and guide the thread during sewing. It is a narrow element of the sewing-textiles hierarchy.
[D05B 89/00] Seam-ripping devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Seam ripping devices, e.g. for ripping out or opening the seam.
[D05B 9/00] Hem-stitch sewing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hem-stitch machines, i.e. machines having one or two needles, and a piercer.  **Glossary:** - Hem: Border of a cloth article doubled back and stitched down.
[D05B 91/00] Hand sewing -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Needles -> D05B85/00 - Needle threading devices -> D05B87/00 - Appliances or methods for making clothes, e.g. for dress making, for tailoring (e.g. boxes for needles) -> A41H
[D05B 91/02] Bodkins -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bodkins, i.e. small pointed instrument used for piercing holes in cloth or instrument with a knobbed point and a large eye for drawing tape or cord through a hem.
[D05B 91/04] Thimbles -- The D05B 91/04 subclass covers thimbles, protective devices worn on the finger during hand sewing to prevent puncture injuries from needles. It is part of the textile hand-sewing tools category in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[D05B 91/06] Work holders or supports -- D05B 91/06 pertains to work holders or supports used in hand-sewing operations, such as thimbles, clamps, frames or similar devices that keep fabric or other materials in position while being stitched, facilitating accurate and efficient manual sewing.
[D05B 91/08] Mushrooms
[D05B 91/10] Hoops or frames -- D05B 91/10 covers the use of hoops, frames or other supporting structures that hold fabric taut during hand sewing operations. These devices, such as embroidery hoops, quilting frames, and tailoring frames, enable precise stitching and prevent fabric distortion.
[D05B 91/12] Tool receptacles -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Boxes for needles or pins -> A41H19/00
[D05B 91/14] Thread-spool pins -- D05B 91/14 relates to pins designed to hold thread spools during hand-sewing operations. These pins secure the spool in place on a needle or holder, facilitating smooth feeding of thread while stitching. The classification falls under the textile and hand-sewing subgroup of the CPC system.
[D05B 91/16] Thread-spool receptacles -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tool receptacles for hand sewing -> D05B91/12 .
[D05B 93/00] Stitches -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stitches and stitch seams, e.g. combined with machine sewing, knitting, strengthened by auxiliary elements.
[D05B 93/02] strengthened by auxiliary elements, e.g. rivets, staples -- This subclass deals with ways of reinforcing stitched textile constructions by adding auxiliary fastening elements such as rivets, staples or comparable devices, which increase the mechanical strength of the seam or stitch line.
[D05B 95/00] Preparatory or finishing operations in connection with hand sewing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparatory or finishing operations in connection with hand sewing, e.g. softening material prior to sewing, flattening, pounding, or pressing stitched seams.
[D05B 95/04] Softening material prior to sewing -- D05B 95/04 covers methods for softening fabrics or other materials before hand-sewing operations. The technique is applied as a preparatory or finishing step to improve pliability and stitch quality. It falls under the textile sewing subclass dealing with preparatory or finishing operations.
[D05B 95/06] Flattening, pounding, or pressing stitched seams -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Creasing presses in shoemaking -> A43D8/44
[D05B 97/00] Hand sewing processes or apparatus for special work or not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand sewing processes or apparatus for special work or not otherwise provided for, e.g. hand-guided apparatus, for darning, using special threads, for attaching buttons or fasteners, for attaching patches.
[D05B 97/02] Hand-guided apparatus -- D05B 97/02 relates to hand-guided apparatus used in hand-sewing operations that are specialised or not otherwise covered, such as devices that help the operator guide a needle, thread or fabric, providing support, alignment or stabilisation while the tool is held in the hand. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for textiles - sewing - hand sewing processes or apparatus.
[D05B 97/04] for darning -- This classification covers hand-sewing processes or apparatus specifically designed for darning, the repair of holes or worn spots in fabric by interlacing yarn. It falls under textiles → sewing → hand-sewing processes or apparatus for special work not otherwise provided for, coded D05B 97/04.
[D05B 97/06] using apparatus with latch or bearded needles -- The entry D05B 97/06 relates to hand-sewing operations that employ devices fitted with a latch or bearded needle to catch and hold a thread loop, enabling special stitching or loop-forming techniques. It falls within the broader group of hand-sewing processes or apparatus for special work not otherwise provided for.
[D05B 97/08] using special threads or wires -- The D05B 97/08 class covers hand-sewing processes or devices that employ special threads or wires, such as metallic, elastic, conductive or otherwise non-standard fibers, for purposes not addressed by ordinary sewing methods. It includes techniques and apparatus specifically adapted to handle these unique materials.
[D05B 97/10] for attaching buttons or fasteners -- D05B 97/10 refers to hand-sewing processes or apparatus specifically intended for attaching buttons or other fasteners to textile materials, covering specialized needles, stitches, or tools used to secure closures where ordinary sewing methods are insufficient.
[D05B 97/12] for attaching patches or like small pieces of fabric -- D05B 97/12 covers hand-sewing techniques or tools used to affix patches or other small fabric sections to a larger textile, addressing special or otherwise unlisted applications. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, sewing, and hand-sewing processes.
[D05C] EMBROIDERING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The embroidering and tufting machines, the embroidered or tufted products, and the base fabrics and inserts.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of garments or of their making -> A41D27/00  **Glossary:** - Embroidering: is forming decorative designs with hand or machine needleworks - Tufting: is projecting yarns drawn through a fabric or making up a fabric so as to produce a surface of raised loops or cut pile
[D05C 1/00] Apparatus, devices, or tools for hand embroidering -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Decorating textiles by transferring fibres to the textile -> D06Q1/14
[D05C 1/02] Work frames -- Work frames are devices that secure fabric in a stretched, flat position to facilitate hand embroidery, enabling accurate stitch placement and tension control. They include embroidery hoops, frames and similar supports used by artisans to hold cloth taut while stitching.
[D05C 1/04] circular -- The CPC subclass D05C 1/04 circular covers hand-embroidery tools that are circular in shape, such as hoops, frames or rings used to keep fabric taut while stitching. These devices belong to the embroidery apparatus hierarchy under textiles-sewing-embroidery.
[D05C 1/06] Needles specially adapted for hand embroidering -- This subclass covers needles that are specifically designed for hand embroidery, featuring modifications such as elongated shafts, specialized eye shapes, or decorative points to facilitate stitching. The needles improve control and penetration in fabric during detailed embroidery work.
[D05C 1/065] Holders for needles or threads -- **Glossary:** - Holders: are means for supporting, storing and organizing needles and threads.
[D05C 1/08] Patterns for hand embroidering -- The D05C 1/08 entry covers patterns, templates, charts or designs that are used as guides for creating hand-embroidered motifs on textiles, providing stitch-by-stitch instructions for artisans.
[D05C 11/00] Devices for guiding, feeding, handling, or treating the threads in embroidering machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices acting directly or indirectly on the thread material used for embroidery.
[D05C 11/02] Machine needles -- D05C 11/02 pertains to machine needles used in devices that guide, feed, handle or treat threads in embroidery machines. The classification covers needles of various shapes and sizes designed for integration into stitching equipment to produce stitches on diverse fabrics and thread types.
[D05C 11/04] Arrangements for fastening or inserting in bars or carriers -- D05C 11/04 covers arrangements that secure or insert components such as bars or carriers within embroidery machines, enabling proper guidance and handling of threads. The classification pertains to devices that fasten or mount these elements to ensure accurate stitching and efficient operation.
[D05C 11/06] Needle-driving or control mechanisms -- The D05C 11/06 subclass relates to the mechanisms that drive or control the needle in embroidery machines, including mechanical, pneumatic, hydraulic or electronic actuators, cams, cranks and timing devices that position, move or synchronize the needle during stitching. It falls under devices for guiding, feeding, handling or treating threads in embroidery equipment.
[D05C 11/08] Thread-tensioning arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thread-tensioning arrangements in embroidering machines.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Thread-tensioning arrangements incorporated inside the shuttle -> D05C11/18
[D05C 11/10] Guides, e.g. resilient -- D05C 11/10 relates to resilient guides used in embroidering machines to control the position of thread during stitching. These devices are designed to flex or deform under tension, helping to maintain smooth thread flow and prevent breakage while the fabric is embroidered.
[D05C 11/12] Rollers -- Rollers are cylindrical components used in embroidery machines to guide, feed, and treat the thread during stitching. They rotate to control thread tension and direction, ensuring smooth movement through the needle zone and improving stitch consistency.
[D05C 11/14] Stop motions responsive to thread tension or breakage -- Devices that stop an embroidery machine's motion when thread tension changes or a thread breaks, preventing errors, damage or missed stitches. These mechanisms monitor thread behavior and automatically halt feed or stitching actions to maintain quality and protect the equipment.
[D05C 11/16] Arrangements for repeating thread patterns or for changing threads -- This subclass covers mechanisms that automate the selection or repetition of thread patterns and the switching of threads during embroidery, such as pattern repeaters, thread selectors and colour-change devices built into the machine.
[D05C 11/18] Shuttles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shuttles, shuttle holders, shuttle driving arrangements, and systems for the preparation of the thread and the bobbin.
[D05C 11/20] Arrangements for cutting the needle or lower threads -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shearing embroidery, e.g. cutting loose threads -> D06C13/04 - Removing floats -> D06C13/06
[D05C 11/22] Arrangements for cutting-out broken threads -- The subclass covers mechanisms incorporated in embroidery machines that detect and remove broken threads, including cutting devices, sensors and automatic systems that isolate and eliminate the defective yarns to maintain stitch quality.
[D05C 11/24] incorporating devices for dyeing or impregnating the threads -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tufting by inserting loops of different colour -> D05C15/34 .
[D05C 13/00] Auxiliary devices incorporated in embroidering machines, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary devices incorporated in embroidering machines, not otherwise provided for, and ancillary apparatus for use with embroidering machines e.g. apparatuses for framing fabric in embroidery hoops, shuttle inserters, holding devices, apparatuses for needle threading, apparatuses for projecting sample designs.
[D05C 13/02] Counting, measuring, indicating, warning, or safety devices -- The D05C 13/02 subclass defines auxiliary devices built into embroidery machines that count stitches or cycles, measure operation parameters, display or signal information, issue warnings, or provide safety functions to protect the machine or operator. It lies under the hierarchy of textiles → sewing → embroidery → auxiliary devices for embroidering machines.
[D05C 13/04] Lubricating devices -- It covers lubricating mechanisms used in embroidery machines to reduce friction and keep needles, thread guides and other moving parts operating smoothly, typically integrated as auxiliary devices that are not otherwise specified.
[D05C 13/06] Apparatus for filling or threading shuttles -- The CPC subclass D05C 13/06 pertains to auxiliary mechanisms that load or guide thread into the shuttles of embroidery machines, providing automated or assisted filling and threading functions not covered elsewhere. These devices are designed to increase efficiency and reduce manual handling in commercial embroidery equipment.
[D05C 15/00] Making pile fabrics or articles having similar surface features by inserting loops into a base material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines and methods for insertion of loops, which can be cut, into a base material.
[D05C 15/02] Rooting of hair in doll heads or wigs -- This subclass deals with methods of securing hair fibers in doll heads or wigs by inserting the hair loops into a base material to create a rooted, pile-like surface, similar to techniques used for making textured fabrics. It is applied when the hair is anchored directly in the head or wig rather than being attached by other means.
[D05C 15/04] Tufting -- **Glossary:** - Tufting: is forming projections of yarns drawn through a fabric so as to produce a surface of raised loops or cut pile
[D05C 15/06] Hand tufting needles -- Hand tufting needles are tools used to create pile fabrics by inserting loops into a base material, commonly employed in embroidery and other textile work. They enable artisans to produce textured surfaces such as carpets or decorative fabrics through manual tufting.
[D05C 15/08] Tufting machines -- D05C 15/08 pertains to tufting machines that form pile fabrics or similar loop-derived surface structures by mechanically inserting yarn loops into a backing material, enabling the production of carpets, rugs and other loop-based textiles.
[D05C 15/10] operating with a plurality of needles, e.g. in one row -- The D05C 15/10 subclass covers embroidery processes that employ several needles at once, such as arranging them in a single row, to form pile fabrics or similar looped-surface articles. It focuses on the mechanical operation of multiple needle feeds rather than single-needle stitching, enabling efficient creation of textured textile surfaces.
[D05C 15/12] in more than one row -- This subclass covers techniques for creating pile or looped surface textures on fabric where the loops are arranged in multiple parallel rows rather than a single line. Methods may include stitching, weaving or knitting that produces rows of inserted loops over a base material.
[D05C 15/14] Arrangements or devices for holding or feeding the base material -- D05C 15/14 covers arrangements or devices that hold or feed the base material during the production of pile fabrics or similar surfaces where loops are inserted into a substrate, such as apparatus for guiding, advancing, or tensioning the fabric while embroidery or pile-forming operations are performed.
[D05C 15/145] Needle plates -- Needle plates are metal or plastic plates attached to industrial embroidery or pile-producing machines, providing a smooth surface that supports the fabric while the needle repeatedly inserts loops to form pile or decorative stitches. They are designed with precise openings and wear-resistant surfaces to ensure consistent loop formation and to protect the underlying mechanisms.
[D05C 15/16] Arrangements or devices for manipulating threads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements or devices for manipulating threads, e.g. yarn adjusters to control length in proximity of the needle.
