[D05C 15/18] Thread feeding or tensioning arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thread feeding or tensioning arrangements, e.g. control of thread feeding.
[D05C 15/20] Arrangements or devices, e.g. needles, for inserting loops -- D05C 15/20 pertains to arrangements or devices-such as needles-employed to insert loops into a base material, creating pile fabrics or articles with similar textured surfaces, a process commonly used in embroidery. It is a subcategory within textiles, sewing, and embroidery for loop-forming techniques.
[D05C 15/22] Loop-catching arrangements, e.g. loopers -- The CPC subclass D05C 15/22 covers loop-catching mechanisms, such as loopers, used in the production of pile fabrics or similar items where loops are inserted into a base material to create a textured surface. This subclass falls under textiles → sewing → embroidery, specifically addressing devices that capture and manipulate loops during the manufacturing process.
[D05C 15/24] Loop cutters -- Loop cutters are tools used to trim or remove the loops that form the pile in embroidered or tufted textiles, allowing adjustment of pile height or removal of defects. They are employed in the manufacturing of pile fabrics where loops are inserted into a base material to create a textured surface.
[D05C 15/26] with provision for producing patterns -- D05C 15/26 covers processes for forming pile or looped fabrics where the technique includes features that generate patterned designs, for example by varying loop size, placement or colour during tufting or embroidery. The provision for patterns may involve programmed needle movements, multiple yarn feeds or selective loop formation to create decorative motifs on a base material.
[D05C 15/28] by moving the base material laterally -- The D05C 15/28 subclass concerns embroidery techniques that form pile-type surfaces by shifting the base material sideways while inserting loops. The lateral movement of the fabric relative to the needle or hook creates looped structures that give the finished article a textured, raised appearance.
[D05C 15/30] by moving the tufting tools laterally -- This subclass covers methods of producing pile or looped fabrics in which the tufting tools are guided side-to-side across the base material, forming loops that create the pile surface. The lateral motion of the tool is the defining feature of the process.
[D05C 15/32] by altering the loop length -- This CPC subclass covers methods for producing pile fabrics or similar loop-based textile surfaces where the length of the inserted loops is deliberately varied to modify texture, appearance or functional properties. It pertains to embroidery-type processes that create the pile effect by altering loop length during stitching.
[D05C 15/34] by inserting loops of different nature or colour -- This subclass covers methods of producing pile fabrics or similar articles by inserting loops that differ in material or colour from the base or from each other, creating decorative or functional contrast. It includes techniques that combine yarns of varying composition or dye to achieve multicoloured or textured surfaces.
[D05C 15/36] by selective cutting of loops -- This subclass concerns techniques for creating pile or loop-based fabrics where the loops are selectively cut after being formed, producing cut-pile or patterned surface textures. It applies to textile processes such as knitting or embroidery that generate loops on a base material and then trim specific loops to achieve the desired decorative effect.
[D05C 15/38] by passing thread material in zig-zag manner through spaced layers of base material and subsequently cutting along a central plane -- This subclass deals with the production of pile or looped surface fabrics by feeding a thread in a zig-zag pattern through spaced layers of a base material, then cutting the assembly along a central plane to reveal loops on both sides. The resulting fabric can be used for decorative or functional purposes such as carpets, upholstery, or embossed textiles.
[D05C 17/00] Embroidered or tufted products -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Products obtained by means of machines and methods of D05C1/00 - D05C15/00 , the base fabrics and the inserts adapted for embroidered work.
[D05C 17/02] Tufted products -- The CPC code D05C 17/02 covers tufted products, which are textile articles made by inserting loops of yarn or fibers into a backing material to create a raised surface, commonly used for carpets, rugs and similar items. These items fall under embroidered or tufted products in the hierarchy that proceeds from textiles to sewing to embroidery.
[D05C 17/023] characterised by the base fabric -- The CPC subclass D05C 17/023 covers embroidered or tufted articles where the defining feature is the type of base fabric employed, such as its material, construction or textile structure, which influences the overall appearance and functional properties of the finished product.
[D05C 17/026] characterised by the tufted pile surface -- The CPC subclass D05C 17/026 covers embroidered or tufted textile items whose surface is defined by a pile of tufts that stand up from the base fabric. It includes products in which the tufted pile is a principal decorative or functional feature, such as plush or velour-like surfaces created by embroidery or tufting processes.
[D05C 3/00] Embroidering machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Multi-needles and multi-head embroidery machines.
[D05C 3/02] with vertical needles -- In the CPC, class D05C covers machines for embroidery. Subgroup 3/02 denotes machines where the needles move vertically, i.e. the needle is oriented up-down rather than horizontal, allowing the fabric to stay stationary while the needle penetrates from above. This arrangement is typical for automatic embroidery machines that form stitches by vertical needle motion.
[D05C 3/04] with horizontal needles -- D05C 3/04 designates embroidering machines that employ horizontal needles, meaning the needles move parallel to the work surface rather than vertically. This arrangement is typical of industrial multi-needle devices that produce complex stitch patterns on fabric. It is a sub-category of textiles → sewing → embroidery → embroidering machines.
[D05C 5/00] Embroidering machines with arrangements for automatic control of a series of individual steps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All kinds of embroidery machines with arrangements for automatic control of a series of individual steps.
[D05C 5/02] by electrical or magnetic control devices -- This subclass covers embroidery machines whose series of stitching steps are governed by electrical or magnetic control devices, such as electronic circuits, solenoids or magnetic sensors. It includes systems that automate needle movement, thread tension and pattern selection through electronic or magnetic means, enabling fully automatic control of the embroidery process.
[D05C 5/04] by input of recorded information, e.g. on perforated tape -- D05C 5/04 pertains to embroidering machines that automatically execute a series of steps by reading pre-recorded data, for example from perforated tape, allowing the pattern to be reproduced without manual input. This sub-class falls under textiles → sewing → embroidering and addresses automated control of individual embroidery operations.
[D05C 5/06] with means for recording the information -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers embroidery machines that include devices for recording pattern information, such as electronic memory, punched tape or other storage media, enabling the machine to automatically reproduce a series of stitches. The recorded data is used to control individual steps of the embroidery process without manual re-programming.
[D05C 7/00] Special-purpose or automatic embroidering machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The embroidering machines where specific applications, e.g. for attaching decorative elements or for boring, can be carried out.
[D05C 7/02] with accessories for peculiar kinds of embroidery -- D05C 7/02 refers to special-purpose or automatic embroidery machines equipped with accessories for particular kinds of embroidery, such as specialized needles, bobbins, tension devices or other tools that enable distinctive stitch patterns or effects.
[D05C 7/04] for boring or jogging -- **Glossary:** - Boring: is making a hole in the product - Jogging: is giving a slight push to a product in order to deform the product surface (e.g. embossing)
[D05C 7/06] for embroidering festoons -- **Glossary:** - Festoons: are decorative chains or strips hanging between two points.
[D05C 7/08] for attaching cords, tapes, bands, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for attaching cords, tapes, bands and sequins.
[D05C 7/10] for separating and burning-off parts of the base fabric -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Producing embroidered textiles by chemical means and transferring embroidered products to textiles -> D06Q1/005
[D05C 7/12] for making cotton buttons -- The CPC subclass D05C 7/12 covers special-purpose or automatic embroidery machines specifically adapted to form cotton buttons. It includes mechanisms, tooling and processes that stitch, shape and finish cotton material into button forms during the embroidery operation. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Textiles → Sewing → Embroidery.
[D05C 9/00] Appliances for holding or feeding the base fabric in embroidering machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Work holders or carriers, feeding arrangements, and systems for holding, stretching, suspending and balancing therefor.
[D05C 9/02] in machines with vertical needles -- The D05C 9/02 subclass covers devices that hold or feed the base fabric in embroidery machines that use vertical needles, such as clamps, rollers, or feeding mechanisms designed to position the material during stitching. These appliances ensure precise fabric movement and tension while the needle penetrates vertically, facilitating consistent embroidery patterns.
[D05C 9/04] Work holders, e.g. frames -- D05C 9/04 covers work-holding devices such as frames that secure or feed the base fabric in embroidery machines. These appliances keep the material taut and correctly positioned during stitching, facilitating precise needle movement and pattern formation.
[D05C 9/06] Feeding arrangements therefor, e.g. influenced by patterns, operated by pantographs -- The D05C 9/06 subclass details devices that control how the base fabric is fed through an embroidery machine, including mechanisms that adjust feeding according to the embroidered pattern and systems driven by pantographs. These arrangements ensure precise fabric movement for accurate stitch placement.
[D05C 9/08] in machines with horizontal needles -- The subclass D05C 9/08 covers appliances in embroidery machines that use horizontal needles, specifically mechanisms for holding or feeding the base fabric during stitching. It is positioned under textiles, sewing, embroidering, and appliances for fabric handling.
[D05C 9/10] Work holders or carriers -- D05C 9/10 pertains to devices that hold or carry the base fabric while it is being embroidered, such as clamps, frames or rollers that secure the material in embroidery machines. These work holders ensure proper positioning and feeding of the fabric during stitching.
[D05C 9/12] Holding or stretching arrangements for the base fabric in embroidery work holders -- Appliances for holding or stretching the base fabric in embroidery machines, such as clamps, frames, rollers or tensioning devices, keep the material taut and correctly positioned while stitching. These arrangements ensure precise needle placement and consistent stitch quality during embroidery work.
[D05C 9/14] Suspension or balancing arrangements therefor -- D05C 9/14 covers suspension or balancing devices used in embroidery machines to hold or feed the base fabric. These arrangements keep the material under proper tension and alignment during stitching, preventing sagging or distortion.
[D05C 9/16] Feeding arrangements therefor -- Appliance that holds, positions or transports the base fabric into an embroidering machine, ensuring proper tension and alignment during stitching. It includes clamps, rollers, guides or other mechanisms that feed the material to the needle system. This sub-class falls under textiles → sewing → embroidery.
[D05C 9/18] Movement of the base fabric in one of the two directions controlled by embroidering needles -- This subclass covers mechanisms that advance the backing fabric in a single direction under the control of the embroidery needles, allowing coordinated stitch placement. It pertains to devices for holding or feeding the base fabric in embroidery machines, classified under D05C 9/18.
[D05C 9/20] Movement of the base fabric controlled by the presser feet -- The D05C 9/20 entry covers mechanisms that move the base fabric in embroidery machines by means of presser feet, including any appliances that hold or feed the fabric while it is being embroidered. This subclass falls under textiles → sewing → embroidering.
[D05C 9/22] Adjusting or registering devices for the base fabric, e.g. for alignment with respect to the needles -- The D05C 9/22 subclass covers devices that adjust or register the base fabric in embroidery machines, ensuring precise alignment of the cloth with respect to the needles. These mechanisms may include guides, clamps, rollers or positioning systems that control the fabric's tension, angle or lateral position during stitching.
[D05D] INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES -- D05D is the CPC index that groups the various sewing-related sub-classes, offering a structured referencing scheme for patents covering textile stitching techniques and equipment. The entry serves as a navigational tool that helps users locate specific innovations within the broader textiles domain.
[D05D 2203/00] Selection of machines, accessories or parts of the same kind -- D05D 2203/00 pertains to the selection of machines, accessories or parts of the same kind used in textile and sewing operations. It includes devices or methods that identify, sort or choose identical or similar equipment for production lines, maintenance or inventory control. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy TEXTILES - SEWING and groups subclasses related to indexing schemes.
[D05D 2205/00] Interface between the operator and the machine -- The CPC subclass D05D 2205/00 relates to the interface between the operator and textile or sewing machines, covering control panels, displays, input devices and ergonomic features that enable human control of the equipment. It includes designs for buttons, touch screens, foot pedals, safety guards and other elements that facilitate user interaction with the machine.
[D05D 2205/02] Operator to the machine -- The D05D 2205/02 subclass covers the interface between the operator and a sewing machine, including controls, handles, pedals, foot actions, and other means by which the user directs machine operation. It addresses ergonomic and functional aspects of the operator's interaction with the equipment.
[D05D 2205/04] Pedals -- D05D 2205/04 covers pedals that serve as the interface between the operator and a sewing or textile machine, allowing the user to control machine functions such as speed or stitch formation by foot pressure. It falls under the textiles-sewing hierarchy for indexing operator-machine control mechanisms.
[D05D 2205/06] Handles -- D05D 2205/06, Handles, covers the design and construction of handles attached to sewing machine components that enable the operator to grip, guide, or manipulate the machine during operation. It falls under the textiles - sewing - indexing scheme associated with subclasses, concerning the interface between the operator and the machine.
[D05D 2205/08] Buttons, e.g. for pattern selection -- D05D 2205/08 classifies sewing-machine controls that use buttons to let the operator choose stitch patterns or other functions. The code falls under textiles, sewing, indexing schemes associated with subclasses, specifically the interface between the operator and the machine. It covers button arrangements that trigger pattern selection.
[D05D 2205/085] combined with a display arrangement, e.g. touch sensitive control panel -- The code D05D 2205/085 designates sewing machines that incorporate a display arrangement, for example a touch-sensitive control panel, as part of the operator-machine interface. Such devices combine the stitching mechanism with visual feedback and selectable functions through a screen or touch interface, facilitating setup, monitoring and adjustment of sewing parameters.
[D05D 2205/12] Machine to the operator -- The CPC subclass D05D 2205/12 covers machines that form the interface between the operator and a sewing apparatus, such as control panels, displays, ergonomic handles and other devices that enable the user to command or monitor the textile-sewing machine. It pertains to the interaction layer within textile-sewing equipment.
[D05D 2205/14] acoustic -- The D05D 2205/14 subclass pertains to acoustic aspects of the operator-machine interface in sewing equipment, covering sound generation, transmission and control for user feedback or noise reduction. It is classified under textiles, sewing.
[D05D 2205/16] Display arrangements -- D05D 2205/16 covers display arrangements on sewing machines that present operational information to the user, such as stitch patterns, settings, or status indicators, forming part of the operator-machine interface within textile sewing equipment.
[D05D 2205/18] Screens -- The CPC subclass D05D 2205/18 covers screens that form the interface between the operator and a sewing machine, such as protective or viewing screens that shield the work area while allowing the user to monitor stitching. It pertains to textile equipment where the screen facilitates control, safety or visibility during sewing operations.
[D05D 2205/32] Safety devices -- This subclass covers safety devices used on sewing machines to protect the operator, such as guards, emergency stops, and protective covers, ensuring a safe interaction between the user and the machine during textile stitching operations.
[D05D 2207/00] Use of special elements -- The CPC subclass D05D 2207/00 relates to sewing techniques that incorporate special elements such as elastic members, reinforcing strips, heating or cooling components, or other functional inserts into the textile during or after stitching. It covers methods, apparatuses and products where these elements are used to impart additional mechanical, thermal or functional properties to the finished fabric.
[D05D 2207/02] Pneumatic or hydraulic devices -- D05D 2207/02 covers sewing or stitching equipment that is powered or actuated by air or fluid pressure, including pneumatic needles, hydraulic feed devices, and other mechanisms that use compressed gas or liquid to move or control the stitching process.
[D05D 2207/04] Suction or blowing devices -- D05D 2207/04 covers sewing apparatus that incorporate suction or blowing mechanisms to move, position or hold material during stitching, such as vacuum feed tables or air-jet fabric transport devices, and is classified under Textiles → Sewing → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses → Use of special elements.
[D05D 2207/05] Magnetic devices -- The D05D 2207/05 subclass covers magnetic devices employed in sewing and textile work, including magnetic needles, fastening tools, and guidance systems that use magnetic forces. It is part of the D05D group for sewing equipment, under the 2207 series that addresses the use of special elements in textile operations.
[D05D 2207/06] Permanent magnets -- D05D 2207/06 covers the use of permanent magnets as functional components in textile articles or sewing processes, including magnetic fasteners, closures and other elements where a permanent magnet provides a holding or aligning function in garments or other fabric products.
[D05D 2209/00] Use of special materials -- D05D 2209/00 relates to sewing methods that utilise special materials such as adhesives, bonding agents, reinforced fibres or other non-thread substances to join textiles. It includes the preparation and application of these materials during the sewing process.
[D05D 2209/02] transparent -- This CPC subclass covers sewing applications involving transparent materials such as clear film, lace or mesh. It includes methods and devices for handling, joining, or finishing fabrics that allow light to pass through. The code is used to classify inventions related to the use of transparency in textile products.
[D05D 2209/04] with high friction -- D05D 2209/04 covers sewing articles that incorporate special materials designed to create high friction between components, such as grip-enhancing fibers, coatings or textures that prevent slippage during stitching. This subclass falls within the textiles hierarchy under sewing and relates to the use of high-friction materials to improve handling or performance.
[D05D 2209/06] with low friction -- D05D 2209/06 pertains to sewing articles that incorporate special low-friction materials, such as coated threads or gliding inserts, to reduce resistance during stitching. The subclass groups inventions that improve textile handling or machine operation by minimizing friction between components.
[D05D 2209/08] elastic, e.g. rubber spring -- The CPC subclass D05D 2209/08 pertains to the incorporation of elastic components, such as rubber springs, into textile or sewing products. It addresses the use of these special flexible materials to provide stretch or tension recovery in fabrics, seams, or garment parts.
[D05D 2209/10] Particular use of plastics -- D05D 2209/10 concerns the particular use of plastics in textile sewing, covering methods and articles where plastic yarns, threads, or other plastic components are employed to provide functions such as reinforcement, coating, binding or decorative effects in sewn goods.
[D05D 2209/12] Metals or metal coatings
[D05D 2209/14] Brushes -- D05D 2209/14 pertains to brushes employed in textile and sewing operations that incorporate special-material constructions, such as polymer-coated or composite bristles, to improve cleaning, spreading, or finishing functions on fabrics. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy TEXTILES → SEWING → INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES → Use of special materials.
[D05D 2209/16] Thick threads or thick material -- The CPC subclass D05D 2209/16 covers sewing applications that employ thick threads or other thick material, particularly when special or unconventional materials are used. It is situated within the hierarchy of Textiles → Sewing and relates to indexing schemes associated with these subclasses.
[D05D 2303/00] Applied objects or articles -- The CPC subclass D05D 2303/00 concerns sewing or stitching applied objects or articles onto a textile base, such as patches, labels, fasteners and other pre-formed components that are attached after the fabric is formed. It includes methods and apparatus for joining these items to garments, upholstery or other textile products using stitching techniques.
[D05D 2303/02] Tape -- In the CPC system, code D05D 2303/02 designates tapes used in textile and sewing applications, such as adhesive, woven or non-woven strips employed for binding, reinforcing, or decorative purposes on fabrics and garments. It covers various structures and materials intended to join or secure textile articles.
[D05D 2303/04] elastic -- The CPC subclass D05D 2303/04 covers elastic elements incorporated into sewn articles, including stretchable threads, bands, strips or similar components that give textiles a reversible stretch. It pertains to devices or materials that provide elasticity within garments, accessories or other sewn products.
[D05D 2303/06] comprising rigid objects, e.g. slide fasteners -- D05D 2303/06 is a CPC subclass under Textiles > Sewing that covers sewing operations involving rigid objects such as slide fasteners. It pertains to methods or apparatus for attaching or handling these solid components during the stitching process.
[D05D 2303/08] Cordage -- This CPC entry classifies cordage - ropes, twines and similar fibrous articles used in sewing, textile or other applications - by their composition, construction and intended uses. It falls under textiles, sewing and applied objects or articles.
[D05D 2303/10] elastic -- D05D 2303/10 covers elastic articles incorporated into textiles, such as stretchable cords, bands or strips used in garments and other sewn products. It pertains to the design, manufacture or application of elastic components that provide flexibility and recovery within sewn items. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy for textiles, specifically the sewing sector's applied objects or articles.
[D05D 2303/12] Rigid objects -- This CPC subclass covers sewing methods and apparatus for attaching textile material to rigid items such as plates, containers, or frames, where the objects are of a specific material or shape. It includes tools and processes adapted to fasten fabrics to non-flexible substrates.
[D05D 2303/14] Buttons -- D05D 2303/14 covers buttons designed for attachment to textile or sewn articles, encompassing a range of shapes, materials and fastening methods used in garment construction and other sewn items.
[D05D 2303/16] with shanks -- CPC class D05D 2303/16 covers sewing operations applied to objects or articles that incorporate a shank, such as shank-type buttons, hooks, or ornamental fasteners, where the shank forms the point of attachment to the textile. It falls under the Textiles → Sewing hierarchy and addresses methods for stitching these shank-equipped items to fabric.
[D05D 2303/18] Sequins -- D05D 2303/18 is a CPC subclass covering sequins, ornamental discoidal or spherical discs attached to fabrics for decorative effect. It addresses the design, attachment methods, and use of these metal-or plastic-covered embellishments in sewn textile articles.
[D05D 2303/20] Small textile objects e.g., labels, beltloops -- D05D 2303/20 classifies small textile items such as labels, belt-loops and similar accessories that are produced by sewing processes. It falls under the broader textiles category and covers applied objects or articles made from fabric rather than primary garments.
[D05D 2303/30] Fibre mats -- D05D 2303/30 designates fiber mats used as textile components, typically in sewing applications, where a mat of interlinked or bonded fibers forms a flexible, planar structure for reinforcement, insulation or decorative purposes. This subclass falls under the broader categories of Textiles, Sewing, and Applied Objects or Articles in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[D05D 2303/40] Electronic or electrical components -- The subclass D05D 2303/40 covers sewing-related articles that incorporate electronic or electrical components, such as powered needles, sensor-enabled fabrics, smart fasteners, or any device where circuitry, batteries, or control units are integrated into textile products for functions like monitoring, illumination, or automated stitching.
[D05D 2303/42] Optical fibers
[D05D 2305/00] Indexing codes related to the work -- D05D 2305/00 is a CPC subclass under TEXTILES-SEWING that relates to indexing codes used to classify and retrieve information about sewing work, such as the type of stitch, garment part or manufacturing step. It provides a systematic scheme for organizing patent documents concerning sewing processes and finished textile articles.
[D05D 2305/02] Folding -- D05D 2305/02 covers methods and apparatus for folding textile articles, including garments, fabric sheets and similar items, during manufacturing or handling. It includes folding machines, mechanisms and processes that reshape textiles into a folded form for storage, packaging or further processing. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy TEXTILES > SEWING.
[D05D 2305/04] longitudinally to the sewing direction -- This CPC subclass D05D 2305/04 pertains to sewing operations where the seam or stitch is oriented longitudinally, i.e. parallel to the primary fabric direction or seam line. It is used to index textile products and processes that align the stitching direction with the lengthwise axis of the material.
[D05D 2305/06] transversally -- The CPC code D05D 2305/06 refers to sewing techniques in which the stitch is placed transversally across the material, i.e., perpendicular to the fabric's length, and is used to join or reinforce textiles in that orientation.
[D05D 2305/08] Cutting the workpiece -- D05D 2305/08 refers to techniques for cutting a textile workpiece in the context of sewing, covering apparatus or processes that slice fabric, trim seams or prepare pieces before stitching. It classifies methods that shape or size the material as part of garment or textile production.
[D05D 2305/10] longitudinally -- D05D 2305/10 is a CPC subgroup in the Textiles-Sewing section covering features, processes or devices that act longitudinally on textile articles, such as seams, fibers or fabrics. It includes methods for aligning, reinforcing or modifying material along its length, typically applied in garment construction, technical textiles or industrial sewing operations.
[D05D 2305/12] transversally -- The CPC subclass D05D 2305/12 pertains to sewing methods in which the stitch is laid transversely across the material, covering crosswise stitching, transverse seam formation and related operations that join or reinforce fabrics in a direction perpendicular to the main grain. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[D05D 2305/14] Winding or unwinding -- The CPC subclass D05D 2305/14 falls within the broader categories of textiles and sewing and pertains to devices or methods for winding or unwinding thread, yarn, or similar material in sewing equipment, covering mechanisms that control the supply and take-up of the working strand.
[D05D 2305/22] Physico-chemical treatments -- D05D 2305/22 covers physico-chemical treatments applied to textiles during or after sewing, such as dyeing, coating, finishing, or other chemical or physical modifications that alter the fabric's properties. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy TEXTILES - SEWING and is used to index patents dealing with these treatment processes.
[D05D 2305/24] Marking -- D05D 2305/24 covers the marking of textile or sewn articles, including the application of symbols, letters, numbers or other identifiers for purposes such as brand labeling, care instructions, safety warnings or product tracking. It pertains to methods and devices that imprint or attach these markings during or after the manufacturing process.
[D05D 2305/26] Heating -- D05D 2305/26 covers methods and apparatuses for heating textile articles or raw fibers during or after sewing operations. It includes heat-setting, ironing, drying, curing and other thermal treatments used to improve dimensional stability, handle or finish of fabrics and garments.
[D05D 2305/265] with steam -- The CPC code D05D 2305/265 designates sewing equipment that employs steam, such as steam-assisted sewing machines or devices that use steam to improve seam formation or iron fabric during stitching. It lies within the textiles-sewing hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[D05D 2305/28] using particular electromagnetic waves -- D05D 2305/28 covers methods of sewing or textile treatment that employ specific electromagnetic radiation, such as laser, infrared or ultraviolet light, to join, seal, or modify fabrics. The code is used for applications where energy beams replace mechanical stitching or adhesives, allowing precise bonding or surface alteration.
[D05D 2305/30] using adhesive -- D05D 2305/30 covers textile and sewing processes in which adhesives are employed to join fabrics, replace or supplement stitching, and create bonded seams or assemblies. It falls under the broader textile category and the sewing subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO. This code is used to index inventions that apply adhesive methods in garment or fabric construction.
[D05D 2305/32] Measuring -- D05D 2305/32 in the Cooperative Patent Classification concerns measuring operations applied to sewn textile articles. It includes devices and methods for determining dimensions, thickness, weight or other physical parameters of fabrics, garments or assembled textile components during or after production. The subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles and sewing.
[D05D 2305/34] Counting -- The CPC code D05D 2305/34 covers features and mechanisms used to count elements during sewing operations, such as stitch, needle-movement or pattern-repeat counters integrated in sewing or embroidery machines. It includes electronic, mechanical or visual counting systems that help monitor production length, quantity or repeat cycles.
[D05D 2305/345] Stitch counting -- The CPC subclass D05D 2305/345 falls under the broader groups TEXTILES - SEWING - INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES. It covers methods, devices or systems for counting stitches during garment or fabric production, facilitating quality control, pattern verification and automated monitoring of stitching processes.
[D05D 2305/36] Quality control -- The CPC code D05D 2305/36 designates methods, devices and procedures for quality control of sewn textile articles, encompassing testing, inspection and monitoring techniques that verify seam integrity, dimensions, material properties and overall product conformity during or after manufacturing.
[D05D 2305/50] Removing cut-out material or waste -- D05D 2305/50 is a Cooperative Patent Classification code in the CPC system, nested under Textiles → Sewing. It covers inventions related to removing cut-out material or waste during sewing operations, such as trimming, scrap extraction, or waste handling devices.
[D05D 2305/60] Pulling or pushing a thread across the workpiece -- D05D 2305/60 belongs to the CPC group for textiles and sewing, specifically covering methods or apparatus that pull or push a thread across a workpiece. It includes mechanisms that advance the thread through the fabric, guide it across the seam or control its tension during stitching operations. This subclass is used to classify inventions that manipulate thread movement to improve stitch formation or material handling.
[D06] TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE -- The D06 subclass of the CPC deals with processes for treating textiles or similar materials, encompassing operations such as bleaching, dyeing, printing, finishing, coating, and other chemical or physical treatments that modify appearance, feel, performance, or durability. It includes methods for preparing fibers, yarns, fabrics, or non-woven webs for further use or end-use applications.
[D06B] TREATING TEXTILE MATERIALS USING LIQUIDS, GASES OR VAPOURS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for treating textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mechanical treatment of natural fibrous or filamentary material to obtain fibres or filaments -> D01B  **Glossary:** - Fabric: yarns in warp or sheet form - Textile material: fabrics, yarns, nonwoven or other fibrous materials - Indefinite length: an extent of material having the characteristics that the longitudinal dimension is effectively infinite in so far as the procedure for advancing or moving the material is concerned - Cheeses, cones or cakes: yarn packages formed by yarn wound on cores
[D06B 1/00] Applying liquids, gases or vapours onto textile materials to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for applying liquids, gases or vapours on to textile materials by spraying, pouring or contact with a material carrying member to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mercerising -> D06B7/00 - Solvent-treatment -> D06B9/00 - Treatment of selected parts of textile materials -> D06B11/00 - With aid of vibration -> D06B13/00
[D06B 1/02] by spraying or projecting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spraying or projecting on all surfaces of the textile material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - From outlets being in, or almost in, contact with the textile material -> D06B1/08
[D06B 1/04] by pouring or allowing to flow on to the surface of the textile material -- D06B 1/04 covers processes in which liquids, gases or vapours are applied to a textile by pouring or allowing them to flow over the surface, used for washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation of fabrics.
[D06B 1/06] flowing along an inclined surface -- The D06B 1/06 subclass covers methods for treating textile materials by allowing liquids, gases or vapours to flow down an inclined surface, thereby achieving actions such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It pertains to the controlled movement of treatment media along a slope to uniformly treat the fabric.
[D06B 1/08] from outlets being in, or almost in, contact with the textile material -- D06B 1/08 covers processes where the applicator outlets-such as nozzles, jets or sprayers-are placed in, or almost in, direct contact with the textile material during treatment. This arrangement is used for operations like washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation, allowing liquids, gases or vapours to be applied efficiently onto the fabric.
[D06B 1/10] by contact with a member carrying the treating material -- The D06B 1/10 subclass covers processes where textile material is treated by contacting it with a member-such as a roller, belt, pad or other support-that carries the liquid, gas or vapour containing the treating agent, enabling washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 11/00] Treatment of selected parts of textile materials, e.g. partial dyeing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatments on selected parts of textile materials, e.g. dyeing patterns on yarns or fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The entire treatment of the surface or the material -> D06B1/00 , D06B3/00 , D06B5/00 - Printing on textile -> B41J3/4078 - Ink-jet dyeing or printing processes for textile -> D06P5/30
[D06B 11/0003] of fibres, slivers or rovings -- D06B 11/0003 covers processes for applying liquids, gases or vapours to selected portions of fibres, slivers or rovings, such as localized dyeing, bleaching or coating. It falls under the CPC subgroup for treating textile materials by partial treatment of specific areas.
[D06B 11/0006] in connection with a spinning step -- A method for treating selected areas of a textile material, such as localized dyeing, that is performed in connection with a spinning step. The process applies liquids, gases or vapours to part of the fabric while it is being spun, enabling targeted coloration or functional treatment without affecting the entire yarn.
[D06B 11/0009] of a yarn in wound form -- The subclass D06B 11/0009 covers processes for treating portions of a yarn while it remains in a wound form, such as on a bobbin or spool. It includes partial dyeing, impregnation, coating or other modifications applied to selected sections of the yarn without unwinding it, enabling targeted treatment of textile material.
[D06B 11/0013] by local injection -- A method for treating specific areas of a textile by injecting a liquid, gas or vapor directly into the targeted region, allowing localized dyeing, coating or functionalization without affecting the rest of the material. This approach falls under the broader category of treatments applied to selected parts of textile fabrics.
[D06B 11/0016] by local suction -- The code D06B 11/0016 covers processes that treat only selected zones of a fabric by applying local suction to draw liquids, gases or vapours into those areas, enabling partial dyeing or finishing while leaving the remainder of the material unaffected.
[D06B 11/002] of moving yarns -- The D06B 11/002 subclass covers methods for treating moving yarns, such as partial dyeing or localized finishing, where only selected portions of the yarn are exposed to liquids, gases or vapours. It pertains to processes that apply treatment while the yarn is in motion, enabling controlled, segment-specific modification of textile fibers.
[D06B 11/0023] by spraying or pouring -- This subgroup covers processes that treat specific areas of textile material by applying liquids, gases or vapours through spraying or pouring, such as partial dyeing, coating or finishing of selected sections of the fabric. It is a narrow division of treating textile materials using liquids, gases or vapours within the broader textile treatment hierarchy.
[D06B 11/0026] by spaced contacts with a member carrying a single treating material -- The D06B 11/0026 subclass concerns processes that treat selected parts of a textile by bringing the material into spaced contact with a carrier that holds a single treating substance, such as a dye, bleach or finish. This enables localized application of the treating material to specific areas of the fabric.
[D06B 11/003] by continuous contact with a member able to bring simultaneously a plurality of treating materials -- D06B 11/003 covers processes for treating selected areas of a textile by keeping the material in continuous contact with a member-such as a roller, pad or sheet-that can simultaneously apply several treating substances like dyes, pigments, or finishes, enabling localized or partial treatment in a single pass.
[D06B 11/0033] by periodical dipping -- The D06B 11/0033 subclass covers a method of partially treating textiles by repeatedly immersing selected areas in a liquid, such as a dye bath, and then removing them, often with intervening steps like drying or rinsing. This periodic dipping allows controlled coloration or finishing of only certain zones of the fabric without affecting the whole material.
[D06B 11/0036] specially disposed for a local treatment -- This subclass covers methods or apparatus that position textile parts to enable localized application of treatment agents such as dyeing, impregnation or coating only on selected areas, using techniques like masking, selective spraying or localized heating. It includes devices that isolate sections of fabric for targeted processing.
[D06B 11/004] in a tensionless state -- The D06B 11/004 subclass covers methods for treating selected portions of textile materials while the fabric remains untensioned. It includes partial dyeing or other finishing operations where the textile is processed in a relaxed state, relying on liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 11/0043] of hanks of yarns -- D06B 11/0043 covers methods for treating selected portions of textile materials, particularly hanks of yarn, using liquids, gases or vapours such as partial dyeing or coating. The classification falls under treatment of textiles with fluids and relates to localized processing rather than whole-fabric treatment.
[D06B 11/0046] hung on supports -- The D06B 11/0046 subclass covers processes for treating selected sections of textile material where the fabric is suspended on supports such as frames, hooks or racks during the application of liquids, gases or vapours. This arrangement enables partial dyeing, coating or other surface treatments while the untreated portions remain untouched. It falls under the broader category of treating textile materials using fluids, specifically for partial treatment operations.
[D06B 11/005] wound on supports -- D06B 11/005 covers processes for treating only selected areas of a textile, typically by applying the material onto a wound or rolled support that directs the treatment to those parts. The method is used for partial dyeing, coating or impregnation where the fabric is bonded to a backing that allows localized exposure to liquids, gases or vapours. It enables precise modification of specific zones without affecting the whole textile.
[D06B 11/0053] laid down on supports -- The subclass D06B 11/0053 covers methods in which textile material is first placed or deposited on a supporting substrate before being treated, such as coating, printing, partial dyeing or other liquid, gas or vapour processes applied to the material while it remains on the support. It includes techniques where the support serves to hold selected parts of the fabric during the treatment.
[D06B 11/0056] of fabrics
[D06B 11/0059] by spraying -- D06B 11/0059 classifies methods for treating selected areas of textile materials, such as partial dyeing, where the treatment medium is applied by spraying. This subgroup falls under treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours, focusing on localized application rather than whole-fabric processing.
[D06B 11/0063] by pouring -- The code D06B 11/0063 covers methods of applying a liquid, gas or vapor treatment to only certain areas of a textile by pouring the medium onto those zones, such as partial dyeing or finishing. This technique enables localized coloration or functionalization without immersing the whole fabric.
[D06B 11/0066] by spaced contacts with a member carrying a single treating material -- The D06B 11/0066 subclass covers processes that treat specific areas of a textile material by applying spaced contacts using a single treating agent carried on a member, such as a pad or roller, to effect localized dyeing, coating or other chemical modification. This method enables selective treatment of parts of the fabric rather than uniform treatment of the whole.
[D06B 11/0069] by continuous contact with a member able to bring simultaneously a plurality of treating materials -- D06B 11/0069 covers a process for treating specific areas of a textile by keeping the fabric in continuous contact with a device that can apply several treating agents at the same time, such as dyes, chemicals or finishes. The method enables simultaneous application of multiple liquids, gases or vapours to selected portions of the material, facilitating partial coloration or functional treatment.
[D06B 11/0073] of articles -- The CPC subclass D06B 11/0073 covers processes for treating selected portions of textile articles, such as applying dye, pigment or finish to only part of a fabric or garment. It includes methods that use liquids, gases or vapours to achieve localized coloration or functional modification without treating the whole material.
[D06B 11/0076] Transfer-treating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment by transfer, e.g. transferring dye from a dyed paper onto a textile material or transferring an adhesive pattern from a tape onto a textile material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Transfer printing apparatus for textile -> B41F16/02 - Apparatus for applying decalcomanias -> B65C
[D06B 11/0079] Local modifications of the ability of the textile material to receive the treating materials, (e.g. its dyeability) -- D06B 11/0079 covers processes that locally modify a textile's capacity to take up dyes, finishes or other treating agents, for example by partial-dyeing or selective treatment of specific areas. The classification is used for methods that change dyeability or related uptake only on selected parts of the fabric.
[D06B 11/0083] the textile material being fibres or slivers -- The D06B 11/0083 subclass covers processes that treat specific portions of textile materials, such as partial dyeing or localized finishing, when the material is presented as fibres or slivers, using liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 11/0086] the textile material being one or more yarns -- D06B 11/0086 refers to processes that treat textile materials consisting of one or more yarns, focusing on the selective treatment of specific areas such as partial dyeing. The methods involve applying liquids, gases or vapours to modify only the chosen parts of the yarns while leaving the remainder unchanged. This subclass falls under treatment of textiles using fluid media.
[D06B 11/0089] the textile material being a surface -- This CPC subclass deals with processes that treat only specific portions of a textile, such as partial dyeing, where the textile itself acts as the surface on which the treatment is applied. It covers methods using liquids, gases or vapours to modify the selected area of the fabric.
[D06B 11/0093] Treatments carried out during or after a regular application of treating materials, in order to get differentiated effects on the textile material -- D06B 11/0093 covers textile processes in which, after a standard application of a treating material such as dye or finish, additional localized or selective steps are performed to produce differentiated effects on the fabric. Examples include partial dyeing, spot finishing, masking or other treatments that modify only selected areas of the textile.
[D06B 11/0096] to get a faded look -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods to obtain dyed textile materials with a faded look, e.g. through sandblasting, laser carving, abrading, washing with pumice or stones in order to obtain a worn-out look on jeans etc.
[D06B 1/12] by rubbing contact, e.g. with brushes or pads -- D06B 1/12 covers the treatment of textile materials by rubbing contact, such as using brushes, pads or similar devices, to apply liquids, gases or vapours for processes like washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating.
[D06B 1/14] with a roller -- This subclass concerns treating textile materials by applying liquids, gases or vapours with a roller. It includes washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating operations where a roller is used to transfer the treatment medium onto the fabric.
[D06B 1/141] where an element is used to mitigate the quantity of treating material on the roller -- D06B 1/141 covers textile treatment processes in which a device or element is employed on a roller to limit the amount of liquid, gas or vapour applied to the fabric, preventing excess treating material from being delivered during washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. This subclass focuses on mechanisms that regulate or reduce the fluid film on rollers to achieve controlled application.
[D06B 1/142] where an element is used to mitigate the quantity of treating material that the textile material can retain  if removing occurs later  see   D06B15/00  and sub-groups -- The subclass D06B 1/142 covers processes where an element is incorporated to limit the quantity of treating material that a textile can retain, making later removal of excess chemicals such as dyes, bleaches or sizing agents easier. It is part of treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours and links to the broader group D06B15/00.
[D06B 1/143] where elements are used to mitigate the quantities of treating material on the roller and on the textile material -- This subclass concerns processes where the roller or contact elements are designed to limit the amount of treating fluid transferred to the textile, such as using metering grooves, porous surfaces or absorbent pads to control wetting. It includes techniques that reduce excess dye, bleach or other liquids during washing, dyeing, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 1/144] the treating material being kept in a trough formed between the roller and non-rotating elements -- This subclass pertains to processes where the treating liquid, gas or vapour is retained in a trough formed between a rotating roller and adjacent non-rotating elements, enabling the material to be applied to textiles during operations such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 1/145] the treating material being kept in the trough formed between two or more rollers -- The D06B 1/145 subgroup covers textile treatment processes in which the treating liquid, gas or vapour is held in a trough formed between two or more rollers that convey the material. By retaining the medium in the roller-created channel, the invention aims to improve uniformity and efficiency during washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation of fabrics.
[D06B 1/146] where the textile material is first passed in a nip before it comes into contact with the treating material -- This subclass covers processes in which a textile web is first passed through a nip formed by rollers or similar devices before being contacted with a liquid, gas or vapor treatment. The nip may pre-compress, pre-heat or otherwise condition the material to improve uptake of the treating medium, and is used in washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating operations.
[D06B 1/147] the textile material being a tubular fabric  takes precedence over  D06B1/146 -- A subclass of CPC D06B 1/147 covers processes that apply liquids, gases or vapours to tubular fabrics for treatment such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation, and it is defined to take precedence over the broader D06B 1/146 subclass for non-tubular textile materials.
[D06B 1/148] the treating material being supplied to the roller by spraying or pouring -- This CPC subclass covers techniques where the treating material is fed to a roller by spraying or pouring, used for processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating textile articles. It belongs to D06B 1/148, the group for applying liquids, gases or vapours to textiles.
[D06B 1/149] the treating material being applied to the textile material without contact with the roller (e.g. by capillarity) -- Subclass D06B 1/149 covers methods of applying treating liquids, gases or vapours to textile materials without direct roller contact, for example by capillary action, to achieve washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It falls under the broader category of treating textiles with fluids or vapours.
[D06B 1/16] the treating material being supplied from inside the roller -- This CPC subclass covers textile treatment operations in which the liquid, gas or vapour used for washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating is delivered from inside a roller that contacts the material. The entry focuses on the internal supply of the treating medium through the roller during the treatment process.
[D06B 13/00] Treatment of textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours with aid of vibration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment of textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours which are enhanced by high frequency vibrations, e.g. ultrasound devices in direct contact with the textile material or not in contact with the textile material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatments of fabrics passing through liquids, i.e. during washing, which are enhanced by low frequency vibrations, e.g. mechanical vibration -> D06B3/205 .
[D06B 15/00] Removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials in association with treatment of the materials by liquids, gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials in association with treatment of the materials by liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 15/005] by squeezing, otherwise than by rollers -- The CPC subclass D06B 15/005 covers methods for removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials after they have been treated with liquids, gases or vapours, specifically using squeezing actions that do not involve rollers. This includes any mechanical pressure applied by hand, presses, or other non-roller devices to expel the processing medium from the fabric.
[D06B 15/02] by squeezing rollers -- The D06B 15/02 subclass covers processes that remove liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials by passing the fabric through squeezing rollers, typically after treatment with liquids, gases or vapours. This method mechanically expels the conditioning medium from the textile.
[D06B 15/025] for tubular fabrics -- This CPC subclass D06B 15/025 covers processes for removing liquids, gases or vapours from tubular fabrics in connection with treatment of the material by fluids and falls under textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and treating textile materials using liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 15/04] by suction -- The D06B 15/04 subclass covers processes that remove liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials during treatments that involve liquids, gases or vapours, specifically using suction as the extraction method. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and treating textile materials with fluids. This subclass is used in patent classification for inventions that employ suction to dry or de-gas fabrics after a treatment step.
[D06B 15/043] by means of a perforated drum -- The CPC subclass D06B 15/043 covers processes that remove liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials during treatment by means of a perforated drum. The rotating drum's perforations allow the unwanted phase to be expelled from the fabric.
[D06B 15/046] included in a pair of squeezing rollers -- D06B 15/046 covers the step of removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials by passing them through a pair of squeezing rollers as part of a liquid, gas or vapour treatment process.
[D06B 15/06] by vibrating the textile material -- The D06B 15/06 subclass covers methods of removing liquids, gases or vapours from textiles during treatment by applying mechanical vibration to the material, aiding drying or evaporation. It is part of the CPC hierarchy for textile treatment using liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 15/08] by scraping -- The subclass D06B 15/08 covers methods for removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials by means of mechanical scraping, such as using blades, rollers or belts to wipe or pull fluid from the fabric surface during or after wet processing. This technique is applied to dry or partially dry textiles without relying on heat or airflow.
[D06B 15/085] by contact with the textile material -- The D06B 15/085 subclass covers methods of removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials by direct contact with the fabric during treatment, such as drying, dewatering or vapor-removal steps integrated into the processing operation. It applies to processes where the removal action occurs through contact with the textile itself rather than external equipment.
[D06B 15/09] by jets of gases -- The D06B 15/09 entry pertains to the removal of liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials during processing, specifically when the expulsion is achieved by directing jets of gases onto the fabric. It falls under the broader category of treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours and addresses drying or de-watering operations using gas-jet techniques.
[D06B 15/10] by use of centrifugal force -- This subclass covers the removal of liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials during processing when centrifugal force is employed. It belongs to the group dealing with treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 15/12] up to the moisture regain of the textile material -- D06B 15/12 covers methods for removing liquids, gases or vapours from textile materials after they have undergone such treatments, limited to the stage where the fabric regains its natural moisture content.
[D06B 17/00] Storing of textile materials in association with the treatment of the materials by liquids, gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for storing the textile materials in association with the treatment of the materials by liquids, gases or vapours, e.g. heat-setting of yarns or fabrics in a steam chamber (steamer).
[D06B 17/005] in helical form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The storing of textile materials in helical form, e.g. heat setting treatments of yarns which lay in helical form on a conveyor passing through a heated tunnel.
[D06B 17/02] in superimposed, i.e. stack-packed, form -- The CPC subclass D06B 17/02 concerns the storage of textile materials during liquid, gas or vapour treatment when the fabrics are placed in a superimposed, i.e., stack-packed, arrangement. It lies within the broader group for treating textile materials using liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 17/04] in wound form -- D06B 17/04 covers the storage of textile material that has been wound, such as in rolls or coils, while it is being treated with liquids, gases or vapours. The classification is used for processes where the wound configuration is maintained during treatment, facilitating handling and uniform exposure.
[D06B 17/06] in festooned form -- D06B 17/06 covers the storage of textile materials during liquid, gas or vapour treatment when the items are arranged in a festooned, looped or hanging configuration. This subclass falls under the broader group for treating textiles with fluids and specifies the particular way the fabrics are suspended while being processed.
[D06B 19/00] Treatment of textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours, not provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for the treatment of textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours not provided for in groups D06B1/00 - D06B17/00 .
[D06B 19/0005] Fixing of chemicals, e.g. dyestuffs, on textile materials  D06B9/02 ,  D06B17/00  -  D06B17/06  take precedence -- A process for fixing chemicals such as dyestuffs onto textile materials, using liquids, gases or vapours that are not covered by more specific CPC groups. It falls under D06B 19/0005, which is part of the broader hierarchy for treating textiles with fluids, and takes precedence over D06B 9/02, D06B 17/00-D06B 17/06.
[D06B 19/0011] by heated air -- D06B 19/0011 classifies processes that treat textile materials using heated air, such as drying, setting, or other finishing operations. It falls under the broader category of treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours where the specific method involves hot air rather than liquid or other gas treatments.
[D06B 19/0017] the textile material passing through a chamber -- This subclass relates to processes in which a textile web or material is conveyed through a treatment chamber where liquids, gases or vapours act on it, without specifying a particular chemical or physical effect. It covers the handling and movement of the textile through the chamber as part of the overall fluid-based treatment operation.
[D06B 19/0023] wherein suction is applied to one face of the textile material -- The CPC subclass D06B 19/0023 falls under treatment of textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours and specifies processes where suction is applied to one face of the fabric, a technique not covered by other groups in the same hierarchy.
[D06B 19/0029] by steam -- This CPC subclass covers processes for treating textile materials with steam, where the steam is applied to modify, clean, shape or finish the fabric. It is situated under treatment of textiles by liquids, gases or vapours that are not otherwise specified.
[D06B 19/0035] the textile material passing through a chamber -- The code covers processes in which a textile web or fabric is conveyed through a chamber where it is exposed to liquids, gases or vapours for treatment. This includes continuous operations such as impregnation, dyeing, coating or curing performed as the material moves through the chamber, and is not allocated to any more specific sub-group.
[D06B 19/0041] wherein suction is applied to one face of the textile material -- This CPC subgroup falls under treatment of textiles by liquids, gases or vapours and specifies that during the process a suction force is applied to one face of the fabric. The suction removes fluid, gas or vapour from that side, aiding penetration, drying or removal of residues.
[D06B 19/0047] by air steam -- The CPC subclass D06B 19/0047 covers textile treatments that employ air-steam, meaning steam conveyed through a stream of air rather than as direct saturated vapor. It is used for processes such as drying, conditioning, or finishing fabrics where the steam's moisture content is reduced by mixing with air, providing gentler heat and controlled humidity.
[D06B 19/0052] the textile material passing through a chamber -- This CPC subclass D06B 19/0052 covers processes in which a textile material passes through a chamber for treatment with liquids, gases or vapours, typically for conditioning, drying, or applying coatings. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and treatment of textile materials using fluids and addresses methods not otherwise specified in other groups.
[D06B 19/0058] wherein suction is applied to one face of the textile material -- D06B 19/0058 covers methods of treating fabrics with liquids, gases or vapours in which suction is applied to one side of the textile, causing the fluid to be drawn through or removed from the material. The technique is used for impregnation, dyeing, cleaning or drying where controlled flow through the fabric is required.
[D06B 19/0064] by a fixing bath  D06B19/0082  takes precedence -- The classification D06B 19/0064 refers to textile treatment processes that employ a fixing bath, where the material is immersed in a liquid to set dyes or finishes. This falls under treatment by liquids, gases or vapours and is superseded by the more specific subgroup D06B 19/0082.
[D06B 19/007] by application of electric energy -- The CPC class D06B 19/007 covers processes that treat textile articles by applying electric energy, often together with liquids, gases or vapours, where the specific method is not captured in other, more detailed groups. It includes techniques such as dielectric heating, plasma treatment or corona discharge used to modify or improve textile properties.
[D06B 19/0076] by contact with a heated surface -- The CPC subclass D06B 19/0076 covers processes for treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours where the treatment is performed by bringing the textile into contact with a heated surface. It is used for methods that do not fall under more specific groups within the broader category of textile treatment by fluids.
[D06B 19/0082] by passing through a bath of liquid metal or a fluidised bed -- The CPC entry D06B 19/0082 describes a textile-treatment process in which fabric or other textile material is passed through a bath of liquid metal or a fluidised bed, representing a specific method of treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours that is not covered by other groups.
[D06B 19/0088] using a short bath ratio liquor -- The subclass D06B 19/0088 covers textile treatment processes that employ a short-bath-ratio liquor, meaning the amount of liquid used relative to the fabric weight is relatively low. It includes methods where fabrics are impregnated, dyed, or finished using such reduced-volume baths, typically to conserve chemicals, reduce waste, or achieve specific surface effects. This entry falls under the broader category of treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours that are not otherwise specified.
[D06B 19/0094] as a foam -- D06B 19/0094 covers textile treatment processes in which a foam is used as the liquid, gas or vapour medium. The classification includes methods where the foam carries dyes, finishes, cleaning agents or other chemicals onto the fabric, providing uniform penetration and reduced consumption of liquids.
[D06B 21/00] Successive treatments of textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Two or more treatments in succession of textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours. Successive treatments involve two or more treatments in a series or sequence.
[D06B 21/02] the treatments being performed in a single container -- D06B 21/02 covers processes in which a textile material undergoes a series of liquid, gas or vapour treatments sequentially within a single container, eliminating the need to transfer the fabric between separate units. It includes integrated operations such as washing, bleaching, dyeing or finishing that are performed in one vessel.
[D06B 23/00] Component parts, details, or accessories of apparatus or machines, specially adapted for the treating of textile materials, not restricted to a particular kind of apparatus, provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Components parts, details or accessories of apparatus for treatment of textile materials.
[D06B 23/02] Rollers -- Rollers are component parts, details or accessories designed for use in machines that treat textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours. They are not limited to any specific type of apparatus and may be employed in various textile-processing equipment for conveying, pressing or feeding fabrics during treatment processes.
[D06B 23/021] Compressive rollers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compression rollers or calenders, e.g. for compressing the textile material or for squeezing out liquid from the textile material.
[D06B 23/023] Guiding rollers -- Guiding rollers are component parts of textile-treating machines that control the path and tension of fabric as it moves through processing steps such as dyeing, printing or finishing. They are not limited to a specific type of apparatus but are generally mounted in various treatment equipment to ensure accurate feed and alignment of the material.
[D06B 23/025] Perforated rollers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. perforated drums for dyeing, washing treatment or imparting a water jet treatment to a fabric.
[D06B 23/026] characterised by particular surface features -- This subclass covers parts or accessories of textile-treating equipment that possess distinctive surface characteristics such as textures, coatings, or patterns which influence the treatment process. The surface features are defined independently of any particular machine type and relate to the design of the component itself.
[D06B 23/028] Rollers for thermal treatment -- This subgroup covers rollers that are part of, or added to, textile-treating machines and are specifically designed for applying heat during processes such as drying, curing, heat-setting or other thermal treatments of fabrics. The rollers may be heated, cooled or otherwise temperature-controlled and are treated as component parts, details or accessories rather than as a complete apparatus.
[D06B 23/04] Carriers or supports for textile materials to be treated -- The CPC subclass D06B 23/04 covers carriers, supports or other component parts and accessories designed to hold textile articles during treatment processes such as washing, dyeing, printing or finishing, where the textiles are exposed to liquids, gases or vapours. It applies to any apparatus-related element that enables the material to be positioned or conveyed while being treated, without being limited to a specific type of machine.
[D06B 23/042] Perforated supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. perforated tubes for dyeing yarn packages.
[D06B 23/045] radially collapsible -- D06B 23/045 covers component parts, details or accessories for machines that treat textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours, which are not tied to any particular apparatus type and are designed to collapse radially, allowing compact storage or flexible operation.
[D06B 23/047] Replacing or removing the core of the package -- D06B 23/047 covers components or accessories for textile-treating machines that enable the core of a package-such as a bobbin, spool or roll core-to be removed, replaced or exchanged during processing with liquids, gases or vapours. It applies to any apparatus part designed for this function, without limiting the type of treatment equipment.
[D06B 23/06] Guiding means for preventing filaments, yarns or threads from sticking together -- The CPC subclass D06B 23/06 covers guiding devices that keep filaments, yarns or threads from adhering to one another while textile materials are treated with liquids, gases or vapours. It includes component parts, details or accessories for such equipment that are not limited to a specific machine type.
[D06B 23/08] Untwisting devices -- Untwisting devices are components fitted to textile treatment equipment that unwind or remove twist from yarns, threads or fabrics during processing. They may be separate units or accessories that can be attached to various machines, and are designed to control or reduce yarn twist to improve handling, dyeing or finishing quality.
[D06B 23/10] Devices for dyeing samples -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for dyeing samples, e.g. small laboratory devices for testing fabric samples.
[D06B 23/12] Means for taking samples from textile materials during or after treatment -- D06B 23/12 covers devices and accessories for extracting samples from textiles while they are being processed or after the treatment, enabling analysis of the material's condition. These sampling means are independent of any specific machine and may be adapted to various liquid, gas or vapor treatment apparatus.
[D06B 23/14] Containers, e.g. vats -- This subclass covers containers such as vats used in the treatment of textiles with liquids, gases or vapours. It includes component parts or accessories of apparatus for textile processing that are not limited to a particular machine type.
[D06B 23/16] with means for introducing or removing textile materials without modifying container pressure -- D06B 23/16 covers components, details or accessories for textile-treating apparatus that allow fabric to be loaded or unloaded without altering the pressure inside the treatment chamber, such as seals, rotary or linear feed mechanisms and lock-out devices that maintain a sealed environment while the material moves.
[D06B 23/18] Sealing arrangements -- The D06B 23/18 subclass covers sealing arrangements for machines and equipment used in treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours. It includes component parts, details and accessories such as gaskets, seals, sealing rings and related hardware that prevent leakage and maintain pressure or fluid containment during textile processing.
[D06B 23/20] Arrangements of apparatus for treating processing-liquids, -gases or -vapours, e.g. purification, filtration or distillation -- The D06B 23/20 subclass covers the layout and integration of equipment used to treat processing liquids, gases or vapours in textile operations, such as systems for purification, filtration or distillation. It includes the design of piping, manifolds, separators and related components that form a complete treatment line, without being limited to any specific type of machine.
[D06B 23/205] for adding or mixing constituents of the treating material -- A component or accessory for machines used in textile treatment that introduces or blends the chemicals, gases or vapours applied to the fabric, facilitating the addition or mixing of the treating material during processing.
[D06B 23/22] for heating -- It covers heating components, details or accessories of machines used for treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours. The subclass falls under CPC group D06B for textile treatment and relates to equipment not limited to a specific apparatus type.
[D06B 23/24] Means for regulating the amount of treating material picked up by the textile material during its treatment -- This subclass covers devices integrated in textile-treating equipment that control how much processing medium-such as dye, finish or lubricant-is transferred to the fabric as it moves through the machine. The means may include adjustable nozzles, rollers, sprays or other mechanisms that limit or meter the amount picked up, regardless of the specific apparatus.
[D06B 23/26] in response to a test conducted on the textile material -- D06B 23/26 covers components, details or accessories of textile-treating machines that are activated or adjusted in response to a test performed on the material, such as sensors, detectors or control units that modify liquid, gas or vapour treatment parameters after measuring properties of the textile during processing.
[D06B 23/28] in response to a test conducted on the treating material -- The D06B 23/28 code refers to component parts of textile treatment apparatus. It involves responses to tests on treating materials. This code is part of the textiles classification hierarchy.
[D06B 23/30] Means for cleaning apparatus or machines, or parts thereof -- D06B 23/30 covers devices and accessories designed to clean machines, apparatus or their components that are used in the treatment of textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours. It includes general cleaning tools not limited to a particular type of equipment.
[D06B 2700/00] Treating of textile materials, e.g. bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating, washing -- This CPC subclass D06B 2700/00 covers processes for treating textile materials, including chemical or physical operations such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating and washing performed with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 2700/02] Rollers or brushes for applying bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid to fibrous materials -- D06B 2700/02 covers rollers or brush assemblies used to apply bleaching, washing or dyeing liquids onto fibrous textiles, facilitating uniform treatment during processes such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising or washing. The class lies within the broader group for treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 2700/04] Partial dyeing, e.g. of fabrics -- The CPC subclass D06B 2700/04 concerns partial dyeing of textile materials, where only selected regions of a fabric are coloured rather than the whole piece. It is part of the treatment of textiles using liquids, gases or vapours, which includes processes such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating and washing.
[D06B 2700/06] Passing warp yarns in unwound or in wound form through bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid -- D06B 2700/06 relates to processes where warp yarns, whether unwound or still wound, are passed through bleaching, washing or dyeing liquids to treat the textile material, falling under the CPC class for treating textiles with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 2700/09] Apparatus for passing open width fabrics through bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid -- The CPC class D06B 2700/09 covers machines that feed open-width textile webs through baths of bleaching, washing or dyeing liquids. It includes apparatus designed to convey fabric continuously through treatment solutions for processes such as bleaching, washing, dyeing, mercerising or impregnation.
[D06B 2700/095] for continuous treatment of open width fabrics, in which the fabric is guided without tension, e.g. superimposed or festooned, in order to extend the duration of treatment -- The CPC subclass D06B 2700/095 covers processes for continuous treatment of open-width fabrics that are guided without tension, for example in superimposed or festooned arrangements, to extend the duration of exposure to liquids, gases or vapours. It falls under textile treatment methods such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating and washing.
