[D06B 2700/10] Apparatus for passing fabrics in roped form through bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid, e.g. for continuous treatment -- D06B 2700/10 refers to machines that move fabric in a rope configuration through liquids used for bleaching, washing or dyeing, allowing a continuous treatment line. Such equipment is used in textile mills to improve efficiency of large-scale processing.
[D06B 2700/105] for batch treatment in winch vats of fabrics in roped form -- This CPC subclass covers batch processing of fabrics that are wound or roped and treated in winch vats, such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating or washing, where the treatment is applied to the entire batch rather than to individual pieces.
[D06B 2700/14] Passing liquid through fabrics or linoleum -- D06B 2700/14 covers processes in which a liquid is passed through a fabric or linoleum, such as impregnation, washing or bleaching, to modify the material's properties. The classification applies to techniques that force or let fluids permeate the textile structure, often under pressure or by gravity, and includes related apparatus.
[D06B 2700/17] Passing liquid through fibrous materials in open containers with a form not determined by the nature of the fibrous material -- Covers methods of moving a liquid through fibrous textile materials held in open vessels where the shape of the container is not dictated by the fabric itself, such as rinsing, washing or impregnating operations performed in generic tanks or troughs.
[D06B 2700/18] Passing liquid through fibrous materials in closed containers with a form not determined by the nature of the fibrous material -- The code D06B 2700/18 covers methods for moving liquid through fabrics inside sealed vessels where the shape of the flow path is defined independently of the fibers themselves. It includes operations such as impregnation, washing or bleaching performed in closed containers, focusing on the equipment and process conditions rather than the textile structure.
[D06B 2700/19] Passing bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid through fibrous materials by centrifuging -- The class D06B 2700/19 covers methods of treating textile or fibrous materials in which a bleaching, washing or dyeing liquid is forced through the material by means of centrifugation. The process uses rotational forces to expel liquid, improving penetration and uniformity during the treatment.
[D06B 2700/25] Sizing, starching or impregnating warp yarns -- The D06B 2700/25 subclass covers processes for applying sizing, starch or other impregnating agents to warp yarns before weaving. Such treatments increase yarn strength, reduce friction and improve handling during textile manufacture. Methods may involve liquid, gas or vapor application and include preparation of the sizing composition.
[D06B 2700/27] Sizing, starching or impregnating fabrics -- D06B 2700/27 designates processes for sizing, starching or impregnating fabrics, where a protective or functional coating is applied to textile material. The treatment may involve liquid, gas or vapor carriers to improve strength, stiffness, water resistance or other properties before further finishing such as dyeing or weaving.
[D06B 2700/28] Impregnating or coating cords or ropes -- D06B 2700/28 covers the treatment of cords or ropes by impregnation or coating, where a liquid, gas or vapor is applied to create a protective, functional or decorative layer. This process falls within textile treatment methods such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising or washing.
[D06B 2700/35] Rollers for machines for treating textiles, e.g. dyeing, drying, sizing -- This subclass covers rollers incorporated in textile treatment equipment such as dyeing, drying or sizing machines, designed to convey, support or press the fabric while it passes through the process. It includes various shapes, surface treatments and drive arrangements for rollers used with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 2700/36] Devices or methods for dyeing, washing or bleaching not otherwise provided for -- Code D06B 2700/36 refers to devices and methods for dyeing, washing or bleaching textiles that are not otherwise classified in more specific subclasses. It encompasses generic or unconventional equipment and processes that apply liquids, gases or vapours to textile materials for coloration, cleaning or whitening.
[D06B 2700/37] Machines for forming hanks -- The CPC subclass D06B 2700/37 pertains to machines that form hanks, the loosely twisted bundles of yarn prepared for subsequent textile operations. It is situated under treatment of textiles with liquids, gases or vapours and includes equipment used in processes such as bleaching, dyeing, mercerising, impregnating and washing.
[D06B 3/00] Passing of textile materials through liquids, gases or vapours to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing, impregnating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for passing textile materials through liquids, gases or vapours to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating.
[D06B 3/02] of fibres, slivers or rovings -- **Glossary:** - Sliver: a continuous strand of loosely assembled fibres without twist - Roving: a condensed sliver in an intermediate state in the yarn production between sliver and yarn that has been drafted, twisted, doubled and redoubled. The production of the first roving operation is sometimes called slubbing.
[D06B 3/04] of yarns, threads or filaments -- D06B 3/04 covers methods of treating yarns, threads or filaments by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours. The processes include washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing, impregnation and similar treatments that modify the textile's properties.
[D06B 3/045] in a tube or a groove -- The code D06B 3/045 covers textile treatment operations where the material is passed through liquids, gases or vapours inside a tube or a groove, such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating. It is a sub-category of treating textile materials using fluids, specifying that the treatment takes place in a confined channel.
[D06B 3/06] individually handled -- D06B 3/06 refers to the treatment of textile materials by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours when each piece is handled separately, encompassing processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating performed on individually handled items.
[D06B 3/08] as hanks
[D06B 3/09] as packages, e.g. cheeses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Dyeing, bleaching, washing etc. of cheeses for example by dipping in a treatment bath (i.e. not pressurized).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forcing liquids, gases or vapours through yarns, threads or filaments (e.g. treating in containers under pressure) -> D06B5/16 .  **Glossary:** - Cheeses or cones: yarn packages formed by yarn wound on cores.
[D06B 3/10] of fabrics
[D06B 3/105] of tubular fabrics -- D06B 3/105 covers the treatment of tubular fabrics, such as hoses or tube-shaped textiles, by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours to achieve washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing, impregnating or similar modifications. The classification is used for processes that treat these continuous tubular materials in a fluid environment.
[D06B 3/12] in zig-zag manner over series of guiding means -- A procedure for treating textiles in which the fabric is conveyed in a zig-zag path over a series of rollers, guides or other directing elements, allowing it to be uniformly exposed to liquids, gases or vapours for washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing, impregnating or similar operations.
[D06B 3/14] in wound form -- The code D06B 3/14 covers processes in which textile material is treated while in a wound or rolled form as it passes through liquids, gases or vapours. It includes washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing, impregnating or similar treatments applied to fabric that remains wound, facilitating efficient use of the treatment medium.
[D06B 3/16] in superimposed, i.e. stack-packed, form -- This sub-class covers textile treatment when the material is arranged in a superimposed, stack-packed form while being passed through liquids, gases or vapours for washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating, addressing the specific handling required for layered batches.
[D06B 3/18] combined with squeezing, e.g. in padding machines -- The D06B 3/18 subclass covers textile treatment processes in which the material is passed through liquids, gases or vapours while being squeezed, such as in padding machines that apply dyes, finishes or other chemicals and then remove excess fluid by pressure. This combines impregnation or coating steps with mechanical squeezing to improve uptake and uniformity.
[D06B 3/185] below the bath level -- The D06B 3/185 subclass deals with textile treatment operations in which the material is placed beneath the surface of a liquid or vapour bath, such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating, so that the treatment occurs below the bath level.
[D06B 3/20] with means to improve the circulation of the treating material on the surface of the fabric -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All means to enhance the fabric treatment, e.g. the use of steam or water jets during a washing or scouring treatment of a moving web transported in a vat or a tank.
[D06B 3/201] the treating material being forced through the textile material -- D06B 3/201 covers processes in which a liquid, gas or vapour is forced through a textile web or fabric to achieve treatment such as washing, dyeing, bleaching or impregnation. The method relies on pressure, padding or similar means to drive the treating medium through the material.
[D06B 3/203] by suction, e.g. by means of perforated drums -- D06B 3/203 covers methods of treating textile material by drawing it through a treatment zone using suction, typically with perforated drums that pull the fabric through liquids, gases or vapours for processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 3/205] by vibrating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment of fabrics passing through liquids, i.e. during washing, which are enhanced by LOW frequency vibrations, e.g. by vibrations induced on the fabric by mechanical devices acting directly on the fabric or by hydraulic waves.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatment of textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours with aid of high frequency vibration, e.g. ultrasound -> D06B13/00 .
[D06B 3/206] the textile material -- D06B 3/206 covers processes in which the textile material itself is passed through liquids, gases or vapours to achieve treatments such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. This subclass falls under treating textile materials using fluids and relates to the handling of the fabric during the treatment operation.
[D06B 3/208] the treating material -- D06B 3/208 covers the substances employed to treat textile materials during processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation, including liquids, gases or vapours that act as the active treating medium. It addresses the composition, preparation and application of these treating materials.
[D06B 3/22] only one side of the fabric coming into contact with the guiding means -- This classification covers textile treatment where the material is passed through liquids, gases or vapours while only one side of the fabric contacts the guiding element such as a roller or belt. The arrangement controls exposure, prevents double-sided contamination and helps achieve uniform dyeing, bleaching or sizing.
[D06B 3/24] of fabrics in roped form -- The D06B 3/24 CPC subclass covers processes for treating fabrics that have been formed into rope-like configurations. It includes passing the roped textiles through liquids, gases or vapours to wash, dye, bleach, size, impregnate or otherwise modify the material. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textile treatment and the use of fluid media for such operations.
[D06B 3/26] in superimposed, i.e. stack-packed, form -- D06B 3/26 covers the treatment of textile materials when they are arranged in a superimposed, stack-packed configuration and passed through liquids, gases or vapours for processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. The classification applies to methods that handle multiple layers of fabric or yarn simultaneously, rather than treating each piece individually.
[D06B 3/28] of fabrics propelled by, or with the aid of, jets of the treating material -- The D06B 3/28 subclass covers processes where fabrics are moved through a treatment zone by jets of the treating liquid, gas or vapor themselves, such as jet-propelled washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It pertains to textile processing that uses the flow of the treatment medium to convey the material.
[D06B 3/30] of articles, e.g. stockings -- This subclass covers methods of treating textile articles, such as stockings, by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours to perform operations like washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 3/32] of open-width materials backwards and forwards between beaming rollers during treatment -- The code D06B 3/32 pertains to methods of moving an open-width textile web forward and backward between beaming rollers while it is being treated with liquids, gases or vapours. This back-and-forth motion allows uniform application of processes such as washing, dyeing, bleaching or impregnation across the width of the material. It falls under the broader class of textile treatment by fluid media.
[D06B 3/325] with means to improve the circulation of the treating material on the surface of the textile material -- This subclass covers treatment of textiles by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours and includes arrangements that improve the circulation of the treating medium over the fabric surface. Such devices aim to enhance uniform distribution, penetration or contact of the treating material for applications such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 3/34] Driving arrangements of machines or apparatus -- The D06B 3/34 subclass pertains to the driving arrangements of machines or apparatus used for treating textile materials by passing them through liquids, gases or vapours. It covers power transmission, motor-driven gearing, belts, pulleys and other mechanisms that propel equipment for washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating fabrics.
[D06B 3/345] Means for controlling the tension in the textile material  in general  D02H13/22 -- The D06B 3/345 entry covers devices that regulate the tension of textile material during liquid, gas or vapour treatments such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating. It includes mechanisms like rollers, clamps, brakes or tension-sensing systems that maintain consistent pull on the fabric to ensure uniform processing and prevent distortion. This classification belongs to the broader group dealing with treatment of textiles by fluids.
[D06B 3/36] Drive control -- The D06B 3/36 entry covers technological features for controlling the drives of equipment that move textile materials through liquids, gases or vapours during treatments such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It includes systems that regulate speed, torque, direction or synchronization of rollers, pumps, conveyors and other drive mechanisms to achieve precise processing conditions. This classification addresses drive-control devices and methods used in textile treatment processes.
[D06B 5/00] Forcing liquids, gases or vapours through textile materials to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing impregnating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for forcing liquids, gases or vapours through textile materials to effect treatment, e.g. washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating.
[D06B 5/02] through moving materials of indefinite length -- **Glossary:** - Materials of indefinite length: (e.g. continuous webs or continuous yarns) are materials having the characteristics that the longitudinal dimension is effectively infinite in so far as the procedure for advancing or moving the material is concerned.
[D06B 5/04] through slivers or rovings -- The D06B 5/04 subclass covers processes that force liquids, gases or vapours through slivers or rovings-continuous bundles of fibers-to treat them. Such treatments include washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation of the textile material before it is further processed.
[D06B 5/06] through yarns, threads or filaments -- The D06B 5/06 subclass covers processes that force liquids, gases or vapours through individual yarns, threads or filaments to treat textile material. This includes washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating by passing the treatment medium along the length of the fibres rather than across a finished fabric.
[D06B 5/08] through fabrics -- The subclass D06B 5/08 covers methods that force liquids, gases or vapours through textile materials to achieve treatment actions such as washing, dyeing, bleaching or impregnating with sizing agents. It falls under the hierarchy of treating textiles by passing fluids or vapours through the fabric.
[D06B 5/10] using centrifugal force -- The code D06B 5/10 covers textile treatment processes that employ centrifugal force to push liquids, gases or vapours through the fabric, such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating. It is a subclass of treating textile materials using liquids, gases or vapours within the CPC hierarchy.
[D06B 5/12] through materials of definite length -- **Glossary:** - Materials of definite length: have to be understood as textile articles, i.e. garments, webs or yarns, with a definite length which can be subjected to treatment in a "batch" process. For example, yarns wound on cheeses or fabric web wrapped around a drum are considered as materials of definite length.
[D06B 5/14] through fibres, slivers or rovings -- The D06B 5/14 subclass covers processes in which liquids, gases or vapours are forced through individual fibres, slivers or rovings to treat the material, such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating the textile before it is formed into fabric.
[D06B 5/16] through yarns, threads or filaments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for forcing liquids, gases or vapours through textile materials to effect treatment, e.g. packages of yarns stacked on rods located in containers and treated under pressure, e.g. autoclave.
[D06B 5/18] through beamed warp -- D06B 5/18 covers processes in which liquids, gases or vapours are forced through a warp that has been beamed to treat the yarns. The method is used for operations such as washing, dyeing, bleaching or sizing of the warp before weaving. It falls under the broader class of treating textile materials with fluids.
[D06B 5/20] through hanks -- The subclass D06B 5/20 covers processes in which liquids, gases or vapours are forced through hanks of yarn or similar continuous textile material to achieve treatment such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It relates to the method of passing the treating medium through the yarn bundle rather than applying it from the outside. This enables uniform penetration and efficient modification of the textile fibers.
[D06B 5/22] through fabrics -- The subclass D06B 5/22 covers processes that force liquids, gases or vapours through textile materials to achieve treatments such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation. It is part of the hierarchy for treating textile materials using liquids, gases or vapours within the broader field of textile treatment.
[D06B 5/24] through articles, e.g. stockings -- The CPC subclass D06B 5/24 covers processes in which liquids, gases or vapours are forced through textile articles - such as stockings - to achieve treatment operations including washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnation.
[D06B 5/26] using centrifugal force -- The CPC subclass D06B 5/26 covers textile treatment processes that employ centrifugal force to drive liquids, gases or vapours through fabrics. It includes operations such as washing, dyeing, bleaching, sizing or impregnating where rotation creates the needed pressure for the treatment.
[D06B 7/00] Mercerising, e.g. lustring by mercerising -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for mercerising textile materials.  **Glossary:** - Mercerisation: is a chemical treatment for cellulosic textile materials which gives the materials a lustrous appearance and improves the strength and affinity for dyes. The treatment involves immersion under tension in a caustic soda solution, which is later neutralised in acid. The treatment produces a permanent swelling of the fibre.
[D06B 7/02] of slivers
[D06B 7/04] of yarns, threads or filaments -- The D06B 7/04 subclass covers mercerising processes applied to yarns, threads or filaments, where the textile material is treated with caustic solutions, gases or vapours to swell the fibres, increase luster and improve dye uptake. It falls under the broader categories of textile treatment and liquid, gas or vapour based methods for treating textile materials.
[D06B 7/06] of hanks of yarns -- The D06B 7/06 subclass covers mercerising or lustring processes applied to hanks of yarn, a treatment that uses liquids, gases or vapours to modify the fibre surface and enhance luster and other properties. It falls under the broader group for treating textile materials with such fluids.
[D06B 7/08] of fabrics of indefinite length -- **Glossary:** - Fabrics of indefinite length: (e.g. continuous webs) are materials having the characteristics that the longitudinal dimension is effectively infinite in so far as the procedure for advancing or moving the material is concerned (to distinguish from a "batch" treatment).
[D06B 7/10] of tubular fabrics or tubular articles -- The D06B 7/10 subclass relates to mercerising operations applied to tubular fabrics or tubular articles such as hoses, sleeves, socks or tube-shaped textiles. It covers processes that use liquids, gases or vapours to modify the surface luster, strength, shrinkage or other properties of these cylindrical textile products.
[D06B 9/00] Solvent-treatment of textile materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for solvent (e.g. ammonia) treatment of textile materials.
[D06B 9/02] solvent-dyeing -- The D06B 9/02 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers solvent-dyeing processes in which textile materials are coloured by applying dye dissolved in a non-aqueous solvent, often using gases, vapours or liquid carriers. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles, and treating textile materials with liquids, gases or vapours.
[D06B 9/04] Successively applying two or more different solvent-based treating materials -- This subclass covers processes where textile fibers or fabrics are treated by sequential application of distinct solvent-based chemicals such as dyes, finishes or cleaning agents, each applied after the previous one to impart layered functional properties.
[D06B 9/06] with recovery of the solvent -- D06B 9/06 covers processes for treating textile materials with solvents in which the solvent is recovered and reused after the treatment. The entry falls under the broader categories of textile treatment using liquids, gases or vapours and focuses on solvent-recycling methods that enhance efficiency and reduce waste.
[D06C] FINISHING, DRESSING, TENTERING OR STRETCHING TEXTILE FABRICS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of textile fabrics  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treating textile materials by liquids, gases or vapours -> D06B - Chemical bleaching, dry-cleaning or washing fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics -> D06L - Dyeing or printing textiles -> D06P - Decorating textiles -> D06Q - Machines and apparatus for drying fabrics, fibres, yarns, or other materials in long lengths, with progressive movement -> F26B13/00  **Glossary:** - Textile fabrics: include yarns in warp or sheet form and nonwovens
[D06C 11/00] Teasing, napping or otherwise roughening or raising pile of textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatuses for teasing, napping or otherwise roughening or raising pile of textile fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For making patterns or designs -> D06C23/02  **Glossary:** - Napping: is a finishing process that raises the surface fibres of a fabric by means of passage over rapidly revolving cylinders covered with metal points or teasel burrs. Outing, flannel and wool broadcloth derive their downy appearance from this finishing process. Napping is also used for certain knit goods, blankets and other fabrics with raised surface. - Teasing: is a similar treatment which raises the nap of fabric
[D06C 13/00] Shearing, clipping or cropping surfaces of textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatuses for shearing, clipping or cropping surfaces of textile fabrics, pile cutting or trimming edges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Making patterns or designs on fabrics -> D06C23/02  **Glossary:** - Cropping: is the process of cutting pile on a fabric to uniform height or cutting loose fibres from the surface of a cloth after weaving.
[D06C 13/02] Arrangements for detecting the presence of a seam in the fabric to lift the shearing blade -- D06C 13/02 pertains to arrangements that sense a seam in a textile fabric and automatically raise the shearing blade, enabling precise shearing, clipping or cropping of fabric surfaces during finishing operations.
[D06C 13/04] Shearing lace or embroidery, e.g. cutting loose threads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shearing lace or embroidery, i.e. removing threads protruding from the surface.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatuses and methods specially adapted for cutting lace or embroidery -> D06H7/16
[D06C 13/06] Removing floats -- The D06C 13/06 subclass covers processes for removing floats-unattached or protruding yarns- from the surface of textile fabrics. Techniques include shearing, clipping or other mechanical actions that smooth the fabric, improving its appearance and functional properties.
[D06C 13/08] Cutting pile loops -- D06C 13/08 is a subclass of textile finishing that deals with cutting the loops of pile fabrics such as carpets, rugs or fleece. It covers methods and apparatus for shearing, clipping or cropping the pile loops to modify surface texture, reduce thickness or create decorative patterns. This operation is performed after the fabric has been woven or knitted and before it is put into final use.
[D06C 13/10] Splitting double pile fabrics -- The code D06C 13/10 covers the process of separating the two piles of a double-pile fabric by shearing, clipping or cropping the material to create a split surface. This finishing operation is used to modify texture, appearance or performance of the textile and falls under the broader categories of textile treatment, finishing, dressing, tentering and stretching.
[D06C 13/12] Trimming hosiery or seamed edges of fabrics -- D06C 13/12 covers the shearing, clipping or cropping of hosiery and the removal of seamed edges on textile fabrics, a finishing step that creates a clean, even edge. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of textile fabrics.
[D06C 15/00] Calendering, pressing, ironing, glossing or glazing textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatuses for calendering, pressing, ironing , glossing or glazing textile fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Making patterns or designs by pressing (e.g. by embossing) -> D06C23/00
[D06C 15/02] between co-operating press or calender rolls -- The D06C 15/02 subclass covers textile finishing operations that use two cooperating press or calender rolls to apply simultaneous heat and pressure for effects such as pressing, ironing, glazing or glossing of fabrics. It falls under the broader categories of textile treatment, finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching, and specifically addresses roll-to-roll contact methods that produce a smooth, glossy or otherwise treated surface.
[D06C 15/04] between rollers and co-operating concave surfaces -- This subclass covers finishing operations where a textile web is passed between a rotating roller and a cooperating concave surface. The configuration is used to apply pressure, heat or shear for calendering, ironing, glossing or glazing of fabrics.
[D06C 15/06] between rollers and co-operating moving surfaces formed of flexible material, e.g. bands -- This subclass covers calendering processes where the zone between rollers is filled with flexible moving elements such as bands that assist in pressing, glossing, or stretching textile fabrics. It includes devices that use such bands as cooperating surfaces to improve uniform pressure and fabric handling.
[D06C 15/08] Rollers therefor -- The D06C 15/08 subclass covers rollers employed in calendering, pressing, ironing, glossing or glazing of textile fabrics, i.e., devices that provide pressure and heat to finish or stretch fabrics.
[D06C 15/10] between flat plates of a press -- The D06C 15/10 subclass relates to processes in which a textile fabric is treated while positioned between two flat plates of a press. This covers calendering, pressing, ironing, glossing or glazing operations where the fabric is subjected to pressure and/or heat between the plates to achieve a smooth, shiny, or stretched finish. It is used for finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching textile fabrics.
[D06C 15/12] for pressing a pile of fabrics -- The CPC subclass D06C 15/12 relates to the finishing step of pressing a pile of fabrics, where a stack of textile material is subjected to pressure-often with heat-to compact, smooth, or set its surface. It falls within the hierarchy of textile treatments, specifically under finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of fabrics.
[D06C 15/14] Beetling -- **Glossary:** - Beetling: Is a process in which round-thread linen or cotton fabric is pounded to give a flat effect. Beetled linen damask has an increased lustre and a leather-like texture. Beetling is also used to give a thready or linen-like appearance to cotton.
[D06C 17/00] Fulling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatuses for fulling.  **Glossary:** - fulling: is a finishing process used usually in the manufacture of woolen and worsted fabrics. The cloth is subjected to moisture, heat friction, chemicals, and pressure which cause it to mat and shrink appreciably in both the warp and filling directions, resulting in a denser, more compact fabric.
[D06C 17/02] by rollers -- Fulling by rollers is a mechanical process that consolidates woven or knitted fabric through pressure and moisture, using roller devices to compact the fibers, reduce thickness, and increase density. This method is employed in textile finishing to improve fabric hand, drape, and dimensional stability.
[D06C 17/04] by hammers or beaters -- The D06C 17/04 subclass covers fulling operations in which textile fabrics are treated with hammers or beaters to compact, clean and shrink the material. This mechanical finishing method is applied especially to woolen or similar fabrics to increase density, improve texture and enhance the overall durability of the fabric.
[D06C 19/00] Breaking or softening of fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for breaking or softening of fabrics.
[D06C 21/00] Shrinking by compressing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compressive devices and corresponding methods carrying out the shrinking action by transporting the fabric between a moving elastic belt and a rotating heated drum, whereby the elastic belt is partially wrapped around the heated drum (called also " sanforizing")  Devices for microcreping. Microcreping, sometimes called "dry microcreping", refers to longitudinal treatment of travelling fabric under substantially dry conditions in which a drive force is produced by pressing the fabric against a drive roll. This positively propels the material through a confined retarding passage, with microcreping action on the material occurring in the transition between driving and retarding regions. Because such microcreping does not depend upon adhesion of the material to the drive surface or a wet condition of the material, a particularly wide range of properties is obtainable. Note that the dry microcreping here must not be confused with wet creping or creping based on adhesion, performed for instance on a Yankee dryer.
[D06C 21/005] Compacting belts -- Compacting belts are conveyor-type belts used in textile finishing to apply compressive forces that cause controlled shrinkage or densification of fabrics during processes such as tentering, stretching, or finishing. They work by pressing the material between rotating or moving belt surfaces, reducing thickness and improving dimensional stability. This equipment falls under CPC D06C 21/005 for shrinking by compressing textiles.
[D06C 23/00] Making patterns or designs on fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Decorating fabrics by mechanical means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Printing textiles -> B41F16/02 , B41F17/003 , B41F17/38 - Decorating by partial dyeing -> D06B11/00 - Reserving parts of the material before dyeing or printing -> D06P5/12 - Decorating textiles by chemical means -> D06Q1/00
[D06C 23/02] by singeing, teasing, shearing, etching or brushing -- D06C 23/02 relates to methods of forming patterns or designs on textile fabrics by means such as singeing, teasing, shearing, etching or brushing. These processes are used in the finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of textiles.
[D06C 23/04] by shrinking, embossing, moiréing, or crêping -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Making of patterns, designs or motifs obtained by shrinking, embossing, moiréing or crêping a fabric. In particular making of patterns obtained by shrinking include tie-dyed and shrunk garments or fabrics.  **Glossary:** - Moiré: is a wavy or watered effect on a textile fabric, especially a corded fabric of silk, rayon or a manufactured fibre. Moiré is produced by passing the fabric between engraved cylinders which press the design into the material, causing the crushed and uncrushed parts the reflect light differently - Crêping: is a treatment imparted by wrinkling or embossing a fabric to give crimped surface and greater fabric bulk
[D06C 25/00] Treating selvedges or other edges, e.g. stiffening -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment for consolidating the edges of a fabric by several means, e.g. with a resin coating, a bonded tape etc.  **Glossary:** - Selvedge: or selvage is the narrow edge of woven fabric that runs parallel to the warp. It is made with stronger yarns in a tighter construction than the body of the fabric to prevent ravelling. A fast selvage encloses all or part of the picks, and a selvage is not fast when the filling threads are cut at the fabric edge after every pick.
[D06C 27/00] Compound processes or apparatus, for finishing or dressing textile fabrics, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compound processes or apparatuses, i.e. involving two or more treatments in succession, for finishing or dressing textile fabrics, not otherwise provided for.
[D06C 2700/00] Finishing or decoration of textile materials, except for bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling -- The classification D06C 2700/00 covers methods and processes for finishing or decorating textile materials, excluding operations such as bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling, and relates to the broader categories of textiles, their treatment, and the finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of fabric.
[D06C 2700/02] Tenters for tentering or drying fabrics -- D06C 2700/02 pertains to tenters employed in the finishing of textiles, specifically machines that stretch and dry fabric by holding it under tension while moisture is removed, ensuring uniform dimensions and surface quality.
[D06C 2700/04] Tenters or driers for fabrics without diagonal displacement -- The CPC entry D06C 2700/04 covers machines that tension or dry textile fabrics while keeping the material aligned straight, i.e., without any diagonal or sideways displacement. These devices are used in the finishing stage of textile production to improve fabric stability and dimensional control before further processing.
[D06C 2700/05] Tenters or driers for fabrics with diagonal displacement -- This subclass covers machines that tension or dry textile fabrics while moving them diagonally across the device, providing stretch or heat treatment in a diagonal direction. Such equipment is used to improve dimensional stability and performance of woven or knitted materials.
[D06C 2700/06] Clips or accessories for tenters or driers -- The subclass D06C 2700/06 pertains to clips, clamps and other ancillary devices employed in the finishing of textile fabrics during tenting or drying operations. These accessories secure, guide or support the material on tenters, dryers or related equipment, facilitating uniform treatment without involving bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling.
[D06C 2700/09] Various apparatus for drying textiles -- This subclass covers equipment used to remove moisture from textile materials after processing, such as dryers, heated chambers and related devices. It applies to drying apparatus for fabrics, excluding methods for bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling.
[D06C 2700/10] Guides or expanders for finishing -- Guides or expanders for finishing are devices used during the final treatment of textile fabrics to control tension, shape or dimensional change, such as stretching or tentering, without involving processes like bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling. They enable precise manipulation of fabrics as they pass through finishing equipment, improving uniformity and mechanical properties. This falls under the textile finishing subclass D06C 2700/10.
[D06C 2700/13] Steaming or decatising of fabrics or yarns -- This CPC subclass D06C 2700/13 covers the process of steaming or decatising textile fabrics or yarns, a finishing operation that removes wrinkles and sets dimensions without involving bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling. It is used for treatments that improve hand, shape, or stability by applying steam, heat and moisture.
[D06C 2700/135] Moistening of fabrics or yarns as a complementary treatment -- The code refers to processes that apply moisture to textile fabrics or yarns as a supplementary step in finishing operations, such as conditioning, humidification or moistening to improve handling, flexibility or preparation for subsequent treatments, while excluding primary dyeing, printing, bleaching and similar treatments.
[D06C 2700/15] Brushing or beating of fabrics for finishing but not for cleaning -- The CPC code D06C 2700/15 covers methods of mechanically treating textile fabrics by brushing or beating to improve surface appearance, texture or nap without cleaning. It is a finishing process distinct from bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling.
[D06C 2700/16] Singeing of fabrics or yarns -- A process that momentarily passes flame or heat over textile surfaces to burn off protruding fibers, producing a smoother, cleaner fabric or yarn and preventing pilling in later use. It is classified under finishing treatments of textiles and is distinct from dyeing, printing, mercerizing or washing operations.
[D06C 2700/17] Flat presses, pressing plates or heating devices for pressing plates -- This subgroup covers equipment that applies uniform pressure and heat to textiles during finishing operations, such as flat presses, pressing plates and associated heating elements, used for shaping, setting, or consolidating fabrics.
[D06C 2700/21] Calenders for drying fabrics -- Calenders designed to dry fabric are covered under this classification, which falls within textile finishing and dressing processes that do not involve bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling. The entry includes apparatus that combines pressure rollers and heat to remove moisture from textiles during treatment, improving dimensional stability and preparation for subsequent finishing steps.
[D06C 2700/26] Glossing of fabrics or yarns, e.g. silk-finish -- Glossing of fabrics or yarns, such as a silk-finish, is a surface treatment applied to textile products to impart a smooth, reflective sheen without involving bleaching, dyeing, printing, mercerising, washing or fulling. It falls under CPC code D06C 2700/26 within the finishing of textile fabrics.
[D06C 2700/29] Mechanical finishing of velvet or needled velvet -- This CPC subgroup covers mechanical operations applied to velvet or needled velvet to alter its surface texture or physical properties, such as brushing, crushing, raising, napping, calendering or other mechanical finishing techniques, without the use of chemical treatments.
[D06C 2700/31] Methods for making patterns on fabrics, e.g. by application of powder dye, moiréing, embossing
[D06C 29/00] Finishing or dressing, of textile fabrics, not provided for in the preceding groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Finishing or dressing of textile fabrics, not provided for in the preceding groups.
[D06C 29/005] hydroentangling -- Hydroentangling is a finishing method where high-velocity water jets are directed onto a web of fibers to interlock them, producing a non-woven or mechanically bonded fabric without additional binders. It is used for textiles that need a soft hand, adjustable thickness, or enhanced durability, and falls under CPC D06C 29/005.
[D06C 3/00] Stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shaping or stretching tubular fabrics upon cores or internal frames -> D06C5/00  **Glossary:** - Tentering: sets the warp and weft of woven fabrics at right angles to each other, and stretches and sets the fabric to its final dimensions. The fabric is stretched by the use of a tenter frame, consisting of chains fitted with pins or clips to hold the selvedges of the fabric, and travelling on tracks. As the fabric passes through a heated chamber, creases and wrinkles are removed, the weave is straightened, and the fabric is dried to its final size.
[D06C 3/02] by endless chain or like apparatus -- This subclass covers methods of stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics by means of an endless chain or similar apparatus. It belongs to CPC group D06C 3/02, which addresses finishing processes that tension or extend fabrics continuously.
[D06C 3/021] Biaxial stretching -- The entry D06C 3/021 details a process in which a textile fabric is stretched simultaneously in two mutually perpendicular directions, known as biaxial stretching, to enhance dimensional stability, orientation, and mechanical properties, often preceding further dyeing or finishing operations.
[D06C 3/023] Chains -- The D06C 3/023 entry covers chains used in the stretching, tentering or spreading of textile fabrics. It includes chain-driven mechanisms, endless chains or chain loops that convey, tension or support fabric during finishing operations. The classification is part of the CPC group for textile treatment.
[D06C 3/025] Driving or guiding means for stenter chains  chains  per se   D06C3/023
[D06C 3/026] Detection means for controlling lateral position of the textile material -- D06C 3/026 relates to detection means that monitor the lateral position of a textile web while it is being stretched, tentered or spread, providing feedback for precise alignment control. The devices are used in finishing processes to ensure uniform positioning of the fabric during treatment.
[D06C 3/028] Devices for feeding material onto or removing it from stenders -- D06C 3/028 covers equipment that feeds fabric onto stender machines or removes it after processing, enabling continuous stretching, tentering or spreading of textile webs. Such devices include rollers, conveyors, suction trays or clamp mechanisms designed to handle the material safely during the finishing stage.
[D06C 3/04] Tentering clips -- Tentering clips are small mechanical devices that grip the edges of a fabric sheet during tentering, keeping the material under uniform tension while it is stretched, dried or heat-set. They enable precise control of fabric dimensions and help prevent distortion during finishing processes.
[D06C 3/06] by rotary disc, roller, or like apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. spreading rollers with spiral patterns to spread the fabric or stretching rollers with reliefs on the roller surface to obtain fabric stretching.
[D06C 3/062] acting on the selvedges of the material only -- D06C 3/062 covers processes for stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics in which the operation is performed only on the selvedges, the edge strands of the material. It is a subclass of finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of textiles that restricts the treatment to the fabric margins rather than the entire surface.
[D06C 3/065] with uncurling of the edges of the fabric -- D06C 3/065 relates to processes for stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics in which the edges of the material are uncurl-ed or straightened. The subclass specifies techniques that simultaneously tension the fabric while correcting edge curl, improving dimensional stability and surface uniformity. It falls under the broader group dealing with finishing, dressing, and mechanical treatment of textile fabrics.
[D06C 3/067] by curved rollers -- The D06C 3/067 subclass covers methods of stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics that use curved rollers to apply tension and shape to the material.
[D06C 3/08] by frames or like apparatus -- This subgroup covers methods for stretching, tentering or spreading textile fabrics using frames, clamps or similar devices that hold the fabric while tension is applied. It includes apparatus such as tenter frames, stretching frames and related mechanisms.
[D06C 3/10] Hooks, pin-blocks, or like apparatus -- The CPC subclass D06C 3/10 covers devices used in the stretching, tentering or spreading of textile fabrics that employ hooks, pin-blocks or similar apparatus to hold, guide or tension the material during treatment. These apparatuses secure the fabric at discrete points, enabling controlled deformation for finishing processes such as setting dimensions or improving drape.
[D06C 5/00] Shaping or stretching of tubular fabrics upon cores or internal frames -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The shaping or stretching of tubular fabrics upon cores or internal frames.
[D06C 5/005] of articles, e.g. stockings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses and methods for the shaping or stretching of tubular fabrics in article form, e.g. apparatuses for loading stockings, pantihose (panty hoses) or socks on boards of fixing/hot-setting machines.
[D06C 7/00] Heating or cooling textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method and apparatuses for heating or cooling textile fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Heating or cooling stockings (e.g. hosiery boarding) -> D06C5/005 - Heating during singeing -> D06C9/00 - Shaping by thermoforming textile or other fibrous material made from plastics fibres -> B29C51/004 - Machines and apparatuses for drying fabrics, fibres, yarns or other materials in long lengths, with progressive movement -> F26B13/00
[D06C 7/02] Setting -- The D06C 7/02 subclass covers processes for setting textile fabrics, where heat, steam, or cooling is applied to fix dimensions, shape, or mechanical properties after finishing operations such as dyeing, finishing or stretching. It includes methods that stabilize the fabric structure by controlled temperature treatment.
[D06C 7/04] Carbonising or oxidising -- The D06C 7/04 class covers thermal processes that carbonise or oxidise textile fabrics, such as controlled heating in oxidising atmospheres to change colour, improve flame resistance or create specific surface characteristics. These treatments are applied during finishing operations that modify the fabric's physical or chemical properties.
[D06C 9/00] Singeing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and apparatuses for singeing textile fabrics.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For making patterns or designs -> D06C23/02  **Glossary:** - Singeing: Is a process of burning off protruding fibres from fabrics to give the fabrics a smooth surface
[D06C 9/02] by flame -- The D06C 9/02 subclass covers the singeing of textile fabrics using an open flame to remove protruding fibers and surface fuzz, thereby improving smoothness and appearance. It falls under finishing processes that treat textiles after weaving or knitting.
[D06C 9/04] by contact with heated elements -- This subclass covers singeing of textile fabrics achieved by direct contact with heated elements, such as rollers or plates, to remove protruding fibers. It falls under the CPC group for finishing, dressing, tentering or stretching of textiles, specifically the treatment of textiles by heating.
[D06F] LAUNDERING, DRYING, IRONING, PRESSING OR FOLDING TEXTILE ARTICLES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The Indexing Codes D06F2101/00 - D06F2101/20 cover user input for the control of domestic laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.  The Indexing Codes D06F2103/00 - D06F2103/70 cover parameters monitored or detected for the control of domestic laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.  The Indexing Codes D06F2105/00 - D06F2105/62 cover systems or parameters controlled or affected by the control systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.  **Glossary:** - Drum: Washing receptacle, rotating drum, washing drum or washing tub; the washing receptacle or drum is where the clothes are placed. Drums are generally perforated and are usually the rotating part of the washing machine, however, in D06F13/00 and subgroups, D06F15/00 and subgroups, D06F17/00 and subgroups, D06F18/00 and subgroups and D06F19/00 and subgroups,the drum is always stationary. - Tub: Enclosing receptacle, external drum, external tub or fixed tub; the tub is the fixed part of the washing assembly, it contains the washing drum as well as the washing fluid; the tub is generally stationary and water-tight. - Washing assembly: Oscillating assembly, oscillating part or suspended part; it is generally composed by both the drum and the tub. - Lifter: Rib, elements mounted to a rotating drum and acting mainly on the clothes - Agitator:: Rotary and/or oscillating device mounted inside a stationary washing drum and acting mainly on the clothes - Impeller: Rotary devices mounted inside a stationary washing drum and acting mainly on the washing liquid
[D06F 1/00] Washing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Washing receptacles suitable for hand washing (e.g. wash-up bowls).  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/02] Wash-tubs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wash-up bowls per se.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/04] the tub walls or bottom having corrugations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/06] Wash-boiler receptacles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wash-up bowls (generally made of metal) which can be heated by e.g. a gas burner.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/08] with special means for water circulation by heat, e.g. fountain-washers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wash-up bowls (generally made of metal) comprising means for concentrating the heat of e.g. a burner and for applying it in such a way (e.g. to a hot water circuit), that steam within the boiler is quickly generated and, together with hot water, it is discharged on the garments to be washed.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/10] Covers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 11/00] Washing machines using rollers, e.g. of the mangle type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 1/12] Racks or other rigid open containers of skeleton or like apertured construction supporting clothes in washing receptacles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wire frame baskets or perforated metal containers suitable for supporting clothes in washing receptacles.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 13/00] Washing machines having receptacles, stationary for washing purposes, with agitators therein contacting the articles being washed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vertical axis washing machines provided with laundry agitators.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plungers or the like adapted for removable mounting on receptacles -> D06F5/04
[D06F 13/02] wherein the agitator has an oscillatory rotary motion only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Glossary:** - Agitator: Rotary and/or oscillating device mounted inside a stationary washing drum and acting mainly on the clothes
[D06F 13/04] wherein the agitator has an axial motion only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 13/06] wherein the agitator has both rotary, e.g. oscillating rotary, motion and axial motion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 13/08] wherein the agitator has a gyratory or orbital motion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vertical axis washing machines, wherein the agitator rotates and/or has an orbital motion.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 15/00] Washing machines having beating, rubbing or squeezing means in receptacles stationary for washing purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plungers or the like adapted for removable mounting on receptacles -> D06F5/04 - Mangle-type washing machines -> D06F11/00
[D06F 15/02] wherein the articles being washed are squeezed by a flexible diaphragm or bag -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 17/00] Washing machines having receptacles, stationary for washing purposes, wherein the washing action is effected solely by circulation or agitation of the washing liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Washing machines, wherein the washing action is only obtained by circulating or agitating the washing fluid.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[D06F 17/12] solely by gases, e.g. air or steam, introduced into the washing liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Washing machines technically close to dishwashers (no drum is present).
[D06F 18/00] Washing machines having receptacles, stationary for washing purposes, and having further drying means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Control of washing or of washing and drying -> D06F33/00 , D06F34/00 - Domestic laundry dryers -> D06F58/00
[D06F 19/00] Washing machines using vibrations for washing purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Washing machines comprising vibrating devices.
[D06F 21/00] Washing machines with receptacles, e.g. perforated, having a rotary movement, e.g. oscillatory movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General concepts regarding the washing machine as a whole;  Machines which do not necessarily spin-dry and which may comprise lifters and/or impellers.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Washing machines with receptacles moving bodily about an axis -> D06F27/00 - Programme-control aspects of washing machines -> D06F33/00
[D06F 2101/00] Indexing scheme associated with groups
[D06F 2101/02] Characteristics of laundry or load -- The D06F 2101/02 subgroup covers specifications of a laundry load such as its size, weight, fabric composition, level of soiling, and other physical attributes that affect how textiles are washed, dried, ironed, pressed, or folded. These characteristics guide the selection of appropriate treatment parameters in textile processing.
[D06F 2101/04] Quantity, e.g. weight -- The CPC code D06F 2101/04 refers to laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding apparatuses that include means for measuring or controlling the quantity of textile articles, such as their weight, during the treatment process. It classifies inventions that monitor or adjust operation based on the amount of fabric present.
[D06F 2101/06] Type or material -- The code D06F 2101/06 designates patents that classify laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding operations according to the type of material of the textile article, distinguishing processes, devices or parameters specific to particular fabrics such as cotton, polyester or blends.
[D06F 2101/10] Spin speed -- The CPC subclass D06F 2101/10 covers the spin speed of washing machines and other laundering equipment, detailing how rapidly the drum rotates during the spin cycle to remove water from textiles. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles, and laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents related to spin-speed control mechanisms.
[D06F 2101/12] Washing temperature -- The CPC subclass D06F 2101/12 defines the parameters for washing temperature used in laundering textile articles. It is nested under textiles, treatment of textiles, and laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles, specifying how temperature is selected or controlled in washing processes.
[D06F 2101/14] Time settings -- D06F 2101/14 covers the specification of time parameters in processes for treating textiles such as laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding. It includes devices or methods that allow users to set, adjust or control the duration of each operation to achieve desired results.
[D06F 2101/16] Target humidity for the drying process, e.g. very-dry cycles -- D06F 2101/16 covers methods that control the humidity level during textile drying, specifying target moisture conditions such as very-dry cycles to improve energy efficiency or product quality. It falls under laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles within the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[D06F 2101/18] Target temperature for the drying process, e.g. low-temperature cycles -- The CPC code D06F 2101/18 covers specifications of the target temperature used during the drying of textile articles, such as setting low-temperature drying cycles to protect fabrics or reduce energy consumption. It falls under the broader class for laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textiles.
[D06F 2101/20] Operation modes, e.g. delicate laundry washing programs, service modes or refreshment cycles -- The CPC subclass D06F 2101/20 covers operation modes for laundering machines, including specialized wash programs for delicate fabrics, service cycles and refreshment functions that adjust parameters such as temperature, duration and mechanical action. It is situated under textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles.

[D06F 2103/00] Parameters monitored or detected for the control of domestic laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers -- This CPC subclass covers the variables sensed or measured to regulate the operation of household laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers. It includes monitoring of temperature, water level, load weight, spin speed, detergent concentration and related sensor data used for cycle control and energy efficiency.
[D06F 2103/02] Characteristics of laundry or load -- The code D06F 2103/02 covers information on the properties of a laundry load that are monitored or detected to control domestic washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers. It includes parameters such as load size, weight, fabric composition, moisture content, soil level or temperature that are used to select or adjust washing, drying or ironing cycles.
[D06F 2103/04] Quantity, e.g. weight or variation of weight -- The code D06F 2103/04 concerns measuring the quantity of laundry, such as weight or its variation, for controlling cycles in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It falls under the textile-treatment hierarchy that includes laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles.
[D06F 2103/06] Type or material -- The CPC class D06F 2103/06 covers parameters that domestic laundry machines monitor or detect concerning the type or material of textile items during washing, drying, ironing, pressing or folding. It pertains to the control logic used in household washers, washer-dryers and dryers to select appropriate cycles based on fabric composition.
[D06F 2103/08] Humidity -- Humidity in this context is the detection or measurement of moisture levels inside domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers to regulate washing, rinsing, drying or ironing cycles. Monitoring humidity enables optimal water use, cycle timing and energy efficiency.
[D06F 2103/10] expressed as capacitance or resistance -- This CPC subclass covers sensors or control techniques that obtain laundry-machine operating parameters from measured electrical capacitance or resistance. It includes methods for detecting load size, water content or fabric condition in domestic washers, washer-dryers or dryers using these electrical properties.
[D06F 2103/12] Temperature -- The D06F 2103/12 subclass concerns the monitoring and detection of temperature parameters in domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers, providing data used to control washing, drying, ironing or folding cycles. It falls within the CPC hierarchy for textile treatment, specifically laundering processes.
[D06F 2103/14] Supply, recirculation or draining of washing liquid -- This subclass deals with mechanisms and methods that provide fresh washing water, recirculate used water, or remove water from the drum in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers. It includes pumps, valves, reservoirs and control strategies for supplying, reusing or draining the washing liquid during the laundry cycle.
[D06F 2103/16] Washing liquid temperature -- The subclass D06F 2103/16 covers the monitoring or detection of the temperature of the washing liquid in domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers, or dryers. It is part of the broader category dealing with the treatment of textiles, specifically laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles. This parameter is used to control the washing cycle and ensure appropriate cleaning conditions.
[D06F 2103/18] Washing liquid level -- The CPC entry D06F 2103/18 covers the detection or monitoring of washing-liquid level in domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It relates to control parameters used to manage the amount of water-based detergent introduced during laundering, drying or ironing cycles for textile articles. This falls under the broader class for treatment of textiles, specifically launder-ing, drying, ironing, pressing or folding.
[D06F 2103/20] Washing liquid condition, e.g. turbidity -- This subclass concerns detection or monitoring of characteristics of the washing liquid, such as turbidity, during operation of household laundry machines. It falls under treatment of textiles, specifically laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding. Used for control systems of washers, washer-dryers, and dryers.
[D06F 2103/22] Content of detergent or additives -- The subclass D06F 2103/22 covers the detection or monitoring of detergent and other additive levels in domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers, enabling the appliance to adjust cycles, water usage or rinsing based on the measured content.
[D06F 2103/24] Spin speed -- D06F 2103/24, titled Spin speed, is part of the CPC group for textiles that deals with laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles. It covers the monitoring or detection of spin speed as a control parameter in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.
[D06F 2103/26] Imbalance -- The D06F 2103/26 imbalance subclass covers methods and sensors that detect uneven load distribution in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, enabling the device to adjust operation for stable spinning and optimal performance.
[D06F 2103/28] Air properties -- The code D06F 2103/28 covers features that monitor or detect air-related characteristics such as temperature, humidity, flow or composition within household washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. These air-property sensors are used to optimise laundering cycles, drying performance and energy use.
[D06F 2103/30] Pressure -- This subclass concerns the measurement or regulation of pressure inside household washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It includes sensors and control strategies that detect water or drum pressure to adjust cycle duration, water consumption or drying temperature, improving washing efficiency.
[D06F 2103/32] Temperature -- This subclass concerns the detection and regulation of temperature in household laundry appliances such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers, covering sensors, controllers and methods that monitor and adjust heating during washing, rinsing or drying cycles.
[D06F 2103/34] Humidity -- The CPC subclass D06F 2103/34 refers to the detection or monitoring of humidity for controlling domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It falls under the treatment of textiles, specifically the laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding stages, and is used to optimise moisture levels during these processes.
[D06F 2103/36] Flow or velocity -- The CPC subclass D06F 2103/36 covers the detection or monitoring of flow or velocity parameters in domestic laundry equipment such as washing machines, washer-dryers or tumble dryers, used to control washing, drying, ironing, pressing or folding cycles. It falls under the broader group for treatment of textiles, specifically laundering and drying processes.
[D06F 2103/38] Time, e.g. duration -- The CPC subclass D06F 2103/38 covers the monitoring or detection of time-related parameters, such as the duration of a cycle, in domestic laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It is situated under treatment of textiles, specifically laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding, and deals with control functions that use timing information to regulate these processes.
[D06F 2103/40] Opening or locking status of doors -- This CPC subclass D06F 2103/40 covers the detection or monitoring of the opening, closing, locking or unlocking status of doors on domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers. It is employed for control functions such as safety interlocks, cycle-start inhibition or status indication during laundering, drying or ironing. The subclass is nested under textiles → treatment of textiles → laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles.
[D06F 2103/42] related to filters or pumps -- The CPC subclass D06F 2103/42 covers features of domestic laundry machines that involve filters or pumps, such as components that remove lint, control water flow, or circulate liquids during washing, drying, or combined washer-dryer cycles. It pertains to the monitoring and control parameters of these elements in household washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers.
[D06F 2103/44] Current or voltage -- The D06F 2103/44 subclass covers the monitoring of electrical parameters such as current or voltage to control the operation of domestic laundry machines, including washing machines, washer-dryers, and dryers. It relates to sensors, measurement circuits, or control logic that use these electrical signals to regulate washing cycles, drying phases, or other laundering functions.
[D06F 2103/46] of the motor driving the drum -- This subclass deals with the detection or monitoring of characteristics of the motor that drives the drum in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers. [D06F 2103/48] of the motor driving the pump -- The code D06F 2103/48 refers to the monitoring or detection of parameters of the motor that drives the pump in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, situated within the broader category of textile treatment, specifically laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles.
[D06F 2103/50] related to heat pumps, e.g. pressure or flow rate -- The subclass D06F 2103/50 pertains to the detection or monitoring of heat-pump related parameters, such as pressure or flow rate, in domestic laundry machines for controlling washing, drying, ironing or folding operations. It is part of the textile-treatment group covering launder-related equipment.
[D06F 2103/52] related to electric heating means, e.g. temperature or voltage -- This CPC entry D06F 2103/52 covers parameters monitored or detected for controlling domestic laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers, specifically related to electric heating means such as temperature or voltage. It falls under treatment of textiles, laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles.
[D06F 2103/54] related to blowers or fans -- This subclass covers features of laundry washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers that involve blowers or fans used to move air for drying or drying-assisted cycles. It falls under the broader group for treatment of textiles, specifically laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding.
[D06F 2103/56] related to air ducts, e.g. position of flow diverters -- A D06F 2103/56 feature pertains to the air-duct system of domestic laundry machines, such as washers, washer-dryers or dryers, and includes the placement or adjustment of flow-diverting components to optimise airflow and drying performance. It is classified under textile treatment processes like laundering and drying.
[D06F 2103/58] related to condensation, e.g. condensate water level -- D06F 2103/58 is a CPC subclass in the textiles-laundering hierarchy that relates to monitoring parameters for household washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers, specifically those concerning condensation such as the level of condensate water.
[D06F 2103/60] related to auxiliary conditioning or finishing agents, e.g. filling level of perfume tanks -- The code D06F 2103/60 covers control parameters in domestic washing, washer-dryer or dryer machines that monitor auxiliary conditioning or finishing agents, for example the level of perfume in a tank, within the broader field of textile laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding.
[D06F 2103/62] related to systems for water or steam used for conditioning or finishing -- This CPC subclass concerns domestic laundry equipment where water or steam systems are used to condition or finish textiles, covering the parameters that are monitored or detected for controlling washing, drying or combined washer-dryer cycles. It falls under textiles-laundering-ironing-folding.
[D06F 2103/64] Radiation, e.g. microwaves -- This subclass covers the use of electromagnetic radiation such as microwaves for monitoring or controlling parameters like temperature, moisture or load in domestic washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers.
[D06F 2103/66] for treatment of laundry otherwise than by drying, e.g. using UV light -- The CPC subclass D06F 2103/66 covers methods and apparatuses for treating washed laundry without using drying, such as applying ultraviolet light, other radiation, or chemical agents to disinfect, deodorise, or otherwise improve the fabric after washing. It falls under the laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles group.

It includes methods and systems that detect and adjust these variables to achieve specific washing, drying or ironing functions.

It is used to control or optimize washing, drying, ironing or folding processes within the broader textile-care hierarchy.
