It falls under the broader hierarchy of textile treatment, focusing on the operational control aspects of domestic and industrial laundry equipment.
[D06F 2105/02] Water supply -- The D06F 2105/02 entry covers the water-supply aspects of laundry equipment, describing how water is provided, regulated or distributed in washing machines, washer-dryers and dryer units used for laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles. It addresses the systems and parameters that are controlled by the machine's control mechanisms to manage water flow during the treatment of textiles.
[D06F 2105/04] from separate hot and cold water inlets -- The D06F 2105/04 subclass covers washing-machine or dryer control functions that manage the mixing of water drawn from separate hot and cold inlets. It relates to systems or parameters affected by the appliance's control electronics for achieving the desired temperature during laundering, drying, ironing or folding operations.
[D06F 2105/06] Recirculation of washing liquids, e.g. by pumps or diverting valves -- The D06F 2105/06 sub-class covers washing-machine systems that recycle or redirect the liquor during a cycle, such as by using pumps or diverting valves to recirculate water, detergent or rinse solution. It applies to the control of liquid flow in washers, washer-dryers or laundry dryers for improved cleaning efficiency.
[D06F 2105/08] Draining of washing liquids -- The code D06F 2105/08 pertains to washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers and covers the mechanisms and control functions that remove used water or other washing liquids from the drum. It includes drainage channels, pumps, valves and the electronic or mechanical controls that regulate liquid discharge during washing or spin cycles.
[D06F 2105/10] Temperature of washing liquids -- The code D06F 2105/10 designates features that control or monitor the temperature of the liquids used in washing, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles. It includes sensors, thermostatic devices and control algorithms that set, adjust or display the water or detergent temperature in washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers.
[D06F 2105/12] Humidity or dryness of laundry -- The code concerns the control of moisture levels in laundry, covering how washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers regulate humidity or dryness during laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles.
[D06F 2105/14] Temperature of laundry -- This group concerns the temperature parameter of laundry during washing, drying, or related processes as controlled by the control systems of washers, washer-dryers and dryers. It includes methods for measuring, setting, or adjusting the temperature of the laundry load.
[D06F 2105/16] Air properties -- Air properties covers the characteristics of the air involved in laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles, such as temperature, humidity, flow rate, pressure and composition, and includes methods and devices for measuring, adjusting or controlling these parameters within the control systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.
[D06F 2105/18] Pressure -- This subclass relates to pressure aspects in laundry equipment, covering pressure sensors, control of water or air pressure, and related parameters that influence the operation of washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers during laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding cycles.
[D06F 2105/20] Temperature -- The code D06F 2105/20 refers to the temperature parameter employed in the treatment of textiles such as laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding, specifically when it is monitored or regulated by the control systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.
[D06F 2105/22] Humidity -- The CPC subclass D06F 2105/22 "Humidity" relates to the control, monitoring or effect of moisture levels in textile-treating appliances such as washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers, covering how humidity influences laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding operations.
[D06F 2105/24] Flow or velocity -- The D06F 2105/24 subgroup addresses the flow or velocity of water, steam or other fluids involved in laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles, concentrating on how the control systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers regulate or monitor fluid movement.
[D06F 2105/26] Heat pumps -- The code D06F 2105/26 refers to heat-pump arrangements incorporated in washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers. It covers heat-pump devices and their control of temperature or energy flow to improve drying or washing efficiency. The classification is used for inventions that use a heat-pump cycle to recycle heat within laundry appliance systems.
[D06F 2105/28] Electric heating -- D06F 2105/28 Electric heating relates to the use of electric heating elements in laundry appliances such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers for drying, ironing, or pressing textile items. The code covers the design, operation and control of the heating system, including temperature regulation and power management, as part of the overall treatment of textiles.
[D06F 2105/30] Blowers -- The D06F 2105/30 subclass covers blowers that are part of the air-handling systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers. These devices generate or direct airflow to assist in drying, drying-aid, or other treatment steps controlled by the appliance's control system.
[D06F 2105/32] Air flow control means -- The CPC subclass D06F 2105/32 covers air-flow control means used in laundry appliances such as washing machines, washer-dryers and dryers. It pertains to systems or parameters that regulate or are influenced by the airflow during the laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding of textile articles.
[D06F 2105/34] Filtering, e.g. control of lint removal devices -- D06F 2105/34 covers the filtering functions used to control lint-removal devices in washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers. It pertains to the design, operation and regulation of lint filters or similar cleaning mechanisms that are managed by the appliance's control system. This subclass falls under the broader category of laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles.
[D06F 2105/36] Condensing arrangements, e.g. control of water injection therefor -- The code D06F 2105/36 covers condensing arrangements in laundry appliances, specifically methods for controlling water injection to promote condensation during drying cycles in washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers.
[D06F 2105/38] Conditioning or finishing, e.g. control of perfume injection -- D06F 2105/38 relates to methods and apparatus that condition or finish textiles during laundering, specifically the control of perfume or scent injection into the wash or rinse cycle. The entry applies to washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers whose control systems regulate this type of treatment.
[D06F 2105/40] using water or steam -- The subclass D06F 2105/40 covers textile care processes in which water or steam is employed as the primary medium, such as washing, rinsing, steaming, or steam-assist in drying and pressing operations. It pertains to the control of machine parameters that regulate the delivery, temperature, pressure or flow of water or steam within washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers.
[D06F 2105/42] Detergent or additive supply -- This subclass covers the means and methods for supplying detergents or other laundry additives in washing machines, washer-dryers or dryers, focusing on the mechanisms, sensors and control parameters that regulate the timing and quantity of the dispense during wash, rinse or drying cycles.
[D06F 2105/44] Opening, closing or locking of doors -- D06F 2105/44 concerns the mechanisms that open, close or lock doors on washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, including the control-system parameters that operate these functions. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under textiles, laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles.
[D06F 2105/46] Drum speed -- D06F 2105/46 covers the control and adjustment of the drum rotational speed in washing machines, washer-dryers and laundry dryers. It includes mechanisms, sensors and software that set or vary the speed to optimise washing, drying, ironing or folding operations. The code is used to classify inventions that modify or monitor drum speed as a parameter of the machine's control system.
[D06F 2105/48] Drum speed -- The D06F 2105/48 subclass concerns the rotational speed of the drum in washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, including how the speed is set, varied or controlled during operation. It covers the influence of drum speed on washing, drying, ironing, pressing or folding actions for textile articles.
[D06F 2105/50] Starting machine operation, e.g. delayed start or re-start after power cut -- Covers control features for initiating operation of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, such as delayed-start timers and automatic re-start after a power interruption. These functions are part of the machine's control system that determines when a laundering cycle begins.
[D06F 2105/52] Changing sequence of operational steps -- The CPC code D06F 2105/52 relates to textile care appliances and covers modifications to the order in which washing, drying, ironing or folding operations are performed, as directed by the machine's control system. It addresses how the sequence of these steps can be changed to improve performance, energy use or fabric handling.
[D06F 2105/54] Changing between normal operation mode and special operation modes, e.g. service mode, component cleaning mode or stand-by mode -- The CPC subclass D06F 2105/54 covers methods or arrangements for switching a washing machine, washer-dryer or laundry dryer from its normal operating mode to a special mode such as service, component-cleaning or stand-by. It relates to control systems that manage these mode changes in textile laundering equipment.
[D06F 2105/56] Remaining operation time -- D06F 2105/56 covers features that display or compute the remaining operation time of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers. It falls under the laundering, drying, ironing, pressing or folding textile articles subclass where the appliance control system manages this parameter.
[D06F 2105/58] Indications or alarms to the control system or to the user -- D06F 2105/58 covers the provision of alerts, signals or warning displays that inform the control system or the user about the status of a washing machine, washer-dryer or laundry dryer, such as cycle completion, errors, imbalances or other operating conditions.
[D06F 2105/60] Audible signals -- The D06F 2105/60 subclass covers audible signals produced by the control systems of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, such as beeps, buzzers or other sound alerts that indicate cycle status, completion or faults. It relates to the use of these sound cues in laundering, drying, ironing or folding operations.
[D06F 2105/62] Stopping or disabling machine operation -- This subclass covers methods and devices that halt or prevent the operation of washing machines, washer-dryers or laundry dryers, including emergency stop functions, automatic shut-off, lockout or safety interlock mechanisms triggered by the machine's control system.

[D06F 33/00] Control of operations performed in washing machines or washer-dryers -- In this group, it is mandatory to add the indexing codes of groups D06F2101/00 - D06F2105/00 .
[D06F 33/04] non-electrically -- The subclass D06F 33/04 covers washing-machine or washer-dryer control systems that operate without electrical power, such as mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic or other non-electric means for regulating cycles, water use, temperature or agitation. It is used for designs where the machine's functions are managed through purely physical mechanisms rather than electronic circuitry.

[D06F 33/10] substantially pneumatically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[D06F 33/30] Control of washing machines characterised by the purpose or target of the control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The controlling processes of washing machines that are classified in this group and its subgroups according to their specific purpose, e.g. the optimization / improvement of the washing phase or the prevention of imbalance or noise. The further information relating to the (manual and/or automatic) input and the output of these controlling processes is classified with the mandatory indexing codes and any details of the control means per se, i.e. the "hardware" is classified in the groups relating to the details of the control means (see special rules of classification for this group and its subgroups).  Concept  The following picture schematically illustrates how a controlling process of a washing machine, washer-dryer or laundry dryer is classified based on how it works. An input signal (i.e. a user input as "manual input" or an automatically detected signal as "automatic input", e.g. by a sensors) is fed into a control unit as "control means", which depending on the purpose of the control sends corresponding command signals to the machine components.  A controlling process for a laundry treating apparatus (e.g. a washing machine) has a specific "control purpose" and implies the presence of a "control means" (e.g. a CPU), whereby the control means is usually a control unit which comprises a user interface (e.g. a control panel) and several sensors for the automatic detection of e.g. temperature, pressure, liquid level, etc.  Moreover, the control unit is usually configured to perform actions as "automatic output" (e.g. switching a heater ON/OFF, regulating the drum speed, etc.), depending on the purpose of the control and based on the input of the user and/or the signals provided by the sensors.  Example: washing machine - control method  Control method for a washing machine employing a control unit adapted to detect the level and the temperature of the washing liquid and to switch the tub heater ON/OFF based on the laundry load, as input by the user, such as to reduce the energy consumption.  Classification of the example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Controlling processes of washer-dryers -> D06F33/50
[D06F 33/32] Control of operational steps, e.g. optimisation or improvement of operational steps depending on the condition of the laundry -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of the individual operational steps or phases of a washing machine cycle.
[D06F 33/34] of water filling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps concerning the water-filling phase.
[D06F 33/36] of washing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps concerning the washing phase.
[D06F 33/37] of metering of detergents or additives -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps concerning the employment of detergents or additives, e.g. determining the appropriate detergent amount based on the type and amount of laundry.
[D06F 33/38] of rinsing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements concerning the rinsing phase, e.g. having the purpose of reducing the amount of water for rinsing the laundry.
[D06F 33/40] of centrifugal separation of water from the laundry -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements concerning the spinning phase.
[D06F 33/42] of draining -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements concerning the draining phase.
[D06F 33/43] Control of cleaning or disinfection of washing machine parts, e.g. of tubs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements having the purpose of cleaning or disinfection of washing machine parts, e.g. of tubs.
[D06F 33/44] Control of the operating time, e.g. reduction of overall operating time -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements concerning the operating time, e.g. reduction of overall operating time.
[D06F 33/46] Control of the energy or water consumption -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of operational steps concerning the reduction of energy or water consumption.
[D06F 33/47] Responding to irregular working conditions, e.g. malfunctioning of pumps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of operational steps concerning the prevention and early detection of potentially dangerous or abnormal working conditions that might cause damage to the user and/or to the machine, e.g. clogging of the drain pump, water leaks, electric malfunctions, etc.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Safety arrangements -> D06F37/42
[D06F 33/48] Preventing or reducing imbalance or noise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of operational steps to reduce and/or prevent imbalance or noise.
[D06F 33/50] Control of washer-dryers characterised by the purpose or target of the control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The controlling processes of washer-dryers that are classified in this group and its subgroups according to their specific purpose, e.g. the optimization / improvement of the washing phase or the prevention of imbalance or noise. The same classification rules set out for D06F33/30 also apply mutatis mutandis to D06F33/50 - D06F33/76 , as shown in the schematic picture below. It is noted that a symbol belonging to the aforementioned range should only be given in case the control process applies specifically to a washer-dryer, and would therefore not work for a washing machine or a laundry dryer.  The further information relating to the (manual) input and the output of these controlling processes is classified with the mandatory indexing codes and any details of the control means per se, i.e. the "hardware" is classified in the groups relating to the details of the control means (see special rules of classification for this group and its subgroups).  Concept  The following picture schematically illustrates how a controlling process of a washing machine, washer-dryer or laundry dryer is classified based on how it works: an input signal (i.e. a user input as "manual input" or an automatically detected signal as "automatic input", e.g. by a sensors) is fed into a control unit as "control means", which depending on the purpose of the control sends corresponding command signals to the machine components.  The preceding image makes reference to the following symbols: D06F33/30 - D06F33/48 , D06F33/50 - D06F33/76 , D06F58/34 - D06F58/52 , D06F2101/00 , D06F2103/00 , D06F34/00 , D06F2105/00 .  Example: washer-dryer  Washer-dryer provided with a control circuit, a control panel, an air heater and a temperature sensor for measuring the temperature of the drying air, whereby the user interface allows the user to set the maximum temperature of the drying air and the control unit is configured to regulate both the heater (e.g. by switching it ON/OFF) and the blower fan based on the temperature of the drying air.  Moreover, according to the description, the control aims to avoid damaging the laundry by overheating.  Classification of the example:  Note: As a general rule, all washer-dryers are classified in D06F25/00 .

[D06F 58/30] Drying processes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Laundry drying processes per se, i.e. machine working methods characterized by several laundry treating steps.  It is noted that in this group the focus is set on the treating process the laundry undergoes.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  The above diagram represents a typical drying method for laundry dryers, in which several laundry treating steps are performed, for example during the drying process the heater and the blower fan are subsequently turned ON/OFF while the drum is rotated.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Controlling a series of operations in domestic laundry dryers -> D06F58/32
[D06F 58/32] Control of operations performed in domestic laundry dryers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General control aspects that are not covered by D06F58/34 - D06F58/52 .
[D06F 58/34] characterised by the purpose or target of the control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The controlling processes of laundry dryers that are classified in this group and its subgroups according to their specific purpose, e.g. the optimization / improvement of the drying phase or the prevention of laundry ignition.  The further information relating to the (manual) input and the output of these controlling processes is classified with the mandatory indexing codes and any details of the control means per se, i.e. the "hardware" is classified in the groups relating to the details of the control means (see special rules of classification for this group and its subgroups).  Concept  The following picture schematically illustrates how a controlling process of a washing machine, washer-dryer or laundry dryer is classified based on how it works. An input signal (i.e. a user input as "manual input" or an automatically detected signal as "automatic input, e.g. by a sensors) is fed into a control unit as "control means" which depending on the purpose of the control sends corresponding command signals to the machine components.  The preceding image makes reference to the following symbols: D06F33/30 - D06F33/48 , D06F33/50 - D06F33/76 , D06F58/34 - D06F58/52 , D06F2101/00 , D06F2103/00 , D06F34/00 , D06F2105/00 .  A controlling process of a laundry treating apparatus (e.g. a laundry dryer) has a specific "control purpose" and implies the presence of a "control means" (e.g. a CPU), whereby the control means is usually a control unit which comprises a user interface (e.g. a control panel) and several sensors for the automatic detection of e.g. air temperature, laundry humidity, liquid level in the condensed water tank, etc.  Moreover, the control unit is usually configured to perform actions as "automatic output" (e.g. switching a heater ON/OFF, regulating the blower fan speed, etc.), depending on the purpose of the control and based on the input of the user and/or the signals provided by the sensors.  First example: laundry dryer - control method  Control method for a laundry dryer employing a control unit adapted to detect the temperature of the drying air and to switch the fan speed and the heater ON/OFF based on the target humidity of the laundry load, as input by the user, such as to reduce the operating time.  Classification of the example:  Second example: washer-dryer  Washer-dryer provided with a control circuit, a control panel, an air heater and a temperature sensor for measuring the temperature of the drying air, whereby the user interface allows the user to set the maximum temperature of the drying air, and the control unit is configured to regulate the heater (e.g. by switching it ON/OFF) and the blower fan based on the temperature of the drying air. The control aims to avoid damaging the laundry by overheating.  Classification of the example:
[D06F 58/36] Control of operational steps, e.g. for optimisation or improvement of operational steps depending on the condition of the laundry -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps, which are focused on the condition of the laundry.
[D06F 58/38] of drying, e.g. to achieve the target humidity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps, which are focused on the achievement of one or more target drying parameters.
[D06F 58/40] Control of the initial heating of the drying chamber to its operating temperature -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps, which are focused on the initial heating phase.
[D06F 58/42] by unheated air -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements having the purpose of steering and/or optimizing the air flow, wherein the air is unheated.
[D06F 58/44] of conditioning or finishing, e.g. for smoothing or removing creases -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements of laundry dryers having the purpose of conditioning or finishing the laundry, e.g. using a laundry-conditioning agent like water, steam, perfume, starch.
[D06F 58/45] Cleaning or disinfection of machine parts, e.g. of heat exchangers or filters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods or control arrangements having the purpose of cleaning or disinfecting of laundry dryer components, e.g. of the lint filter, condenser, air duct, etc.
[D06F 58/46] Control of the operating time -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control methods concerning the operating time, e.g. reduction of overall operating time.
[D06F 58/48] Control of the energy consumption -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps concerning the reduction of energy consumption.
[D06F 58/50] Responding to irregular working conditions, e.g. malfunctioning of blowers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps concerning the prevention and early detection of potentially dangerous or abnormal working conditions that might cause damage to the user and/or to the machine, e.g. clogging of the lint filter, laundry ignition, electric malfunctions, etc.
[D06F 58/52] Preventing or reducing noise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The control of individual operational steps to reduce and/or prevent imbalance or noise.
