[D06M 13/244] with compounds containing sulfur or phosphorus -- This subclass covers processes for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods with low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain sulfur or phosphorus, such as dyeing, finishing or functionalisation steps. It is used when the treatment does not fall under any other more specific textile-treatment categories.
[D06M 13/248] with compounds containing sulfur -- D06M 13/248 covers processes for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods with low-molecular organic compounds that contain sulfur, such as sulfides, thiols, sulfonic acids or related agents, when the treatment is not covered by more specific subclasses. It falls under the broader class for textile treatment not provided for elsewhere in the CPC hierarchy.
[D06M 13/252] Mercaptans, thiophenols, sulfides or polysulfides, e.g. mercapto acetic acid -- This CPC subclass covers the use of low molecular weight sulfur-containing organic compounds such as mercaptans, thiophenols, sulfides and polysulfides for treating textiles, for example applying mercapto-acetic acid to fibres or fabrics to modify properties like dyeability or antibacterial performance. It falls under the broader category of chemical treatments of textiles not covered elsewhere.
[D06M 13/256] Sulfonated compounds -- D06M 13/256 covers the use of low-molecular-weight sulfonated organic compounds on fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile articles. These treatments may include sulfonic acids, their salts or esters applied for dyeing, finishing, softening or other functional effects not covered in other subclasses.
[D06M 13/262] Sulfated compounds -- The CPC entry D06M 13/262 falls under textiles-treatment not elsewhere classified and covers the use of non-macromolecular organic sulfated compounds to treat fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. It includes processes where such sulfated agents are applied to modify properties such as dye uptake, hand, or resistance.
[D06M 13/265] containing halogen atoms -- The code D06M 13/265 covers textile treatments that employ low-molecular-weight organic compounds which contain halogen atoms, such as chlorinated or brominated solvents, dyes, or finishers. It is used for processes that modify fibres, yarns or fabrics by applying these halogen-bearing substances to impart specific properties or effects.
[D06M 13/268] Sulfones -- Treating textile fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with non-macromolecular organic compounds that contain the sulfone functional group. The process involves applying sulfone-based agents to modify properties such as flame resistance, water repellency or durability. This falls under the broader class of textile treatments not covered elsewhere.
[D06M 13/272] Unsaturated compounds containing sulfur atoms -- This group includes the application of unsaturated sulfur-containing organic compounds, such as thiophenes, thioacrylates or sulfide polymers, to fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods. These agents are used for effects such as curing, cross-linking, antimicrobial activity or coloration that are not covered by other specific classes. The treatment employs low-molecular-weight compounds rather than macromolecular resins.
[D06M 13/275] Vinylthioethers -- Vinylthioethers are low molecular weight organic compounds containing a vinyl group bonded to a thioether, used as textile finishing agents to impart properties such as softness, water repellency or antimicrobial activity. They are applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics in processes covered by CPC D06M 13/275.
[D06M 13/278] Vinylsulfonium compounds -- Vinylsulfonium compounds are low-molecular-weight organic reagents used to modify or finish fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods by chemical treatment. In the CPC they fall under D06M 13/278, which covers textile treatments that employ non-macromolecular organic chemicals not otherwise classified.
[D06M 13/282] with compounds containing phosphorus -- This subclass covers processes for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods using low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain phosphorus, where the treatment is not otherwise specified. It includes applications such as flame-retardant finishes, water-repellent or anti-microbial treatments applied by impregnation, coating or similar methods.
[D06M 13/285] Phosphines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with phosphines, phosphine oxides, phosphine sulfides, phosphinic or phosphinous acids or derivatives thereof, i.e. compounds of general formula H3PO2 or derivatives thereof.  Derivatives thereof are also including phosphonium.
[D06M 13/288] Phosphonic or phosphonous acids or derivatives thereof -- D06M 13/288 covers the use of phosphonic or phosphonous acids and their derivatives to treat fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The chemicals are non-macromolecular organic compounds applied for effects such as dye-fixing, flame- retardancy, water-repellency or antimicrobial protection, and the class is limited to treatments not specified elsewhere in the textiles section.
[D06M 13/29] containing halogen atoms -- This subclass covers the application of low-molecular-weight organic chemicals that contain halogen atoms to treat or finish textile materials such as fibers, yarns, or fabrics. These treatments are used to impart properties such as water repellency, dye affinity, or flame resistance and are not classified elsewhere in the textile-treatment hierarchy.
[D06M 13/292] Mono-, di- or triesters of phosphoric or phosphorous acids -- The subclass D06M 13/292 covers textile treatments that employ mono-, di- or triesters of phosphoric or phosphorous acids as non-macromolecular organic agents applied to fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. Such esters are used for modification, finishing or protective functions not classified elsewhere in the textile-treatment hierarchy.
[D06M 13/295] containing polyglycol moieties -- This CPC subclass covers textile treatments where the applied non-macromolecular organic compound includes polyglycol groups. It pertains to processes that apply such agents to fibers, yarns or fabrics for functions like softening, antistatic or moisture management.
[D06M 13/298] containing halogen atoms -- This CPC group covers processes for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain halogen atoms such as chlorine, bromine or fluorine, imparting specific chemical or physical modifications. The treatment is not covered by any more specific textile-treatment subclass.
[D06M 13/313] Unsaturated compounds containing phosphorus atoms, e.g. vinylphosphonium compounds -- The CPC subclass D06M 13/313 covers the use of unsaturated organic compounds that contain phosphorus, such as vinylphosphonium derivatives, for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials. These agents are applied to modify surface characteristics, improve dye uptake or confer functional properties to the textile product.
[D06M 13/322] with compounds containing nitrogen -- The subclass covers processes for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain nitrogen, such as nitrogen-based dyes, water-repellent agents or other functional finishes. It applies when the treatment is not covered by more specific textile categories.
[D06M 13/325] Amines -- The D06M 13/325 subclass covers the use of amines as non-macromolecular organic chemicals for treating fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, including applications such as bleaching, scouring, dyeing, finishing and other textile treatments not classified elsewhere.
[D06M 13/3255] Vinylamine; Allylamine -- D06M 13/3255 covers the use of the low-molecular-weight organic compounds vinylamine and allylamine for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, where the treatment is not elsewhere specified in the classification. This falls under textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and specifically treatment not provided for elsewhere in class.
[D06M 13/328] the amino group being bound to an acyclic or cycloaliphatic carbon atom -- It covers textile treatment processes that employ low-molecular-weight organic compounds in which an amino group is attached to an acyclic or cycloaliphatic carbon atom, such as aliphatic amines used as finishes. These treatments modify fibers, yarns or fabrics to impart properties like softness, dye uptake, antimicrobial activity, or other functional effects.
[D06M 13/33] containing halogen atoms -- The CPC subclass D06M 13/33 covers textile treatments that employ non-macromolecular organic compounds which contain halogen atoms, such as chlorine, bromine, fluorine or iodine. It addresses processes and treated textile articles where halogen-substituted chemicals are applied to fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods.
[D06M 13/332] Di- or polyamines -- This sub-group covers the use of di- or polyamine compounds in textile processing. It includes methods where such low-molecular-weight amines are applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics for purposes such as dye fixation, cross-linking, antimicrobial treatment or improvement of mechanical properties. The classification is within the broader class of treatments of textiles not provided for elsewhere using non-macromolecular organic chemicals.
[D06M 13/335] having an amino group bound to a carbon atom of a six-membered aromatic ring -- This CPC subclass D06M 13/335 covers textile treatments that employ small organic molecules containing an aromatic six-membered ring with an amino group attached to a carbon atom. It includes processes in which such compounds are applied to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods to impart desired functional properties.
[D06M 13/338] Organic hydrazines -- The D06M 13/338 subclass covers textile-related processes in which organic hydrazine compounds are employed to treat fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, where the treatment is not otherwise classified. It includes methods using these small-molecule hydrazines for finishing actions such as bleaching, dyeing, grafting or other chemical modifications of the material.
[D06M 13/342] Amino-carboxylic acids -- This subclass covers the use of amino-carboxylic acids such as glycine, aspartic acid, or their derivatives to treat fibers, yarns or fabrics. The acids are applied as non-polymeric agents to modify properties like dye uptake, crease resistance or antistatic performance. It falls under treatment of textiles not provided elsewhere in the classification.
[D06M 13/345] Nitriles -- The D06M 13/345 subclass covers the use of non-macromolecular nitrile compounds-organic molecules containing a carbon-nitrogen triple bond-to treat fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. These treatments impart properties such as water-repellency, oil resistance, or durability, and are classified under textile treatments not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 13/348] unsaturated, e.g. acrylonitrile -- The code D06M 13/348 concerns the use of low-molecular-weight unsaturated compounds, such as acrylonitrile, for treating fibres, yarns or fabrics. It falls under textile treatments not provided for elsewhere, where non-macromolecular organic chemicals are applied to modify surface properties or performance.
[D06M 13/35] Heterocyclic compounds -- D06M 13/35 covers the use of heterocyclic organic compounds in textile treatment processes that are not provided for elsewhere in the class. It relates to applying these non-macromolecular chemicals to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods for purposes such as dyeing, finishing or functionalisation.
[D06M 13/352] having five-membered heterocyclic rings -- D06M 13/352 covers textile treatment processes that employ non-macromolecular organic compounds containing five-membered heterocyclic rings to treat fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. It is a subclass of treatments not provided for elsewhere in the textile section.
[D06M 13/355] having six-membered heterocyclic rings -- This subclass covers textile-oriented processes in which fabrics, yarns, fibers or similar fibrous goods are treated with low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain a six-membered heterocyclic ring, such as pyridine, pyrimidine or quinoline derivatives. The treatment may impart properties like coloration, antimicrobial activity, water repellency or flame resistance and is not classified elsewhere in the textile-treatment hierarchy.
[D06M 13/358] Triazines -- The subclass D06M 13/358 covers the application of triazine compounds to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials for treatments not defined elsewhere, typically as non-macromolecular finishers, dyes, or functional agents that modify properties such as flame retardancy, antimicrobial activity or water repellency.
[D06M 13/364] Cyanuric acid -- D06M 13/364 covers the use of cyanuric acid as a non-macromolecular organic compound for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile products, typically to impart specific functional properties such as flame retardancy or UV protection. This subclass falls under treatments of textiles not covered elsewhere.
[D06M 13/368] Hydroxyalkylamines -- D06M 13/368 concerns the application of hydroxyalkylamine compounds as low-molecular-weight chemical treatments for fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. These agents are used to modify textile properties such as softness, dye affinity, antistatic behaviour or dimensional stability.
[D06M 13/372] containing etherified or esterified hydroxy groups -- D06M 13/372 covers textile treatments where the material is treated with non-macromolecular organic compounds that contain etherified or esterified hydroxy groups. It falls under the category of treatments not provided for elsewhere, applied to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods.
[D06M 13/376] Oximes -- This subclass covers the use of oxime compounds for processing textile fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The classification is used for processes that treat textiles with non-macromolecular organic chemicals not covered elsewhere.

[D06M 13/392] Nitroso compounds -- This subgroup concerns the application of nitroso organic compounds to textile materials such as fibres, yarns or fabrics to modify their properties. The classification groups any process where a nitroso compound is employed as the active treating agent.

[D06M 13/402] Amides -- The code D06M 13/402 covers the use of amide compounds in the treatment of fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous articles, where the amides act as non-macromolecular organic agents to modify properties such as dye affinity, softness, durability or resistance to wear.

It falls under treatment of textiles not elsewhere specified and involves non-macromolecular organic compounds.

[D06M 13/41] Amides derived from unsaturated carboxylic acids, e.g. acrylamide -- This subclass covers the use of low-molecular-weight amides derived from unsaturated carboxylic acids, such as acrylamide, to treat fibers, yarns or fabrics. The treatment imparts properties like improved dye uptake, softness or wrinkle resistance without forming polymeric coatings.

[D06M 13/422] Hydrazides -- Hydrazides are low-molecular-weight organic compounds applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics to modify properties such as dye uptake, fire resistance or antimicrobial activity. [D06M 13/425] Carbamic or thiocarbamic acids or derivatives thereof, e.g. urethanes -- The D06M 13/425 subclass covers the use of carbamic or thiocarbamic acids and their derivatives, such as urethanes, as non-macromolecular organic chemicals for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other textile articles. [D06M 13/432] Urea, thiourea or derivatives thereof, e.g. biurets -- This subclass deals with applying urea, thiourea or related derivatives such as biurets to fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, using these low-molecular-weight organic compounds to impart or improve properties like flame retardancy, shrink-resistance, dye uptake or other functional characteristics.
[D06M 13/435] Semicarbazides -- The code D06M 13/435 covers the use of semicarbazide compounds for treating textile fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous materials. [D06M 13/438] Sulfonamides -- The CPC subclass D06M 13/438 covers the use of sulfonamide compounds for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous articles, typically as antimicrobial or preserving agents. It includes processes where non-macromolecular sulfonamides are applied to confer resistance to bacterial growth, mould or related deterioration during or after textile manufacturing.
[D06M 13/44] containing nitrogen and phosphorus -- This subclass covers textile treatments that employ non-macromolecular organic compounds containing both nitrogen and phosphorus. It includes processes for applying such chemicals to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods to impart properties like flame retardancy, antimicrobial action or other functional finishes.
[D06M 13/447] Phosphonates or phosphinates containing nitrogen atoms -- D06M 13/447 is a CPC subclass under textile treatment that concerns the use of nitrogen-containing phosphonates or phosphinates as non-macromolecular organic agents for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. It covers processes in which these compounds are applied to modify surface properties, improve durability, or impart functional performance to textiles.
[D06M 13/453] Phosphates or phosphites containing nitrogen atoms -- This subclass covers the use of nitrogen-containing phosphates or phosphites as textile treatment agents. It includes preparations where the phosphorus compound contains nitrogen atoms and is applied to fibre, yarn, fabric or other textile materials to impart properties such as flame retardancy, antistatic performance or durability.
[D06M 13/46] Compounds containing quaternary nitrogen atoms -- This subclass covers the use of organic compounds that contain quaternary nitrogen atoms as agents for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials. Such quaternary nitrogen compounds are applied for effects such as antimicrobial, antistatic, dye-fixing or other functional finishes not covered elsewhere. The classification lies within the broader category of textile treatments not otherwise specified.
[D06M 13/461] Quaternised amin-amides from polyamines or heterocyclic compounds or polyamino-acids -- The D06M 13/461 subclass covers textile treatments that employ quaternised amino-amide compounds derived from polyamines, heterocyclic substances or poly-amino-acids. It pertains to the application of these non-macromolecular organic agents to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods in order to modify their properties. This entry falls under the broader category of miscellaneous textile treatments not specified elsewhere.
[D06M 13/463] derived from monoamines -- The D06M 13/463 subclass covers processes for treating fibres, threads, yarns or fabrics using non-macromolecular organic compounds that are derived from monoamines. It includes methods that apply these low-molecular-weight chemicals to modify or improve textile properties such as colour, durability, softness or resistance, where the treatment falls outside more specific categories. This subclass is situated under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 13/467] derived from polyamines -- This subclass includes processes for applying textile treatments that use compounds derived from polyamines. Such agents are non-macromolecular organic chemicals applied to fibres, yarns or fabrics to impart properties like softness, antistatic behaviour, dye-affinity or improved hand.
[D06M 13/47] derived from heterocyclic compounds -- D06M 13/47 covers processes for treating fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or similar fibrous goods with small organic compounds that are derived from heterocyclic structures. The chemicals are non-macromolecular and are applied for purposes such as dyeing, finishing or other functional modifications not classified elsewhere in the textile-treatment hierarchy.
[D06M 13/473] having five-membered heterocyclic rings -- D06M 13/473 refers to textile treatments that employ non-macromolecular organic compounds containing five-membered heterocyclic rings, such as imidazoles, thiazoles or oxazoles, to modify fibers, yarns or fabrics. The provision covers processes not classified elsewhere, involving functionalisation, finishing or performance enhancement of textile substrates with these heterocyclic agents.
[D06M 13/477] having six-membered heterocyclic rings -- The D06M 13/477 subclass concerns textile treatment processes that employ small organic compounds containing six-membered heterocyclic rings, such as pyridine or quinoline derivatives, to modify fibers, yarns, or fabrics. These non-macromolecular agents are used to impart functional properties like coloration, antimicrobial activity, or improved flame resistance.
[D06M 13/48] containing the ethylene imine ring -- D06M 13/48 classifies textile treatment methods that use non-macromolecular organic compounds containing an ethylene imine ring, applied to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods.
[D06M 13/487] Aziridinylphosphines -- This subclass covers the use of aziridinylphosphine compounds as treatment agents for fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The chemicals are non-macromolecular organics applied to modify surface properties, improve dye uptake or confer functional performance. It falls under textile treatment not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 13/493] perfluorinated -- The subclass D06M 13/493 refers to the application of perfluorinated compounds for treating textile materials. It covers processes that apply these non-macromolecular organic substances to fibers, yarns, or fabrics to impart water-repellent, oil-repellent or stain-resistant properties. The treatment is used where no other more specific subclass applies.
[D06M 13/50] with organometallic compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with organometallic compounds; with organic compounds containing boron, silicon, selenium or tellurium atoms but also with complexes of metals with organic compounds.
[D06M 13/503] without bond between a carbon atom and a metal or a boron, silicon, selenium or tellurium atom -- A method for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile products using low-molecular-weight organic compounds that do not contain a carbon-metal, carbon-boron, carbon-silicon, carbon-selenium or carbon-tellurium bond. The process falls under textile-treatment classes not covered elsewhere in the scheme.
[D06M 13/507] Organic silicon compounds without carbon-silicon bond -- D06M 13/507 covers the use of organic silicon compounds that do not contain a carbon-silicon bond for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials. These chemicals are applied as non-macromolecular agents for surface modification, finishing or other specialized textile treatments not classified elsewhere.
[D06M 13/51] Compounds with at least one carbon-metal or carbon-boron, carbon-silicon, carbon-selenium, or carbon-tellurium bond
[D06M 13/513] with at least one carbon-silicon bond -- This subclass covers methods of treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous textile goods with non-macromolecular organic compounds that contain at least one carbon-silicon bond. The treatment is classified under textile processing not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 13/5135] Unsaturated compounds containing silicon atoms -- This subclass covers the use of silicon-containing unsaturated organic compounds in textile treatment, such as silicones with vinyl or allyl groups applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics to impart water repellency, softness, or other functional finishes.
[D06M 13/517] containing silicon-halogen bonds -- A process for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods with low-molecular-weight organic compounds that contain silicon-halogen (Si-X) bonds, where X is a halogen atom. The treatment aims to impart properties such as water repellency, oil resistance, or flame retardancy by applying these silicon-based chemicals to the textile surface. This falls under the "treatment, not provided for elsewhere in class" category for textile treatments.
[D06M 13/52] combined with mechanical treatment -- This subclass covers processes that treat fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or similar fibrous goods with low-molecular organic compounds while simultaneously applying mechanical action such as beating, rubbing, pressing or stretching to achieve the desired textile properties.
[D06M 13/522] Fulling -- Fulling is a finishing operation for textiles in which fibers, threads, yarns or fabrics are treated with non-macromolecular organic compounds to cause controlled shrinkage, thickening and densification, often improving hand, insulation and surface smoothness. The process may involve moisture, heat, pressure or agitation to achieve the desired compacted structure. It falls under the cooperative patent classification D06M 13/522 for textile treatments not covered elsewhere.
[D06M 13/525] Embossing -- Embossing refers to the process of imparting a raised or recessed pattern onto textiles, fibres, threads, yarns or fabrics by applying pressure, heat or a combination of both, often with non-macromolecular organic compounds that set or stabilize the design. This treatment modifies the surface texture for decorative, functional or anti-slip purposes and is classified under the CPC subclass D06M 13/525.
[D06M 13/53] Cooling -- The D06M 13/53 subclass covers processes for giving fabrics a cooling sensation by applying non-macromolecular organic chemicals. It includes treatments that embed cooling agents such as menthol derivatives or phase-change compounds into fibres, yarns or finished textiles to lower skin temperature during wear.
[D06M 13/535] Suction -- The D06M 13/535 "Suction" subclass covers textile treatment processes that employ vacuum or suction to remove liquids, gases, or particulates from fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous products. It includes methods where suction is used as a primary or auxiliary step, such as de-watering, drying, or cleaning, often in combination with non-macromolecular organic compounds. This subclass is situated under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 14/00] Graft polymerisation of monomers containing carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds on to fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics, or fibrous goods made from such materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The graft polymerisation of monomers containing carbon-carbon double bonds onto fibrous materials according to the nature of the substrate.  This graft polymerisation can be carried out by using wave energy or particle radiation, for example plasma, microwaves, UV light, gamma rays etc...( D06M14/18 - D06M14/34 ).
[D06M 14/02] on to materials of natural origin -- This subgroup of D06M 14 deals with graft polymerisation of unsaturated monomers onto natural-origin textile substrates such as fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous products, enabling covalent attachment of polymer chains to improve material properties.
[D06M 14/04] of vegetal origin, e.g. cellulose or derivatives thereof -- D06M 14/04 covers graft polymerisation of unsaturated carbon-carbon monomers onto textile materials that are of vegetal origin, such as cellulose or its derivatives. The process creates a covalent bond between the monomer and fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, modifying their surface properties without altering the bulk material. This subclass falls under treatment of textiles not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[D06M 14/06] of animal origin, e.g. wool or silk -- The D06M 14/06 subclass covers graft polymerisation of carbon-carbon unsaturated monomers onto textile materials of animal origin such as wool or silk, forming polymer layers on fibres, yarns or fabrics to modify their properties. It falls under treatments of textiles not otherwise specified.
[D06M 14/08] on to materials of synthetic origin -- The CPC subclass D06M 14/08 covers graft polymerisation of monomers with carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods that are of synthetic origin. It is placed under "Treatment of textiles or the like - treatment, not provided for elsewhere in class" within the broader textiles category.
[D06M 14/10] of macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- This subclass deals with graft polymerisation of monomers that contain only carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds onto fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The resulting macromolecular compounds are formed by reactions involving exclusively C=C double bonds, without other functional groups. It falls under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere in the CPC.
[D06M 14/12] of macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- The CPC subclass D06M 14/12 covers grafting of macromolecular compounds onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods where the macromolecules are prepared by reactions other than simple carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bond polymerisation, and are then attached to the textile substrate via graft polymerisation of unsaturated monomers. It pertains to textile treatments not classified elsewhere and includes processes that bond pre-formed polymers to the material surface.
[D06M 14/14] Polyesters -- The D06M 14/14 subclass covers graft polymerisation of polyester monomers containing carbon-carbon unsaturation onto textile substrates such as fibres, threads, yarns, or fabrics. It includes processes that bind polyester chains to the surface of fibrous goods to modify their properties, and is grouped under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 14/16] Polyamides -- D06M 14/16 covers the graft polymerisation of polyamide monomers containing carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous substrates, creating modified textile materials with enhanced properties. This subclass is used for processes where polyamide grafts are covalently attached to textile surfaces. It falls under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 14/18] using wave energy or particle radiation -- The D06M 14/18 subclass covers processes that graft polymerise unsaturated monomers onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods by employing wave energy such as ultraviolet, microwave or laser radiation, or particle radiation like electron beams. This method is used to modify textile surfaces when the treatment is not covered by any other more specific subclass. It falls within the broader category of textile treatments not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 14/20] on to materials of natural origin -- Graft polymerisation of monomers that contain carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds is applied to fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods made from natural-origin materials, forming covalent links between the polymer chains and the substrate. This treatment modifies the surface properties of the textile, such as dye affinity, water resistance or mechanical strength, without altering the bulk composition.
[D06M 14/22] of vegetal origin, e.g. cellulose or derivatives thereof -- This subclass covers graft polymerisation of carbon-carbon unsaturated monomers onto textile fibres, threads, yarns or fabrics that are of vegetal origin such as cellulose or its derivatives, forming covalent bonds that alter the material's properties. The treatment is used to impart improved durability, water resistance, or other functional characteristics to the fibrous goods.
[D06M 14/24] of animal origin, e.g. wool or silk -- The D06M 14/24 subclass covers the graft polymerisation of unsaturated monomers onto animal-origin fibres such as wool or silk, applied to threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. This treatment modifies the surface chemistry of the fibres to impart desired functional properties not covered by other textile-treatment classes.
[D06M 14/26] on to materials of synthetic origin -- The CPC subclass D06M 14/26 pertains to graft polymerisation of monomers that contain carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods made of synthetic origin, in order to alter their surface or bulk properties. This treatment falls under the broader category of textile processing methods that are not covered by other specific classes.
[D06M 14/28] of macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- The D06M 14/28 subclass covers graft polymerisation where monomers with carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds are covalently attached to fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous materials, forming macromolecular compounds. The process is limited to reactions that involve only those carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds, without additional functional-group chemistry.
[D06M 14/30] of macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- D06M 14/30 covers textile treatments in which macromolecular compounds are graft-polymerised onto fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, but where the polymer is produced by methods other than simple carbon-to-carbon unsaturated-bond reactions, such as condensation, copolymerisation with additional functional groups or other non-addition pathways.
[D06M 14/32] Polyesters -- D06M 14/32 covers the graft polymerisation of polyester-forming monomers that contain carbon-carbon double bonds onto textile substrates such as fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods, providing a means to modify surface properties that are not addressed in other textile-treatment classes.
[D06M 14/34] Polyamides -- The CPC subclass D06M 14/34 covers graft polymerisation of monomers with carbon-carbon unsaturation onto fibre-based materials, specifically when polyamides are used as the grafted polymer. It falls under the textile-treatment group for processes not classified elsewhere, detailing methods of attaching polyamide chains to fabrics, yarns or other fibrous goods.
[D06M 14/36] on to carbon fibres -- D06M 14/36 covers the graft polymerisation of monomers containing carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds onto carbon fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous materials, creating modified textile surfaces. The process is used to impart new chemical or physical properties to the fibres.
[D06M 15/00] Treating fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics, or fibrous goods made from such materials, with macromolecular compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with macromolecular compounds.
[D06M 15/01] with natural macromolecular compounds or derivatives thereof -- This subclass covers processes for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile items using natural macromolecular compounds such as proteins, polysaccharides, or their derivatives. The treatment may involve coating, finishing, or imparting specific functional properties derived from these biobased substances.
[D06M 15/03] Polysaccharides or derivatives thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with polysaccharides or derivatives thereof that cannot be covered by subgroups D06M15/035 - D06M15/13 .  For example documents disclosing treatment with chitin, chitosan or cylodextrin are classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treatment of fibrous materials with low molecular weight sugars, glucose, saccharose etc. -> D06M13/148
[D06M 15/035] Polymeric alcohol xanthates -- This subclass covers the application of polymeric alcohol xanthates, a type of macromolecular compound, for treating fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The treatment aims to modify surface properties such as dye affinity, softness, antistatic performance or other functional characteristics.
[D06M 15/05] Cellulose or derivatives thereof -- This subclass covers the use of cellulose and its chemically modified forms as macromolecular agents for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials. It includes processes where cellulose, regenerated cellulose, cellulose esters, ethers or other derivatives are applied to improve properties such as strength, durability, absorbency or finish. The treatment may be performed by impregnation, coating, grafting or other chemical modification techniques.
[D06M 15/055] with the residual liquors derived of the sulfatic process for the preparation of cellulose -- The D06M 15/055 subclass covers processes that treat fibres, yarns or fabrics using the residual liquors obtained from the sulfite pulping method employed to prepare cellulose. It pertains to the application of these sulfite-derived solutions for modifying or finishing textiles where no other specific treatment is defined. This falls within the broader category of textile treatments not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 15/07] Cellulose esters -- D06M 15/07 relates to the use of cellulose ester polymers to treat textile materials such as fibers, yarns or fabrics. The process involves applying or impregnating these macromolecular compounds to modify properties like durability, smoothness or resistance to moisture.
[D06M 15/09] Cellulose ethers -- D06M 15/09 designates the use of cellulose ether macromolecular compounds for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials, falling under the broader class of textile treatments not covered elsewhere. The subclass pertains to applying these water-soluble or water-insoluble cellulose derivatives to modify properties such as softness, absorbency or dimensional stability.
[D06M 15/11] Starch or derivatives thereof -- The D06M 15/11 subclass covers the treatment of fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile articles with starch or its derivatives, using these macromolecular compounds as finishes such as sizing, thickening, binding or hand-improving agents. It is part of the broader group for textile treatments not covered elsewhere, focusing on starch-based chemical modifications to enhance processing or performance.
[D06M 15/13] Alginic acid or derivatives thereof -- The subclass D06M 15/13 covers the treatment of fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods with macromolecular compounds, specifically using alginic acid or its chemical derivatives to modify the material's properties such as absorbency, durability or surface characteristics.
[D06M 15/15] Proteins or derivatives thereof -- The D06M 15/15 subclass concerns the treatment of fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with protein-based macromolecular compounds or their derivatives, such as enzymes, gelatin, silk fibroin or similar substances, to alter surface, mechanical or functional properties. It falls under the broader category of textile treatments not provided for elsewhere in the classification.
[D06M 15/155] Treatment in the presence of salts derived from amphoteric metal hydroxides -- The D06M 15/155 entry covers processes for treating fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with macromolecular compounds while salts derived from amphoteric metal hydroxides-such as aluminum, zinc or magnesium hydroxide salts-are present, acting as catalysts, buffers or fixing agents to improve the performance of the treatment.
[D06M 15/17] Natural resins, resinous alcohols, resinous acids, or derivatives thereof -- D06M 15/17 covers the application of natural resins, resinous alcohols, resinous acids and their derivatives to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The treatment is intended to modify properties such as water repellency, stiffness, durability or finish and is classified under textile treatment not provided elsewhere.
[D06M 15/19] with synthetic macromolecular compounds -- This subclass relates to processes for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods by applying synthetic macromolecular compounds, such as polymer coatings, finishes, or impregnation agents. It covers the use of man-made polymers to impart properties like water-repellency, durability, or elasticity, where the treatment is not specified in other textile-treatment subclasses.
[D06M 15/195] sulfated or sulfonated -- This subclass covers the treatment of fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials with macromolecular compounds that are sulfated or sulfonated, typically to impart properties such as improved dye uptake, water repellency, or antistatic behavior. The processes include immersion, padding, coating or other methods of applying these chemically modified polymers to the textile substrate.
[D06M 15/21] Macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds.
[D06M 15/227] of hydrocarbons, or reaction products thereof, e.g. afterhalogenated or sulfochlorinated -- D06M 15/227 covers the treatment of fibres, yarns, fabrics or similar fibrous goods with hydrocarbons or their reaction products, such as halogenated or sulfo-chlorinated compounds, applied as macromolecular agents to modify textile properties. This subclass falls under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 15/233] aromatic, e.g. styrene -- A process for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other textile goods with macromolecular compounds that include aromatic units, such as those derived from styrene. It falls under the "treatment, not provided for elsewhere in class" category of textile processing.
[D06M 15/244] of halogenated hydrocarbons -- The D06M 15/244 subclass covers textile treatments that employ macromolecular halogenated hydrocarbons, such as polymeric chlorinated or fluorinated compounds, to impart properties like flame resistance, water repellency or oil resistance to fibres, yarns or fabrics. This falls under the broader category of treatments not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 15/248] containing chlorine -- The subclass D06M 15/248 pertains to the treatment of textile fibres, yarns or fabrics with macromolecular compounds that contain chlorine. It includes processes where chlorinated polymers are applied to impart properties such as flame resistance, water repellency or antimicrobial action.
[D06M 15/252] containing bromine -- The subclass D06M 15/252 covers textile treatments in which the macromolecular finishing agent contains bromine, such as brominated polymers or resins applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics to provide flame-retardant, antimicrobial or other functional properties. It belongs to the broader group of treatments not provided for elsewhere in the class.
[D06M 15/256] containing fluorine -- The subgroup D06M 15/256 pertains to textile treatments in which the applied macromolecular compounds contain fluorine. Such fluorinated polymers are used to give fibers, yarns, or fabrics enhanced properties like water- and oil-repellency, stain resistance, or altered surface energy.
[D06M 15/263] of unsaturated carboxylic acids

The treatment bonds or crosslinks these macromolecules to the textile surface, imparting properties such as improved durability, water-repellency or chemical resistance.
[D06M 15/2735] of unsaturated carboxylic esters having mercapto groups -- This subclass covers processes for treating fibres, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials with unsaturated carboxylic esters that contain mercapto (-SH) groups, using these macromolecular compounds to modify the textile's surface, improve durability or impart functional properties.

[D06M 15/29] containing a N-methylol group or an etherified N-methylol group -- The CPC subclass D06M 15/29 falls under textile treatment not elsewhere specified and covers processes that apply macromolecular agents containing an N-methylol group or an etherified N-methylol group to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. The treatment imparts functional finishes such as cross-linking, water-repellency or durability.

[D06M 15/65] containing epoxy groups -- The sub-class D06M 15/65 covers textile treatments that employ macromolecular compounds bearing epoxy groups, applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics to provide adhesive, cross-linking or protective functions. Such epoxy-containing finishes improve durability, resistance to chemicals or mechanical wear, and enable bonding of subsequent layers.
[D06M 15/651] comprising carboxylic groups -- This subclass covers processes for treating fibers, yarns, fabrics or other textile materials with macromolecular compounds that contain carboxylic groups, such as polymers bearing -COOH functionality. [D06M 15/673] containing phosphorus and nitrogen in the main chain -- A subclass for textile treatment that uses macromolecular compounds whose main polymer chain contains phosphorus and nitrogen atoms, applied to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. [D06M 15/687] containing atoms other than phosphorus, silicon, sulfur, nitrogen, oxygen or carbon in the main chain -- This subgroup covers the treatment of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other textile products with macromolecular compounds whose main chain incorporates heteroatoms other than phosphorus, silicon, sulfur, nitrogen, oxygen or carbon, such as boron- or halogen-containing polymers. It is used for imparting special properties not attainable with conventional organic polymers.
[D06M 15/693] with natural or synthetic rubber, or derivatives thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with synthetic or natural rubber or its derivatives, like latexes.
[D06M 15/70] combined with mechanical treatment -- D06M 15/70 covers processes where textiles are treated with macromolecular compounds while simultaneously undergoing mechanical actions such as stretching, kneading, or pressing. The combined chemical and mechanical treatment modifies surface or bulk properties of fibers, yarns, or fabrics. It is used to improve characteristics like durability, softness, or water resistance.
[D06M 15/705] Embossing -- The D06M 15/705 subclass covers embossing of textiles, where pressure, heat or chemical treatment creates raised patterns or textures on fabrics or other fibrous goods, often using macromolecular compounds to set or enhance the embossed design. This process falls under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 15/71] Cooling -- D06M 15/71 concerns methods for cooling fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods by applying macromolecular compounds that provide a cooling effect, such as phase-change or moisture-evaporating agents. The treatment is intended to enhance the comfort or performance of the textile material.
[D06M 15/715] Suction -- Suction is a method for treating fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods with macromolecular compounds, where the liquid reagent is drawn into or through the material by creating a pressure differential. This technique can improve the uniformity and penetration of finishes such as polymers, resins or coatings, and is classified under cooperative patent code D06M 15/715.
[D06M 16/00] Biochemical treatment of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics, or fibrous goods made from such materials, e.g. enzymatic -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with either living microorganisms like enzymes or bacteria or compounds which have an activity towards living microorganisms like compositions for killing living microorganisms or insect repellent compositions, antibacterial compositions, antimicrobial compositions, fungicide compositions and antifouling compositions.
[D06M 16/003] with enzymes or microorganisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of textile materials with enzymes like cellulases, lipases, endoglucanases, proteases etc... and/or living or dead microorganisms.
[D06M 16/006] with wool-protecting agents; with anti-moth agents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of wool with compositions or compounds having a protecting effect against living organisms like moth but also bacteria or living organisms which might deteriorate wool.
[D06M 17/00] Producing multi-layer textile fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The production of multilayer textile fabrics by superposition of different layers which are bonded by chemical treatment; i.e. an adhesive layer is applied between the different layers.
[D06M 17/02] by applying cellulose derivatives as adhesives -- The D06M 17/02 subclass covers processes for producing multi-layer textile fabrics in which cellulose-derived adhesives are applied to bond the layers. It falls under treatment of textiles not covered elsewhere, specifically addressing adhesive-based lamination of fabrics.
[D06M 17/04] by applying synthetic resins as adhesives -- D06M 17/04 covers processes for producing multi-layer textile fabrics in which synthetic resin adhesives are applied to bond the layers together. The classification falls under treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere, focusing on resin-based lamination techniques.
[D06M 17/06] Polymers of vinyl compounds -- D06M 17/06 relates to the use of vinyl-based polymers in the production of multilayer textile fabrics. It covers methods of forming, bonding or coating textile sheets with polymeric vinyl films to create composite or laminated fabrics.
[D06M 17/08] Polyamides -- The D06M 17/08 subclass covers the production of multi-layer textile fabrics that incorporate polyamides, such as nylon, either as the primary material or as a reinforcing layer. It includes processes for forming, bonding, or otherwise treating polyamide-based layers within composite textile structures.
[D06M 17/10] Polyurethanes -- D06M 17/10 covers the use of polyurethane polymers in the manufacture of multilayer textile fabrics, where the polymer acts as a binder, coating, or film to bond layers together, enhance durability, and provide specific functional properties such as waterproofing, flexibility, or abrasion resistance. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles, and treatments not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 19/00] Treatment of feathers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of feathers at any stage, i.e. their preparation for an ulterior use, e.g. removing waxes or any chemical treatment to improve or modify their properties.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The dyeing of feathers -> D06P
[D06M 2101/00] Chemical constitution of the fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or fibrous goods made from such materials, to be treated -- The CPC subclass D06M 2101/00 concerns the chemical constitution of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods that are intended for treatment. It addresses the composition of these textile materials before any further processing, falling under the broader category of treatments of textiles not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 2101/005] Asbestos fibres -- D06M 2101/005 relates to asbestos fibers as the material of textiles or fibrous goods whose chemical constitution is being treated. It falls under treatment of textiles not elsewhere specified, focusing on the composition of fibers, threads, yarns or fabrics made from asbestos. This subclass is used to classify inventions concerning the use or modification of asbestos fibers in textile applications.
[D06M 2101/02] Natural fibres, other than mineral fibres -- D06M 2101/02 concerns the chemical constitution of natural textile fibres such as cotton, wool, silk or plant-based fibers, excluding mineral fibers. It falls within the "treatment, not provided for elsewhere in class" subcategory, covering the composition of fibers, threads, yarns or fabrics before any specific chemical or physical processing is applied.
[D06M 2101/04] Vegetal fibres -- D06M 2101/04 covers the chemical composition of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous products that are made from plant-derived (vegetal) fibres and are subject to a treatment not covered elsewhere in the classification. It falls under textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and treatment not provided for elsewhere.
[D06M 2101/06] cellulosic -- This subclass covers chemical treatments specific to cellulosic fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous articles made from cellulosic materials. It includes processes that modify the chemical constitution of such textiles where no other more specific subclass applies.
[D06M 2101/08] Esters or ethers of cellulose -- The subclass D06M 2101/08 covers textile materials whose cellulose base has been chemically modified by forming esters or ethers, creating cellulose derivatives such as cellulose acetate, ethyl cellulose or hydroxyethyl cellulose. These modified fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods are treated to impart properties like improved dye affinity, strength, or dimensional stability.
[D06M 2101/10] Animal fibres -- D06M 2101/10 covers chemical treatments applied specifically to animal fibers such as wool, hair or silk, focusing on modifying their chemical constitution. The classification includes processes that alter the fibers, threads, yarns or fabrics made from animal-derived materials, where the treatment is not covered elsewhere in the textile class.
[D06M 2101/12] Keratin fibres or silk -- The subclass D06M 2101/12 relates to the chemical constitution of protein-based fibres such as keratin fibres (e.g., wool) and silk, covering their structure and composition when treated in ways not covered elsewhere in the textiles class. It is used for inventions involving the makeup of these natural fibres for textile applications.
[D06M 2101/14] Collagen fibres -- The CPC subclass D06M 2101/14 defines collagen fibers, describing their chemical constitution and use in textile or fibrous products when such treatment is not covered elsewhere, including the preparation, modification and incorporation of these protein-based fibers into fabrics or other textile materials.
[D06M 2101/16] Synthetic fibres, other than mineral fibres -- The D06M 2101/16 entry deals with the chemical constitution of synthetic fibres that are not mineral-based, including their composition in threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous products. It falls under the treatment-of-textiles subclass where the treatment is not covered elsewhere, focusing on the material makeup rather than processing steps.
[D06M 2101/18] Synthetic fibres consisting of macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- D06M 2101/18 relates to synthetic fibres whose polymer chains are built exclusively through carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bond reactions, such as polymerisation of olefins or dienes, producing polyolefin or similar macromolecules. The classification is used for textile treatments where the fibre chemistry is defined by this type of polymer structure.
[D06M 2101/20] Polyalkenes, polymers or copolymers of compounds with alkenyl groups bonded to aromatic groups -- This subclass covers the chemical treatment of textiles, fibers, yarns or fabrics using polyalkene polymers or copolymers derived from compounds that have alkenyl groups bonded to aromatic groups. It is indexed under CPC code D06M 2101/20 within the treatment of textiles not provided for elsewhere, focusing on the chemical constitution of the material.
[D06M 2101/22] Polymers or copolymers of halogenated mono-olefins -- D06M 2101/22 refers to polymers or copolymers made from halogenated mono-olefins that are used in chemical treatments of textile fibres, yarns or fabrics to impart properties such as flame resistance, durability or other functional benefits. It falls within the CPC subgroup for the chemical constitution of treated textiles.
[D06M 2101/24] Polymers or copolymers of alkenylalcohols or esters thereof -- Code D06M 2101/24 pertains to polymers and copolymers derived from alkenyl alcohols or their esters that are applied in the chemical treatment of textiles such as fibres, yarns or fabrics. These compounds are employed to modify the physical or chemical properties of the material, providing characteristics like enhanced durability, water repellency or other functional benefits.
[D06M 2101/26] Polymers or copolymers of unsaturated carboxylic acids or derivatives thereof -- D06M 2101/26 covers polymers or copolymers derived from unsaturated carboxylic acids and their derivatives that are applied in textile treatment not classified elsewhere, focusing on the chemical constitution of fibers, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. This entry falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles, and miscellaneous textile treatments.
[D06M 2101/28] Acrylonitrile -- Acrylonitrile refers to the synthetic monomer used to produce acrylic fibres and other polymeric textiles, defining the chemical constitution of fibres, threads, yarns or fabrics that incorporate this material. This subclass covers treatments of textile goods involving acrylonitrile where no more specific classification exists.
[D06M 2101/30] Synthetic polymers consisting of macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds
[D06M 2101/32] Polyesters -- D06M 2101/32 covers polyesters as chemical constituents of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods that are treated in ways not covered elsewhere in the textile treatment subclass. It relates to the formulation and use of polyester polymers for modifying the properties of textile materials.
[D06M 2101/34] Polyamides -- The D06M 2101/34 subclass pertains to polyamide polymers used as fibers, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods that are subject to a specific treatment not covered elsewhere. It classifies the chemical constitution of these polyamide materials when they are processed or altered.
[D06M 2101/36] Aromatic polyamides -- D06M 2101/36 covers aromatic polyamides, a class of synthetic polymers where the polymer backbone contains aromatic rings linked by amide groups. These materials are used in textile fibers and fabrics requiring high strength, heat resistance, and chemical durability.
[D06M 2101/38] Polyurethanes -- D06M 2101/38 designates polyurethane substances employed to alter the chemical constitution of textile fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods for treatments that are not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[D06M 2101/40] Fibres of carbon -- D06M 2101/40 covers carbon fibres used as textile fibres, describing their chemical constitution and structure when incorporated into threads, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods. It pertains to the material composition of carbon-based fibres for textile applications that are not covered by more specific treatment subclasses.
[D06M 2200/00] Functionality of the treatment composition and/or properties imparted to the textile material -- D06M 2200/00 covers the functional aspects of textile-treatment compositions, focusing on the properties they confer to the fabric such as durability, water repellency, flame resistance, or other performance attributes. It applies to treatments not classified elsewhere in the textiles hierarchy.
[D06M 2200/01] Stain or soil resistance -- The D06M 2200/01 entry concerns textile treatments that provide stain or soil resistance, describing the functional characteristics of the coating or finish and the resulting protective properties imparted to the fabric. It is used for compositions and methods aimed at preventing discoloration or fouling of textiles.
[D06M 2200/05] Lotus effect -- The CPC subclass D06M 2200/05, titled Lotus effect, covers textile treatments that create a superhydrophobic surface mimicking the self-cleaning properties of lotus leaves. It relates to functional finishes that impart water repellency, dirt resistance and low surface energy to fabrics.
[D06M 2200/10] Repellency against liquids -- The code D06M 2200/10 covers treatments that give textile materials the ability to repel liquids. It includes compositions and methods that impart water-, oil- or other liquid-repellent properties to fabrics, where the treatment type is not covered by more specific subclasses. This subclass is part of the broader group for textile treatments whose function is defined by the properties conferred.
[D06M 2200/11] Oleophobic properties -- Subgroup D06M 2200/11 covers textile treatments that confer oleophobic characteristics, meaning the fabric repels oil and oil-based liquids. It includes compositions and processes designed to create a surface that resists oil adsorption, improving stain resistance and ease of cleaning.
[D06M 2200/12] Hydrophobic properties -- The CPC entry D06M 2200/12 covers textile treatments that provide hydrophobic characteristics, including compositions and methods that make fabrics water-repellent. It is classified under treatments not provided for elsewhere, focusing on the functionality of the treatment and the properties imparted to the textile material.
[D06M 2200/20] Treatment influencing the crease behaviour, the wrinkle resistance, the crease recovery or the ironing ease -- This subclass addresses textile treatments that alter how fabrics crease, resist wrinkles, recover their shape, or become easier to iron. It includes chemical or physical processes applied to fibers, yarns, or finished textiles to impart improved crease behaviour, enhanced wrinkle resistance, and better crease-recovery performance.
[D06M 2200/25] Resistance to light or sun, i.e. protection of the textile itself as well as UV shielding materials or treatment compositions therefor -- The D06M 2200/25 subclass covers textile treatments that give fabrics resistance to light or sunlight. It includes compositions and methods that protect the material itself and provide ultraviolet-shielding, such as UV-absorbing finishes or additives. The focus is on functional properties that improve durability against solar exposure.
[D06M 2200/30] Flame or heat resistance, fire retardancy properties -- D06M 2200/30 covers textile treatments that impart flame or heat resistance, providing fire-retardant properties to fabrics. It includes compositions and processes designed to reduce flammability, slow combustion, or prevent ignition of treated textiles.
[D06M 2200/35] Abrasion, pilling or fibrillation resistance -- The entry D06M 2200/35 covers textile treatments that enhance a fabric's resistance to mechanical wear, specifically improving abrasion durability and preventing pilling or fibrillation. It includes compositions, processes, or additives applied to textiles to impart these properties, focusing on functional performance rather than specific material types. This subclass is used for inventions where the primary goal is to maintain surface integrity under use.
[D06M 2200/40] Reduced friction resistance, lubricant properties -- The D06M 2200/40 subclass covers textile treatments that give fabrics reduced friction or lubricating characteristics, such as coatings or finishes that lower mechanical resistance and improve slip or handling. It includes any composition or process that imparts these low-friction or lubricant properties to textiles where no more specific subclass applies.
[D06M 2200/45] Shrinking resistance, anti-felting properties -- The CPC subclass D06M 2200/45 pertains to textile treatments that give fabrics shrinking resistance and anti-felting properties. It covers compositions or processes that impart these functional characteristics to textiles where no more specific subclass applies.
[D06M 2200/50] Modified hand or grip properties -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, code D06M 2200/50 relates to textile treatments that modify the hand- or grip properties of fabrics, such as softness, roughness, or tactile feel, to achieve a desired functional performance. It falls under the category for treatments of textiles not provided for elsewhere and emphasizes the functional effect of the applied composition.
[D06M 23/00] Treatment of fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics or fibrous goods made from such materials, characterised by the process -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials by specific processes or when the chemical compound or composition is applied in a particular form, e.g. in powder form, encapsulated, in the form of foam etc.
[D06M 23/005] Applying monomolecular films on textile products like fibres, threads or fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of textile material by forming monomolecular layers of compounds on the textile surface; i.e. very thin layers, in general of one molecule thickness.  The layer by layer deposition of molecules on the textile surface is also classified here: e.g. deposition of an anionic layer which is covered by a cationic layer, this might repeated several times.
[D06M 23/02] Processes in which the treating agent is releasably affixed or incorporated into a dispensing means -- This subclass covers textile treatment processes where the chemical or functional agent is temporarily attached to or embedded in a carrier that releases it during treatment. The carrier may be a pad, roller, film or other dispensing device that can be removed or left behind after use.
[D06M 23/04] Processes in which the treating agent is applied in the form of a foam -- The D06M 23/04 subclass covers textile treatment processes where the active agent is delivered as a foam, applied to fibres, yarns, fabrics or other fibrous goods to modify their properties. Foam application enables uniform penetration, efficient use of chemicals and controlled reaction conditions.
[D06M 23/06] Processes in which the treating agent is dispersed in a gas, e.g. aerosols -- This subclass covers textile-treatment methods in which the active agent is introduced as a gas-borne dispersion, such as an aerosol, allowing uniform application to fibers, yarns or fabrics. It includes processes that atomise or mix the treating substance with a carrier gas before contacting the material.
[D06M 23/08] Processes in which the treating agent is applied in powder or granular form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of textile materials with treating agents in granular form, like powders and in any size of particles, i.e. also including nanoparticles or microparticles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The treatment of textile materials with encapsulated treating agents -> D06M23/12
[D06M 23/10] Processes in which the treating agent is dissolved or dispersed in organic solvents -- This subclass covers methods of treating textile fibres, yarns or fabrics where the treating agent is dissolved or dispersed in an organic solvent, such as for bleaching, dyeing or finishing. The solvent may be water-miscible or non-aqueous and the process includes application, impregnation and subsequent removal or curing.
[D06M 23/105] Processes in which the solvent is in a supercritical state -- D06M 23/105 covers textile treatment processes that employ a solvent in a supercritical state to modify the surface or bulk properties of fibres, yarns, or fabrics. The supercritical fluid acts as a medium for cleaning, coating, impregnating, or chemically reacting with the material, providing enhanced penetration and diffusion compared with conventional liquids.
[D06M 23/12] Processes in which the treating agent is incorporated in microcapsules -- D06M 23/12 covers textile-treatment processes in which the functional agent is incorporated into microcapsules applied to fibers, yarns or fabrics, providing controlled release of the treating substance during use or subsequent washing.
[D06M 23/14] Processes for the fixation or treatment of textile materials in three-dimensional [3D] forms
[D06M 23/16] Processes for the non-uniform application of treating agents, e.g. one-sided treatment -- D06M 23/16 pertains to processes that apply treating agents to textiles in a non-uniform manner, for example applying a finish to only one side of a fibre, yarn or fabric. The entry covers methods where the treatment is deliberately limited to selected areas rather than the whole material.
[D06M 23/18] for the chemical treatment of borders of fabrics or knittings -- This subclass relates to processes that chemically treat the edges or borders of woven or knitted textiles, applying finishes, dyes, protective coatings or other agents to the border areas to improve durability, appearance or functional properties.
[D06M 2400/00] Specific information on the treatment or the process itself not provided in -- The D06M 2400/00 subclass gathers disclosures that provide specific details about a textile treatment or the associated process when such information is not covered by any other defined subclass. It is used for generic or unconventional treatment data that cannot be placed in more narrowly defined categories. This entry therefore serves as a catch-all for non-specified textile-treatment information.
[D06M 2400/01] Creating covalent bondings between the treating agent and the fibre -- This subclass covers processes that form covalent bonds between a chemical treating agent and textile fibres, thereby permanently attaching the agent to the material. The methods involve reacting functional groups on the fibre and the agent to create stable chemical linkages, improving durability and performance of the treatment.
[D06M 2400/02] Treating compositions in the form of solgel or aerogel -- The CPC subclass D06M 2400/02 covers textile treatments that employ compositions presented as sol-gel or aerogel materials. It is used for processes where the specific treatment method is not otherwise classified, focusing on the application of these gel-based formulations to fabrics or similar substrates.
[D06M 7/00] Treating fibres, threads, yarns, fabrics, or fibrous goods made of other substances with subsequent freeing of the treated goods from the treating medium, e.g. swelling, e.g. polyolefins  D06M10/00  takes precedence; treating fibres or filaments made of glass, mineral -, or slag wool  C03C ; carbon fibres  D01F11/10 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of fibrous materials with subsequent freeing of the treated good from treating medium: these group and subgroup are not used.
[D06M 7/005] made of asbestos
[D06N] WALL, FLOOR, OR LIKE COVERING MATERIALS, e.g. LINOLEUM, OILCLOTH, ARTIFICIAL LEATHER, ROOFING FELT, CONSISTING OF A FIBROUS WEB COATED WITH A LAYER OF MACROMOLECULAR MATERIAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibrous webs coated with at least one layer of a macromolecular material to impart specific properties and/or to be used in a particular application, such as wall or floor coverings, e.g. linoleum, PVC floor coverings, carpets or artificial turf, artificial leather, building materials including roofing membranes or underlays, wearing apparel, airbags, footwear, gloves, upholstery, tents, canopy, tarpaulins.  Wallpaper comprising a textile layer.  Methods or apparatus for making these products.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Laminates in general (exception: product obtained by extrusion coating) -> B32B - Fibrous web coated only with nanomaterials -> B82Y30/00 , B82Y40/00 - Treatment of glass fibres or filaments -> C03C25/00 - Treatment of ceramic fibres or natural or artificial stone fibres -> C04B41/00 - Fibre reinforced materials -> C08J5/04 , B29C70/00 - Adhesive tapes -> C09J7/00 - Mechanical aspects of the treatment of textiles and apparatus used therefore -> D06B - D06C - Treatment of fibrous webs with only inorganic and/or organic non-macromolecular materials -> D06M - Only impregnation of fibrous webs -> D06M - Treatment of fibres, threads or yarns with polymeric materials -> D06M14/00 , D06M15/00 , D06M17/00 , D06M23/00 - coated webs which retain the character of paper or cardboard -> D21 - Coated paper -> D21H  **Glossary:** - Fibrous web: Woven, non-woven, knitted or tufted fabric. - Coated with a macromolecular material: Macromolecular material or polymer composition applied by e.g. brushing, flowing, sprinkling, spraying, doctor blading, casting or extruding a solution, dispersion, melt or paste.
[D06N 1/00] Linoleum -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Floor coverings obtained by applying a linoleum composition onto a fibrous web.
[D06N 2201/00] Chemical constitution of the fibres, threads or yarns -- This subclass defines patents concerning the chemical composition of the fibrous component - fibres, threads or yarns - used in wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It addresses the molecular structure, polymer type or additives that form the base web before it is coated with a macromolecular layer.
[D06N 2201/02] Synthetic macromolecular fibres -- D06N 2201/02 pertains to synthetic macromolecular fibres, covering the chemical structure and composition of fibres, threads or yarns made from polymeric materials. It includes fibres used in textiles and in wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer.
[D06N 2201/0209] Elastomeric, elastic fibres, e.g. spandex, lycra -- The CPC subclass D06N 2201/0209 covers elastomeric or elastic fibers such as spandex or lycra, which are used in textile applications where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular layer. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles, wall/floor or like covering materials, and chemical constitution of fibres, threads or yarns.
[D06N 2201/0218] Vinyl resin fibres -- Vinyl-resin fibres are textile filaments produced from polymerised vinyl compounds, forming a synthetic fibrous web that can be used alone or as a base for coating with macromolecular layers in products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. This entry covers the chemical constitution and manufacturing of those vinyl-based fibers within the broader class of treated textile materials.
[D06N 2201/0227] Aromatic vinyl resin, e.g. styrenic (co)polymers -- The CPC subgroup D06N 2201/0227 refers to aromatic vinyl resins, particularly styrenic (co)polymers, employed in the treatment of textile materials such as fibrous webs coated with a macromolecular layer for wall, floor or similar covering applications. These resins serve as the chemical constitution of the coating that imparts protective, decorative or functional properties to the textile substrate.
[D06N 2201/0236] Vinyl halide, e.g. PVC, PVDC, PVF, PVDF -- The subclass D06N 2201/0236 covers vinyl-halide polymers such as PVC, PVDC, PVF and PVDF when used to form the macromolecular coating on fibrous webs for textile-based wall, floor or similar coverings like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, emphasizing the chemical constitution of these coating materials.
[D06N 2201/0245] Acrylic resin fibres -- Acrylic resin fibres are synthetic textile filaments produced by polymerising acrylic monomers into a macromolecular coating on a fibrous web, yielding fibers with high durability, resistance to weathering and a wool-like feel. They are employed in wall, floor and other covering materials where chemical composition of the yarn is a key performance factor.
[D06N 2201/0254] Polyolefin fibres -- Polyolefin fibres are synthetic filaments derived from polyolefin polymers such as polypropylene or polyethylene, characterised by a hydrocarbon backbone that imparts chemical resistance, low moisture absorption and good mechanical strength. They are used in fibrous webs for wall, floor and covering materials including linoleum, oilcloth and artificial leather, and fall under the chemical-constitution sub-class of textile treatments.
[D06N 2201/0263] Polyamide fibres -- The CPC entry D06N 2201/0263 covers polyamide fibres, a class of synthetic polymers used to form threads, yarns and fabrics. It details the chemical constitution of these fibres, including their molecular structure and composition, for applications such as wall-, floor- and other covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with macromolecular layers.
[D06N 2201/0272] Aromatic polyamide fibres -- Aromatic polyamide fibres are synthetic filaments made from aromatic polyamide polymers such as aramids, providing high tensile strength, heat resistance and chemical stability. They are used in high-performance textiles, protective clothing and reinforcement layers for wall, floor or roofing coverings where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular matrix.
[D06N 2201/0281] Polyurethane fibres -- The subclass D06N 2201/0281 covers fibres, threads or yarns whose chemical structure is based on polyurethane polymers. It pertains to textile materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt and similar fibrous webs that are coated with a macromolecular layer, defining the composition of the polymeric fibres themselves.
[D06N 2201/029] Fluoropolymer fibres -- Fluoropolymer fibres are textile yarns whose chemical constitution is based on fluorinated polymers, giving them high resistance to chemicals, heat and moisture. They are used in specialized fabrics and coated coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt, where a fibrous web is combined with a macromolecular coating.
[D06N 2201/04] Vegetal fibres -- D06N 2201/04 covers vegetal fibres used in textiles, referring to fibers derived from plants such as cotton, linen, jute, hemp and sisal, and their chemical constitution for use in fibrous webs that are subsequently coated with macromolecular layers for wall, floor or covering materials.
[D06N 2201/042] Cellulose fibres, e.g. cotton -- The CPC subclass D06N 2201/042 covers cellulose-based fibres such as cotton, focusing on their chemical composition and structure when used in fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings. It pertains to the material science of natural polymer fibres employed in textiles and coated substrates.
[D06N 2201/045] Lignocellulosic fibres, e.g. jute, sisal, hemp, flax, bamboo -- The subgroup D06N 2201/045 groups natural lignocellulosic fibres such as jute, sisal, hemp, flax and bamboo, focusing on their chemical constitution and characteristics when used as fibrous webs for wall, floor or similar covering materials. It is part of the hierarchy covering treatment of textiles and fibrous web coatings with macromolecular layers.
[D06N 2201/047] Wood fibres -- The D06N 2201/047 subclass covers wood fibres employed in fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather, where the web is later coated with a macromolecular layer. It concerns the chemical constitution of the wood-based fibres, threads or yarns used in these textile-like materials.
[D06N 2201/06] Animal fibres, e.g. hair, wool, silk -- D06N 2201/06 covers animal-derived fibres such as hair, wool and silk, focusing on their chemical constitution within fibrous webs used for wall, floor or similar coverings. The code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification for treatments of textiles and related materials.
