[D06N 2201/08] Inorganic fibres -- D06N 2201/08 classifies inorganic fibres-such as glass, carbon, mineral or ceramic filaments-used in the manufacture of fibrous webs that are later coated with macromolecular layers for wall, floor or similar coverings. It covers the chemical composition and structural features of these non-organic threads and yarns as they relate to treated textile materials.
[D06N 2201/082] Glass fibres -- D06N 2201/082 classifies glass fibres employed as the fibrous component in wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather, where the fibres are coated with a macromolecular layer. The entry focuses on the chemical constitution of the glass fibres, threads or yarns used in these composites.
[D06N 2201/085] Metal fibres -- D06N 2201/085 covers metal fibres, i.e., fibres whose core or coating is composed of metallic elements, used in textiles such as linoleum, artificial leather or roofing felt. The subclass addresses the chemical constitution of the metal component of fibres, threads or yarns employed as reinforcing or functional elements in wall, floor or similar covering materials.
[D06N 2201/087] Carbon fibres -- The subclass D06N 2201/087 covers carbon fibres, which are high-strength, high-modulus filaments made primarily of elemental carbon and used as the fibrous component in textiles such as coated fabrics, linoleum, artificial leather, roofing felt and similar wall or floor coverings. It addresses the chemical constitution of these fibres, threads or yarns, distinguishing them from other fibre types within the treatment of textiles hierarchy.
[D06N 2201/10] Conjugate fibres, e.g. core-sheath, side-by-side -- D06N 2201/10 covers conjugate fibres such as core-sheath or side-by-side constructions, where two or more distinct polymeric or fibrous constituents are combined in a single filament to impart specific mechanical, aesthetic or functional properties, often used in textiles and composite wall, floor or covering materials.
[D06N 2201/12] Fibres being in the form of a tape, strip or ribbon -- This subclass covers fibrous materials whose individual fibers are manufactured as flat tapes, strips or ribbons rather than round yarns, typically for use in coated web coverings such as linoleum or artificial leather. It pertains to the chemical constitution of such fibers and their treatment in textile applications.
[D06N 2203/00] <NO TITLE>
[D06N 2203/02] Natural macromolecular compounds or derivatives thereof -- D06N 2203/02 covers natural macromolecular compounds or their derivatives used to form the coating layer on fibrous webs that become wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The classification falls under the broader group for treatment of textiles, specifically for covering materials made of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular film. It includes substances derived from natural polymers applied in the manufacturing of these coated textile products.
[D06N 2203/022] Natural rubber -- This subclass relates to the use of natural rubber as the macromolecular coating applied to fibrous web substrates such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, i.e. wall, floor or similar covering materials. It covers processes and formulations for coating textile-derived webs with natural rubber to produce protective or decorative coverings.
[D06N 2203/024] Polysaccharides or derivatives thereof -- D06N 2203/024 covers the use of polysaccharides and their derivatives in the treatment of textile-based wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It relates to fibrous webs coated with a macromolecular layer, where the polysaccharide component serves as a binder, film-forming or functional additive in the coating.
[D06N 2203/026] Cellulose or derivatives thereof -- The code D06N 2203/026 covers cellulose and its derivatives as the macromolecular coating material applied to fibrous webs used for wall, floor and similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It classifies textile treatments where a cellulose-based layer provides the functional surface.
[D06N 2203/028] Starch or derivatives thereof -- The CPC subclass D06N 2203/028 covers the use of starch and its derivatives in processes for treating fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. It addresses applying these carbohydrate-based binders or coatings to improve adhesion, flexibility or durability of the macromolecular layer.
[D06N 2203/04] Macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- The D06N 2203/04 subclass covers polymers that are produced solely through reactions of carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds, such as addition or condensation polymerizations, and are applied as coating layers on fibrous webs used for wall, floor or similar coverings like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. These macromolecular compounds serve to provide protection, durability or decorative characteristics to the textile-based substrate.
[D06N 2203/041] Polyacrylic -- The D06N 2203/041 subclass concerns the use of polyacrylic polymers as coating materials for fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar covering products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt; it falls under the treatment of textiles where a macromolecular layer is applied to a fibrous substrate.
[D06N 2203/042] Polyolefin (co)polymers -- The class D06N 2203/042 includes the use of polyolefin and polyolefin copolymers as the macromolecular coating layer applied to fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The polymers serve as the primary material that imparts durability, flexibility and water-resistance to the textile-based substrate.
[D06N 2203/044] Fluoropolymers -- The subclass D06N 2203/044 pertains to fluoropolymer materials used as the coating layer on fibrous webs such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. Fluoropolymers give the covering chemical resistance, low friction and water-repellency, improving durability of wall, floor or similar coverings. This entry is situated under treatment of textiles and like covering materials.
[D06N 2203/045] Vinyl (co)polymers -- The D06N 2203/045 subclass concerns vinyl (co)polymers employed as the macromolecular coating on fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It groups chemical compositions and formulations of vinyl-based polymer layers designed to impart durability, flexibility and water-resistance to these textile-derived substrates.
[D06N 2203/047] Arromatic vinyl (co)polymers, e.g. styrene -- The CPC subclass D06N 2203/047 covers the use of aromatic vinyl polymers, for example polystyrene, as coating materials on fibrous webs to produce wall, floor or similar covered textiles such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2203/048] Polyvinylchloride (co)polymers -- The CPC subclass D06N 2203/048 refers to the use of polyvinylchloride (PVC) and its copolymers as the macromolecular coating layer on fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, where the textile substrate is treated with this polymer coating.
[D06N 2203/06] Macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- The CPC subclass D06N 2203/06 covers macromolecular compounds, such as polymers or resins, that are produced by processes not limited to reactions of carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds, and are used to coat fibrous webs for applications like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt or similar wall and floor covering materials.
[D06N 2203/061] Polyesters -- The D06N 2203/061 entry concerns polyester polymers employed as the macromolecular coating layer on fibrous webs used for wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It falls under the treatment of textiles or the like, specifically for coated covering materials. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[D06N 2203/063] Polycarbonates -- The subclass D06N 2203/063 covers the use of polycarbonate polymers as a macromolecular coating layer on fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes polycarbonate resins applied to provide durability, transparency or chemical resistance to the textile-based substrate.
[D06N 2203/065] Polyamides -- This subclass pertains to the use of polyamide polymers as the macromolecular coating layer on fibrous webs to form wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It includes processes for applying, curing or bonding polyamides to the web to impart durability, wear resistance and structural integrity.
[D06N 2203/066] Silicon polymers -- The subclass D06N 2203/066 relates to the use of silicon-based polymers as the macromolecular coating applied to fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar covering products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It addresses the formulation, application and properties of these silicon polymers that provide durability, water resistance and flexibility to the textile-based coverings.
[D06N 2203/068] Polyurethanes -- This subclass covers the use of polyurethanes in the manufacture or treatment of textile-based wall, floor or similar covering materials, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, where a fibrous web is coated with a polyurethane macromolecular layer.
[D06N 2203/08] Bituminous material, e.g. asphalt, tar, bitumen -- This subclass deals with the application of bituminous substances-such as asphalt, tar or bitumen-as coating layers on fibrous textile webs that are used for wall, floor or similar coverings. It covers the preparation of these macromolecular-based surface treatments within the broader field of textile treatment.
[D06N 2205/00] Condition, form or state of the materials -- This subclass concerns the physical condition, form or state of fibrous-web covering materials that carry a macromolecular coating, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It addresses features like texture, thickness, flexibility, moisture content, curing or any treatment that alters the material's inherent properties during or after coating.
[D06N 2205/02] Dispersion -- The subclass concerns the preparation, application or use of a dispersion of solid or liquid particles within a fibrous web that is coated with a polymeric layer for wall, floor or similar covering materials. It includes processes that modify surface characteristics such as colour, texture, durability or functional performance by applying or embedding the dispersed phase.
[D06N 2205/023] Emulsion, aqueous dispersion, latex -- The CPC code D06N 2205/023 covers emulsions, aqueous dispersions and latexes employed in the processing of fibrous textiles that are later coated with a macromolecular layer to form wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It pertains to the condition, form or state of these coating materials during treatment.
[D06N 2205/026] Plastisol -- Plastisol is a liquid dispersion of polymer particles, usually polyvinyl chloride, suspended in plasticizers that can be applied to a fibrous web and then cured to form a flexible, continuous coating. It is commonly used to produce wall, floor and similar coverings such as linoleum, artificial leather or oilcloth, giving the material durability, water-resistance and a uniform surface.
[D06N 2205/04] Foam -- The D06N 2205/04 subclass covers the use of foam as a material condition for fibrous webs that are coated with a macromolecular layer and employed as wall, floor or similar covering articles such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It pertains to the presence of a cellular polymer structure that provides cushioning, insulation or bulk in the treated textile product.
[D06N 2205/045] Froth -- This subclass refers to the occurrence or formation of froth on or within wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. It is used to classify patents dealing with the detection, prevention or handling of froth during manufacturing or use of these textile-based coverings.
[D06N 2205/06] Melt -- The CPC subclass D06N 2205/06 covers the melt condition of the macromolecular layer applied to fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It pertains to the state of the polymer coating while molten, which is relevant to processes that spread, bond or cure the material on the textile substrate.
[D06N 2205/08] Microballoons, microcapsules -- This CPC subgroup defines the incorporation of microballoons or microcapsules into a fibrous web that is coated with a polymeric layer, intended for wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The micro-structures provide functions like insulation, cushioning, weight reduction or controlled release of agents, altering the condition, form or state of the treated textile-based substrate.
[D06N 2205/10] Particulate form, e.g. powder, granule -- The CPC subclass D06N 2205/10 covers wall, floor or similar coverings formed from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer that are presented in particulate form such as powders or granules. It relates to the treatment of textiles or like materials, specifically the condition, form or state of the material.
[D06N 2205/103] Nanoparticles -- Class D06N 2205/103 designates the condition or state of wall, floor or similar covering materials-fibrous webs coated with macromolecular layers when they contain or are treated with nanoparticles. It addresses textile-based coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that incorporate nanoscale particles to modify properties.
[D06N 2205/106] Scrap or recycled particles -- This subclass deals with wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a polymer layer where scrap or recycled particles are incorporated, such as in linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It addresses the condition or form of the material when waste or reclaimed particles are used, classified under CPC D06N 2205/106.
[D06N 2205/12] Platelets, flakes -- The D06N 2205/12 sub-group covers plate-shaped or flake-like particles incorporated into fibrous webs or coated layers for textile and covering material treatments. These platelet or flake additives are used to modify surface properties such as abrasion resistance, waterproofing, conductivity, or decorative appearance. It pertains to the condition, form or state of the coating material within the broader class of treated textile coverings.
[D06N 2205/14] Fibrous additives or fillers -- D06N 2205/14 covers the use of fibrous additives or fillers incorporated into wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a polymeric layer. These additives may modify mechanical, thermal or surface properties of products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt, etc.
[D06N 2205/16] Solution -- The CPC entry D06N 2205/16 "Solution" covers the use of liquid chemical solutions applied to fibrous webs that are subsequently coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It relates to the condition, form or state of these wall, floor or covering materials during the coating process, including the preparation, treatment or modification of the solution itself.
[D06N 2205/18] Scraps or recycled materials -- D06N 2205/18 pertains to the condition, form or state of wall, floor and similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, specifically addressing the use of scraps, waste or recycled components in products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2205/20] Cured materials, e.g. vulcanised, cross-linked -- D06N 2205/20 pertains to cured textile-derived materials where a fibrous web, such as linoleum or artificial leather, has been chemically or thermally cross-linked, vulcanised or otherwise solidified. The subclass includes the condition of the coating after curing, affecting properties like durability and flexibility.
[D06N 2205/22] Partially cured -- D06N 2205/22 covers wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer which has only been partially cured. The term denotes a state where the polymer coating has begun to set but has not reached its final hardened condition, affecting handling, further processing or intermediate properties.
[D06N 2205/24] Coagulated materials -- D06N 2205/24 refers to coagulated materials used as the coating layer on fibrous webs that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The subclass addresses the composition, preparation or properties of coagulated polymeric or macromolecular substances that provide a protective or decorative surface, and falls under the treatment of textiles in the CPC hierarchy.
[D06N 2205/243] by heating, steam -- The subclass D06N 2205/243 covers methods of treating wall, floor or similar covering materials-fibrous webs coated with macromolecular layers-by applying heat or steam to modify their condition, form or state, such as drying, curing or setting the coating.
[D06N 2205/246] by extracting the solvent -- This subclass covers processes for wall, floor or similar covering sheets made of a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, where the step of removing the solvent used to apply the coating is specified. The solvent extraction may be achieved by evaporation, drying, or other means to solidify the coating and finish the product.
[D06N 2207/00] Treatments by energy or chemical effects -- This subclass groups processes that modify fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-materials formed from a fibrous base coated with a macromolecular layer-by applying energy (heat, radiation, plasma) or chemical agents. The treatments aim to alter surface properties, improve durability, adhesion, waterproofing or other functional characteristics of the coated textile product.
[D06N 2207/02] using vibration -- The subclass D06N 2207/02 covers processes that treat wall, floor or similar covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt formed from a fibrous web coated with a polymer layer-by applying mechanical vibration. Vibration is used to improve bonding, densify the structure, cure or otherwise modify the coating during the treatment. This falls under the CPC section for textile treatments using energy or chemical effects.
[D06N 2207/04] using steam -- The CPC subclass D06N 2207/04 covers the use of steam to treat wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes processes where steam is applied to cure, bond, densify or otherwise modify the material's physical or chemical properties, falling under treatments by energy or chemical effects.
[D06N 2207/06] using liquids, e.g. water -- This subclass concerns processes that employ liquids such as water to treat fibrous-web materials coated with a macromolecular layer, including linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. The liquids may be used for cleaning, curing, chemical modification or other energy based effects to improve surface or performance characteristics.
[D06N 2207/08] using gas -- The subclass D06N 2207/08 covers processes that treat fibrous-web covering materials coated with a macromolecular layer by applying a gas, such as for curing, polymerising, cross-linking or otherwise modifying the coating. It includes any method where the gaseous medium effects chemical or energetic changes to the material's surface or bulk.
[D06N 2207/10] using flames -- The CPC subclass D06N 2207/10 covers the use of flame or combustion processes to treat fibrous web materials that are coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The flame treatment is applied to modify the physical or chemical properties of these wall, floor or similar covering materials.
[D06N 2207/12] by wave energy or particle radiation -- D06N 2207/12 covers methods for treating fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that have a macromolecular coating, using wave energy or particle radiation. The subclass includes processes that employ electromagnetic waves, lasers, microwaves or particle beams to cure, cross-link, modify or otherwise alter the coating or substrate. It is used for energy-based treatments rather than chemical or mechanical actions.
[D06N 2207/123] using electromagnetic radiation, e.g. IR, UV, actinic light, laser, X-ray, gamma-ray, microwave, radio frequency -- The subclass D06N 2207/123 covers methods for treating fibrous web-based wall, floor or similar covering materials coated with a macromolecular layer by exposing them to electromagnetic radiation such as infrared, ultraviolet, laser, X-ray, microwave or radio-frequency energy. The radiation is used to cure, dry, cross-link, modify or otherwise alter the coating or the substrate.
[D06N 2207/126] using particle radiation, e.g. ion, electron, neutron -- D06N 2207/126 covers processes that treat fibrous web coverings-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-by exposing them to particle radiation, including ions, electrons or neutrons, to alter the material's properties. This falls under the broader class for energy- or chemical-based textile treatments.
[D06N 2207/14] Corona, ionisation, electrical discharge -- The CPC subclass D06N 2207/14 covers the use of corona, ionisation or electrical discharge to treat fibrous webs such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous base coated with a macromolecular layer. These energy-based processes modify surface properties, improve adhesion or impart functional characteristics, and fall under the broader category of textile and covering-material treatments.
[D06N 2209/00] Properties of the materials -- The D06N 2209/00 subclass concerns the analysis and evaluation of the physical, mechanical, chemical and functional characteristics of fibrous webs that have been coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It includes methods for measuring properties like tensile strength, flexibility, durability, water resistance, thermal stability and surface texture, and the assessment of performance under various conditions.
[D06N 2209/02] having acoustical properties -- The subgroup D06N 2209/02 covers wall, floor or similar coverings made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer that are designed to provide acoustic performance, such as sound absorption or insulation. It applies to products like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt when they are engineered to reduce noise transmission.
[D06N 2209/025] Insulating, sound absorber -- D06N 2209/025 covers fibrous sheet materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer and possess insulating or sound-absorbing properties. The classification applies to treatments that enhance thermal or acoustic performance of these textile-based covering materials.
[D06N 2209/04] having electrical or magnetic properties -- This subclass covers textile-based covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt whose fibrous web is coated with a polymer layer and that are engineered to possess electrical or magnetic characteristics, for uses like static dissipation, electromagnetic shielding or heating.
[D06N 2209/041] Conductive -- This CPC subgroup D06N 2209/041 relates to fibrous webs such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer and possess electrical conductivity. It classifies products whose conductive property is a primary functional characteristic, encompassing conductive flooring, wall or other covering materials.
[D06N 2209/043] Insulating -- This CPC subclass concerns the insulating properties of fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer. It addresses how these textile-based sheets provide thermal, acoustic or electrical insulation.
[D06N 2209/045] Magnetic, paramagnetic -- The subclass D06N 2209/045 covers wall, floor or similar covering materials-fibrous webs coated with a macromolecular layer-that possess magnetic or paramagnetic characteristics. It relates to textiles or artificial leather products engineered to exhibit magnetic behavior for functions such as shielding, sensing or actuation.
[D06N 2209/046] Anti-static -- The D06N 2209/046 entry defines anti-static measures for fibrous-web coverings that have a macromolecular coating, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It concerns treatments that give the material a reduced tendency to accumulate static electricity, improving its electrical properties for wall, floor or similar applications.
[D06N 2209/048] Electromagnetic interference shielding -- The entry covers methods and materials for providing electromagnetic interference shielding to fibrous web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, and roofing felt, where a macromolecular coating is applied to impart shielding properties. It falls under CPC D06N 2209/048 within the textile treatment of wall, floor or similar coverings.
[D06N 2209/06] having thermal properties -- D06N 2209/06 defines textiles or wall-floor covering laminates that incorporate a fibrous web coated with a polymer layer and are distinguished by specific thermal characteristics, such as insulation, heat resistance or conductivity, as found in linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2209/062] Conductive -- The D06N 2209/062 entry covers conductive treatments applied to fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer. It includes methods for imparting electrical conductivity by incorporating conductive particles, films or coatings, enabling functions like static dissipation or electro-heating.
[D06N 2209/065] Insulating -- D06N 2209/065 covers insulating characteristics of wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer. The subclass addresses how the material resists heat, sound or moisture transmission.
[D06N 2209/067] Flame resistant, fire resistant -- This subclass covers fibrous web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, and roofing felt that have been treated or formulated to resist flame or fire. It relates to the material's fire-resistant properties and performance.
[D06N 2209/08] having optical properties -- D06N 2209/08 classifies fibrous web textiles coated with a macromolecular layer-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-when the finished material is characterized by specific optical properties, including reflectivity, translucency, color-shifting or light-modulating effects. This subclass addresses the treatment of such coverings for purposes where their visual or light-interaction features are essential.
[D06N 2209/0807] Coloured -- The subclass covers coloured covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer. It relates to the application of pigments, dyes or stains that give the coated web a specific hue, and to the properties imparted by the colour.
[D06N 2209/0815] on the layer surface, e.g. ink -- This subclass covers processes for applying substances to the outer layer of fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather, where the base coating is a macromolecular film and the added material is placed on the surface, for example inks used for printing or marking.
[D06N 2209/0823] within the layer by addition of a colorant, e.g. pigments, dyes -- This subclass concerns methods for imparting colour to the coating layer of fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt by incorporating pigments, dyes or other colourants into the macromolecular material. It addresses the treatment of the surface layer rather than the underlying fabric.
[D06N 2209/083] Multi-coloured -- The D06N 2209/083 entry "Multi-coloured" covers wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and display more than one colour, relating to the colour-property of the coating.
[D06N 2209/0838] Bright, glossy, shiny surface -- This subclass concerns wall, floor or similar covering materials made of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer that are treated to have a bright, glossy or shiny surface appearance. It includes processes, additives or surface treatments that create or enhance a reflective finish on products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2209/0846] Matt, dull surface -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/0846 concerns methods for imparting a matt, dull surface finish to wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, which consist of a fibrous web coated with a polymer layer. This entry addresses treatments that intentionally reduce gloss to achieve a non-reflective appearance.
[D06N 2209/0853] Opaque -- D06N 2209/0853 covers the opaque characteristic of wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, where the coating prevents light transmission through the substrate.
[D06N 2209/0861] Transparent -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/0861 covers transparent wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web, such as linoleum or artificial leather, coated with a transparent macromolecular layer. It relates to treatments of textiles where the coating provides visibility through the material while retaining the underlying fibrous structure.
[D06N 2209/0869] Translucent -- The D06N 2209/0869 subclass covers fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, or roofing felt that have been coated with a macromolecular layer and are designed to be translucent, allowing light to pass through while retaining the material's protective and structural properties.
[D06N 2209/0876] Reflective -- D06N 2209/0876 covers textile-based wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and are characterized by reflective properties. It applies to products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt where the surface is designed to reflect light or other radiation.
[D06N 2209/0884] Refractive -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/0884 concerns the refractive properties of wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes inventions that address the control, measurement or modification of light refraction, refractive index or related optical characteristics of these treated textile-based coverings.
[D06N 2209/0892] Luminescent, fluorescent, phosphorescent -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/0892 covers luminescent, fluorescent and phosphorescent properties applied to fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer, relating to treatments that give these textile-based wall, floor or similar coverings light-emitting characteristics.
[D06N 2209/10] having mechanical properties -- The subclass D06N 2209/10 covers specifications of mechanical properties-such as tensile strength, elasticity, stiffness, impact resistance or abrasion durability-of wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer (e.g., linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt).
[D06N 2209/101] Vibration damping, energy absorption -- D06N 2209/101 covers the incorporation of vibration-damping or energy-absorption features into wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. The entry relates to treatments that give these textile-based coverings the ability to reduce or absorb mechanical vibrations.
[D06N 2209/103] Resistant to mechanical forces, e.g. shock, impact, puncture, flexion, shear, compression, tear -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/103 covers fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer and are characterised by resistance to mechanical forces, including shock, impact, puncture, flexion, shear, compression and tear.
[D06N 2209/105] Resistant to abrasion, scratch -- This CPC subclass D06N 2209/105 covers wall, floor or similar covering textiles such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and are characterized by resistance to abrasion-scratch.
[D06N 2209/106] Roughness, anti-slip, abrasiveness -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/106 covers the roughness, anti-slip and abrasive properties of coated fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It relates to measuring, modifying or improving surface texture to provide slip resistance or controlled abrasion in wall, floor or similar covering materials.
[D06N 2209/108] Slipping, anti-blocking, low friction -- D06N 2209/108 covers methods and additives that give fibrous-web coverings-including linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt-a slipping, anti-blocking or low-friction surface. The classification relates to treatments that modify the surface properties of these textile-derived materials to reduce friction and prevent them from sticking together.
[D06N 2209/12] Permeability or impermeability properties -- The D06N 2209/12 subclass addresses the permeability or impermeability characteristics of fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer. It concerns how these wall, floor or similar coverings allow or resist the passage of fluids, gases or vapours.
[D06N 2209/121] Permeability to gases, adsorption -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/121 covers the gas-permeability and adsorption characteristics of wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes testing methods, performance specifications and treatments that affect how gases pass through or are adsorbed by these coated textile composites.
[D06N 2209/123] Breathable -- The D06N 2209/123 class covers wall, floor or similar covering materials - such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt - made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer that is designed to permit the passage of air or moisture, i.e., breathable coverings.
[D06N 2209/125] Non-permeable -- A non-permeable textile covering is a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer that blocks the passage of liquids or gases, providing a barrier against moisture, vapor and fluids. Such treated materials are used for wall, floor or roof coverings like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt where impermeability is required.
[D06N 2209/126] Permeability to liquids, absorption -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/126 covers the determination or description of how liquids permeate and are absorbed by fibrous-web fabrics coated with macromolecular layers, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, focusing on the material's permeability and absorption characteristics.
[D06N 2209/128] Non-permeable -- This subgroup covers non-permeable wall, floor or covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, where the coating prevents fluid or gas passage. It pertains to the treatment of textiles to render them impermeable.
[D06N 2209/14] having chemical properties -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/14 covers wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and are characterized by specific chemical properties of the coating. This entry relates to the treatment of textiles where the chemical nature of the coating influences performance.
[D06N 2209/141] Hydrophilic -- The CPC code D06N 2209/141 covers the hydrophilic characteristics of wall, floor or similar covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-made from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer. It relates to how these treated textiles absorb or interact with water.
[D06N 2209/142] Hydrophobic -- D06N 2209/142 relates to hydrophobic treatments for fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer. The subclass addresses methods or compositions that impart water-repellent properties to these wall, floor or similar textile materials. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for treatment of textiles or the like.
[D06N 2209/143] Inert, i.e. inert to chemical degradation, corrosion resistant -- D06N 2209/143 covers wall, floor or similar covering textiles-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular layer that provides inertness to chemical degradation and resistance to corrosion. The classification applies to the material properties rather than the manufacturing process.
[D06N 2209/145] Oleophobic -- The D06N 2209/145 "Oleophobic" subclass covers textile or covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt or similar fibrous webs-whose surface has been treated to repel oils and other oily substances. It pertains to the property of oil resistance imparted by coatings, finishes or treatments applied to the fibrous substrate.
[D06N 2209/146] Soilproof, soil repellent -- The D06N 2209/146 subclass pertains to fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that have been treated to become soil-proof or soil-repellent. The classification covers the application of macromolecular coatings that give the material resistance to soil adhesion and staining.
[D06N 2209/147] Stainproof, stain repellent -- D06N 2209/147 relates to coatings or treatments that make wall, floor or similar covering textiles such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt resistant to staining. The classification covers stain-proof or stain-repellent substances applied to a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer. It is used to improve the appearance and durability of such treated coverings.
[D06N 2209/148] Superabsorbing -- D06N 2209/148 covers superabsorbing fibrous webs that are coated with a macromolecular layer and used as wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. The classification pertains to textile treatments that impart high liquid-absorption capacity to these coated webs, enabling applications in flooring, roofing felt and comparable products.
[D06N 2209/16] having other properties -- D06N 2209/16 classifies wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, where the claim pertains to properties of the material that are not covered by more specific subclasses. It is situated under textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and focuses on other material properties.
[D06N 2209/1607] Degradability -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/1607 covers the degradability of wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes inventions that impart, enhance or measure the ability of these coated textiles to break down under environmental or biological conditions.
[D06N 2209/1614] Biodegradable -- D06N 2209/1614 covers fibrous web textile coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt whose macromolecular coating is formulated to be biodegradable. The entry addresses the material's ability to break down by biological processes, providing environmentally friendly disposal or composting options.
[D06N 2209/1621] Water-soluble, water-dispersible -- D06N 2209/1621 covers water-soluble or water-dispersible agents used to treat fibrous webs that are coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt and similar wall or floor coverings. The classification pertains to the properties of these coating materials that enable dispersion or dissolution in water.
[D06N 2209/1628] Dimensional stability -- The subclass D06N 2209/1628 deals with the dimensional stability of wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It addresses treatments that prevent shrinkage, stretching or warping of these coated textiles to maintain their size and shape during use.
[D06N 2209/1635] Elasticity -- D06N 2209/1635 covers the elasticity characteristics of wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt. It relates to how these treated textiles stretch and recover.
[D06N 2209/1642] Hardnes -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/1642 relates to methods and apparatus for measuring the hardness of coated textile products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt and similar wall or floor coverings that consist of a fibrous web with a macromolecular coating. It addresses the determination of resistance to indentation, penetration or deformation as a material property.
[D06N 2209/165] Odour absorbing, deodorizing ability -- D06N 2209/165 relates to fibrous web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that have a macromolecular coating and are treated to give the material odour-absorbing or deodorising properties.
[D06N 2209/1657] Printability -- The D06N 2209/1657 "Printability" class defines the properties of fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that have been coated with a macromolecular layer, focusing on how readily inks, dyes or other printing media can be applied and retained. It addresses surface texture, absorbency and adhesion characteristics that affect the quality and durability of printed designs on these wall, floor or similar covering materials.
[D06N 2209/1664] Releasability -- The D06N 2209/1664 subclass covers the releasability of wall, floor or similar covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular layer, focusing on how readily the coating can be detached for manufacturing, repair or recycling purposes.
[D06N 2209/1671] Resistance to bacteria, mildew, mould, fungi -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/1671 covers methods and materials that give fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt resistance to bacterial, mildew, mould and fungal growth. It falls under treatment of textiles and wall, floor or like covering materials, emphasizing antimicrobial properties of the coating layer.
[D06N 2209/1678] Resistive to light or to UV -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/1678 covers fibrous-web wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer and are formulated to resist light exposure or ultraviolet radiation. This property is specified for textiles and treated fabrics used as protective or decorative coverings.
[D06N 2209/1685] Wear resistance -- The D06N 2209/1685 subclass pertains to wear-resistance features of wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It covers technologies that enhance the surface's ability to withstand abrasion, scraping or other mechanical wear during use.
[D06N 2209/1692] Weather resistance -- The CPC subclass D06N 2209/1692 defines weather-resistance characteristics of fibrous-web covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer. It falls under treatment of textiles for wall, floor or similar coverings and addresses durability against moisture, temperature and UV exposure.
[D06N 2211/00] Specially adapted uses -- The D06N 2211/00 subclass refers to specially adapted uses of fibrous-web coverings that are coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It includes inventions that employ these materials for particular performance functions-e.g., fire resistance, sound dampening, antimicrobial action or other tailored properties beyond ordinary covering purposes.
[D06N 2211/02] Agriculture -- The D06N 2211/02 subclass covers fibrous webs coated with a macromolecular layer that are specially adapted for agricultural uses, such as protective covers, mulch films, horticultural mats, or greenhouse liners. It includes items like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt employed to cover soil, shield crops or provide a durable, weather-resistant surface in farming applications.
[D06N 2211/04] Belts -- The CPC entry D06N 2211/04 covers belts that form part of wall, floor or similar coverings made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum or artificial leather. It includes belts specially adapted for reinforcing, supporting or conveying these coated textile substrates in flooring, roofing or other covering applications.
[D06N 2211/06] Building materials -- D06N 2211/06 covers building materials formed from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt used as wall, floor or similar coverings. The classification includes textiles that have been specially adapted for these construction applications.
[D06N 2211/063] Wall coverings -- The D06N 2211/063 class covers wall coverings made from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, intended for walls, floors or similar surfaces. It falls under the treatment of textiles and the broader category of wall, floor or like covering materials, and includes specially adapted uses of these coated textile products.
[D06N 2211/066] Floor coverings -- This subclass deals with floor coverings that are formed from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, and are specially adapted for use on walls, floors or similar surfaces.
[D06N 2211/08] Cleaning articles -- The D06N 2211/08 subgroup covers cleaning articles designed for wall, floor or similar covering materials made from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. These items include mops, wipes, pads or other implements formulated to remove dirt or residues without damaging the coated surface.
[D06N 2211/10] Clothing -- The subclass D06N 2211/10 concerns clothing fabricated from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It addresses specially adapted uses of these coated textile materials for garments and similar wear.
[D06N 2211/103] Gloves -- The D06N 2211/103 subclass defines gloves made from a fibrous web such as linoleum or oilcloth that is coated with a macromolecular layer to provide protective or functional properties. These gloves are treated as specially adapted uses of textile-based covering materials.
[D06N 2211/106] Footwear -- The D06N 2211/106 subclass concerns footwear that incorporates a fibrous web base coated with a polymeric or macromolecular layer, similar to materials used for linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It pertains to specially adapted uses of such covered textile structures in shoes and related footwear products.
[D06N 2211/12] Decorative or sun protection articles -- D06N 2211/12 relates to textiles that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and are intended for decorative purposes or to shield against sunlight. It includes wall, floor or similar coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt where appearance or UV protection is required.
[D06N 2211/122] Curtains -- The D06N 2211/122 subclass deals with curtains fabricated from fibrous webs that are coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It falls under textile treatment for wall, floor or similar covering materials and addresses specially adapted uses of these coated textiles as window or room coverings.
[D06N 2211/125] Awnings, sunblinds -- The subclass D06N 2211/125 includes awnings and sunblinds that are formed from a fibrous web, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, that has been coated with a macromolecular (polymer) layer and is intended for use as wall, floor or similar coverings. It is a specialized type of textile treatment where the coated web functions as a protective or decorative covering material.
[D06N 2211/127] Table cloth -- The CPC entry D06N 2211/127 covers table cloths-fabric or artificial-leather sheets formed from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer and intended for use as a wall, floor or similar covering on tables. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and wall-floor-type covering materials.
[D06N 2211/14] Furniture, upholstery -- The CPC code D06N 2211/14 covers furniture and upholstery made from fibrous web materials that are coated with a macromolecular layer, such as oilcloth, artificial leather, or linoleum used as wall, floor or similar coverings. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and specially adapted uses for such coated fibrous fabrics.
[D06N 2211/16] Geotextiles -- Geotextiles are specially adapted wall or floor covering materials made from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as polymer film or resin. They are used in civil-engineering applications to separate, filter, reinforce, protect, or drain soil and other construction substrates. This entry falls under the treatment of textiles for covering materials like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, and roofing felt.
[D06N 2211/18] Medical, e.g. bandage, prostheses or catheter -- D06N 2211/18 covers fibrous web materials coated with a macromolecular layer that are specially adapted for medical purposes such as bandages, prosthetic devices or catheters. The entry falls under textiles treated for use as wall, floor or similar coverings, but focuses on their application in healthcare settings.
[D06N 2211/20] Packaging -- This CPC subclass D06N 2211/20 relates to packaging of fibrous-web coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that have a macromolecular coating. It covers methods, containers or arrangements for protecting, storing or transporting these treated textile sheets.
[D06N 2211/22] Patches, e.g. medical patches, repair patches -- The CPC subclass D06N 2211/22 relates to patches formed from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, designed for specialized uses such as medical or repair patches applied to wall, floor or similar covering materials like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2211/24] Personal care -- This CPC subclass groups personal-care uses of wall, floor or similar covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, when they are specially adapted for hygiene, cosmetic or skin-treatment applications.
[D06N 2211/26] Vehicles, transportation -- This subclass concerns fibrous web materials coated with a macromolecular layer that are specially adapted for use in vehicles and other transportation means, such as interior floorings, wall linings, and protective coverings in cars, trucks, aircraft or similar transport equipment.
[D06N 2211/261] Body finishing, e.g. headliners -- The subclass D06N 2211/261 relates to methods and materials for finishing vehicle interior surfaces, such as headliners, by applying a coated fibrous web like linoleum, artificial leather or similar covering. It covers the treatment of these textiles where a macromolecular layer is bonded to the web to provide decorative, protective or acoustic properties.
[D06N 2211/262] Constructional panels -- The code D06N 2211/262 covers constructional panels such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer for use as wall, floor or similar coverings. These panels are designed for specially adapted applications in building interiors and other surfaces.
[D06N 2211/263] Cars -- D06N 2211/263 concerns the use of coated fibrous webs-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-in automotive applications. It classifies methods and products where these wall-, floor- or like covering materials are specially adapted for interior or exterior parts of cars, fitting within the CPC hierarchy for specially adapted uses of such coverings.
[D06N 2211/265] Trains -- The D06N 2211/265 subclass covers fibrous-web textiles such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer and are specially adapted for use in trains, serving as wall, floor or other interior covering materials in railway vehicles.
[D06N 2211/266] Ships -- D06N 2211/266 concerns the use of textile-based wall, floor or covering materials, i.e., fibrous webs coated with macromolecular layers, specifically adapted for ships, such as deck coverings, hull linings and interior furnishings.
[D06N 2211/267] Aircraft -- D06N 2211/267 - Aircraft. This subclass groups patents concerning aircraft that employ wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, which consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer. It includes designs where the aircraft structure or interior uses these textile-based coverings for protective or decorative purposes.
[D06N 2211/268] Airbags -- This code refers to airbags, flexible safety devices formed from a fibrous web that is coated with a polymer film or similar macromolecular layer. The structure is designed to inflate rapidly on impact, providing a protective cushion in vehicles, aircraft or other equipment. It is treated as a specially adapted use of coated textile-type covering material.
[D06N 2211/28] Artificial leather -- Artificial leather comprises a fibrous web-often textile or non-woven substrate-coated with a macromolecular polymer layer to imitate the appearance and performance of natural leather. It is used for wall, floor and other covering applications such as linoleum, oilcloth, roofing felt and similar materials where a durable, flexible, leather-like surface is required.
[D06N 2211/30] Filters -- The D06N 2211/30 classification refers to filters incorporated in wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt, where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular layer and specially adapted for filtering purposes.
[D06N 2213/00] Others characteristics -- The subclass D06N 2213/00 covers additional or miscellaneous properties of wall, floor or similar covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, when those characteristics are not addressed by more specific categories. It includes any other functional or physical traits of these coated textile-based coverings.
[D06N 2213/02] All layers being of the same kind of material, e.g. all layers being of polyolefins, all layers being of polyesters -- The code D06N 2213/02 covers multilayer wall, floor or similar covering sheets in which every layer is made from the same polymer material, for example all polyolefin layers or all polyester layers, applied to a fibrous web that forms the substrate.
[D06N 2213/03] Fibrous web coated on one side with at least two layers of the same polymer type, e.g. two coatings of polyolefin -- The CPC subclass D06N 2213/03 covers fibrous webs-such as non-woven textiles-where only one face is coated with a minimum of two successive layers of the same polymer, typically a polyolefin. This structure is used in wall, floor or similar covering materials like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2213/04] Perforated layer -- The D06N 2213/04 classification covers perforated layers applied to fibrous webs that have been coated with a macromolecular material, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, intended for use as wall, floor or similar coverings. It includes structures and processes that create holes or openings in the coated web to provide breathability, drainage, or decorative effects.
[D06N 2213/045] the coating layer does not completely close the openings between the fibres -- The code D06N 2213/045 covers textile-based wall, floor or similar coverings in which the applied macromolecular coating is deliberately porous, leaving the spaces between the fibrous substrate partially open. This applies to products such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt where the coating does not completely seal the fibre openings.
[D06N 2213/06] Characteristics of the backing in carpets, rugs, synthetic lawn -- The CPC subclass D06N 2213/06 concerns the properties of the backing layer used in carpets, rugs and synthetic lawns. It addresses aspects such as material composition, thickness, strength, flexibility and adhesion of the fibrous or polymeric backing that supports the pile or surface covering. This falls under the broader category of treated textile coverings for walls, floors or similar applications.
[D06N 2213/061] Non-continuous back coating or pre-coat -- This group pertains to methods of applying a back coating or pre-coat to a fibrous web that is later covered with a continuous macromolecular layer, where the coating is applied in a discontinuous pattern such as spots, stripes or patches rather than a uniform film. It is used for products like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2213/063] Porous back coating or pre-coat -- This classification pertains to treatments of textile-like covering materials where a fibrous backing is provided with a porous coating applied before the main macromolecular layer, often to improve adhesion, ventilation, or to act as a carrier for subsequent layers. It covers porous back coatings or pre-coats applied to materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather, roofing felt.
[D06N 2213/065] Two back coatings one next to the other -- This CPC subclass concerns wall, floor or similar covering sheets made from a fibrous web that carries a macromolecular layer on its back, where the backing comprises two separate coating layers positioned side-by-side. The arrangement is intended to provide combined or complementary functions such as moisture resistance and mechanical strength in products like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 2213/066] having an adhesive on the undersurface to allow removal of the whole carpet, rug or synthetic lawn from the floor, e.g. pressure sensitive adhesive -- This CPC subclass pertains to floor covering products such as carpets, rugs or synthetic lawns that incorporate an adhesive on the back side to enable the entire item to be peeled off the substrate, typically using a pressure-sensitive adhesive. It falls under textile treatment for wall, floor or similar coverings composed of a fibrous web with a macromolecular coating.
[D06N 2213/068] Releasability between at least two of the layers -- The code pertains to techniques or materials that enable two or more layers of a fibrous-web covering-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt coated with a macromolecular film-to be separated or detached from each other, facilitating recycling, repair or re-configuration. It includes release agents, release coatings or layer designs that provide reversible bonding between the layers.
[D06N 3/00] Artificial leather, oilcloth or material obtained by covering fibrous webs with macromolecular material, e.g. resins, rubber or derivatives thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artificial leather.  Any polymer coated fibrous web, e.g. airbags, or wall or floor coverings, characterised by:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floor coverings comprising a fibrous substrate coated on the top surface with at least one layer of polymer characterised only by their relief structure, colour effects or physical or chemical aspects of the layers -> D06N7/0007 , D06N7/0028 , D06N7/0039 - Floor coverings comprising a fibrous top layer being coated at the back with at least one polymer layer, e.g. carpets, artificial turf, mats, rugs -> D06N7/0063 - Carpet underlays -> D06N7/0089
[D06N 3/0002] characterised by the substrate -- The D06N 3/0002 subclass covers artificial leather, oilcloth and similar wall-, floor- or covering materials formed by coating a fibrous web substrate with a macromolecular layer such as resin or rubber, where the substrate itself is the defining feature of the product. It groups items whose primary characteristic is the type of fibrous base that receives the coating.
[D06N 3/0004] using ultra-fine two-component fibres, e.g. island/sea, or ultra-fine one component fibres (< 1 denier) -- The subclass D06N 3/0004 covers processes that form wall, floor or similar coverings such as artificial leather or oilcloth by applying ultra-fine fibres-either two-component island-/-sea fibres or single-component fibres finer than one denier-to a fibrous web and then coating it with a macromolecular material such as resin or rubber. This method improves texture and performance of the resulting coated textile.
[D06N 3/0006] using woven fabrics -- This sub-group concerns processes in which woven fabrics are used as the base web that is coated, impregnated or otherwise treated with macromolecular materials such as resins, rubber or derivatives to produce artificial leather, oilcloth, roofing felt and similar covered textile products. The methods include applying the coating, drying, curing or bonding steps specifically adapted to the woven substrate.
[D06N 3/0009] using knitted fabrics -- The subclass D06N 3/0009 covers methods that employ knitted fabrics as the fibrous substrate or reinforcement when producing wall, floor or similar covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or coated felts. It includes steps where the knitted textile is coated with resins, rubber or related macromolecular layers to create the finished product.
[D06N 3/0011] using non-woven fabrics -- D06N 3/0011 covers processes that employ non-woven fabrics as the base material when producing wall, floor or similar coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt, where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular layer like resin or rubber.
[D06N 3/0013] using multilayer webs -- The CPC subclass D06N 3/0013 covers methods for producing artificial leather, oilcloth and similar wall, floor or roofing coverings by applying multilayer webs, where a fibrous base is coated with one or more layers of macromolecular material such as resins, rubber or their derivatives. It focuses on the construction of composite sheets through successive layering to achieve desired properties.
[D06N 3/0015] using fibres of specified chemical or physical nature, e.g. natural silk -- This subgroup covers methods that employ fibers of a defined chemical or physical character-such as natural silk-to form or treat fibrous webs that are subsequently coated with a macromolecular layer for use as artificial leather, oilcloth, roofing felt or similar wall, floor or covering materials.
[D06N 3/0018] Collagen fibres or collagen on fibres -- The subclass D06N 3/0018 concerns the incorporation of collagen fibers themselves or the application of a collagen coating onto fibers that form a fibrous web, typically used in artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or similar wall-, floor- or roof-covering materials where the collagen acts as a binder, reinforcement or functional surface layer.
[D06N 3/002] Asbestos fibres -- D06N 3/002 designates the use of asbestos fibres in the production of wall, floor or similar covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or roofing felt. The subclass addresses fibrous webs that are reinforced or coated with a macromolecular layer where asbestos provides strength, fire resistance or other functional properties. It falls under treatment of textiles and covering material technologies.
[D06N 3/0022] Glass fibres -- Glass fibres are thin, continuous filaments produced by drawing molten glass into threads, which are incorporated into fibrous webs to reinforce materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt. In the Cooperative Patent Classification they are classified under D06N 3/0022, covering glass-fibres used as a reinforcing layer in macromolecularly coated textiles and similar wall, floor or covering products.
[D06N 3/0025] Rubber threads; Elastomeric fibres; Stretchable, bulked or crimped fibres; Retractable, crimpable fibres; Shrinking or stretching of fibres during manufacture; Obliquely threaded fabrics -- The D06N 3/0025 subclass covers technologies for producing and processing elastic filaments such as rubber threads, elastomeric fibres and crimped or bulked yarns, including methods that cause fibres to shrink or stretch during manufacture and the creation of obliquely threaded fabrics.
[D06N 3/0027] Rubber or elastomeric fibres -- This subclass covers fibrous materials made from rubber or other elastomers that are used as reinforcement or decorative components in flexible coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall, floor or roofing felts. The fibres consist of polymeric elastomeric compounds, often combined with a fibrous web, and are intended to impart elasticity, durability and resistance to wear.
[D06N 3/0029] Stretchable fibres; Stretching of fibres during manufacture -- The D06N 3/0029 entry concerns the creation and processing of stretchable fibres, specifically methods for stretching fibres during manufacture to impart elasticity. It applies to products such as artificial leather, oilcloth and other fibrous webs coated with macromolecular layers, enabling flexible, durable covering materials.
[D06N 3/0031] Retractable fibres; Shrinking of fibres during manufacture -- The D06N 3/0031 subclass covers processes that cause the fibres in a fibrous web, such as those used for artificial leather or oilcloth, to retract or shrink during manufacturing. This shrinkage can tighten the web, improve dimensional stability or enhance the coating's adhesion to the macromolecular layer.
[D06N 3/0034] Polyamide fibres  for elastomeric fibres  D06N3/0025 -- This subclass concerns the use of polyamide filaments as elastomeric components in coated fibrous substrates such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall or floor coverings, where the fibrous web is overlaid with a macromolecular layer. It includes the selection, formulation or processing of polyamide fibres that impart stretch or recovery to these treated textile materials.
[D06N 3/0036] Polyester fibres  for elastomeric fibres  D06N3/0025
[D06N 3/0038] Polyolefin fibres  for elastomeric fibres  D06N3/0025
[D06N 3/004] using flocked webs or pile fabrics upon which a resin is applied; Teasing, raising web before resin application -- D06N 3/004 covers processes in which a fibrous web that has been flocked or formed into a pile is first teased or raised to increase its surface texture before a resin or similar macromolecular coating is applied, producing artificial leather, oilcloth or related covered textiles.
[D06N 3/0043] characterised by their foraminous structure; Characteristics of the foamed layer or of cellular layers  foraminous structure obtained by stretching  D06N3/0029 -- D06N 3/0043 covers artificial leather, oilcloth and similar wall or floor coverings in which a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular material and features a foraminous, foamed or cellular structure, often produced by stretching the coating. The entry deals with the characteristics of that porous layer.
[D06N 3/0045] obtained by applying a ready-made foam layer; obtained by compressing, crinkling or crushing a foam layer, e.g. Kaschierverfahren für Schaumschicht -- This subclass covers processes where a pre-formed foam sheet is bonded to a fibrous web or the foam is mechanically deformed (compressed, crinkled, crushed) to create a coated covering material such as artificial leather or oilcloth. It includes lamination or embossing methods that apply or shape the foam layer on the substrate.
[D06N 3/0047] obtained by incorporating air, i.e. froth -- The code covers processes for producing fibrous-web coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth or similar wall and floor materials in which the macromolecular coating is formed by introducing air, creating a froth or cellular structure in the resin, rubber or other polymer layer. This technique yields a lightweight, porous coating that can improve flexibility, insulation or bulk.
[D06N 3/005] obtained by blowing or swelling agent -- D06N 3/005 covers methods for producing fibrous-web-based coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth or similar wall- and floor-covering materials in which a macromolecular coating is formed or expanded by the use of a blowing or swelling agent. The technique creates a foamed or swollen layer that bonds to the web, giving the finished product increased thickness, flexibility and resistance.
[D06N 3/0052] obtained by leaching out of a compound, e.g. water soluble salts, fibres or fillers; obtained by freezing or sublimation; obtained by eliminating drops of sublimable fluid -- The subclass D06N 3/0052 concerns processes for producing coated fibrous web materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or roofing felt by removing unwanted components, for example leaching water-soluble salts, fibers or fillers, or by physical means such as freezing, sublimation or the elimination of sublimable liquids. These methods are applied to textiles that consist of a fibrous backing overlaid with a macromolecular coating.
[D06N 3/0054] obtained by mechanical perforations -- The subclass D06N 3/0054 covers fabrics such as artificial leather, oilcloth or other coated fibrous webs in which holes are created by mechanical means such as punching, drilling or cutting. These perforations are produced to provide ventilation, texture or decorative effects while retaining the protective coating of the material.
[D06N 3/0056] characterised by the compounding ingredients of the macro-molecular coating  D06N3/005  takes precedence
[D06N 3/0059] Organic ingredients with special effects, e.g. oil- or water-repellent, antimicrobial, flame-resistant, magnetic, bactericidal, odour-influencing agents; perfumes  D06N3/0065  takes precedence
[D06N 3/0061] Organic fillers or organic fibrous fillers, e.g. ground leather waste, wood bark, cork powder, vegetable flour; Other organic compounding ingredients; Post-treatment with organic compounds -- This subclass covers the use of organic fillers such as ground leather waste, wood bark, cork powder or vegetable flour, and other organic compounding ingredients in the manufacture of coated fibrous textiles like artificial leather or oilcloth, including any post-treatment with organic compounds.
[D06N 3/0063] Inorganic compounding ingredients, e.g. metals, carbon fibres, Na 2 CO 3 , metal layers; Post-treatment with inorganic compounds -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of inorganic ingredients-metals, carbon fibres, metal layers, sodium carbonate and similar compounds-in the compounding of artificial leather, oilcloth or other fibrous webs coated with macromolecular material, and includes post-treatment operations that apply such inorganic compounds to the finished material.
[D06N 3/0065] Organic pigments, e.g. dyes, brighteners -- The subclass D06N 3/0065 refers to the use of organic pigments such as dyes and brighteners for colouring or enhancing fibrous-web coverings that are coated with a macromolecular layer, for example artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or similar wall, floor or roofing materials. It includes pigments that are incorporated into the resin, rubber or other polymeric coatings to impart colour or optical effects.
[D06N 3/0068] Polymeric granules, particles or powder, e.g. core-shell particles, microcapsules -- The CPC subclass D06N 3/0068 covers polymeric granules, particles or powders-including core-shell particles and microcapsules-used to form the macromolecular coating on fibrous webs that become artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum, roofing felt and similar wall or floor coverings.
[D06N 3/007] characterised by mechanical or physical treatments  D06N3/0029 ,  D06N3/0031  take precedence -- The subclass D06N 3/007 covers mechanical or physical treatments applied to wall, floor or similar covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or fibrous webs coated with a macromolecular layer. It includes operations like stretching, compressing, calendaring, embossing, texturing or other finishing processes that alter the physical characteristics of the coated web.
[D06N 3/0072] Slicing; Manufacturing two webs at one time -- D06N 3/0072 relates to processes for slicing and simultaneously forming two webs of coated fibrous material, such as artificial leather, oilcloth or other macromolecule-covered textiles. The entry covers methods that produce a pair of sheets in one operation, typically by cutting a multilayer web or by co-extruding and separating two layers.
[D06N 3/0075] Napping, teasing, raising or abrading of the resin coating  raising, napping of the web before coating  D06N3/004 -- The class covers mechanical surface-treating steps applied to a fibrous web before it receives a resin coating, including napping, teasing, raising or abrading to create a textured or roughened substrate that enhances adhesion of the subsequent macromolecular layer.
[D06N 3/0077] Embossing; Pressing of the surface; Tumbling and crumbling; Cracking; Cooling; Heating, e.g. mirror finish -- The D06N 3/0077 subclass covers surface-finishing operations applied to fibrous webs that have been coated with a macromolecular layer, such as artificial leather, oilcloth, or similar wall- and floor-covering materials. It includes techniques like embossing, pressing, tumbling, crushing, cracking, as well as controlled cooling or heating to achieve finishes ranging from textured patterns to mirror-like surfaces.
[D06N 3/0079] Suction, vacuum treatment -- Suction or vacuum treatment refers to using reduced pressure to draw liquids, gases or vapours into, through, or out of fibrous webs that are later coated with macromolecular layers such as resins, rubber or synthetic leather. The technique helps to remove trapped air, improve impregnation of coating materials and achieve more uniform adhesion on textiles used for artificial leather, oilcloth, roofing felt and similar covering products.
[D06N 3/0081] by wave energy or particle radiation  D06N3/08  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass D06N 3/0081 covers the manufacturing of artificial leather, oilcloth or similar fibrous-web materials that are coated with a macromolecular layer such as resins or rubber, where the coating or the substrate is subjected to wave energy (e.g., ultrasound, microwave) or particle radiation (e.g., electron beam) to effect bonding, curing or other treatment. This technique is used to create durable, flexible covering materials for floors, walls, roofing and similar applications.
[D06N 3/0084] by electrical processes, e.g. potentials, corona discharge, electrophoresis, electrolytic -- The D06N 3/0084 subclass covers the manufacture of artificial leather, oilcloth and similar coated fibrous web materials using electrical techniques such as applied potentials, corona discharge, electrophoresis or electrolytic processes to deposit or cure the macromolecular coating. This includes methods that rely on electric fields or currents to form, bond or modify the polymer layer on the substrate.
[D06N 3/0086] characterised by the application technique -- This subgroup covers artificial leather, oilcloth and similar fibrous-web materials where the defining feature is the technique used to apply the macromolecular coating, such as spraying, dip coating, roll coating or other application methods. Patents classified here are distinguished by the specific coating process rather than the composition of the coating itself.
[D06N 3/0088] by directly applying the resin  D06N3/0045  takes precedence -- D06N 3/0088 covers processes in which a resin, rubber or similar macromolecular material is applied directly onto a fibrous web to form a coated sheet such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt. The classification is used for methods that create a continuous, macromolecular layer on the textile substrate, and it supersedes the broader D06N 3/0045 entry on coating textiles.
