[D06N 3/009] by spraying components on the web  powder  D06N3/0093 -- The D06N 3/009 subclass covers processes for producing artificial leather, oilcloth, or similar fibrous-web coverings in which the coating components are applied to the web in powder form by spraying. This method involves depositing a particulate layer of resins, rubber, or related macromolecular materials onto the fibrous substrate before curing or bonding. It is a specific technique within the broader treatment of textiles for wall, floor or other covering materials.
[D06N 3/0093] by applying resin powders; by sintering -- A process for producing artificial leather, oilcloth or similar coated fibrous sheets in which a fine resin powder is distributed over the web and then fused by sintering, creating a continuous macromolecular layer that binds the fibers and yields a flexible, durable covering material.
[D06N 3/0095] by inversion technique; by transfer processes -- D06N 3/0095 concerns methods for forming coated covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or linoleum by applying the macromolecular layer through inversion or transfer techniques. These processes involve moving a polymer film from a carrier or reversing the web so the coating adheres to the fibrous substrate, often by calendering, lamination or printing.
[D06N 3/0097] Release surface, e.g. separation sheets; Silicone papers -- D06N 3/0097 concerns release surfaces such as separation sheets and silicone-coated papers that are applied to fibrous webs, including artificial leather, oilcloth and similar materials, to provide a non-sticking layer for processing, handling or finishing operations.
[D06N 3/02] with cellulose derivatives -- D06N 3/02 concerns artificial leather, oilcloth and similar covering materials made by applying a macromolecular layer that contains cellulose derivatives such as cellulose acetate or nitrates to a fibrous web. The subclass covers the preparation of these wall, floor or other coverings where the polymeric coating is based on modified cellulose.
[D06N 3/04] with macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- This subgroup concerns artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall- or floor-covering materials that consist of a fibrous web coated with a polymer formed solely by reactions involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds, such as addition polymerisation of olefins. It covers the preparation and treatment of these coated textiles where the macromolecular layer is generated through such C-C unsaturation reactions.
[D06N 3/042] Acrylic polymers  D06N3/045  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coating compositions comprising polymers made exclusively from acrylic monomers.
[D06N 3/045] with polyolefin or polystyrene (co-)polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coating compositions comprising homo- or copolymers of olefins or styrenic monomers, including olefin / acrylic copolymers.
[D06N 3/047] with fluoropolymers -- D06N 3/047 covers artificial leather, oilcloth or similar fibrous webs that are coated with macromolecular substances where the coating comprises fluoropolymers. The classification addresses processes and products in which a fibrous base is impregnated or surface-treated with fluoropolymer-based resins to impart properties such as chemical resistance, low friction or water repellency. It falls within the broader group of wall, floor or like covering materials.
[D06N 3/06] with polyvinylchloride or its copolymerisation products -- The CPC subclass D06N 3/06 covers fibrous web coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt that are coated with a macromolecular layer, specifically when the coating comprises polyvinylchloride or its copolymerisation products. It relates to treatment of textiles used as wall, floor or similar coverings.
[D06N 3/065] PVC together with other resins except polyurethanes  with polyurethanes  D06N3/144
[D06N 3/08] with a finishing layer consisting of polyacrylates, polyamides or polyurethanes -- Materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt that are built from a fibrous web covered with a macromolecular coating, where the outer finishing layer is specifically composed of polyacrylates, polyamides or polyurethanes.
[D06N 3/10] with styrene-butadiene copolymerisation products -- This subclass deals with the manufacture or treatment of wall, floor or similar covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth or roofing felt, where a fibrous web is coated with a layer that includes styrene-butadiene copolymerisation products.
[D06N 3/103] Thermosetting synthetic rubbers -- The D06N 3/103 subclass covers thermosetting synthetic rubbers applied as coating layers on fibrous webs to produce artificial leathers, oilcloths and similar covering materials. These rubbers cure by heat to form a cross-linked network that provides durability, chemical resistance and stability to the finished textile-based product.
[D06N 3/106] Elastomers -- D06N 3/106 Elastomers covers elastomeric substances such as rubber, synthetic resins or other polymeric materials that are applied as a continuous coating layer on fibrous webs to produce flexible, durable coverings like artificial leather, oilcloth or similar wall, floor or roof materials. It classifies the use of these macromolecular coatings in textile-type composites.
[D06N 3/12] with macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- D06N 3/12 covers artificial leather, oilcloth and similar materials made from a fibrous web that is coated with macromolecular compounds such as resins or rubbers, where the coating polymers are produced by processes other than simple carbon-to-carbon unsaturated-bond reactions. This sub-class falls under treatment of textiles for wall, floor or covering applications.
[D06N 3/121] with polyesters, polycarbonates, alkyds  oils  D06N3/16 -- The entry D06N 3/121 covers processes for treating fibrous webs that are coated to form wall, floor or similar covering materials such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt, where the coating layer is formed from polyesters, polycarbonates, alkyds or oils. It specifies the use of these macromolecular resins to create the protective or decorative surface.
[D06N 3/123] with polyesters -- The subclass D06N 3/123 concerns artificial leathers, oilcloths and similar wall or floor covering sheets in which the fibrous web is coated with a polyester-based macromolecular layer. It includes methods and compositions that apply thermoplastic or thermosetting polyester resins, polyester rubbers or their derivatives to create the protective coating. This polyester coating provides durability, flexibility and resistance suitable for covering materials such as linoleum, roofing felt or synthetic leather.
[D06N 3/125] with polyamides -- This subclass concerns the production of wall, floor or similar coverings such as artificial leather or oilcloth in which a fibrous web is impregnated or coated with a polyamide-based macromolecular layer, typically a resin or rubber derivative, to form a durable, flexible covering material.
[D06N 3/126] Poly-amino acids, e.g. polyglutamates -- The D06N 3/126 subclass pertains to poly-amino acids, for example polyglutamates, employed as binders or coating agents on fibrous webs to produce artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall, floor or roofing coverings. These macromolecular materials provide adhesion, flexibility and durability to the treated textile substrate.
[D06N 3/128] with silicon polymers -- D06N 3/128 covers artificial leather, oilcloth and similar fibrous-web coverings where the coating layer contains silicon polymers such as silicone resins or elastomers. It falls under treatment of textiles for wall, floor or other coverings, describing the use of silicon-based macromolecular materials to impart specific properties.
[D06N 3/14] with polyurethanes -- The D06N 3/14 subclass pertains to fibrous-web coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum or roofing felt that are coated with a polyurethane-based layer. It includes processes and products where polyurethanes serve as the macromolecular coating material, providing flexibility, durability and resistance.
[D06N 3/141] mixture of two or more polyurethanes in the same layer -- This subclass deals with the preparation of a coating layer in which two or more polyurethane polymers are blended together and applied onto a fibrous substrate to form artificial leather, oilcloth or similar composite coverings. It includes methods of mixing, curing and applying the polymer mixture in a single continuous layer.
[D06N 3/142] mixture of polyurethanes with other resins in the same layer -- The D06N 3/142 classification concerns single-layer coatings in which a polyurethane resin is blended with other resin types to form a composite coating on fibrous webs such as artificial leather, oilcloth or similar wall, floor or roofing materials. This mixture is applied as a single macromolecular film that gives the material its protective and decorative properties.
[D06N 3/143] with polyurethanes and other polycondensation or polyaddition products, e.g. aminoplast -- The D06N 3/143 subclass concerns artificial leather, oilcloth and similar wall or floor covering materials whose fibrous web is coated with a polymer layer formed from polyurethanes or other polycondensation- or polyaddition-derived resins such as aminoplasts, providing a durable, flexible surface for products like linoleum, roofing felt and rubber-based laminates.
[D06N 3/144] with polyurethane and polymerisation products, e.g. acrylics, PVC -- D06N 3/144 relates to artificial leather, oilcloth and comparable wall, floor or covering materials formed by coating a fibrous web with a macromolecular layer, specifically using polyurethane or polymerisation products such as acrylics or PVC.
[D06N 3/145] two or more layers of polyurethanes -- The CPC subclass D06N 3/145 covers processed textile-based coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth or similar fibrous webs that have been coated with a macromolecular layer, when the coating consists of at least two distinct polyurethane layers. It pertains to the method and product features of multilayer polyurethane laminates applied to fibrous substrates for use as wall, floor or other covering materials.
[D06N 3/146] characterised by the macromolecular diols used -- The subclass D06N 3/146 covers fibrous web coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall or floor materials in which the coating polymer is formed essentially from macromolecular diols, for example polyester, polyether or polyurethane resins derived from diol monomers. It defines the use of these diol-based macromolecules to impart the protective or decorative layer on the fibrous substrate.
[D06N 3/147] characterised by the isocyanates used -- This group covers processes for making coated fibrous webs such as artificial leather, oilcloth or similar materials, where the coating layer is formed with isocyanate-based polymers like polyurethane. The isocyanates are the key reactive component that creates the macromolecular film on the textile substrate.
[D06N 3/148] (cyclo)aliphatic polyisocyanates -- D06N 3/148 classifies the use of cycloaliphatic or aliphatic polyisocyanates in the manufacture or surface treatment of fibrous-web coverings such as artificial leather, oilcloth, linoleum and similar wall or floor materials. The polyisocyanates serve as cross-linking agents to cure resin, rubber or polymer layers applied to the textile substrate, imparting durability and chemical resistance.
[D06N 3/16] with oil varnishes -- D06N 3/16 covers processes for producing artificial leather, oilcloth or similar covered textiles by applying oil-based varnishes as the macromolecular coating. It pertains to treating fibrous webs with oil resin layers to create waterproof or leather-like surfaces.
[D06N 3/18] with two layers of different macromolecular materials ; -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibrous webs coated with two layers of different macromolecular materials present on the same or on different surfaces or the fibrous web.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - W ith a finishing layer consisting of polyacrylates polyamides or polyurethanes -> D06N3/08
[D06N 3/183] the layers are one next to the other -- This subgroup deals with coverings such as artificial leather or oilcloth where the fibrous web and the macromolecular coating lie adjacent to each other rather than being stacked or interleaved. It concerns wall, floor or similar materials formed from a single fibrous substrate topped with a continuous layer of resin, rubber or similar polymer.
[D06N 3/186] one of the layers is on one surface of the fibrous web and the other layer is on the other surface of the fibrous web -- D06N 3/186 covers laminates in which a fibrous web is coated on each side with different macromolecular layers, such as a resin-based coating on one face and a rubber or polymer coating on the opposite face, resulting in artificial leather, oilcloth, or similar composite covering materials.
[D06N 5/00] Roofing materials comprising a fibrous web coated with bitumen or another polymer, e.g. pitch  compositions of bituminous materials  C08L95/00 , roof covering  E04D5/00 , roofing underlays  E04D12/002 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roofing materials or products that are useful as roofing material (specified in the claims or description) comprising a fibrous web coated with at least one layer of a macromolecular material, i.e. thermoplastic, elastomer, bitumen or asphalt.  In D06N5/006 the fastening means should be part of the structure of the material, such as a strip of a self-adhesive at the edge of the roofing membrane or a meltable strip or layer allowing welding of the roofing membrane to a support or to another membrane.  This group does not cover:
[D06N 5/003] coated with bitumen -- D06N 5/003 covers roofing and similar covering materials made from a fibrous web such as linen, oilcloth or artificial leather that is coated with bitumen or another polymer, forming a waterproof layer for roofs, floorings or underlays.
[D06N 5/006] characterised by the means to apply it to a support or to another roofing membrane, e.g. self-adhesive layer or strip -- D06N 5/006 covers roofing products formed from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular material such as bitumen or polymer that are provided with a means for attaching the sheet to a substrate or another roofing membrane, for example by incorporating a self-adhesive layer or a pressure-sensitive strip. This subclass falls under treatments of textile-based wall, floor or covering materials used as roofing membranes.
[D06N 7/00] Flexible sheet materials not otherwise provided for, e.g. textile threads, filaments, yarns or tow, glued on macromolecular material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coated fibrous webs:  Wallpaper comprising a textile layer.  Floor coverings comprising a fibrous substrate coated with at least one layer of a polymer on the top surface characterised by the relief structure, colour effects or the physical or chemical aspects of the layers.  Floor coverings comprising a fibrous top layer coated at the back with at least one polymer layer, e.g. carpets, rugs, synthetic turf.  Webs coated with fibres, e.g. flocked materials.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floor coverings comprising a fibrous substrate coated on the top surface with at least one layer of polymer characterised only by the fibrous substrate, compounding ingredients of the macromolecular coating, polymer material, a foamed, cellular or perforated coating, mechanical or physical treatments or application technique. -> D06N3/00
[D06N 7/0002] Wallpaper or wall covering on textile basis -- D06N 7/0002 covers wallpaper or wall coverings whose base is a textile material, such as a fibrous web, felt, or woven fabric that is subsequently coated or laminated with a macromolecular layer (e.g., polymer film, resin). The classification includes flexible sheet products not otherwise listed, where the textile substrate provides strength or texture and the coating supplies surface finish, durability, or decorative properties.
[D06N 7/0005] Floor covering on textile basis comprising a fibrous substrate being coated with at least one layer of a polymer on the top surface -- This classification covers flexible floor coverings whose primary structure is a textile fibrous web that is coated on its upper side with one or more polymer layers to form a durable surface. Examples include polymer-coated linoleum, oilcloth or synthetic-leather mats.
[D06N 7/0007] characterised by their relief structure -- The CPC subclass D06N 7/0007 covers flexible sheet materials such as fibrous webs that have been coated with a macromolecular layer and are distinguished by a relief structure on the surface. The relief may be a pattern of raised or recessed features formed in the coating, providing texture, enhanced grip, decorative effect or functional properties. This subclass is used for inventions in coated textiles, linoleum, artificial leather and similar floor, wall or roof coverings characterised by such surface relief.
[D06N 7/001] obtained by mechanical embossing -- The D06N 7/001 subclass covers flexible sheet materials such as textile threads, yarns or tow that are bonded to a macromolecular coating and then given a pattern by mechanical embossing. It pertains to wall, floor or similar covering materials like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt, where the surface relief is created mechanically rather than chemically.
[D06N 7/0013] obtained by chemical embossing (chemisches Prägen) -- The entry D06N 7/0013 covers methods that give a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer a textured surface by chemical embossing, where reagents cause localized swelling, hardening or removal to form a pattern. This technique is used for flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum, artificial leather and similar wall- or floor-cover materials.
[D06N 7/0015] use of inhibitor for the blowing agent or inhibitor for the kicker, e.g. trimellitic anhydride, triazole -- D06N 7/0015 covers the use of chemical inhibitors, such as trimellitic anhydride or triazole, to control the blowing agent or kicker during the manufacture of flexible sheet materials formed from a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, for applications like linoleum or artificial leather.
[D06N 7/0018] use of kicker for the blowing agent, e.g. Beschleunigungsverfahren -- D06N 7/0018 covers methods that employ a kicker to introduce or accelerate a blowing agent during the production of flexible sheet materials comprising a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 7/0021] use of a swelling agent -- The subclass D06N 7/0021 covers processes that apply a swelling agent to a fibrous web before it is coated with a macromolecular layer, such as linoleum, artificial leather or roofing felt. The agent expands the fibres or substrate to improve penetration and adhesion of the coating, facilitating the manufacture of flexible wall, floor or similar covering materials.
[D06N 7/0023] obtained by physical means, e.g. differential heating or differential irradiation; masking certain areas during treating -- D06N 7/0023 covers physical methods for treating flexible sheet covering materials-such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt-by applying differential heating or irradiation, often with masked areas to create patterned or selective modifications in the macromolecular coating. This includes techniques that locally vary temperature or light exposure to achieve desired surface properties.
[D06N 7/0026] obtained by moulding, e.g. moulding table (tapis moule) -- D06N 7/0026 designates flexible sheet products formed by molding a fibrous web that has been coated with a macromolecular layer, such as laminated linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. The classification covers processes where the coated web is shaped on a moulding table (tapis moule) to produce wall, floor or similar covering materials. It applies to items not otherwise specified in higher-level subclasses.
[D06N 7/0028] characterised by colour effects, e.g. craquelé, reducing gloss  terrazzo by sintering  D06N7/0057 -- This subgroup covers flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather that are formed from a fibrous web coated with a polymer layer and are specially treated to produce colour effects such as craquelé or a reduced gloss, often by sintering processes used for terrazzo.
[D06N 7/0031] mixture of two or more dyes, pigments, brighteners in the same layer -- The D06N 7/0031 entry covers compositions in which two or more dyes, pigments or brighteners are blended together in a single coating layer applied to flexible sheet or web substrates such as fibrous mats coated with a polymer film, typical of materials like linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. This classification is used for processes that create a uniform, multi-color or optically enhanced surface in one coating step.
[D06N 7/0034] two or more different colour layers -- This subclass covers coated flexible sheet materials in which the macromolecular coating is applied in two or more separate colour layers, such as multi-coloured linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. It includes methods for forming, joining or treating such multilayered colour structures on fibrous webs.
[D06N 7/0036] characterised by their backing, e.g. secondary backing, back-sizing -- This subclass covers flexible sheet materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and are defined by the presence of a backing, for example a secondary backing or back-sizing, which provides added strength, stability or surface properties.
[D06N 7/0039] characterised by the physical or chemical aspects of the layers -- This subgroup covers flexible sheet materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt that consist of a fibrous base coated with a macromolecular layer, and where the patent claims focus on the physical or chemical properties of the coating layers.
[D06N 7/0042] Conductive or insulating layers; Antistatic layers; Flame-proof layers -- The subclass D06N 7/0042 covers the preparation of flexible sheet or coated-textile materials that incorporate conductive, insulating, antistatic or flame-proof layers. It includes methods for applying electrical-conductivity or dielectric films, antistatic treatments and fire-retardant coatings to fibrous webs such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt.
[D06N 7/0044] Sealing or barrier layers, e.g. against solvents, asphalt, plasticisers -- The CPC subclass D06N 7/0044 covers sealing or barrier layers that are applied to flexible sheet materials - composed of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular film, such as linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather. The layers protect the material from penetration by solvents, asphalt, plasticisers and similar agents.
[D06N 7/0047] Special extra layers under the surface coating, e.g. wire threads -- The CPC subclass D06N 7/0047 designates flexible sheet or covering materials that incorporate an additional reinforcement layer beneath the surface coating, such as embedded wire threads, to improve strength or dimensional stability. It applies to products like linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular material and further reinforced.
[D06N 7/0049] Fibrous layer(s); Fibre reinforcement; Fibrous fillers -- The subclass D06N 7/0049 covers the use of fibrous layers, fibre reinforcement or fibrous fillers applied to a fibrous web that is subsequently coated with a macromolecular material, forming flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. It addresses the incorporation of textile threads, yarns, filaments or tow into the coating to enhance strength, durability or other functional properties.
[D06N 7/0052] Compounding ingredients, e.g. rigid elements  compounding ingredients of the macromolecular coating  D06N3/0056
[D06N 7/0055] Particulate material such as cork, rubber particles, reclaimed resin particles, magnetic particles, metal particles, glass beads
[D06N 7/0057] Layers obtained by sintering or glueing the granules together -- The CPC subclass D06N 7/0057 covers processes for producing flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum or artificial leather by forming a continuous layer from granules that are bonded together through sintering or adhesive gluing. It pertains to treatments where a fibrous web is coated with a macromolecular material and the resulting granules are consolidated into a uniform layer.
[D06N 7/006] characterised by the textile substrate as base web  for intermediate fibrous webs  D06N7/0049 -- This CPC subgroup covers flexible sheet products whose primary layer is a textile substrate used as a base web for one or more additional fibrous layers, typically coated or bonded with a macromolecular material. It includes composite coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that are not otherwise classified, and pertains to the structure and manufacture of these textile-based sheets.
[D06N 7/0063] Floor covering on textile basis comprising a fibrous top layer being coated at the back with at least one polymer layer, e.g. carpets, rugs, synthetic turf -- The subclass D06N 7/0063 covers floor-covering products that use a textile-based top layer bonded to at least one polymer layer on the back, such as carpets, rugs or synthetic turf, where the fibrous surface provides the visible surface and the polymer coating forms the backing.
[D06N 7/0065] characterised by the pile -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Carpets, rugs, mats or artificial lawn characterised by the pile yarns, e.g. differently dyed, of different length, of at least two different materials.  Carpets, rugs, mats or artificial lawn, wherein the pile yarns are fused, soften or melted at the back of the primary backing should be classified in this subgroup, independently if a back coating is also present or not.
[D06N 7/0068] characterised by the primary backing or the fibrous top layer -- D06N 7/0068 covers flexible sheet covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web serving as the primary backing or top layer, onto which a macromolecular coating is applied. The classification targets products where the fibrous component is integral to the sheet's structure rather than merely an additive.
[D06N 7/0071] characterised by their backing, e.g. pre-coat, back coating, secondary backing, cushion backing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Carpets, rugs, mats or artificial lawn characterised by their backing, e.g. pre-coat, back coating, stabilizing layers, secondary backing, cushion backing.
[D06N 7/0073] the back coating or pre-coat being applied as an aqueous dispersion or latex -- The code D06N 7/0073 covers the application of a back coating or pre-coat to flexible sheet materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt, where the coating is applied as an aqueous dispersion or latex. This treatment prepares the fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer for improved adhesion, durability or surface properties.
[D06N 7/0076] the back coating or pre-coat being a thermoplastic material applied by, e.g. extrusion coating, powder coating or laminating a thermoplastic film -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Carpets, rugs, mats or artificial lawn, wherein the pre-coat or the back coating is made from a hot melt composition comprising at least a thermoplastic polymer and a tackifying resin. -> D06N7/0078
[D06N 7/0078] the back coating or pre-coat being applied as a hot melt -- **Glossary:** - Hot melt adhesive, coating or composition: Adhesive, coating or composition comprising a thermoplastic polymer and a tackifying resin, e.g. rosin esters, hydrocarbon resins, polyterpene resin. Other additives, such as fillers or a wax can also be present.
[D06N 7/0081] with at least one extra fibrous layer at the backing, e.g. stabilizing fibrous layer, fibrous secondary backing -- The code D06N 7/0081 refers to flexible sheet or covering materials that incorporate a backing layer reinforced by at least one additional fibrous sheet, such as a stabilising or secondary fibrous layer, to improve strength or dimensional stability.
[D06N 7/0084] with at least one layer obtained by sintering or bonding granules together -- This subclass covers flexible sheet or covering materials made from a fibrous web that is coated with a macromolecular layer in which at least one layer is formed by sintering or bonding granules together, such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt where the coating is produced by granule consolidation.
[D06N 7/0086] characterised by the cushion backing, e.g. foamed polyurethane -- D06N 7/0086 covers flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt that consist of a fibrous web coated with a macromolecular layer and feature a cushioning backing, typically a foamed polyurethane layer, to provide added softness or impact resistance.
[D06N 7/0089] Underlays -- The D06N 7/0089 subclass covers underlay materials placed beneath flexible sheet coverings such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather or roofing felt. These underlays consist of a fibrous web, often textile yarns or tow, bonded to a macromolecular coating and provide support, moisture resistance or insulation for the finished covering.
[D06N 7/0092] Non-continuous polymer coating on the fibrous substrate, e.g. plastic dots on fabrics -- The subclass deals with fibrous textiles that are given a discontinuous polymer coating, for example scattered plastic particles or dots applied to the surface instead of a continuous film. It includes methods for attaching these non-continuous polymer elements to fabrics, threads, yarns or similar substrates.
[D06N 7/0094] Fibrous material being coated on one surface with at least one layer of an inorganic material and at least one layer of a macromolecular material -- The CPC subclass D06N 7/0094 covers fibrous webs-such as textile threads, filaments, yarns or tow-coated on a single side first with at least one inorganic layer and subsequently with at least one macromolecular (polymeric) layer, used for flexible sheet or covering materials like linoleum, oilcloth or artificial leather.
[D06N 7/0097] Web coated with fibres, e.g. flocked -- The classification refers to flexible sheet structures where a fibrous web-textile threads, filaments, yarns or tow-is bonded to a macromolecular layer to produce a flocked surface. It applies to wall, floor or similar covering materials such as linoleum, oilcloth, artificial leather and roofing felt that are not covered by other specific categories.
[D06P] DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Fibre: Fibre is a relatively short, elongated member of natural or artificial material. - Thread: Thread is an assembly of yarns or filaments usually produced by twisting. - Yarn: Yarn is a unitary assembly of fibres or filaments, usually produced by spinning. - Fabric: Fabric is a planar textile structure produced by interlacing yarns, threads, fibres or filaments by a weaving, knitting or non-woven process.
[D06P 1/00] General processes of dyeing or printing textiles, or general processes of dyeing leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances in any form, classified according to the dyes, pigments, or auxiliary substances employed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General processes for dyeing, compositions for dyeing or printing classified according to the chemical structure of the dyes or the compounds used.  Special processes for dyeing, e.g. dyeing with dyes in special form, specific dyeing recipes, specific dyes used like phosphorescent dyes, obtaining special effects like multicolour dyed textile materials, e.g. different colours on the same thread.  All the compounds which might be used in dye baths or printing pastes or inks, i.e. dyes, mineral, organic or macromolecular compounds.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Dyeing of glass fibres -> C03C - Dyeing of paper -> D21H
[D06P 1/0004] General aspects of dyeing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The general aspects of dyeing which are not well specified or broadly defined or dyeing processes which cannot be covered by any other subgroups.  Dyeing processes using enzymes in the dye bath.
[D06P 1/0008] Dyeing processes in which the dye is not specific (waste liquors) -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile where the dye is not specific, or dyeing with waste liquors, i.e. dyeing with dye baths which have already been used, dyeing by immersing the textile several times in the same bath.
[D06P 1/0012] Effecting dyeing to obtain luminescent or phosphorescent dyeings -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/0012 covers processes for dyeing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances where the aim is to produce luminescent or phosphorescent colours. It includes any general dyeing or printing method that employs light-emitting dyes, pigments or auxiliaries to create a glow-in-the-dark or fluorescent effect.
[D06P 1/0016] Dye baths containing a dyeing agent in a special form such as for instance in melted or solid form, as a floating film or gel, spray or aerosol, or atomised dyes -- This CPC subclass D06P 1/0016 covers dye baths in which the dyeing agent is provided in a special physical form such as melted or solid state, a floating film or gel, a spray, an aerosol or atomised particles, and is used in dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, fur or other macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/002] Processing by repeated dyeing, e.g. in different baths -- This subclass covers methods where a textile or similar material is subjected to a series of dyeing steps, typically in separate baths, to achieve desired color effects or properties. It includes repeated dyeing cycles, possibly with different dyes or auxiliaries, used for textiles, leather, furs, or polymeric sheets.
[D06P 1/0024] Dyeing and bleaching in one process -- D06P 1/0024 covers methods that combine dyeing and bleaching in a single operation for textiles, leather, fur or solid polymeric materials. These processes apply dyes or pigments together with bleaching agents, allowing simultaneous coloration and removal of natural pigments or impurities.
[D06P 1/0028] Dyeing with a coacervate system -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/0028 covers textile dyeing processes that employ a coacervate system, where the dye is incorporated into a phase-separated polymer-rich colloidal mixture to facilitate uptake by the material. This method is used for dyeing fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric substrates and is characterized by the specific use of coacervates as the dye-carrying medium.
[D06P 1/0032] Determining dye recipes and dyeing parameters; Colour matching or monitoring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specific processes for dyeing when specific details are provided on the process, e.g. it is disclosed when a specific compound is added to the dye bath, when temperature raises are occurring, when dyeing times are particular, important or well described.
[D06P 1/0036] Dyeing and sizing in one process -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing and sizing in one process but also the dyeing and finishing in one process, i.e. when the finishing agent is applied simultaneously with the dye, e.g. dyeing combined with a softening treatment.
[D06P 1/004] Dyeing with phototropic dyes; Obtaining camouflage effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing with dyes changing of colours according to the circumstances, e.g. color indicators or thermochromic dyes.  Dyed textile materials changing colours according to light orientation.  Textile materials printed with certain patterns to obtain camouflage fabrics, or dyed with specific dyes; e.g. which are reflecting or absorbing infrareds. These textile materials are generally used for military purposes.
[D06P 1/0044] Dyeing with a dye in volatilised form -- A process in which the dye is applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid polymers in a volatilised state, such as vapor, gas or aerosol, to achieve coloration. This falls under the general dyeing or printing of textiles, specifically the class of methods classified by the type of dye or auxiliary used, and is identified by the CPC code D06P 1/0044.
[D06P 1/0048] Converting dyes  in situ  in a non-appropriate form by hydrolysis, saponification, reduction with split-off of a substituent -- This CPC subclass D06P 1/0048 covers processes that convert dyes in-situ when they are initially in an unsuitable form, using hydrolysis, saponification or reduction that splits off a substituent, thereby rendering the dye usable during textile dyeing or printing.
[D06P 1/0052] Dyeing with polymeric dyes -- D06P 1/0052 covers dyeing processes that employ polymeric dyes, which are high-molecular-weight dye compounds applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. The entry falls under the general category of dyeing or printing textiles and specifies methods that use these polymer-based colorants to achieve improved fixation and fastness.
[D06P 1/0056] Dyeing with polymeric dyes involving building the polymeric dyes on the fibres  dyes  per se   C09B -- This subclass covers processes in which polymeric dyes are formed directly on textile fibres, creating the colourant in situ rather than applying a pre-made dye. It includes methods where the polymeric dye is built up on the substrate during dyeing, encompassing both fibre and polymer chemistry aspects. The classification falls under general dyeing or printing of textiles.
[D06P 1/006] by using dyes with polymerisable groups, e.g. dye ---CH=CH2 -- The code D06P 1/006 relates to dyeing or printing processes that employ dyes containing polymerisable groups such as vinyl moieties, allowing the dye to covalently bond to the substrate during treatment. This approach is used for textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials to improve fastness.
[D06P 1/0064] by using reactive polyfunctional compounds, e.g. crosslinkers -- This CPC subclass covers dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials that employ reactive polyfunctional compounds such as crosslinking agents. The method utilizes chemicals that form covalent bonds with the substrate, improving fixation of dyes or pigments. It is part of the D06P hierarchy dealing with the use of specific auxiliary substances in textile coloration.
[D06P 1/0068] Non-heterocyclic compounds -- The D06P 1/0068 subclass groups non-heterocyclic dye, pigment or auxiliary compounds employed in general textile, leather, fur or solid polymer dyeing and printing processes, classified according to the substances used rather than their heterocyclic structures. These compounds are covered in the broader hierarchy of treatment of textiles, dyeing or printing.
[D06P 1/0072] Heterocyclic compounds -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/0072 groups heterocyclic compounds used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in general textile dyeing or printing processes, including applications to leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It covers any heterocyclic chemical structures employed to impart colour or modify the properties of the substrate during treatment.
[D06P 1/0076] Dyeing with mineral dye -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials with mineral dyes like carbon black or ferrous oxides.
[D06P 1/008] Preparing dyes  in situ   D06P1/12 ,  D06P1/145  take precedence -- D06P 1/008 covers methods for preparing dyes in situ during the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, where the dye or pigment is formed on or within the material rather than being added as a pre-made composition. The subclass includes processes that generate colorants directly on the substrate and is subordinated to the more specific sub-entries D06P 1/12 and D06P 1/145.
[D06P 1/0096] Multicolour dyeing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Dyeing processes to obtain multicoloured textiles, i.e. different colours on the same piece, space dyeing, dyeing mixtures of fibres having different dye affinities with dyes of different classes.
[D06P 1/02] using azo dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/02 concerns dyeing or printing processes that specifically employ azo dyes, which are organic compounds featuring a nitrogen-nitrogen double bond as the chromophore. It covers the use of these dyes for colouring textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials, focusing on the chemical nature of the dye rather than the substrate or printing technique.
[D06P 1/04] not containing metal -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/04 covers general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular substances that use dyes, pigments or auxiliaries which do not contain metal. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES - TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE - DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES. This subclass is used by both the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[D06P 1/06] containing acid groups -- D06P 1/06 refers to dyeing or printing processes in which the coloring agents, pigments or auxiliaries used contain acid functional groups such as carboxylic, sulfonic or phosphoric acids. These acid-group substances are employed to improve fixation, solubility or interaction with textile fibres, leathers or other macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/08] cationic azo dyes -- The D06P 1/08 subclass covers dyeing processes that employ cationic azo dyes, which are positively charged azo compounds used as coloration agents for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It groups methods where the dye's cationic nature is the principal auxiliary characteristic.
[D06P 1/10] containing metal -- The D06P 1/10 subclass covers general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ metal-containing dyes, pigments, mordants or auxiliary agents. It includes methods in which metals are part of the coloring compounds or are used to fix the colour, regardless of the specific textile type or printing technique.
[D06P 1/12] prepared -- This subclass covers methods where dyes, pigments or auxiliaries are pre-prepared before application to textiles, leather, fur or polymeric materials. It includes steps such as mixing, grinding, dissolving or other pre-treatment of the coloring agents prior to the dyeing or printing operation.
[D06P 1/122] the textile material containing one component -- The subclass concerns textile goods in which the material itself incorporates a single component, such as a fiber or fabric that already contains a dye, pigment, or auxiliary substance. It is used for classifying general dyeing or printing processes where the textile's colour or functional property derives from that one incorporated element.
[D06P 1/125] one or both of the components having fibre-reactive groups -- The entry covers dyeing or printing processes in which at least one of the reacting components bears fibre-reactive groups that can form covalent bonds with the textile substrate. It includes methods where the dye, pigment, or auxiliary contains such reactive groups, enabling the colourant to become chemically attached to fibres, leathers, furs or macromolecular solids during treatment.
[D06P 1/127] using a stabilised diazo component, e.g. diazoamino, anti-diazotate or nitrosamine R-N=N-OK, diazosulfonate, hydrazinesulfonate, R-N=N-N-CN -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that use stabilized diazo compounds-such as diazoamino, anti-diazotate, nitrosamine derivatives, diazosulfonates or hydrazinesulfonates-as the coloring agents or auxiliaries on textiles, leather, furs or polymeric substrates. The stabilised diazo component enables controlled coloration during treatment.
[D06P 1/13] using azomethine dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes in which azomethine (Schiff-base) dyes are used to colour textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials, including the methods of applying these dyes with suitable auxiliaries.
[D06P 1/14] using phthalocyanine dyes without vatting -- A process for dyeing fabrics that employs phthalocyanine dyes directly, without the use of a vatting step to convert the dye into a soluble form. This method simplifies the treatment, reduces chemicals and water consumption, and is suited to heat-stable or high-temperature dyeing operations.
[D06P 1/145] using phthalocyanine dyes prepared  in situ -- The subclass D06P 1/145 covers textile or leather dyeing and printing methods that employ phthalocyanine dyes formed directly in the dye bath rather than added as pre-manufactured pigments. It pertains to general dyeing or printing processes in which the phthalocyanine colourant is generated in situ from precursor compounds during treatment.
[D06P 1/16] using dispersed, e.g. acetate, dyestuffs -- D06P 1/16 covers general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid polymers that employ dispersed dyestuffs, typically acetate-based pigments. The classification is used for methods where the dye particles are finely suspended in a carrier before being applied to the material.
[D06P 1/18] Azo dyes -- This CPC subclass covers the use of azo dyes in dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It includes processes where coloration is achieved with azo compounds, irrespective of the specific auxiliary agents or application method, and is grouped under D06P 1/00 general processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 1/19] Nitro dyes -- D06P 1/19 pertains to nitro dyes, a class of synthetic dyes containing one or more nitro groups, used in general processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. The entry covers the preparation, formulation and use of these dyes and related auxiliary substances in textile treatment.
[D06P 1/20] Anthraquinone dyes -- D06P 1/20 covers anthraquinone dyes, a class of organic pigments that contain the anthraquinone chromophore and are used to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. These dyes are applied in general dyeing or printing processes, often with auxiliary substances to improve fixation, shade depth or fastness.
[D06P 1/22] using vat dyestuffs -- D06P 1/22 covers the use of vat dyestuffs in dyeing or printing textile materials, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, specifying the application stage where these water-insoluble dyes are reduced, absorbed and then oxidised to develop colour. It falls under general processes of dyeing or printing textiles in the CPC hierarchy.
[D06P 1/221] Reducing systems; Reducing catalysts -- This subclass covers the use of reducing agents and catalysts that facilitate the conversion of dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances to their active, soluble forms during textile, leather, fur or macromolecular material dyeing and printing. It includes formulations, combinations and application methods for such reducing systems.
[D06P 1/222] Oxidising agents -- D06P 1/222 covers the use of oxidising agents in dyeing or printing operations for textiles, leather, furs and polymeric materials, where the agents serve to develop or fix colourants. This includes any process in which oxidisers are applied as the primary means of converting dye precursors or facilitating fixation, irrespective of the specific dyes, pigments or auxiliaries used.
[D06P 1/224] using vat dyes in unreduced pigment state -- The subclass D06P 1/224 covers processes that employ vat dyes while they remain in the unreduced, insoluble pigment state rather than being chemically reduced to a soluble form before application. It relates to dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular materials using these pigments.
[D06P 1/225] using acid vat method -- D06P 1/225 covers the use of the acid vat method in dyeing or printing textiles, where vat dyes are solubilised and applied under acidic conditions to achieve coloration of fabrics, leathers, furs or other macromolecular materials. The process is a specific variant within general dyeing/printing techniques classified by the auxiliary substances employed.
[D06P 1/227] Specific dyes not provided for in groups  D06P1/228  -  D06P1/28 -- This subgroup covers dyeing or printing operations that employ particular dyes or pigment formulations not specifically enumerated in the neighboring subclasses. It includes processes for textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular materials where the dyeing agent falls outside the listed groups, regardless of auxiliary substances used.
[D06P 1/228] Indigo -- D06P 1/228 covers the use of indigo, a blue dye obtained from natural or synthetic sources, in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular materials. It includes the preparation of indigo baths, reduction and oxidation steps, and any auxiliary substances required to achieve the characteristic blue shade.
[D06P 1/24] Anthraquinone dyes -- D06P 1/24 covers anthraquinone dyes, a class of organic colorants based on the anthraquinone structure used in textile, leather, fur and polymer dyeing or printing. These dyes are applied as pigments, dyes or auxiliaries in general dyeing processes defined by the dye chemistry.
[D06P 1/26] Phthalocyanine dyes -- D06P 1/26 covers the use of phthalocyanine dyes in textile, leather, fur or polymer coloration. These synthetic compounds are noted for intense blues and greens and are applied in dyeing or printing processes according to the CPC hierarchy under treatment of textiles.
[D06P 1/28] Esters of vat dyestuffs -- D06P 1/28 classifies the use of esters formed from vat dyestuffs in textile and related dyeing processes. It includes the preparation, formulation and application of these esterified dyes for coloring fabrics, leathers, furs or polymeric materials. The subclass is part of the broader group covering general dyeing and printing methods according to the type of dye or auxiliary used.
[D06P 1/30] using sulfur dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/30 covers dyeing or printing operations that employ sulfur dyes as the primary coloring agents for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It includes general processes where sulfur dyes and any needed auxiliary substances are used to achieve the desired colouration.
[D06P 1/305] SO 3 H-groups containing dyes -- D06P 1/305 covers dyes that incorporate sulfonic acid (SO3H) groups, which increase water solubility and affinity for fibers. These SO3H-functionalized dyes are used in dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, and other polymeric materials. The class includes compounds where the sulfonic acid moiety is part of the chromophore or auxochrome.
[D06P 1/32] using oxidation dyes -- D06P 1/32 concerns processes that use oxidation dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, where the dye is generated or developed through an oxidative chemical reaction. It falls within the broader class of general dyeing or printing methods for fabrics and related materials.
[D06P 1/34] using natural dyestuffs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing with natural dyestuffs, of plant or animal origins.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Dyeing with mineral dyes -> D06P1/0076
[D06P 1/36] using mordant dyes -- The D06P 1/36 subclass covers textile, leather, fur or solid-polymer dyeing and printing processes that employ mordant dyes, i.e., dyes fixed to the material with a metallic or inorganic mordant. It groups methods where the mordant is applied as part of the dyeing or auxiliary system.
[D06P 1/38] using reactive dyes -- The D06P 1/38 subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that employ reactive dyes, which chemically bond to the fibers of textiles, leather, fur, or solid macromolecular substrates. It includes any method where the reactive dye forms a covalent link with the material, providing high fastness and resistance to washing. This subclass groups all such procedures regardless of the specific auxiliary chemicals used.
[D06P 1/382] reactive group directly attached to heterocyclic group -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/382 covers dye- or pigment-based textile processes in which the reactive functional group is covalently bonded directly to a heterocyclic moiety. This includes formulations and methods that rely on such directly attached reactive groups to achieve fixation of dyes or pigments onto fibers, leathers, furs or polymeric substrates during dyeing or printing operations.
[D06P 1/384] reactive group not directly attached to heterocyclic group -- This subclass covers dyes, pigments or auxiliaries used in textile, leather, fur or polymer dyeing and printing where the reactive functionality is present but is not directly bonded to a heterocyclic moiety; the reactive group is linked through an intervening chain or spacer, distinguishing it from compounds with a directly attached heterocycle.
[D06P 1/39] using acid dyes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials with acid dyes, which can also be called anionic dyes as well as the dyeing of textile materials with direct dyes as they are also generally bearing some anionic solubilising groups.
[D06P 1/40] using acid dyes without azo groups -- This subclass covers methods of dyeing textiles, leather, furs or related macromolecular materials using acid dyes that do not contain azo chromophores. It relates to general dyeing processes where non-azo acid dyes are employed as the coloring agents, including any auxiliary substances required for fixation.
[D06P 1/41] using basic dyes -- D06P 1/41 covers textile dyeing processes that employ basic, or cationic, dyes, which are water-soluble colourants that attach to fibres by ionic attraction. The subclass includes methods for applying these dyes to fabrics, leathers, furs or solid macromolecular substances, and is part of the broader category of general dyeing or printing processes.
[D06P 1/42] using basic dyes without azo groups -- D06P 1/42 covers dyeing or printing processes that employ basic (cationic) dyes which do not contain azo linkages. It is a sub-group of the general textile dyeing category, focusing on non-azo basic colourants for fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric materials.
[D06P 1/44] using insoluble pigments or auxiliary substances, e.g. binders -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[D06P 1/445] Use of auxiliary substances before, during or after dyeing or printing -- This subclass covers the application of auxiliary chemicals such as wetting agents, mordants, leveling agents or fixatives that are employed before, during, or after the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials to improve dye uptake, fixation, uniformity or fastness.
[D06P 1/46] using compositions containing natural macromolecular substances or derivatives thereof -- The D06P 1/46 entry covers dyeing or printing processes that employ compositions made from natural macromolecular substances-such as proteins, polysaccharides, or their derivatives-or from materials derived from these biopolymers, applied to textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It focuses on the formulation and use of such bio-based agents as carriers, binders, or auxiliaries in coloration procedures.
[D06P 1/48] Derivatives of carbohydrates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The carbohydrates used as auxiliary agent in dye baths or textile inks as well as binders in printing pastes.
[D06P 1/50] Derivatives of cellulose -- The D06P 1/50 subclass covers dye- or printing-related processes that use cellulose-based derivatives such as viscose, cellulose acetate, and other regenerated or chemically modified celluloses, whether applied to fabrics, leather, furs or solid polymer forms. It includes methods, conditions, and auxiliaries specifically adapted to these cellulose materials.
[D06P 1/52] using compositions containing synthetic macromolecular substances -- D06P 1/52 covers dyeing or printing processes that employ compositions containing synthetic macromolecular substances, such as polymers or resin-based additives, to modify coloration, fixation, or performance of textiles, leathers, furs or solid macromolecular materials. The classification applies to general methods where these synthetic agents are integral to the treatment.
[D06P 1/5207] Macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions involving only carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing or printing of textile materials using polyaddition polymers.
[D06P 1/5214] Polymers of unsaturated compounds containing no COOH groups or functional derivatives thereof -- This subclass covers polymeric auxiliaries used in dyeing or printing textiles that are formed from unsaturated monomers and contain no carboxyl (COOH) groups or their functional derivatives. Such polymers serve as binders, film-formers or fixatives in processes involving dyes, pigments or other coloring agents.
[D06P 1/5221] Polymers of unsaturated hydrocarbons, e.g. polystyrene polyalkylene
[D06P 1/5228] Polyalkenyl alcohols, e.g. PVA -- It covers polyalkenyl alcohol polymers, especially polyvinyl alcohol, employed as dyes, pigments, or auxiliary agents in textile dyeing and printing processes. These substances serve as binders, thickening agents or film-forming fixatives for fabrics, leather, furs and other macromolecular substrates.
[D06P 1/5235] Polyalkenyl halides, e.g. PVC -- This subclass covers the use of polyalkenyl halide polymers such as polyvinyl chloride as carriers, binders, films or auxiliary substances in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/5242] Polymers of unsaturated N-containing compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Polymers of unsaturated N-containing compounds like vinyl amines or polyacrylamides or polyallylamines.
[D06P 1/525] Polymers of unsaturated carboxylic acids or functional derivatives thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Polymers of unsaturated carboxylic acids or functional derivatives thereof like esters, salts or amides of unsaturated carboxylic acids.  Polyacrylates, polyacrylamides, polymethylmethacrylates etc... are covered by this group.
[D06P 1/5257] (Meth)acrylic acid -- (Meth)acrylic acid is a monomeric organic acid used as an auxiliary in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather or polymeric substrates. It can be employed to form polymerizable dyes, enhance fixation, or modify fiber surfaces, and is classified according to its role in the dye-pigment system.
[D06P 1/5264] Macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions involving only unsaturated carbon-to-carbon bonds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing or printing textile materials using polycondensation polymers as auxiliary agents.
[D06P 1/5271] Polyesters; Polycarbonates; Alkyd resins
[D06P 1/5278] Polyamides; Polyimides; Polylactames; Polyalkyleneimines -- The subgroup D06P 1/5278 addresses the use of polyamides, polyimides, polylactams and polyalkyleneimines as dye- or printing-related substances, covering their formulation, application and processing in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/5285] Polyurethanes; Polyurea; Polyguanides -- D06P 1/5285 covers the use of polyurethane, polyurea and polyguanide polymers as binders, finishes or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and similar macromolecular materials, including methods for preparing and applying these polymeric systems.
[D06P 1/5292] containing Si-atoms -- D06P 1/5292 covers dyeing or printing processes in which the dye composition contains silicon-bearing compounds, such as silanes, siloxanes or organosilicon pigments. It falls under general textile-dyeing methods, specifically addressing the use of Si-atoms as functional or auxiliary components.
[D06P 1/54] Substances with reactive groups together with crosslinking agents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing or printing of textile materials with macromolecular substances combined with a crosslinking agent in the same dye bath.
[D06P 1/56] Condensation products or precondensation products prepared with aldehydes -- The entry D06P 1/56 covers condensation or pre-condensation products formed with aldehydes that are used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in general textile dyeing or printing processes, including related treatments of leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It encompasses the preparation and application of such aldehyde-derived compounds within the broader category of textile coloration.
[D06P 1/58] together with other synthetic macromolecular substances -- D06P 1/58 covers dyeing or printing processes in which the textile, leather, fur or solid macromolecular material is treated together with other synthetic macromolecular substances, such as polymeric carriers, fixatives or auxiliary agents that aid colour uptake or performance. The classification groups these processes under general textile dyeing/printing based on the auxiliary substances employed.
[D06P 1/60] using compositions containing polyethers -- D06P 1/60 covers dyeing or printing processes that employ compositions containing polyethers as carriers, dispersants, or auxiliary agents. These polyether-based formulations are used to improve the solubility, fixation or uniformity of dyes, pigments or other coloring substances on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/607] Nitrogen-containing polyethers -- This subclass covers nitrogen-containing polyether compounds employed as dyes, pigments, or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, or synthetic macromolecular materials. It includes their formulation, application methods and related processing steps, focusing on the chemical nature of the polyether rather than the final product.
[D06P 1/6073] containing CON=, OCON=, SO 2 N=, OSO 2 N= groups
[D06P 1/6076] addition products of amines and alkylene oxides or oxiranes
[D06P 1/613] Polyethers without nitrogen -- D06P 1/613 pertains to polyether polymers that contain no nitrogen atoms, used as auxiliaries, binders or carriers in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, fur or macromolecular materials. These nitrogen-free polyethers serve to improve dye uptake, fixation or dispersion without acting as reactive dyes themselves.
[D06P 1/6131] Addition products of hydroxyl groups-containing compounds with oxiranes -- This subclass covers the products formed when compounds that contain one or more hydroxyl groups react with oxiranes (epoxy compounds). The resulting addition products are used as dyes, pigments, mordants or other auxiliaries in textile, leather, fur or polymer dyeing and printing processes. It falls under the CPC group D06P 1/6131 for general dyeing and printing treatments.
[D06P 1/6133] from araliphatic or aliphatic alcohols -- This subgroup covers dyeing or printing processes where the dye or pigment is derived from aryl-aliphatic or aliphatic alcohols, used as colorants or auxiliaries in textile, leather, fur, or polymer treatments. It includes preparation of dyes, pigments, mordants, or printing inks that originate from such alcohols.
[D06P 1/6135] from aromatic alcohols or from phenols, naphthols
[D06P 1/6136] Condensation products of esters, acids, oils, oxyacids with oxiranes -- The subclass D06P 1/6136 covers condensation products obtained by reacting esters, acids, oils or oxy-acids with oxiranes (epoxy compounds), which are used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/6138] Polymerisation products of glycols, e.g. Carbowax, Pluronics -- D06P 1/6138 covers polymerised glycol derivatives such as polyethylene glycol (Carbowax) and block copolymers like Pluronics used as carriers, dispersants or auxiliaries in textile dyeing and printing processes. These compounds act as solubilising agents, wetting agents or to improve dye uptake.
[D06P 1/62] using compositions containing low-molecular-weight organic compounds with sulfate, sulfonate, groups -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that employ liquid or solid formulations in which the active coloring agents are low-molecular-weight organic compounds that carry sulfate or sulfonate groups. Such compositions are used to impart colour to textiles, leather, furs or synthetic macromolecular materials, and the classification includes the methods of applying these agents.
[D06P 1/621] Compounds without nitrogen -- D06P 1/621 pertains to non-nitrogen-containing compounds employed as dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances in the general processes of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE → DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES, covering all such treatments regardless of the specific dye class used.
[D06P 1/622] Sulfonic acids or their salts
[D06P 1/623] Aliphatic, aralophatic or cycloaliphatic -- The subclass D06P 1/623 covers dyeing or printing processes that employ aliphatic, aryl-aliphatic or cycloaliphatic dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, focusing on the chemical nature of the coloration substances used.
[D06P 1/625] Aromatic -- The D06P 1/625 subclass covers aromatic dyes, pigments and auxiliary chemicals employed in textile, leather, fur or macromolecular substance dyeing and printing processes, focusing on compounds whose core structure contains an aromatic ring system. It is a narrow refinement of general dyeing and printing methods that are classified according to the type of colourant used.
[D06P 1/626] Sulfocarboxylic acids -- Sulfocarboxylic acids are a group of organic compounds containing both sulfonic and carboxylic acid groups that are used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and other macromolecular materials. They function by offering improved water solubility, affinity for fibers and often provide bright, wash-fast coloration. This subclass is indexed under CPC code D06P 1/626.
[D06P 1/627] Sulfates -- Sulfates are covered as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents used in general textile, leather, fur or macromolecular substance dyeing and printing processes. The classification includes any method where sulfate compounds serve as the primary coloring or fixing material.
[D06P 1/628] Compounds containing nitrogen -- Compounds containing nitrogen are chemical substances-such as azo, anthraquinone, or heterocyclic dyes-that incorporate nitrogen atoms and are employed as dyes, pigments, or auxiliaries in textile, leather or fur dyeing and printing processes. They are classified according to the specific nitrogen-based structures and their function in the coloration or finishing of fabrics.
[D06P 1/64] using compositions containing low-molecular-weight organic compounds without sulfate or sulfonate groups -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that employ low-molecular-weight organic compositions lacking sulfate or sulfonate groups, such as certain non-ionic dyes, auxiliaries or solvents. It pertains to treating textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances using these specific chemical agents.
[D06P 1/642] Compounds containing nitrogen
[D06P 1/6421] Compounds containing nitrile groups -- The subclass D06P 1/6421 includes any dye, pigment or auxiliary used in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that contains one or more nitrile (C≡N) groups. It covers nitrile-bearing organic compounds employed to impart colour, improve fixation or modify processing properties in these treatments.

It groups such chemicals used as colourants or processing agents in general textile treatment processes.

Such chemicals may act as coupling agents, reactive intermediates or mordants to facilitate fixation of dyes, pigments or other colour-forming species.
[D06P 1/6424] Compounds containing isocyanate or isothiocyanate groups -- The D06P 1/6424 subclass covers chemical compounds that contain isocyanate or isothiocyanate groups and are used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in general textile, leather, fur or solid polymer dyeing and printing processes. These substances are classified according to the specific reactive groups they contain, reflecting their role in fixing colour or modifying material properties during treatment.
[D06P 1/6425] Compounds containing hydrazine or azo groups -- D06P 1/6425 covers chemical compounds that incorporate hydrazine or azo functional groups and are employed as dyes, pigments or auxiliaries in textile, leather, fur or macromolecular material processing. These substances provide coloration through the characteristic chromophoric properties of the N-N double bond or the hydrazine moiety.
[D06P 1/6426] Heterocyclic compounds -- The D06P 1/6426 subclass groups heterocyclic compounds used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances in the treatment of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It is part of the general processes for dyeing or printing, classified according to the chemical nature of the colouring agents.

These substances are classified according to the presence of the aminoxide moiety.

It falls under the general textile treatment category and specifies the use of such ring-based colorants for coloring textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.

These nitrogen-bearing molecules are used as dyes, fixing agents or auxiliaries to improve colour uptake, shade depth or fastness on fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric substrates.

These compounds are classified according to the dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances employed in the treatment.
[D06P 1/649] Compounds containing carbonamide, thiocarbonamide or guanyl groups -- In the CPC section D06P, subgroup 1/649 covers the use of carbonamide, thiocarbonamide and guanyl-containing compounds as dyes, pigments or auxiliaries for textile, leather, fur or macromolecular substance processing. These chemicals function as colouring agents or mordants in general dyeing or printing operations.
[D06P 1/6491] (Thio)urea or (cyclic) derivatives -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/6491 pertains to the use of thio-urea compounds or their cyclic derivatives as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular materials. It covers processes in which these chemicals act as colourants, mordants, reducing or leveling agents.
[D06P 1/6492] (Thio)urethanes; (Di)(thio)carbamic acid derivatives; Thiuramdisulfide -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/6492 concerns thio-urethanes, di-(thio)carbamic acid derivatives and thiuram-disulfide when employed as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the general processes of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances.
[D06P 1/6493] Carbodiimides (=N=C=N=) -- Carbodiimides (N=C=N) are chemical coupling agents used in dyeing and printing processes for textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances. In this classification they cover the application of carbodiimide reagents to activate dyes, pigments or auxiliaries, facilitating bonding to fibers or substrates. The entry is part of the broader CPC group for general dyeing or printing methods based on the chemicals employed.
[D06P 1/6494] Compounds containing a guanyl group R-C-N=, e.g. (bi)guanadine, dicyandiamid amidines -- D06P 1/6494 covers dye or printing auxiliaries that contain a guanyl group (R-C-N=) such as biguanidine, dicyandiamide and related amidines. These compounds are used to modify the coloration, fixation or fastness of textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials during dyeing or printing processes.
[D06P 1/6495] Compounds containing carbonamide -RCON= (R=H or hydrocarbons) -- This subclass covers the use of carbonamide derivatives where the carbonyl group is attached to hydrogen or hydrocarbon substituents as dyes, pigments or auxiliaries for coloring textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. The compounds are defined by the R-C=O-NH-R′ (R = H or hydrocarbon) structure.
[D06P 1/6496] Condensation products from carboxylic acids and hydroxyalkyl amine (Kritchewski bases) -- The entry concerns the use of condensation products obtained by reacting carboxylic acids with hydroxyalkyl amines, commonly called Kritchewski bases, as dye-intermediates or auxiliaries in textile dyeing and printing processes. It covers their preparation, properties and application in treating fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric materials.
[D06P 1/6497] Amides of di- or polyamines; Acylated polyamines -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/6497 relates to amides formed from di- or polyamines, including their acylated polyamine derivatives, when employed as dyes, pigments or auxiliary substances in the general processes of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 1/6498] Compounds containing -CONCO-, e.g. phthalimides, hydantoine; Compounds containing RCONHSO 2 R (R=H or hydrocarbon) -- This subclass pertains to chemicals employed in textile dyeing or printing that contain the -CONCO- group, such as phthalimides and hydantoins, as well as compounds of the formula RCONHSO2R where R is hydrogen or a hydrocarbon chain, used as auxiliaries or dyes.
[D06P 1/651] Compounds without nitrogen -- D06P 1/651 covers textile-dyeing or printing processes that employ organic colourants, pigments or auxiliaries lacking nitrogen atoms, such as certain azo, anthraquinone or phthalocyanine compounds. [D06P 1/65118] Compounds containing hydroxyl groups -- D06P 1/65118 refers to any chemical compounds that contain one or more hydroxyl groups and are used as dyes, pigments, or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, or polymeric materials. These substances are classified based on the presence of hydroxyl functionality, which can affect solubility, reactivity, and affinity for fibers.

These compounds are selected for their reactivity with substrates during treatment processes.
[D06P 1/6515] Hydrocarbons -- D06P 1/6515 concerns the use of hydrocarbon compounds as dyes, pigments, or auxiliary agents in the general processes of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It includes methods where hydrocarbons serve as colourants or processing aids within the broader textile treatment classification.
[D06P 1/65156] Halogen-containing hydrocarbons -- Halogen-containing hydrocarbons are organic compounds where hydrogen atoms are substituted by halogen elements, and they are employed as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances. Their presence influences color development, fastness and processing characteristics in these general dyeing processes.

[D06P 1/65187] Compounds containing sulfide or disulfide groups -- This subclass covers chemical compounds that contain sulfide or disulfide functional groups and are used as dyes, pigments, auxiliaries or mordants in processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or macromolecular solids. [D06P 1/653] Nitrogen-free carboxylic acids or their salts -- The subclass D06P 1/653 covers the use of nitrogen-free carboxylic acids and their salts as auxiliary chemicals in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. [D06P 1/66] containing quaternary ammonium groups -- This subclass deals with textile dyeing or printing processes that employ compounds bearing quaternary ammonium groups, typically used as surfactants, mordants or antimicrobial agents to improve fixation and performance of dyes on fabrics.
[D06P 1/667] Organo-phosphorus compounds -- The subclass D06P 1/667 includes the use of organo-phosphorus compounds as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular materials. It groups formulations and methods that employ these phosphorus-based substances to colour or treat such substrates.
[D06P 1/673] Inorganic compounds -- Code D06P 1/673 in the CPC hierarchy under TEXTILES, TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE, DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES refers to inorganic compounds used as dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances. It includes metal-based colourants, inorganic pigments and related processing aids.
[D06P 1/67308] Hydroxylamine or derivatives thereof -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/67308 covers the use of hydroxylamine and its derivatives as auxiliary chemicals in general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. These compounds are employed to modify dye uptake, fixation or shade development.
[D06P 1/67316] Acids -- Acids are treated as chemical auxiliaries used in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or polymeric substances, primarily to adjust pH, act as mordants, or aid the fixation of dyes and pigments.
[D06P 1/67325] Complex acids, e.g. phosphomolybdic, phosphotungstic, molybdotungstic -- The subclass covers the use of complex inorganic acids such as phosphomolybdic, phosphotungstic and molybdotungstic acids as mordants, fixing agents or catalysts in dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, fur or polymeric materials.
