[D06P 1/67341] of elements different from the alkaline or alkaline-earth metals or with anions containing those elements  D06P1/67383  takes precedence -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that use dyes, pigments, or auxiliaries containing elements other than alkali or alkaline-earth metals, or whose anions contain those elements. It falls within the general textile dyeing and printing group and is superseded by the more specific subclass D06P 1/67383.
[D06P 1/6735] of alkaline or alkaline-earth metals with anions different from those provided for in  D06P1/67341 -- This subclass covers general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials in which the coloring agent contains an alkaline or alkaline-earth metal combined with an anion that is not listed in the related subclass D06P 1/67341. It focuses on the use of alternative metal-anion complexes as dye or auxiliary substances.
[D06P 1/67358] Halides or oxyhalides -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes where halide or oxyhalide compounds are employed as coloring agents, mordants, or auxiliaries. It includes the use of inorganic halides such as chlorine, bromine, iodine, or their oxy-derived species to modify or fix dyes on textiles, leather, fur or polymeric substrates.
[D06P 1/67366] Phosphates or polyphosphates -- The CPC subclass D06P 1/67366 includes processes that employ phosphates or polyphosphates as auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leathers, furs or related macromolecular materials. These compounds are used for buffering, mordanting, dispersion, or as fixing agents to improve colour uptake and fastness.
[D06P 1/67375] with sulfur-containing anions  sulfates  D06P1/6735 -- This subclass covers general dyeing or printing processes that employ dyes, pigments or auxiliaries containing sulfur-bearing anions, especially sulfate groups, for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It groups methods where the colorants are classified by the presence of these sulfur-containing anions.
[D06P 1/67383] containing silicon -- The entry relates to dyeing or printing operations in which silicon-containing substances are used, such as organosilicon dyes, silicone-based binders or other silicon-based auxiliaries applied to textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. It classifies processes according to the presence of silicon in the formulation rather than the type of substrate.
[D06P 1/67391] Salts or oxidising-compounds mixtures -- A mixture of salts or oxidising compounds used in dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, where the combination serves to modify, fix or develop the colour, often acting as mordants, bleaching agents or oxidising aids in the overall process.
[D06P 1/81] using dyes dissolved in inorganic solvents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials dyed with inorganic solvents like liquid carbon dioxide.
[D06P 1/90] using dyes dissolved in organic solvents or aqueous emulsions thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials using organic solvents.
[D06P 1/908] using specified dyes -- This subclass covers processes in which a particular dye or pigment is deliberately selected and applied during the dyeing or printing of textile-leather, furs or polymeric materials. It includes any method where the identity of the dye is a defining feature of the process, irrespective of the auxiliary chemicals used.
[D06P 1/92] in organic solvents -- The subgroup D06P 1/92 covers dyeing or printing operations in which the material-textile, leather, fur or solid polymer-is processed using organic solvents as the dye-carrying medium, including any auxiliary substances needed for solvent-based coloration.
[D06P 1/922] hydrocarbons -- D06P 1/922 covers the use of hydrocarbons-such as aliphatic or aromatic liquids, oils, waxes, and petroleum fractions-as carriers, solvents, diluents, or processing aids in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, or other solid macromolecular materials, classified according to the auxiliary substances employed.
[D06P 1/924] Halogenated hydrocarbons -- D06P 1/924 relates to the use of halogenated hydrocarbons as solvents, carriers or auxiliary agents in general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. It covers how these compounds dissolve dyes, enhance penetration and help control drying.
[D06P 1/926] Non-halogenated hydrocarbons -- The entry D06P 1/926 covers the use of non-halogenated hydrocarbon liquids as solvents, carriers or auxiliaries in general dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It includes methods where such hydrocarbons replace halogenated solvents for applying dyes, pigments or related auxiliaries.
[D06P 1/928] Solvents other than hydrocarbons -- The code D06P 1/928 covers the use of non-hydrocarbon solvents such as water, alcohols, glycols, esters or other polar liquids as carriers for dyes, pigments or auxiliary agents in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric substances, including their selection, preparation and application.
[D06P 1/94] using dyes dissolved in solvents which are in the supercritical state -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials with dye which are dissolved in a solvent which is in a supercritical state, e.g. supercritical CO2 or carbon dioxide.
[D06P 1/96] Dyeing characterised by a short bath ratio -- D06P 1/96 covers dyeing processes in which the bath ratio - the volume of dye liquor relative to the weight of the textile - is kept short, typically low enough to reduce liquor consumption and shorten processing time while still achieving satisfactory colour uptake.
[D06P 1/965] Foam dyeing  mechanical features  D06B19/0094 -- Foam dyeing relates to the mechanical aspects of applying dye to textiles using a foam carrier, covering equipment, agitation, and handling techniques that control foam formation, transfer, and penetration of dye into fabrics or leather. It focuses on the process conditions and machinery required to achieve uniform coloration with foam-based systems.
[D06P 2001/0084] Non-aqueous dyeing in an inorganic medium -- The CPC code D06P 2001/0084 designates processes for dyeing textiles or other substrates using a non-aqueous inorganic medium. It includes the preparation, application and fixation of dyes in solvents or carriers that contain inorganic compounds instead of water, together with related equipment and pretreatment steps.
[D06P 2001/0088] Aqueous dyeing characterised by a short bath ratio -- D06P 2001/0088 covers aqueous dyeing processes that employ a short or low bath ratio, meaning the amount of water relative to the material being dyed is minimized. The classification addresses methods that achieve efficient dye uptake while reducing water consumption and energy use, often used in textile finishing and related industries.
[D06P 2001/0092] Foam dyeing -- CPC D06P 2001/0092 concerns the use of foam as a carrier for dyeing textile or other fibrous materials. It covers processes in which a foam containing dye is applied to the substrate, allowed to penetrate, and then fixed by drying, steaming or other curing steps. The entry includes methods for producing the dye-laden foam and for treating woven, knitted, non-woven or coated materials.
[D06P 2001/901] Hydrocarbons -- This CPC subgroup covers the use, preparation and processing of hydrocarbon compounds as feedstock or intermediates in the manufacture of synthetic polymeric materials, including their selection, purification and conversion steps.
[D06P 2001/903] Halogenated hydrocarbons -- This CPC code pertains to halogenated hydrocarbons, including compounds where hydrogen atoms are replaced by fluorine, chlorine, bromine or iodine, used as solvents, refrigerants, fire-suppression agents and intermediates in organic synthesis. It falls under the D06P main group for inorganic and organometallic chemistry.
[D06P 2001/905] Non-halogenated hydrocarbons -- D06P 2001/905 covers the preparation, processing, and use of non-halogenated hydrocarbon compounds, including their synthesis, purification, and catalytic conversion in chemical and industrial applications. The entry includes methods for producing aliphatic and cyclo-aliphatic hydrocarbons without halogen substituents, as well as related catalysts, reactors, and process conditions.
[D06P 2001/906] Solvents other than hydrocarbons -- D06P 2001/906 pertains to the use of non-hydrocarbon solvents for cleaning, degreasing, or surface-treatment processes, covering aqueous or organic solvent systems that exclude hydrocarbons.
[D06P 3/00] Special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, or dyeing leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances in any form, classified according to the material treated -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing or printing according to the material treated as well as the chemical nature of the dyes used.
[D06P 3/001] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/001 covers processes that employ acid dyes for coloring textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, detailing the specific methods and conditions for applying these dyes in dyeing or printing operations.
[D06P 3/002] using basic dyes -- D06P 3/002 covers processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ basic dyes. The class addresses the use of cationic dye molecules to colour fibres such as wool, silk, nylon or acetate, typically involving alkaline conditions to fix the dye. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles within the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[D06P 3/003] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/003 covers textile, leather or fur dyeing processes that employ vat or sulfur dyes, which are water-insoluble pigments that become soluble after chemical reduction or oxidation. The classification includes the preparation, application and fixation steps specific to these dye types.
[D06P 3/004] using dispersed dyes -- The entry D06P 3/004 concerns processes that use dispersed dyes-water-insoluble pigment particles suspended in a carrier-for dyeing synthetic textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials, describing the preparation of the dye bath, the penetration of the particles into the substrate and the fixation steps. This falls under the special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/005] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that use metallisable dyes containing metal particles or mordant dyes that need a metallic mordant to fix the colour on textiles, leather, furs or polymeric substrates. The techniques may involve pre-treatment with metal salts, application of metallic pigments, or combined mordanting and dyeing steps to achieve metallic sheen or improved fastness.
[D06P 3/006] using polymeric dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that employ polymeric dyes, which are large-molecule colorants applied to textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular substances. It groups methods where the dye itself is a polymer, distinguishing them from conventional low-molecular-weight dyes.
[D06P 3/007] preparing dyes  in situ -- D06P 3/007 covers processes that form dye compounds directly on the material being treated, rather than applying pre-made dyes. It includes in-situ chemical reactions and synthesis of coloring agents on fibers, leather, fur or polymer surfaces during dyeing or printing. The class is part of special textile dyeing processes in the CPC.
[D06P 3/008] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/008 covers processes in which reactive dyes are applied to textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials, forming covalent bonds with the substrate to achieve high colour fastness. The classification includes methods of dye fixation, washing and post-treatment that rely on the reactivity of the dye.
[D06P 3/02] Material containing basic nitrogen -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/02 covers dyeing or printing processes applied to materials that contain basic nitrogen groups, such as amines or nitrogen-containing polymers. It includes methods and formulations specifically designed for substrates whose chemistry includes these basic nitrogen functionalities.
[D06P 3/021] using acid dyes -- This subgroup covers processes that colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances by applying acid dyes, typically from aqueous solutions under acidic conditions. It includes the use of heating, auxiliaries or other treatments required to bond the dyes to protein- or polyamide-based fibres.
[D06P 3/022] using basic dyes -- This CPC subclass covers the use of basic (cationic) dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid-polymeric materials, typically in processes where the dye bonds to negatively charged fibre sites. It includes methods for preparing, fixing and treating the dyed substrate.
[D06P 3/023] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/023 covers special dye-application methods that employ vat or sulfur dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials, regardless of their form. The classification groups processes where these water-insoluble or sulfur-based colorants are solubilized, applied and fixed on the substrate, distinguishing them from other dyeing techniques.
[D06P 3/024] using dispersed dyes -- This subclass covers processes that apply dispersed dyes-water-insoluble pigment particles-to textile materials, especially synthetic fibers, through methods such as high-temperature or carrier-mediated dyeing, supercritical CO₂, or other techniques that enable the dye particles to infiltrate and fix within the fabric.
[D06P 3/025] using metallised dyes -- D06P 3/025 covers processes that employ metallised dyes - dyes containing metal particles or complexes - to impart color to textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular substances during dyeing or printing. The metal component can enhance shade, give metallic effects, or provide functional properties such as conductivity or UV resistance.
[D06P 3/026] using polymeric dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/026 concerns the use of polymeric dyes for treating textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, describing processes where the dye is a high-molecular-weight polymer that forms covalent or strong interactions with the material, resulting in enhanced colour fastness and specific functional properties.
[D06P 3/027] preparing dyes  in situ -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/027 covers processes in which dyes are generated directly on the textile, leather, fur or polymer during the dyeing or printing operation, rather than being pre-manufactured and applied. It includes in-situ synthesis, coupling or activation of dye precursors on the material being treated.
[D06P 3/028] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/028 covers the use of reactive dyes in specialized dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances, focusing on the chemical interaction that forms covalent bonds between the dye and the material. This subclass groups methods where the dye reacts with the substrate to achieve fixation, colour fastness, and specific performance characteristics.
[D06P 3/04] containing amide groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials comprising amide groups and which are not covered by any other subgroups ( D06P3/24 , D06P3/30 , D06P3/32 ) like silk.
[D06P 3/041] using basic dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/041 refers to textile or leather dyeing processes that employ basic, or cationic, dyes. It covers the use of these positively charged pigments in special dyeing or printing methods applied to fabrics, leathers, furs or solid macromolecular substances, distinguishing them from other dye categories such as acid or direct dyes.
[D06P 3/042] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/042 covers special dyeing processes that employ vat or sulfur dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, focusing on the chemical reduction and oxidation steps required to fix these dark-colored pigments. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for treatment of textiles, specifically the sub-category of dyeing or printing processes.
[D06P 3/043] using dispersed dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing processes that use dispersed dyes-water-insoluble pigment particles-primarily for synthetic fibers and other treated materials. It includes the preparation of the dye dispersion, its application to the textile, and the fixation or post-treatment steps required to develop the colour.
[D06P 3/045] dyeing and degumming silk -- D06P 3/045 designates the specific methods for dyeing silk and for its degumming, the process that removes the natural sericin coating before coloration. It falls under the CPC section for special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, classified by the material treated.
[D06P 3/046] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- D06P 3/046 covers the use of metallisable or mordant dyes in dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, within the broader classes of treatment of textiles and textiles.
[D06P 3/047] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/047 covers the use of polymeric dyes in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. The subclass focuses on how these polymer-based colourants are applied to achieve improved fastness, uniformity and specific functional properties.
[D06P 3/048] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- This subclass covers methods for preparing and applying non-azo dyes directly onto textiles, leather, fur or other solid macromolecular substrates, including steps such as fixing, bonding or developing the color on the material itself. It excludes processes that use azo-type dyes and falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles classified by the material treated.
[D06P 3/06] using acid dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/06 refers to processes that use acid dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, typically when the dye is taken up by the material's cationic sites in aqueous or other media. It is a special-process sub-category within the broader hierarchy of treatment, dyeing or printing of textiles.
[D06P 3/08] using oxidation dyes -- A method of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials that relies on oxidation dyes, which develop their colour only after an oxidising step, usually applied from an aqueous bath and fixed by agents that promote oxidation. This subclass covers the specific processes and conditions needed to achieve the colour change through oxidation.
[D06P 3/10] using reactive dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/10 covers textile or leather dyeing processes that employ reactive dyes, which form covalent bonds with the fibers to achieve high colour fastness. It is part of the special processes for dyeing or printing textiles, grouping methods that use these chemically reactive colourants on various substrates.
[D06P 3/12] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/12 covers processes in which azo dyes are synthesized directly on the textile, leather, fur or polymer substrate during dyeing or printing operations. The method involves forming the azo coupling reaction on the material itself, allowing the dye to develop in situ and providing specific color and fastness properties.
[D06P 3/14] Wool -- D06P 3/14 designates the subclass covering special dyeing or printing processes applied to wool, including pretreatment, mordanting and finishing operations performed on wool in any form.
[D06P 3/141] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/141 refers to the use of vat or sulfur dyes in the special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It belongs to the hierarchy TEXTILES > TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE > DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES and specifies how these water-insoluble dyes are applied to achieve deep, colour-fast shades on various materials.
[D06P 3/143] using dispersed dyes -- It refers to a special process of dyeing or printing textiles in which dispersed dyes are employed, typically for synthetic fibers such as polyester or nylon. The entry covers methods of applying these water-insoluble dyes, including high-temperature dyeing, carrier-mediated processes or sublimation, to achieve uniform coloration of the material.
[D06P 3/145] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/145 deals with the application of polymeric dyes in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles, and dyeing or printing textiles, classified according to the material treated.
[D06P 3/146] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- This subclass covers processes in which non-azo dyes are prepared directly on textiles, leather, furs or similar substrates, rather than being applied as pre-made solutions. It includes steps for forming or developing the dye on the material before fixation, and falls under special dyeing or printing procedures.
[D06P 3/148] using reactive dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/148 concerns the application of reactive dyes in dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It is part of the special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, which falls under treatment of textiles or the like within the broader textiles category.
[D06P 3/16] using acid dyes -- The D06P 3/16 subclass covers special dyeing or printing processes that employ acid dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials, classifying the method according to the material being treated. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles, and dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/18] using basic dyes -- D06P 3/18 covers processes that employ basic, also called cationic, dyes for coloring textiles, leather, furs, or polymeric materials. It includes methods where the dye is applied in its alkaline form to achieve fixation on substrates that have anionic sites. The subclass is part of the broader group for special dyeing and printing processes.
[D06P 3/20] using mordant dyes -- D06P 3/20 covers processes for dyeing or printing textiles in which a mordant is employed to fix the colour. The classification applies to methods that use a separate mordant compound to form a complex with the dye, improving fastness on fibers, leather, furs or solid polymers.
[D06P 3/22] Preparing azo dyes on the fibre -- This subclass covers methods where azo dyes are formed directly on textile fibers, involving in-situ synthesis of the dye on the material rather than pre-made dye application. It includes processes where the fibre is treated with diazo components and coupling agents to generate the azo chromophore on the fabric.
[D06P 3/24] Polyamides -- D06P 3/24 covers dyeing, printing and related finishing operations applied to polyamide fibers and fabrics, such as nylon, including any pretreatment, coloring agents, or post-treatment steps specific to this polymer class.
[D06P 3/241] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/241 relates to special dyeing or printing processes that employ acid dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, typically applied to protein-based fibres such as wool or silk and to synthetic polymers that can absorb acid dyes. It includes any preparatory or auxiliary steps specific to the acid-dyeing method, such as mordanting, exhaustion control or fixation treatments.
[D06P 3/242] using basic dyes -- D06P 3/242 concerns the use of basic, also called cationic, dyes in the coloration of textiles, leather, furs, or solid polymeric materials. It includes processes where the dye is applied in its basic form or after conversion to a basic dye, and the classification addresses the methods and conditions for fixing such dyes onto the substrate.
[D06P 3/243] using vat or sulfur dyes, indigo -- The D06P 3/243 subclass covers dyeing processes that use vat or sulfur dyes, such as indigo, applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It specifies special procedures for these dye types within the broader category of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/245] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- D06P 3/245 covers methods of dyeing or printing textiles that involve the use of metallisable or mordant dyes, where the dye contains metal ions or a mordant to fix the colour onto the material. It is part of the CPC hierarchy for textiles, under treatment of textiles, dyeing or printing, and addresses special processes applied to various substrates, including leather, furs, or polymeric forms.
[D06P 3/246] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/246 covers special dye- or print-processing methods in which polymeric dyes are used to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. The entry details how these large-molecule colourants are formulated, applied and fixed during treatment, fitting within the broader hierarchy of textile dyeing and printing processes.
[D06P 3/247] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/247 covers processes for applying non-azo dyes directly onto textiles, leathers, furs or polymeric substrates, focusing on the preparation of the dye on the material itself rather than prior synthesis. It includes steps such as pre-treatment, impregnation, fixation and washing that enable the coloured compound to bond with the substrate. The subclass is used to classify inventions that improve dye uptake, fastness or environmental performance of non-azo dyeing.
[D06P 3/248] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/248 covers the use of reactive dyes in textile dyeing or printing processes. Reactive dyes chemically bond to the substrate, providing high fastness on fibers such as cotton, wool, silk, as well as leather or polymeric materials. The subclass includes any method that employs reactive dye formulations to achieve coloration.
[D06P 3/26] using dispersed dyestuffs -- D06P 3/26 covers textile or leather dyeing processes that employ dispersed dyestuffs, typically used on synthetic fibers and other solid macromolecular materials. The subclass groups methods where the dye is applied in a finely divided, water-insoluble form, allowing penetration and fixation during the special treatment step. It falls under the broader category of special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/28] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/28 covers the preparation of azo dyes directly on the textile, leather, fur or solid polymer material being treated, where the dye is formed in situ on the substrate during the dyeing or printing operation. This special process falls under the CPC section for textile treatment, specifically the sub-group dealing with material-specific dye-formation methods.
[D06P 3/30] furs -- D06P 3/30 covers methods and processes for dyeing or printing furs, including special treatments applied to animal pelts and similar materials. It includes techniques for coloring, patterning, or otherwise imparting appearance to fur substrates, whether in raw or processed form.
[D06P 3/3008] using acid dyes -- This subclass covers processes that colour fibres, yarns, fabrics, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular substances by applying acid dyes, typically in an acidic aqueous bath where the dye is attracted to basic groups in the material. It includes the preparation of the dye bath, fixing agents and any specific steps needed to achieve uniform uptake.
[D06P 3/3016] using monazo acid dyes -- The CPC subgroup D06P 3/3016 covers textile or leather dyeing and printing processes that specifically employ monazo acid dyes, a class of mono-azo compounds that bind to fibers through ionic interactions. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, distinguishing the use of these dyes from other acid or azo dye applications.
[D06P 3/3025] dis-polyazo acid dyes -- D06P 3/3025 covers dis-polyazo acid dyes, a group of acid dyes containing two azo linkages that form polymeric structures. They are employed in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs and other macromolecular materials, offering high affinity and vivid coloration. The classification groups them by the material treated and the polymeric nature of the dye.
[D06P 3/3033] using basic dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/3033 covers processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ basic (alkaline) dyes. Basic dyes are cationic compounds that bind to negatively charged fibers such as acrylics, polyester and wool, often requiring high temperature, carriers or fixing agents. This subclass groups methods that use these dyes to achieve coloration in various material forms.
[D06P 3/3041] using vat, sulfur or indigo dyes -- This CPC subclass covers the use of vat, sulfur or indigo dyes in special processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid-macromolecular substances. It falls under D06P 3/3041, a subcategory of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/305] with oxidation dyes -- D06P 3/305 covers special dyeing or printing processes that employ oxidation dyes, which develop colour through an oxidation reaction, for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It is a subclass of the CPC hierarchy dealing with treatment of textiles, specifically the dyeing or printing stage.
[D06P 3/3058] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- D06P 3/3058 covers special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that use metallisable or mordant dyes to fix colour. It is part of the hierarchy for treatment of textiles, specifically the sub-group for dyeing or printing according to the material treated.
[D06P 3/3066] using polymeric dyes -- This subgroup covers processes that employ polymeric dyes - high-molecular-weight coloring agents - during dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, fur, or solid macromolecular substances, emphasizing the method of applying or fixing the polymeric dye.
[D06P 3/3075] preparing azo dyes on the material -- This CPC subclass concerns methods for preparing azo dyes directly on the material being treated, such as fibers, leather, furs or polymeric substrates, during dyeing or printing processes. It includes in-situ formation of the azo chromophore on the substrate as part of the finishing operation.
[D06P 3/3083] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/3083 covers processes in which non-azo dyes are prepared directly on the textile, leather, fur or polymer substrate during dyeing or printing. It includes methods that form the colourant in situ on the material rather than applying a pre-made azo dye.
[D06P 3/3091] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/3091 covers processes that employ reactive dyes for dyeing or printing textiles and related substrates. Reactive dyes form covalent bonds with the material, giving high wash- and light-fastness, and are used in batch, continuous, pad-dyeing and similar techniques. The subclass groups methods where the dye chemistry, rather than the fabric type, defines the process.
[D06P 3/32] leather -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/32 covers special processes for dyeing or printing leather, including methods that treat leather as a material distinct from fabric or other solid macromolecular substances. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles, treatment of textiles or the like, and dyeing or printing textiles, focusing specifically on leather finishing techniques.
[D06P 3/3206] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/3206 covers the use of acid dyes in textile and leather dyeing or printing operations. It relates to special processes where acid-soluble dyes are applied to protein fibers such as wool and silk, regenerated cellulose, nylon or other macromolecular substrates, detailing the conditions and methods for effective coloration.
[D06P 3/3213] monoazo -- The code D06P 3/3213 denotes the use of monoazo dyes in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. It falls under the special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, where the colorant is a single-azo compound providing specific shade properties and fastness. This classification groups patents that employ such monoazo compounds as the principal coloring agents.
[D06P 3/322] using monoazo premetallised dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes in which monoazo premetallised dyes are employed; these are colourants that contain a metal atom covalently linked to a single azo group before application. The premetallisation improves shade intensity, fixation and fastness on textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular substrates, and the subclass groups all such special dyeing methods.
[D06P 3/3226] dis-polyazo -- D06P 3/3226 covers special dyeing or printing processes that employ dis-polyazo compounds, a type of polymeric azo dye, on textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials. The subclass addresses the preparation, application and fixation of these polymeric azo colourants in the treatment of fabrics.
[D06P 3/3233] using dis-polyazo premetallised dyes -- The code D06P 3/3233 designates a special process for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances using dis-polyazo pre-metallised dyes. It falls within the CPC hierarchy under treatment of textiles, dyeing or printing, classified by the material treated. The method involves applying dyes that contain multiple azo groups and pre-metallised complexes to achieve coloration.
[D06P 3/324] using basic dyes -- The D06P 3/324 subgroup covers textile, leather, fur or polymer dyeing and printing operations that employ basic (cationic) dyes. It includes the preparation, application and fixation steps specific to these positively charged dyes, distinguishing them from processes using other dye classes. This classification groups methods where the dye's basic nature is central to the treatment.
[D06P 3/3246] using vat, sulfur or indigo dyes -- D06P 3/3246 covers special textile-dying processes that employ vat, sulfur or indigo dyes, detailing how these particular colourants are applied to fabrics, leather or similar macromolecular materials. It falls under the hierarchy of textile treatment, specifically the dyeing or printing of textiles.
[D06P 3/3253] using oxidation dyes -- The code D06P 3/3253 denotes a specialized dyeing method that uses oxidation dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. These dyes develop their hue through an oxidative reaction after application, providing precise control of shade and fastness. The process falls under special dyeing and printing techniques within the broader textile treatment hierarchy.
[D06P 3/326] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/326 deals with dyeing or printing processes that utilize metallisable or mordant dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric substances. It describes the use of metal-containing dyes or mordants to fix colour, often to achieve special shades or enhanced colour fastness.
[D06P 3/3266] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/3266 deals with dyeing or printing processes that use polymeric dyes-high-molecular-weight dye molecules-on textiles, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular substances. It covers the application, fixation and development steps where the polymeric dye imparts colour and often enhances fastness or adds functional properties.
[D06P 3/3273] preparing azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/3273 addresses processes that form azo dyes directly on the substrate, such as textiles, leather, or polymers, by generating the dye on the material rather than applying a pre-made pigment. Techniques include in-situ diazotisation and coupling reactions carried out on the treated surface, enabling precise coloration and reduced waste.
[D06P 3/328] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- The D06P 3/328 subclass covers special processes for applying non-azo dyes directly onto the material being treated, such as textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, rather than using pre-formed dyes. This falls under the broader category of dyeing or printing textiles and specifies preparation of the dye on the substrate itself.
[D06P 3/3286] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/3286 covers processes in which reactive dyes are employed to colour textiles, leather, fur or polymeric substrates. The method relies on a chemical reaction between the dye and the fibre to form a covalent bond, giving high fastness - and colour strength. It includes variations in dye formulation, application technique and fixing conditions.
[D06P 3/3293] mineral dyeing -- D06P 3/3293 covers methods of dyeing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ mineral dyes, such as inorganic pigments or metal-based colourants, either as the primary colourant or in combination with other dyes. The entry addresses the specific preparation, application and fixation steps required for mineral-based coloration of these materials.
[D06P 3/34] Material containing ester groups -- D06P 3/34 covers dyeing or printing operations in which the material being treated contains ester functional groups, such as polyester, acetate or other ester-based fibers, as well as leather or fur that have been esterified. The classification relates to the chemical nature of the substrate rather than the specific dye or printing method.
[D06P 3/341] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/341 covers processes in which acid dyes are employed to colour textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials. The subclass is part of the CPC group for special dyeing or printing methods, typically applied to protein-based fibres such as wool and silk, where the dye is fixed by an acidic bath.
[D06P 3/342] using basic dyes -- This subgroup covers processes in which basic (cationic) dyes are applied to textiles, leather, furs or other solid polymeric materials. [D06P 3/343] using vat or sulfur dyes -- This subclass covers textile (or leather, fur, solid macromolecular) dyeing processes that employ vat or sulfur dyes, detailing the specific steps and conditions required for these chemical dye types. It is part of the broader group on special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/345] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/345 covers special dyeing or printing processes that employ metallisable or mordant dyes to colour textiles, leather, fur or solid macromolecular substances, using a binding agent or mordant to fix the colour. It is part of the overall CPC group for treatment of textiles and related materials.
[D06P 3/346] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/346 concerns the application of polymeric dyes in textile processing. It covers special dyeing or printing methods where polymer-based coloring agents are used on fabrics, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials.
[D06P 3/347] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- This CPC subclass deals with methods for applying non-azo dyes directly onto textiles, leather, fur or other solid polymeric materials during the dyeing or printing stage. It covers processes that incorporate the dye preparation step on the substrate itself rather than pre-forming the dye elsewhere. The focus is on special dyeing techniques that avoid azo compounds.
[D06P 3/348] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/348 covers the use of reactive dyes in textile and related material processing, focusing on special dyeing or printing methods where the dye forms a covalent bond with fibers, leather, furs or other macromolecular substances. This entry falls under the broader categories of textile treatment, dyeing or printing, and material-specific processes.
[D06P 3/36] using dispersed dyestuffs -- D06P 3/36 covers processes in which dispersed dyestuffs are employed to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials. It includes methods of applying these particulate pigments during dyeing or printing operations, such as using pastes, suspensions or spray techniques.
[D06P 3/38] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/38 covers processes in which azo dyes are synthesized directly on the material being treated, such as fabric, leather or other macromolecular substrates. It includes on-material coupling of diazo components and coupling components to produce the colored azo compound during dyeing or printing.
[D06P 3/40] Cellulose acetate -- D06P 3/40 covers the dyeing or printing of cellulose acetate, a semi-synthetic polymer used for fibers, films and other textile forms. The classification includes methods, auxiliaries and conditions specific to treating this material, whether in fabric, leather-like sheets or molded articles.
[D06P 3/401] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/401 covers the use of acid dyes in textile dyeing processes, especially for protein fibers such as wool and silk and for synthetic polyamides. It includes methods where the dye is applied from acidic aqueous solutions to achieve coloration. This falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/402] using basic dyes -- This CPC subclass D06P 3/402 covers processes that use basic dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles within the treatment of textiles hierarchy.
[D06P 3/403] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/403 relates to special dyeing processes that employ vat or sulfur dyes on textiles, leather, fur or polymeric substances. [D06P 3/405] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- D06P 3/405 covers textile dyeing or printing processes that employ metallisable dyes or mordant dyes to achieve metallic effects or enhanced colour fixation on fabrics, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. The subclass includes methods that use metallic pigments or chemical mordants to bind the dye to the material being treated.
[D06P 3/406] using polymeric dyes -- This subclass covers methods of dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances in which the colourant is a polymeric dye. It includes any special process that employs such high-molecular-weight dyes to achieve coloration or patterning of the material.
[D06P 3/407] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/407 covers a specialized step in dyeing or printing where non-azo dyes are prepared directly on the material being treated. [D06P 3/408] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/408 covers the use of reactive dyes in special dyeing or printing processes applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, distinguishing the method according to the material being treated. [D06P 3/42] using dispersed dyestuffs -- D06P 3/42 relates to methods that use dispersed dyestuffs-particulate colorants suspended in a carrier-to dye or print textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric substances. It is classified under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles within the CPC hierarchy.
[D06P 3/44] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- The subclass D06P 3/44 covers methods for synthesizing azo dyes directly on the textile substrate or other treated material. It includes in-situ formation of the chromophore by coupling diazonium components with coupling components while the material is in place, allowing coloration without prior isolation of the dye.
[D06P 3/46] Cellulose triacetate
[D06P 3/461] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/461 covers special dyeing processes that employ acid dyes, which are water-soluble dyes applied from acidic solutions to substrates such as cellulose fibers, wool, silk, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials. The classification focuses on the method of using these dyes rather than the specific textile or product formed.
[D06P 3/462] using basic dyes -- D06P 3/462 refers to processes that use basic (cationic) dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, detailing the specific steps, conditions or auxiliaries employed when applying such dyes during dyeing or printing operations.
[D06P 3/463] using vat or sulfur dyes -- This subclass covers methods that apply vat or sulfur dyes to textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials. The processes involve reducing the dye to a soluble form, immersing the material, and then oxidizing the dye to develop the colour. It is used for deep, fast shades on cellulose fibres and other substrates.
[D06P 3/465] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/465 relates to processes that employ metallisable or mordant dyes when dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials. It covers special dyeing or printing steps in which a metal-based or mordant compound is used to fix colour or impart metallic effects.
[D06P 3/466] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/466 relates to special dyeing or printing processes that employ polymeric dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, emphasizing the application technique rather than the dye chemistry. It is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy under TEXTILES → TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE → DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES.
[D06P 3/467] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/467 covers the preparation of non-azo dyes directly on the substrate being treated, such as fabric, leather, fur or solid macromolecular materials. It includes methods where the dye precursor is applied to the material and then chemically transformed in situ to a colored, non-azo compound, enabling coloration without using azo-based pigments.
[D06P 3/468] using reactive dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/468 covers textile or leather dyeing processes that employ reactive dyes, which form covalent bonds with the substrate to achieve high colour fastness. It includes methods for applying these dyes to fibers, fabrics, furs or solid macromolecular materials in any form.
[D06P 3/48] using dispersed dyestuffs -- D06P 3/48 covers the use of dispersed dyestuffs in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs and solid polymeric materials. It relates to methods where pigment particles are dispersed in a carrier and applied to the substrate, often under high-temperature or high-pressure conditions to achieve coloration.
[D06P 3/50] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- It concerns methods in which azo dyes are synthesized directly on the substrate during dyeing rather than being pre-formed and later applied. The entry applies to textiles, leather, furs and other solid macromolecular materials, focusing on in-situ preparation of azo colourants on the material being treated.
[D06P 3/52] Polyesters
[D06P 3/521] using acid dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/521 covers processes that employ acid dyes to colour fibres such as wool, silk, nylon or other protein or polyamide materials, including the steps of dye uptake, fixation and washing. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/522] using basic dyes -- D06P 3/522 covers special dyeing or printing processes in which basic (cationic) dyes are applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It includes methods that exploit the affinity of these positively charged dyes for negatively charged fibers such as wool, silk or certain synthetics, detailing the preparation, fixation and washing steps specific to basic dye usage.
[D06P 3/523] using vat or sulfur dyes -- This subclass covers processes in which textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances are coloured by applying vat or sulfur dyes, which are water-insoluble pigments that must be chemically reduced or otherwise treated to become soluble before they are applied and then oxidised to develop the final hue on the material.
[D06P 3/525] using metallisable or mordant dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/525 covers special dyeing or printing processes that employ metallisable dyes or mordant dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, specifying the use of these dye types to achieve coloration or functional effects in the treated material.
[D06P 3/526] using polymeric dyes -- This subclass covers processes where polymeric dyes are employed to impart colour to textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It includes methods of applying, fixing, or developing colour using high-molecular-weight dye compounds, distinguishing them from traditional low-molecular-weight dyes.
[D06P 3/527] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- The code D06P 3/527 covers methods for applying dyes that do not contain azo groups directly to textiles, leather, furs or other polymeric substrates. It includes steps for preparing the dye on the material surface, such as in-situ synthesis or impregnation, aimed at achieving uniform coloration without pre-formed azo compounds.
[D06P 3/528] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/528 covers textile or related material dyeing processes that employ reactive dyes, which form covalent bonds with the substrate to achieve fast colour. The subclass includes methods for applying such dyes to fabrics, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular substances, distinguishing them from other dyeing techniques.
[D06P 3/54] using dispersed dyestuffs -- This subgroup covers methods for dyeing or printing textiles where the colour is applied using dispersed dyestuffs, typically pigment-based dyes that are insoluble in the carrier medium and dispersed as fine particles. It includes processes for applying such dyes to fabrics, leather, furs, or polymeric substrates.
[D06P 3/56] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- The subclass D06P 3/56 covers processes in which azo dyes are formed directly on the substrate during dyeing or printing, including the in-situ synthesis of the azo compound on textiles, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular materials.
[D06P 3/58] Material containing hydroxyl groups -- D06P 3/58 covers special dyeing or printing processes applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that contain hydroxyl groups, such as cellulose-based fibers, polyesters, polyurethanes and other polymers with -OH functionality. The classification groups methods that exploit these hydroxyl-rich materials for colouration, including pretreatment, fixation and post-treatment steps tailored to the chemistry of the -OH groups.
[D06P 3/60] Natural or regenerated cellulose -- The D06P 3/60 subclass groups dyeing or printing processes that are specifically applied to textiles or leathers composed of natural or regenerated cellulose, such as cotton, linen, viscose or rayon. It includes any special treatment steps, formulations or equipment used to colour or pattern these cellulose-based materials.
[D06P 3/6008] using acid dyes -- This subclass covers processes that use acid dyes to colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, typically applying the dyes from aqueous solutions under acidic conditions. It belongs to the group of special processes of dyeing or printing textiles in the CPC hierarchy.
[D06P 3/6016] using basic dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/6016 covers special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ basic (cationic) dyes, which bind to negatively charged sites on the material. It is part of the hierarchy for treatment of textiles, specifically dyeing or printing, classified by the material treated.
[D06P 3/6025] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/6025 covers the use of vat or sulfur dyes for dyeing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It is classified under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles within the broader hierarchy of textile treatment.
[D06P 3/6033] using dispersed dyes -- This subclass covers processes that dye or print textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances by using dispersed dyes, which are water-insoluble organic pigments typically applied to synthetic fibers. It includes any special method where the dye is applied in a finely divided, suspended form.
[D06P 3/6041] using specified dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/6041 covers textile, leather, fur or solid polymer dyeing processes that employ particular, pre-selected dyes. It specifies the use of defined dye families or individual dyes as the operative element of the treatment, rather than generic dyeing steps, and is applied when the choice of dye itself is a distinguishing technical feature.
[D06P 3/605] dyeing with polymeric dyes; building polymeric dyes on fibre  dyes  per se   C09B -- D06P 3/605 covers textile dyeing methods that employ polymeric dyes, including the formation of the dye polymer directly on the fibre or the use of pre-formed polymeric colourants. The subclass addresses processes where the polymer chain is built in situ on the substrate or applied as a macromolecular dye, extending the C09B category of polymeric dyes to practical textile treatment.
[D06P 3/6058] by using dyes with polymerisable groups, e.g. dye ---CH=CH2 -- D06P 3/6058 covers special dye-or printing processes in which the colourant bears polymerisable groups, such as a terminal -CH=CH₂, allowing the dye to co-polymerise with the substrate or a binder during treatment. This enables covalent fixation of the colour to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials, improving wash fastness and durability. It is a sub-group of the CPC class for dyeing or printing textiles by material-specific methods.
[D06P 3/6066] by using reactive polyfunctional compounds, e.g. crosslinkers -- A special dyeing or printing method in which reactive polyfunctional compounds, such as crosslinkers, are applied to the textile or leather substrate. The compounds react with the material and the colourant to form covalent bonds, enhancing colour fixation, wash fastness and durability. This process is classified under D06P 3/6066 for treatments using such reactive agents.
[D06P 3/6075] non-heterocyclic compounds -- The code D06P 3/6075 covers dyeing or printing processes that use non-heterocyclic compounds, i.e., organic substances without hetero-atoms in a ring structure. It includes dyes, pigments and auxiliaries based on aliphatic, aromatic or polymeric molecules applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials, classified according to the material treated.
[D06P 3/6083] heterocyclic compounds -- D06P 3/6083 refers to heterocyclic compounds employed in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, classified according to the material being treated. The entry lies within the CPC hierarchy under Textiles → Treatment of textiles or the like → Dyeing or printing textiles. It groups patents that use heterocyclic chemicals as dyes, auxiliaries or mordants in these specialized finishing operations.
[D06P 3/6091] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- The subclass D06P 3/6091 covers processes for preparing non-azo dyes directly on the material being treated, such as fabrics, leather or polymers, during dyeing or printing operations. It includes techniques that form the colourant in situ on the substrate rather than applying a pre-formed azo dye, and is classified under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/62] using direct dyes -- This subclass covers processes that colour textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances by applying direct dyes, which are water-soluble colourants that bond to the material without a separate fixing agent. It includes the methods of application, fixation and any post-treatment steps used to develop the colour.
[D06P 3/64] using mordant dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/64 covers textile dyeing or printing processes that employ mordant dyes, where a metallic or inorganic compound is first applied to the fabric to fix subsequent organic dyes. It includes methods for treating textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances using such pre-treatment steps.
[D06P 3/66] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/66 covers the use of reactive dyes in dyeing or printing processes, where the dye forms a covalent bond with the substrate such as textile fibers, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials. It is a special process within the broader category of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/663] reactive group directly attached to heterocyclic group -- This subclass covers textile dyeing or printing processes that employ chemicals in which a reactive functionality is bound directly to a heterocyclic ring, enabling covalent bonding to fibers such as cellulose, wool or synthetic polymers. It includes methods and compositions where the reactive group is attached without an intervening linker, facilitating efficient fixation of colourants during treatment.
[D06P 3/666] reactive group not directly attached to heterocyclic group -- D06P 3/666 covers special dyeing or printing processes in which a reactive functional group is used but is not directly bonded to a heterocyclic ring. The classification applies to treatments of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances where the reactive moiety is separated from any heterocycle, enabling alternative fixation or coloration mechanisms. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for special processes of dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 3/68] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- The D06P 3/68 subclass covers processes in which azo dyes are formed directly on the material being treated, such as fabric, leather, fur or other polymeric substrates, rather than being prepared separately and then applied. It includes methods where the diazotisation and coupling steps occur in situ on the textile or related material to achieve coloration.
[D06P 3/685] one or both components having fibre-reactive group -- D06P 3/685 covers dyeing or printing processes in which the dye, the textile component, or both contain a fibre-reactive group that can form covalent bonds with the substrate. This special-process subclass is used for permanent fixation of colour on fibres, leather, fur or solid macromolecular materials by chemical reaction rather than merely adsorption.
[D06P 3/70] Material containing nitrile groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of fibrous materials containing nitrile groups, like acrylic fibres.
[D06P 3/701] using vat or sulfur dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/701 covers special dyeing processes in which vat or sulfur dyes are employed on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It belongs to the hierarchy TEXTILES → TREATMENT OF TEXTILES OR THE LIKE → DYEING OR PRINTING TEXTILES.
[D06P 3/702] dyeing of material in the gel state -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/702 covers processes for dyeing materials that are in a gel form, such as gelatinous polymers, gels derived from textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It is a specialized branch of textile treatment that details how colourants are introduced into a gel matrix before the material solidifies.
[D06P 3/704] using metallisable or mordant dyes or dyeing by cupro-ionic method -- D06P 3/704 covers special dyeing processes that employ metallisable or mordant dyes, including the cupro-ionic method, for treating textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It is part of the CPC hierarchy for treatment of textiles, specifically dyeing or printing, and classifies these material-specific techniques.
[D06P 3/705] using polymeric dyes -- D06P 3/705 covers methods that employ polymeric dyes-high-molecular-weight colourants-when dyeing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substrates. The classification is part of the CPC section dealing with special dyeing or printing processes, distinguishing the use of polymer-bound colour molecules from conventional low-molecular dyes.
[D06P 3/707] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- This CPC subclass deals with methods for applying or formulating non-azo dyes directly onto the material being treated, such as fabrics, leather or polymer surfaces, covering steps of preparation, fixation and development of the dye on the substrate.
[D06P 3/708] using reactive dyes -- The D06P 3/708 subclass covers processes that apply reactive dyes to textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials, where the dye forms a covalent bond with the substrate during dyeing or printing. It includes the specific steps, conditions and auxiliaries used to achieve fixation of reactive dyes.
[D06P 3/72] using dispersed dyestuffs -- D06P 3/72 covers textile dyeing processes that employ dispersed dyestuffs, which are finely ground, water-insoluble pigments transferred to the fiber by high-temperature, high-pressure treatment, commonly used for synthetic fibers such as polyester.
[D06P 3/74] using acid dyes -- D06P 3/74 covers the use of acid dyes in special dyeing or printing processes applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, and is organised according to the material being treated. It includes methods that employ acid-soluble dyes for fibres such as wool, silk or nylon.
[D06P 3/76] using basic dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing or printing processes that employ basic (cationic) dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, arranged according to the material treated. It includes methods such as padding, exhaustion or other application techniques in which the basic dye is the primary coloring agent.
[D06P 3/78] Preparing azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/78 covers methods in which azo dyes are synthesized directly on the textile or other material being coloured, rather than being applied as pre-formed dye solutions. The process combines dye formation and fixation in a single step, typically involving coupling reactions on the substrate. It is used for efficient coloration of fabrics, leather, furs and polymeric substrates.
[D06P 3/79] Polyolefins -- D06P 3/79 covers the use of polyolefin substances such as polyethylene or polypropylene in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather or similar materials. It includes methods for applying dyes or inks to polyolefin fibers or films and related treatment steps.
[D06P 3/791] using acid dyes -- The subgroup D06P 3/791 covers special dye-application processes that employ acid dyes, which are water-soluble dyes requiring an acidic bath to fix on substrates such as protein-based fibers (wool, silk) and leather. It pertains to the method of using these dyes within the broader context of textile dyeing and printing.
[D06P 3/792] using basic dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/792 covers textile and related dyeing processes that employ basic (cationic) dyes, which typically bond to fibres with a negative charge such as acrylic, polyester or nylon. It includes methods of applying these dyes-exhaustion, padding, spraying, printing or other techniques-focused on achieving colouration of the material treated.
[D06P 3/793] using vat or sulfur dyes -- D06P 3/793 relates to special dyeing processes that employ vat or sulfur dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials. It covers the preparation, application and fixation of these dyes, which are water-insoluble pigments rendered soluble for coloration before being regenerated in the substrate.
[D06P 3/794] using dispersed dyes -- This subclass covers dyeing processes that use dispersed dyes, which are water-insoluble pigments finely ground and mixed in a carrier before application to synthetic fibres such as polyester, as well as to leather, furs or other macromolecular materials. It includes methods for dispersing, fixing and developing colour with this type of dye.
[D06P 3/795] using metallisable or mordant dyes, dyeing premetallised fibres -- The subclass D06P 3/795 covers textile dyeing processes that employ metallisable or mordant dyes, specifically when the fibers have been pre-metallised. It includes methods for applying metallic compounds to fabrics or leather to achieve coloration, reflective effects, or enhanced fastness.
[D06P 3/796] using polymeric dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/796 covers special dyeing or printing processes in which polymeric dyes are employed, applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It details methods that utilize these large-molecule colorants to achieve coloration, emphasizing the material-specific techniques for applying polymer-based dyes.
[D06P 3/797] preparing non-azo dyes on the material -- The code D06P 3/797 covers processes where non-azo dyes are synthesized directly on textiles, leather, furs or polymeric substrates during treatment instead of being applied as pre-formed pigments. It includes in-situ preparation of colourants as a special dyeing or printing step classified according to the material treated.
[D06P 3/798] using reactive dyes -- D06P 3/798 relates to the application of reactive dyes in special dyeing or printing processes for textiles and similar materials such as leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It covers methods where the dye forms a covalent bond with the substrate, providing high colour fastness. This subclass falls under the textile treatment hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[D06P 3/799] preparing azo dyes on the material -- D06P 3/799 covers the preparation of azo dyes directly on the material being treated, such as fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric substrates. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles where the dye is formed in-situ on the substrate rather than being applied as a pre-made dye.
[D06P 3/80] Inorganic fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing or printing of inorganic fibres, like glass.
[D06P 3/82] Textiles which contain different kinds of fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials made of blends of fibres, like blends of polyester and cotton fibres, wool and polyamide etc...with one dye or with mixtures of dyes.  This group and subgroups are not aligned on the IPC.
[D06P 3/8204] fibres of different chemical nature -- The code D06P 3/8204 designates processes for dyeing or printing textiles whose material is composed of fibres of different chemical nature, for example blends of natural and synthetic fibres, and specifies methods for treating such mixed-fiber fabrics.
[D06P 3/8209] mixtures of fibres containing amide groups -- D06P 3/8209 covers mixtures of fibres that contain amide groups, such as polyamide blends, used in textile processing. It is classified under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, organized by the material being treated.
[D06P 3/8214] mixtures of fibres containing ester and amide groups -- The CPC subclass D06P 3/8214 concerns mixtures of fibres that contain both ester and amide groups, typically polymeric fibres combined to give specific performance characteristics. It falls under special processes for dyeing or printing textiles where such blended fibre materials are treated.
[D06P 3/8219] mixtures of fibres containing hydroxyl and amide groups -- D06P 3/8219 covers the preparation and use of blends of fibers that contain both hydroxyl groups, typical of cellulose fibers, and amide groups, typical of protein fibers, for textile dyeing or printing processes. It addresses the handling, pretreatment and finishing of such mixed-fiber fabrics to achieve uniform coloration or patterning.
[D06P 3/8223] mixtures of fibres containing hydroxyl and ester groups -- This subclass includes textile articles that consist of blended fibers where at least one component carries hydroxyl groups and another carries ester groups, such as mixtures of cellulose-based fibers with polyester or ester-modified synthetics, and the related processing steps for dyeing or printing.
[D06P 3/8228] using one kind of dye -- This subclass covers methods of dyeing textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials in which a single dye is employed throughout the process. It relates to special dyeing techniques classified by the material treated, focusing on processes that do not use multiple dyes or color blends.
[D06P 3/8233] using dispersed dyes -- D06P 3/8233 covers the use of dispersed dyes in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It pertains to special dyeing processes where the dye is applied in a dispersed form, typically in aqueous or organic media, to achieve uniform colour without solubilizing the dye. This subclass is nested under textile treatment and dyeing or printing categories.
[D06P 3/8238] using different kinds of dye -- D06P 3/8238 relates to dyeing or printing processes in which two or more different dyes are applied, either sequentially or simultaneously, to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It is used to classify special methods that combine distinct dye types to achieve particular colour effects, functional properties or material characteristics.
[D06P 3/8242] using dispersed and acid dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/8242 covers textile dyeing processes that employ both dispersed dyes and acid dyes, typically for synthetic fibres or blends where each dye type targets different polymer phases. It includes methods for applying the two dye families in a single treatment step or sequentially to achieve uniform coloration or specific functional effects on the material.
[D06P 3/8247] using dispersed and vat, sulfur or indigo dyes -- The subclass D06P 3/8247 covers special dyeing processes that employ dispersed, vat, sulfur or indigo dyes on textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances. It includes methods for applying these particular dye families to achieve coloration in the specified material categories.
[D06P 3/8252] using dispersed and reactive dyes -- D06P 3/8252 covers textile dyeing methods that employ both dispersed and reactive dyes, typically on synthetic or blended fibres, to achieve uniform colour while taking advantage of the high-temperature solubility of dispersed dyes and the covalent bonding of reactive dyes. It addresses combined-dye processes that allow simultaneous fixation and colour development.
[D06P 3/8257] using dispersed and azo dyes prepared  in situ -- A process for dyeing textiles in which dispersed dyes and azo dyes are generated directly on the material during treatment, rather than being pre-manufactured, allowing colour development in situ on fibers or fabrics. This method is used for synthetic fibers and other macromolecular substances where the dye is formed and fixed in the same step.
[D06P 3/8261] mixtures of fibres containing nitrile groups -- The subclass D06P 3/8261 covers textile mixtures that incorporate fibres bearing nitrile groups, such as polyacrylonitrile or nitrile-containing filaments, either alone or combined with other natural or synthetic fibres. It includes methods and products where these nitrile-containing fibres are blended for dyeing, printing or other finishing processes.
[D06P 3/8266] mixtures of fibres containing hydroxyl and nitrile groups -- This subclass covers the preparation and use of fibre mixtures that contain polymers bearing hydroxyl groups together with polymers bearing nitrile groups, for example blends of cellulose-based fibres and polyacrylonitrile. It includes methods for combining, treating, or dyeing such mixed fibres in textile processes.
[D06P 3/8271] mixtures of fibres containing amide and nitrile groups -- D06P 3/8271 covers textile treatment methods that involve blends of fibres containing amide- and nitrile-group polymers, such as polyamides and acrylics. It includes processes for preparing, dyeing, printing or finishing these mixed-fiber fabrics, focusing on the interaction of the two polymer types during treatment.
[D06P 3/8276] mixtures of fibres containing ester groups -- This subclass covers textile treatment processes for blends of fibers that contain ester groups, such as polyester or other ester-based polymers, describing how these mixtures are dyed or printed. It falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles, classified according to the material treated.
[D06P 3/828] mixtures of fibres containing hydroxyl groups -- D06P 3/828 covers textile treatment processes that involve mixtures of fibres possessing hydroxyl groups, such as cotton, rayon, cellulose-based or other polymeric fibres that can form hydrogen bonds. The subclass addresses how these blended fibres are dyed, printed or otherwise processed, taking advantage of their reactive hydroxyl chemistry.
[D06P 3/8285] mixtures of cellulose and polyolefin fibres -- The code D06P 3/8285 refers to textile treatments involving blends of cellulose-based fibers, such as cotton or rayon, with polyolefin fibers like polypropylene or polyethylene. It covers special dyeing or printing processes applied to these mixed-material fabrics, which are classified by the type of material treated.
[D06P 3/829] mixtures of cellulose and animalized fibres -- D06P 3/829 covers methods and compositions for treating textiles that combine cellulose fibers such as cotton or viscose with animal-derived fibers like wool or silk, focusing on the preparation, dyeing or printing of these blended materials. The classification is used for processes that adapt conventional textile treatments to the unique properties of mixed natural fibers.
[D06P 3/8295] mixtures of fibres containing nitrile and ester groups
[D06P 3/854] containing modified or unmodified fibres, -- D06P 3/854 designates processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances in which the material contains either modified or unmodified fibers. The subclass falls under special processes of dyeing or printing textiles and addresses how the presence of such fibers influences the treatment.
[D06P 5/00] Other features in dyeing or printing textiles, or dyeing leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances in any form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All the dyeing or printing aspects which are not covered by the previous groups, e.g. after-treatments of dyed materials, special aspects or processes for printing, physical treatments affecting dyeing, treatments modifying dye affinity.
[D06P 5/001] Special chemical aspects of printing textile materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only the special chemical aspects of printing of textile materials which are not covered by any other groups, e.g. printing pastes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special chemical aspects of transfer printing -> D06P5/003 - D06P5/009 - Special chemical aspects of ink-jet printing -> D06P5/30
[D06P 5/002] Locally enhancing dye affinity of a textile material by chemical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All the chemical treatments, locally or on the whole surface to enhance the dye affinity of the textile material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Physical treatments to enhance dye affinity -> D06P5/20
[D06P 5/003] Transfer printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Textile materials dyed by transfer printing, the pre-treatment of textile materials to make them suitable for transfer printing, materials used for transfer printing of textiles like transfer sheets and processes for the transfer printing of textile materials.
[D06P 5/004] using subliming dyes -- D06P 5/004 concerns processes that apply dyes by sublimation, where a solid dye is vaporised and transferred directly onto textiles, leather, furs or other macromolecular materials, producing a permanent colour without a liquid medium. This method is used for dyeing or printing fabrics and related substrates.
[D06P 5/005] on resin-treated fibres -- This CPC subclass covers processes or methods concerning the application of resin treatments to fibres, used to modify fibre properties before dyeing or printing, including impregnation, coating or bonding of synthetic or natural fibres with polymeric resins. It relates to textile treatment steps that improve affinity, durability or functional performance.
[D06P 5/006] using specified dyes -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/006 covers methods of dyeing or printing textiles that employ particular dyes, including specific dye types, concentrations or application conditions. It falls under the broader categories of textiles, treatment of textiles and dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 5/007] using non-subliming dyes -- This CPC subclass D06P 5/007 covers processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances that employ dyes which do not sublimate during application. It includes any method where the dye is applied in a non-volatile form, such as aqueous or solvent-based systems.
[D06P 5/008] Migrating dyes -- Migrating dyes are colorants that transfer from a carrier medium onto textile fibers during dyeing or printing, typically through diffusion or sublimation, and include classes such as disperse, vat, and some plasma-treated dyes. This feature is covered in the CPC code D06P 5/008 under textiles-treatment-dyeing or printing.
[D06P 5/009] Non-migrating dyes -- The D06P 5/009 subclass concerns non-migrating dyes used in the dyeing or printing of textiles and related materials. These dyes are formulated to stay fixed on fibers, preventing colour bleed during laundering or exposure to water. The class includes dyes for fabrics, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances that do not transfer from the dyed substrate.
[D06P 5/02] After-treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All the after-treatments of dyed or printed textile materials to modify their properties, e.g. to soften them, to enhance their fastness to wash, light, sweat, etc...or to remove unfixed dyes.
[D06P 5/04] with organic compounds -- This CPC subclass covers dyeing or printing operations in which organic compounds are employed as the primary coloring agents, mordants, solvents or auxiliary chemicals. The scope includes treating textile materials, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances in any form using such organic substances.
[D06P 5/06] containing nitrogen -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/06 covers features in textile dyeing or printing processes that involve nitrogen-containing substances, such as dyes, pigments, or auxiliaries derived from or bearing nitrogen atoms. It includes methods and formulations where nitrogen groups play a functional role in coloration or fixation.
[D06P 5/08] macromolecular -- This subclass addresses the use of macromolecular compounds such as polymers, resins, or high-molecular-weight dyes in the dyeing or printing of textiles, leather, furs, or solid macromolecular materials. It covers methods, formulations, and treatments where the colorant is a macromolecule rather than a low-molecular-weight dye.
[D06P 5/10] with compounds containing metal -- This subgroup covers dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials that employ metal-containing compounds such as metal-complex dyes, mordants or metal-based auxiliaries to achieve colouration or fixation. It includes methods where the metal entity forms part of the dye molecule or acts as a catalyst in the treatment.
[D06P 5/12] Reserving parts of the material before dyeing or printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The partial treatment with chemical compounds of textile materials to locally decrease their dye affinity, the application of printing pastes to prevent dyes from accessing the fibre.
