The physical hindrance for dyes to access the fibres like making knots on fabrics.  These treatments are carried out before dyeing to produce completely white or lighter areas on the dyed textile materials.
[D06P 5/13] Fugitive dyeing or stripping dyes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The bleaching / decolouration or removal of dyes of dyed/printed textile materials.  The fugitive dyeing of textile materials, i.e. temporary dyeing of textile materials with dyes which are not fast or are unstable in normal conditions.
[D06P 5/131] with acids or bases -- This subclass deals with dyeing or printing processes that employ acids or bases to treat textiles, leather, furs or solid-polymeric substances, usually for pH adjustment, mordanting, or fixing of colourants.
[D06P 5/132] with oxidants -- The D06P 5/132 subclass defines dyeing or printing procedures that incorporate oxidizing agents, covering the use of oxidants to develop, fix or modify colors on textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials during treatment. It specifies that the oxidant is a key step in the coloration process.
[D06P 5/134] with reductants -- The D06P 5/134 subclass covers textile or leather dyeing and printing processes that employ reductants, such as reducing agents used to convert insoluble vat dyes into soluble forms for fixation on the material. It includes methods, apparatus and chemical formulations where the reductant is a key step in achieving colour development on fabrics or related macromolecular substrates.
[D06P 5/135] with combined acids or bases + oxidants + reductants means -- This subgroup covers dyeing or printing operations in which a mixture of acids or bases is applied together with both an oxidizing agent and a reducing agent in the same treatment step. The method is used for colouring textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric substances.
[D06P 5/137] with other compounds -- This subclass covers processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that incorporate additional chemical agents beyond the primary dye or pigment. It includes the use of auxiliaries, mordants, carriers, or other compounds to modify or enhance coloration.
[D06P 5/138] fugitive dyeing -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/138, titled fugitive dyeing, relates to methods of applying dyes that are intentionally temporary or easily removed from textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, typically for short-term coloration, testing or pattern creation.
[D06P 5/15] Locally discharging the dyes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The discharge printing of textile materials, partial discolouration, the uncomplete removal of dye from dyed or printed textile materials, the producing of stone washed effects or the ageing of dyed textile materials.
[D06P 5/151] with acids or bases -- D06P 5/151 covers dyeing or printing textile materials, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances where acids or bases are employed as reagents to fix, develop or modify the colorants. The classification includes any treatment in which the pH of the bath is adjusted by adding acids or bases to achieve the desired hue or fixation.
[D06P 5/153] with oxidants -- D06P 5/153 classifies methods for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or other solid macromolecular substances in which an oxidant is employed to develop or fix the colour. The oxidising agent may be applied before, during or after the colouring step to achieve the desired hue or improve fastness.
[D06P 5/155] with reductants -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/155 covers dyeing or printing processes for textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that employ reducing agents. It includes any method where a reductant is used to develop or fix colour, modify dye uptake, or alter the chemical state of the substrate during treatment.
[D06P 5/156] with combined A+B+C means -- This subgroup deals with dyeing or printing processes in which the treatment employs a combined A+B+C system, a mixture of three complementary components such as a fixing agent, a catalyst or a mordant used together to achieve the desired colour-fastness.
[D06P 5/158] with other compounds -- It covers dyeing or printing processes where additional compounds beyond the primary colorants are employed, such as mordants, auxiliaries or other chemicals. The classification applies to textiles, leather, furs and solid macromolecular substances in any form.
[D06P 5/17] Azo dyes -- Azo dyes are organic compounds containing one or more azo (-N=N-) groups that serve as coloring agents in textile, leather, fur and polymer dyeing and printing. Their structure allows vivid hues and good fastness, and they are widely used for cotton, wool, synthetic fibers and other macromolecular substrates. The classification covers ancillary aspects such as auxiliary chemicals, processing conditions and post-treatment related to azo-based coloration.
[D06P 5/20] Physical treatments affecting dyeing, e.g. ultrasonic or electric -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All the physical treatments affecting dyeing which are carried out before, during of after dyeing to modify dyeing affinity, to fix the dyes, to remove the dye or to help in the dyeing process.
[D06P 5/2005] Treatments with alpha, beta, gamma or other rays, e.g. stimulated rays -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of textile materials with e.g. ionizing radiations, gamma rays, ultraviolet, laser, infrared, at any stage of the dyeing process.
[D06P 5/2011] Application of vibrations, pulses or waves for non-thermic purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment with waves, e.g. microwaves for non thermic purposes or application of ultrasonic radiations.
[D06P 5/2016] Application of electric energy -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The application of electric energy to the textile material istelf, like a plasma treatment of the textile material or application of electric energy to the dye bath, like electrolysis of some components of the dye bath.
[D06P 5/2022] Textile treatments at reduced pression, i.e. lower than 1 atm -- The code D06P 5/2022 covers textile, leather, fur or macromolecular substrate treatments performed at reduced pressure, meaning at pressures below atmospheric level (under 1 atm). It includes any dyeing, printing or related processing steps that exploit sub-atmospheric conditions to affect penetration, fixation or coloration, and is a specialized sub-class within the broader category for other features in dyeing or printing textiles.
[D06P 5/2027] before dyeing -- The D06P 5/2027 subclass covers processes applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials before the actual dyeing step, such as scouring, bleaching, desizing, pretreating, impregnating or other preparatory treatments that condition the substrate for subsequent coloration.
[D06P 5/2033] during dyeing -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/2033 covers any specific features, steps, or additives that are applied during the dyeing stage of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, such as methods, conditions, or auxiliaries used while the material is being coloured.
[D06P 5/2038] after dyeing -- The subgroup D06P 5/2038 covers operations carried out after the dyeing of textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances, such as fixing, washing, rinsing, drying, or applying post-dyeing finishes that improve colour fastness, remove unfixed dye or add protective treatments.
[D06P 5/2044] Textile treatments at a pression higher than 1 atm -- D06P 5/2044 covers methods of treating textiles at pressures greater than one atmosphere, including dyeing, printing, and related processes for leather, furs, or solid macromolecular substances. It falls within the hierarchy of textile treatment, specifically the high-pressure segment of dyeing or printing.
[D06P 5/205] before dyeing -- The code D06P 5/205 covers processes carried out before dyeing, such as pretreatment steps that ready fibers, yarns, fabrics, leather, furs or polymeric substances for later coloration. It includes mechanical, chemical or physical operations like cleaning, scouring, bleaching or applying mordants to improve dye uptake.
[D06P 5/2055] during dyeing -- The D06P 5/2055 subgroup concerns actions carried out while the material is being dyed, including the addition of auxiliaries, adjustment of process conditions, or any treatment applied to the textile, leather, fur or polymer substrate during dye uptake.
[D06P 5/2061] after dyeing -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/2061 covers processes applied to textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular materials after the dyeing step, including finishing, fixing, washing, drying, treatment to improve colour fastness, or any other post-dyeing operations that modify the dyed substrate.
[D06P 5/2066] Thermic treatments of textile materials -- Thermic treatments of textile materials cover heat-based processes applied to fabrics, yarns, leather, fur, or solid polymeric substances to alter physical or chemical properties such as colour, dimensional stability, texture, or durability. Methods include steaming, ironing, heat setting, drying, curing, and related temperature-controlled operations performed before, during, or after dyeing or printing.
[D06P 5/2072] before dyeing -- D06P 5/2072 covers processes applied to textiles before dyeing, such as pre-treatment steps that prepare the material for coloration, including cleaning, scouring, bleaching and chemical conditioning.
[D06P 5/2077] after dyeing -- This CPC subclass covers any operations performed on textiles, leather, furs or solid polymeric materials after the dyeing step, including fixing, washing, conditioning, finishing or other post-dyeing treatments that modify the dyed product.
[D06P 5/2083] heating with IR or microwaves -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/2083 covers the use of infrared or microwave energy to heat textiles, leather, furs or polymeric materials during dyeing or printing operations. It includes methods and apparatus that apply such radiant heating to improve fixation or color development.
[D06P 5/2088] by fluid-bed techniques -- D06P 5/2088 designates processes for dyeing or printing textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substances that use fluid-bed techniques, in which the material is treated by contact with a fluidised stream of fine particles carrying the dye or pigment.
[D06P 5/2094] by molten masses -- The CPC subclass D06P 5/2094 covers dyeing or printing methods that apply colourants in a molten state, such as melted dyes, pigments or resins, onto textiles, leather, furs or solid macromolecular substrates. It pertains to using a liquid-like mass of the material to achieve coloration rather than dispersions or solutions.
[D06P 5/22] Effecting variation of dye affinity on textile material by chemical means that react with the fibre -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The treatment of textile materials with chemical compounds that react with the fibre, i.e. which are forming a chemical bonding with the fibre, to increase or improve the dye affinity of the textile material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The treatment of textile materials to locally or completely decrease their dye affinity -> D06P5/12 - Physical treatment to increase dye affinity or dye uptake -> D06P5/20 - Treatment to improve hydrophilic properties -> D06M
[D06P 5/225] Aminalization of cellulose; introducing aminogroups into cellulose -- The entry covers chemical reactions that convert cellulose into aminal derivatives by attaching amino-containing groups to its polymer chains. These modifications create new functional sites that enhance dye uptake, improve affinity for pigments, or enable further chemical processing of the fiber. The methods include reacting cellulose with aldehydes and amines or related reagents to form stable aminal linkages.
[D06P 5/30] Ink jet printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[D06P 7/00] Dyeing or printing processes combined with mechanical treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The dyeing of textile materials combined with mechanical treatment like stretching, drawing, twisting, pressing, calendering, embossing etc.  The stone-washing of dyed textile materials when it is carried out in presence of pumice stones or any other abrading material.
[D06P 7/005] Dyeing combined with texturising or drawing treatments -- The D06P 7/005 subclass covers processes in which textiles are dyed while simultaneously undergoing texturising or drawing operations that mechanically deform, stretch, or otherwise alter the fabric's surface or dimensions. It integrates coloration with mechanical finishing steps to achieve combined aesthetic and structural effects in a single treatment.
[D06Q] DECORATING TEXTILES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The decoration of textile materials by chemical action or by the fixation of particulate on the textile surface.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Textile threads, filaments, yarns or tow, glued on macromolecular material -> D06N7/00
[D06Q 1/00] Decorating textiles -- D06Q 1/00 covers methods and apparatus for applying decorative designs, colors or patterns to textile materials. This includes printing, dyeing, coating, embossing, or other surface treatments that impart aesthetic effects to fabrics, garments or other textile products.
[D06Q 1/005] Producing embroidered textiles by chemical means; Transferring embroidered products to textiles  embroidered products  D05C17/00 -- The CPC subclass D06Q 1/005 covers techniques for creating embroidered patterns on fabric using chemical processes instead of mechanical stitching, as well as methods for transferring pre-made embroidered pieces onto textiles. It includes the use of adhesives, coatings or other chemical treatments to fix or embed the embroidery onto the base material.
[D06Q 1/02] Producing patterns by locally destroying or modifying the fibres of a web by chemical actions, e.g. making translucent -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The swelling or the partial dissolution or melting of fibres to make patterns, the treatment of fibres by chemical means to make them translucent, the production of so called "lace" by chemical means.  All the treatment are chemical treatments.
[D06Q 1/04] by metallising -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The metallisation of textile materials, only for decorative purposes.
[D06Q 1/06] by local treatment of pile fabrics with chemical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The decoration of pile fabrics like carpets or sponge towels by locally treating them with chemical compositions.
[D06Q 1/08] by fixation of mechanical effects, e.g. calendering, embossing or Chintz effects, using chemical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The decoration of textile materials by fixation of mechanical effects by chemical means.
[D06Q 1/10] by treatment with, or fixation of, a particulate material, e.g. mica, glass beads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The fixation of decorative particulate materials like glass beads, glass particles, gem stones , glitters, plastic particles, feathers, mica, fibres etc for decorative purposes generally with the aid of an adhesive composition but not necessary.  The flocking of textile materials when flock fibres are directly deposited onto the surface of the textile materials.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The local deposition of pigment with a binder, i.e. pigment printing -> D06P1/44 - The treatment of textile materials with stone materials or sand to discolour them i.e. to create a stone washed effect -> D06P5/15 and D06P7/00
[D06Q 1/12] by transferring a chemical agent or a metallic or non-metallic material in particulate or other form, from a solid temporary carrier to the textile -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The transfer of the decorative material or the transfer of the adhesive composition to the textile.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The transfer printing of textile materials -> D06P5/003
[D06Q 1/14] by transferring fibres, or adhesives for fibres, to the textile -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The transfer of fibres or adhesives for fibres to the textile, e.g. the flocking of textile when the flock or the adhesive are first deposited on an intermediate support before their application to the textile surface.
[D07] ROPES -- D07 covers ropes, cords and twines made from natural or synthetic fibres, including their manufacturing processes, treatments and applications such as lifting, tensioning or tying. It also includes related accessories like splices, terminations and woven or braided structures.
[D07B] ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ropes and cables and the associated machines and methods for their manufacturing. The main focus of ropes and cables must be to bear tensile loads.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shoe laces -> A43C1/00 , A43C9/00 - Belts -> B66B7/062 , F16G1/12 - Roundsling -> B66C1/12 - Yarns and threads -> D02G3/00 - Cables for bridge construction filled with concrete -> E01D19/16 - Hoses -> F16L11/00 - Electrical cables for electricity transport (except if several wires do not serve to conduct electricity but are designed to cope with tensile load D07B1/147 ) -> H01B - Umbilicals -> H01B7/046 , H01B7/0072 , F16L9/00 , F16L11/00 , E21B  **Glossary:** - Ropes and cables: F lexible elongate entities manufactured respectively from fibres/filaments and wires. They can only cope with tensile loads. They cannot bear shear, compressive or torsion forces. Cables may be strand like or rope like. A cable has normally the following structure: wire - strand - rope/cable.Fibrous ropes are always rope like. A fibrous rope has normally the following structure: filament / textile yarn / rope yarn - strand - rope. Fibrous ropes often have a construction different from a laid rope, e.g. a parallel yarn rope, a Kernmantle rope or a braided rope.
[D07B 1/00] Constructional features of ropes or cables -- **Definition:** This place covers:  constructional features of ropes and cables.
[D07B 1/005] Composite ropes, i.e. ropes built-up from fibrous or filamentary material and metal wires -- **Definition:** This place covers:  ropes built-up from fibrous or filamentary material and metal in which both have a tensile strength functionality.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ropes built-up from fibrous or filamentary material and metal in which only one of them has a tensile strength functionality -> D07B1/0686  **Glossary:** - Built-up: the material providing tensile strength, normally within a strand
[D07B 1/02] Ropes built-up from fibrous or filamentary material, e.g. of vegetable origin, of animal origin, regenerated cellulose, plastics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  only artificial filamentary material including treated natural materials (e.g. viscose).  **Glossary:** - Fibrous material: considered to have a limited length whereas filamentary material has an indefinite length.
[D07B 1/025] comprising high modulus, or high tenacity, polymer filaments or fibres, e.g. liquid-crystal polymers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ropes built-up from glass fibres -> D07B1/02 with the additional Indexing Code D07B2205/3003 - Opes built-up from carbon fibres -> D07B1/02 with the additional Indexing Code D07B2205/3007
[D07B 1/04] with a core of fibres or filaments arranged parallel to the centre line -- The D07B 1/04 subclass covers ropes or cables whose core consists of fibres or filaments that run parallel to the centre line of the rope. This arrangement defines the structural makeup of the core, distinguishing it from twisted or braided cores, and is relevant to the constructional features of textile ropes.
[D07B 1/06] Ropes or cables built-up from metal wires, e.g. of section wires around a hemp core -- This CPC subclass covers ropes or cables built by winding metal wires around a hemp core, combining metal and natural fibers for added strength. It falls under the textiles hierarchy, specifically ropes, and addresses the constructional features of such composite ropes.
[D07B 1/0606] Reinforcing cords for rubber or plastic articles -- D07B 1/0606 covers reinforcing cords made of rope or cable elements that are incorporated into rubber or plastic products to improve strength, durability or dimensional stability. It includes cords of natural, synthetic or blended fibers that are embedded or wound into items such as tires, hoses, belts or molded components, focusing on the specific function of reinforcement rather than the rope's basic construction.
[D07B 1/0613] the reinforcing cords being characterised by the rope configuration -- The subclass D07B 1/0613 covers reinforcing cords whose design is defined by the rope configuration, such as laid, braided or twisted forms that give the cord its structural properties. It falls under constructional features of ropes or cables within the textiles-ropes hierarchy.
[D07B 1/062] the reinforcing cords being characterised by the strand configuration -- This CPC subclass D07B 1/062 belongs to the textiles group, under ropes and constructional features of ropes or cables. It covers reinforcing cords whose defining characteristic is the configuration of the strands that form the cord, focusing on how the strands are arranged or twisted. The code is applied to patents describing strand-arrangement details in rope reinforcement.
[D07B 1/0626] the reinforcing cords consisting of three core wires or filaments and at least one layer of outer wires or filaments, i.e. a 3+N configuration -- The subclass D07B 1/0626 concerns reinforcing cords for ropes or cables that comprise three core wires or filaments surrounded by at least one additional layer of outer wires or filaments, described as a 3+N configuration. It falls under constructional features of ropes or cables in the textile hierarchy.
[D07B 1/0633] having a multiple-layer configuration -- The D07B 1/0633 subclass covers ropes or cables that are constructed with a multiple-layer configuration, where several concentric layers of material are combined to enhance strength, flexibility, or protection. It falls under the broader textiles hierarchy, specifically within ropes, then ropes or cables in general, and finally constructional features of ropes or cables.
[D07B 1/064] the reinforcing cords being twisted and with at least one wire exchanging place with another wire -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose reinforcing cords are twisted together and, during the twisting, at least one wire changes its position relative to another wire, producing an interlaced or braided arrangement within the rope structure.
[D07B 1/0646] comprising longitudinally preformed wires -- The subclass D07B 1/0646 covers ropes or cables that are made with wires that have been longitudinally pre-formed before assembly, a constructional feature that influences the rope's strength, flexibility and handling. It is nested under textiles, ropes and general ropes or cables.
[D07B 1/0653] in the core -- The code D07B 1/0653 covers constructional features that are situated in the core of ropes or cables, meaning any element located in the central part of the strand such as fillers, inner coverings, or the material that forms the rope's backbone. It applies to designs where the core's composition or structure contributes to the overall performance or handling of the rope or cable.
[D07B 1/066] the wires being made from special alloy or special steel composition -- This CPC subclass covers constructional features of ropes or cables in which the individual wires are formed from special alloy or specially formulated steel compositions. It is indexed under D07B 1/066 within the textiles-ropes-ropes or cables in general hierarchy.
[D07B 1/0666] the wires being characterised by an anti-corrosive or adhesion promoting coating -- This CPC subclass (D07B 1/0666) covers constructional features of ropes or cables in which the individual wires are treated with a coating that prevents corrosion or promotes adhesion to other materials, such as adhesives or polymer matrices. It relates to the textile category of ropes and cables in general.
[D07B 1/0673] having a rope configuration -- The CPC subclass D07B 1/0673, classified under textiles, ropes, ropes or cables in general, and constructional features of ropes or cables, covers specifications of a rope's shape or arrangement, such as its lay, braid, or twisted form as disclosed in patent documents.
[D07B 1/068] characterised by the strand design -- The D07B 1/068 subclass covers ropes or cables whose distinguishing feature is the design of their strands, including specific arrangements such as parallel, twisted, braided or other geometric patterns that influence strength, flexibility or handling. It falls under constructional features of ropes within the broader textile and rope categories.
[D07B 1/0686] characterised by the core design -- The CPC subclass D07B 1/0686 refers to ropes or cables whose core is defined by a specific design or configuration, such as a particular twist, braid, material composition or geometric arrangement. This core design influences the overall mechanical properties and performance of the rope, distinguishing it from other constructional variants.
[D07B 1/0693] having a strand configuration -- The code D07B 1/0693 covers constructional features of ropes or cables that relate specifically to the strand configuration. It classifies ropes whose strands are arranged in a particular way, such as twisted, braided, parallel or with special geometric patterns, distinguishing them from other rope designs. This entry falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes and ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 1/08] the layers of which are formed of profiled interlocking wires, i.e. the strands forming concentric layers -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose construction features concentric layers made of profiled interlocking wire strands that lock together to form the rope. The design provides enhanced mechanical stability and load distribution by using shaped wires that engage each other in each layer.
[D07B 1/10] with a core of wires arranged parallel to the centre line -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose core consists of individual wires placed side-by-side parallel to the rope's longitudinal axis. It falls under the textile group for ropes, focusing on constructional features of the core arrangement.
[D07B 1/12] Ropes or cables with a hollow core -- In the CPC hierarchy under Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general → Constructional features of ropes or cables, entry D07B 1/12 pertains to ropes or cables that incorporate a hollow core, a design used to reduce weight or permit the passage of other materials.
[D07B 1/14] Ropes or cables with incorporated auxiliary elements, e.g. for marking, extending throughout the length of the rope or cable -- The D07B 1/14 subclass covers ropes or cables that incorporate auxiliary elements extending along their entire length, such as markers, identification strips, or other functional inserts. It pertains to constructional features of ropes or cables that integrate these components for purposes like visibility, reinforcement, or added utility.
[D07B 1/141] comprising liquid, pasty or powder agents, e.g. lubricants or anti-corrosive oils or greases -- D07B 1/141 covers ropes or cables that are manufactured with liquid, pasty or powder substances such as lubricants or anti-corrosive oils and greases to improve performance or durability. It falls under the textiles class, specifically the constructional features of ropes or cables.
[D07B 1/142] for ropes or rope components built-up from fibrous or filamentary material -- This subclass covers ropes and rope components that are assembled from fibrous-or-filamentary material, such as twisted, braided or plaited strands, rather than from solid or monolithic elements. It includes the constructional features and manufacturing steps specific to building up the rope from individual fibers or filaments.
[D07B 1/144] for cables or cable components built-up from metal wires -- D07B 1/144 classifies constructional features of ropes or cables that are assembled from metal wires, covering the way wire strands, cores and lay are combined to form cable components. The subclass is used for patents describing the structure, geometry and assembly methods of wire-built cables and their parts.
[D07B 1/145] comprising elements for indicating or detecting the rope or cable status -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. elements for indicating or detecting wear or elongation by raising temperature.
[D07B 1/147] comprising electric conductors or elements for information transfer  D07B1/145  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  electric conductors or elements for signal and information transfer in general from one end to the other end.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - ropes or cables comprising elements for indicating or detecting the rope or cable status e.g. electric wire to detect breakage within the rope -> D07B1/145  **Glossary:** - Tether: a connection rope in the field of renewable wind-energy
[D07B 1/148] comprising marks or luminous elements -- D07B 1/148 classifies rope or cable designs that incorporate identifying marks or luminous components, such as reflective strips, painted bands, or embedded lights, to enhance visibility, safety or traceability. It falls under constructional features of ropes within the textiles category.
[D07B 1/16] Ropes or cables with an enveloping sheathing or inlays of rubber or plastics -- The D07B 1/16 subclass covers ropes or cables that are protected or reinforced by an external sheath or internal inlays made of rubber, plastic or similar synthetic materials, detailing their constructional features and the methods used to apply these polymeric coverings.
[D07B 1/162] characterised by a plastic or rubber enveloping sheathing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  sheathings extending beyond the perimeter of the tension members of the rope or cable.
[D07B 1/165] characterised by a plastic or rubber inlay -- **Definition:** This place covers:  inlays situated inside the perimeter of the tension members of the rope or cable.
[D07B 1/167] having a predetermined shape -- D07B 1/167 classifies ropes or cables that are produced with a fixed, predetermined shape, such as a specific cross-section, profile or twist pattern, to meet particular functional or structural requirements. The entry covers any constructional feature that gives the rope a set geometry rather than a shape that can be altered after manufacture.
[D07B 1/18] Grommets -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roundslings -> B66C1/12  **Glossary:** - Grommet: a sling with an eye
[D07B 1/185] characterised by the eye construction -- The CPC code D07B 1/185 classifies ropes or cables that are defined by an eye construction, meaning they terminate in a loop or eye for attachment or fastening. It falls under the hierarchy of textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general → constructional features of ropes or cables.
[D07B 1/20] Buoyant ropes, e.g. with air-filled cellular cores -- Defines ropes or cables that incorporate buoyancy, typically by containing air-filled cellular cores, allowing them to float in water. Such buoyant ropes are used where visibility or flotation is required, for example in marine mooring or rescue lines.
[D07B 1/22] Flat or flat-sided ropes -- The D07B 1/22 subclass defines ropes and cables whose cross-section is flat or has flat sides, typically created by arranging yarns or fibers side-by-side through braiding, plaiting or other textile processes. These flat-profile ropes are used where a low-profile, flexible line is required, such as in certain lifting, securing or decorative applications.
[D07B 1/24] Ropes or cables with a prematurely failing element -- The CPC subclass D07B 1/24, placed under TEXTILES > ROPES > ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL > Constructional features of ropes or cables, covers ropes or cables that contain a deliberately weak or sacrificial element designed to fail prematurely, typically to protect the main cord by breaking under overload conditions.
[D07B 2201/00] Ropes or cables -- Ropes or cables are flexible assemblies of fibers, yarns, or other materials twisted, braided, or woven to transmit tensile forces or support loads. The classification includes all types of rope and cable constructions, materials, and manufacturing methods, irrespective of their specific applications.
[D07B 2201/10] Rope or cable structures -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/10 covers the design and construction of rope or cable structures, including the arrangement, interlacing, braiding, or winding of individual strands into a composite form. It addresses features such as core-sheath configurations, reinforcement patterns, and methods for achieving desired flexibility, strength, and load-bearing characteristics in textile-based ropes and cables.
[D07B 2201/1004] General structure or appearance -- The subclass D07B 2201/1004 concerns the overall design and visual characteristics of ropes or cables, including their shape, cross-section, braid or lay pattern, core-sheath arrangement, and dimensions that define the general structure or appearance of the product.
[D07B 2201/1008] Several parallel ropes -- The CPC code D07B 2201/1008 defines products that consist of several parallel ropes, typically assembled for increased strength or load-bearing capacity. It falls under the textiles section, specifically ropes or cables in general, and is used to classify rope constructions employed in a variety of industrial and consumer applications.
[D07B 2201/1012] characterised by their internal structure -- The CPC sub-group D07B 2201/1012 covers ropes and cables whose distinguishing feature is the internal structure of the strand or core, such as the arrangement, composition, or bundling of fibers, wires or other elements. It includes any rope or cable defined by how its internal components are organized rather than by external shape or material.
[D07B 2201/1014] characterised by being laid or braided from several sub-ropes or sub-cables, e.g. hawsers -- The D07B 2201/1014 subclass covers ropes or cables that are formed by laying or braiding together multiple smaller ropes or sub-cables, such as hawsers. This includes structures where individual strands are combined to create a larger, stronger rope, describing the method of construction rather than the material.
[D07B 2201/1016] characterised by the use of different strands -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose construction incorporates different strands, for example strands of varying material, size, twist or function, rather than a uniform set of identical strands. The variation is intended to impart specific mechanical or functional properties to the rope.
[D07B 2201/102] including a core -- D07B 2201/102 classifies ropes or cables that incorporate a core, such as a central strand, wire or synthetic element that provides additional strength, stiffness or functionality to the outer covering. The code is used for patents describing the structure of rope with a built-in core in the broader textile rope category.
[D07B 2201/1024] Structures that change the cross-sectional shape -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/1024 covers ropes or cables that incorporate mechanisms or designs allowing the cross-sectional shape to be altered during use, such as expandable, contractible or shape-changing sections that adjust stiffness, grip or flexibility. It pertains to textile structures where the geometry can be varied to meet different performance requirements.
[D07B 2201/1028] characterised by the number of strands -- The subclass D07B 2201/1028 covers ropes or cables whose defining characteristic is the number of individual strands they contain, independent of material or overall construction. It is used when the strand count is a primary feature for classifying the rope or cable.
[D07B 2201/1032] three to eight strands respectively forming a single layer -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/1032 covers ropes or cables constructed with three to eight individual strands that are arranged side-by-side in a single outer layer, rather than being twisted or woven into multiple layers. It applies to textile-based rope designs where the strand count and single-layer layout are the defining features.
[D07B 2201/1036] nine or more strands respectively forming multiple layers -- A rope or cable constructed from nine or more individual strands that are organized into several concentric layers, creating a multi-layered structure for increased strength and flexibility. This configuration falls under the textiles-ropes subgroup in the CPC.
[D07B 2201/104] twisted -- The D07B 2201/104 subclass covers ropes or cables whose construction is obtained by twisting a plurality of strands together. It includes twisted-rope structures regardless of material, such as natural fibres, steel or synthetic polymer strands, and addresses the geometry and process of twisting that gives the rope its strength and flexibility.
[D07B 2201/1044] characterised by a value or range of the pitch parameter given -- D07B 2201/1044 classifies ropes or cables whose design is defined by a specific value or range of the pitch parameter, such as the distance between successive turns or twists. This subclass falls under TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL and addresses products where pitch is a distinguishing technical feature.
[D07B 2201/1048] using regular lay, i.e. the wires or filaments being parallel to rope axis -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose constituent wires or filaments are arranged in a regular lay, meaning they run parallel to the rope axis rather than being twisted. It includes methods and structures where the lay pattern is uniform.
[D07B 2201/1052] using lang lay, i.e. the wires or filaments being inclined relative to the rope axis -- This sub-class covers ropes or cables whose individual wires or filaments are arranged with a helical inclination, i.e., the strands are laid around the rope axis rather than running straight. The classification applies to any rope-type structure where the lay angle of the constituent elements is a defining feature.
[D07B 2201/1056] using alternate lay, i.e. the wires or filaments in the strands being oppositely inclined relative to the rope axis -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/1056 covers ropes or cables whose wires or filaments are laid in an alternate lay, meaning the strands are twisted in opposite directions relative to the rope axis. It includes methods and apparatus for producing and using such oppositely inclined strand constructions.
[D07B 2201/106] Pitch changing over length -- Pitch changing over length refers to ropes or cables whose twist or helix pitch varies along the strand, either gradually or in sections, to modify stiffness, flexibility, or load distribution.
[D07B 2201/1064] characterised by lay direction of the strand compared to the lay direction of the wires in the strand -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/1064 falls under textiles, ropes, ropes or cables in general. It designates ropes or cables where the lay direction of the strand differs from the lay direction of the wires within that strand. This characteristic is used for distinguishing rope constructions.
[D07B 2201/1068] having the same lay direction -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose strands are twisted or laid in the same direction along the length of the rope, i.e., a uniform or same-lay construction, irrespective of material or specific rope type.
[D07B 2201/1072] Compact winding, i.e. S/S or Z/Z -- A compact winding method or device for ropes or cables, in which the strands are wound in the same sense (S/S) or in opposite sense (Z/Z), enabling a tighter, space-saving coil. This entry falls under the textiles-ropes hierarchy and covers the specific arrangement of the windings to improve handling or storage.
[D07B 2201/1076] Open winding -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/1076 "Open winding" covers a method of winding ropes or cables in which the strands are arranged in an open, non-tight configuration, typically to ease later processing, reduce stress, or allow insertion of additional elements. It falls under the textile group for ropes, specifically ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/108] Cylinder winding, i.e. S/Z or Z/S -- D07B 2201/108 classifies the process of winding rope or cable onto a cylinder, specifying the S/Z or Z/S helix orientation. The entry falls under the textiles hierarchy, covering general rope and cable manufacturing techniques.
[D07B 2201/1084] Different twist pitch -- The CPC code D07B 2201/1084, titled Different twist pitch, classifies ropes or cables that are defined by variations in the pitch of their twists. It applies to textile rope products where the spacing between turns is a distinguishing technical feature.
[D07B 2201/1088] false twisted -- False twisted describes a rope construction in which the strands are intertwined without an inner core, giving the rope an apparent twist while lacking a true helical lay. The method produces a loosely bound, often decorative or low-stress rope that resembles a twisted cable but does not have the structural integrity of a fully twisted rope.
[D07B 2201/1092] Parallel strands -- Parallel strands refer to individual yarn or filament bundles that are aligned side-by-side rather than twisted together, forming a flat or ribbon-like component of a rope or cable. This arrangement allows uniform load distribution and is used in certain rope constructions where flexibility or specific mechanical properties are required.
[D07B 2201/1096] braided -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/1096 falls under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general and covers braided ropes or cables. It pertains to the construction, manufacturing methods or structural features of ropes formed by interlacing multiple strands in a braid, regardless of the material used. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents dealing with such braided rope technologies.
[D07B 2201/20] Rope or cable components -- D07B 2201/20 covers the individual parts that make up ropes or cables, such as strands, fibers, sheaths, connectors, and other structural elements used to assemble or reinforce the final product. It includes components specifically designed for textile-based ropes and cables, irrespective of the material or end-use.
[D07B 2201/2001] Wires or filaments -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2001 covers wires or filaments used as components of ropes or cables. It includes metal, synthetic or composite strands that form the load-bearing elements of textile rope structures. The classification falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes and general rope or cable constructions.
[D07B 2201/2002] characterised by their cross-sectional shape -- D07B 2201/2002 covers ropes or cables that are distinguished by the shape of their cross-section. The sub-class includes any rope or cable where the profile-circular, elliptical, rectangular, or other geometric form-is a defining technical feature, and it sits within the broader hierarchy of textiles, ropes, and general ropes or cables.
[D07B 2201/2003] flat -- Flat ropes or cables are rope-like elements having a substantially rectangular or planar cross-section, formed by arranging yarns or strands side-by-side rather than round. They are used in textile applications such as belts, straps, conveyor belts and similar flat-shaped load-bearing products.
[D07B 2201/2004] triangular -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2004, titled "triangular", pertains to ropes or cables that have a triangular cross-section, a form used in various textile applications. It is nested under the hierarchy Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2005] oval -- The CPC code D07B 2201/2005 designates ropes or cables that have an oval cross-section. It covers products where the longitudinal strands are arranged to form an oval shape rather than a round or flat profile, typically for specific handling, strength or aesthetic requirements in textile applications. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of textiles, ropes, and general rope or cable categories.
[D07B 2201/2006] characterised by a value or range of the dimension given -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2006 covers ropes or cables whose patent claims specify a particular dimensional value or a range for that dimension, such as diameter, length, pitch or similar measures. It lies under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general and classifies inventions that use dimensional parameters as a defining feature.
[D07B 2201/2007] characterised by their longitudinal shape -- This CPC subgroup deals with ropes and cables that are defined primarily by the shape of their lengthwise profile, such as round, flat, rectangular or other longitudinal forms. It classifies products where the cross-sectional geometry is the key distinguishing feature, irrespective of material or construction type.
[D07B 2201/2008] wavy or undulated -- This subclass covers ropes or cables that are deliberately formed with a wavy or undulating profile, providing flexibility, shock-absorption or decorative appearance. It includes designs and manufacturing methods that give the whole rope or its individual strands a sinusoidal shape for functional or aesthetic purposes.
[D07B 2201/2009] characterised by the materials used -- In CPC subclass D07B 2201/2009, ropes or cables are classified according to the material from which they are made, such as natural fibers, synthetic polymers, metal wire or composite blends, focusing on material composition rather than their construction or specific use.
[D07B 2201/201] characterised by a coating -- D07B 2201/201 covers ropes or cables that are distinguished by having a coating applied to their surface. The coating may be of a polymer, rubber, plastic, metal or other material to provide protection, improve grip, resistance to wear, weather, chemicals, or to impart other functional properties.
[D07B 2201/2011] comprising metals -- The D07B 2201/2011 subgroup covers ropes or cables that incorporate metal elements, such as steel wires, metal cores, or metal strands, either wholly or partially. It includes constructions where metal is a constituent material of the rope or cable used in textile applications.
[D07B 2201/2012] comprising polymers -- The D07B 2201/2012 subgroup concerns ropes or cables whose principal structural elements are formed from polymers, such as synthetic fibers, thermoplastic or thermoset polymeric materials. It includes products and manufacturing methods in which polymeric components serve as the core, sheath or reinforcement of the rope.
[D07B 2201/2013] comprising multiple layers -- The D07B 2201/2013 subclass covers ropes or cables that are built with multiple distinct layers, such as braided, stranded or laminated constructions, where at least two layers are combined to provide added strength, protection, or specific performance characteristics. It includes any rope or cable whose design relies on layered architecture rather than a single homogeneous strand.
[D07B 2201/2014] Compound wires or compound filaments -- D07B 2201/2014 covers compound wires or compound filaments, i.e., constructions formed by joining two or more different wires or filaments to create a single structural element for use in ropes or cables. The classification includes methods of producing such composites and their specific cross-sectional arrangements, enabling enhanced mechanical or functional properties.
[D07B 2201/2015] Strands -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/2015 covers strands that are used as the basic elements of ropes or cables, describing their construction, materials and forms before they are assembled into the final rope or cable product. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL → Ropes or cables, and includes details on strand geometry, twisting, braiding and any reinforcing components.
[D07B 2201/2016] characterised by their cross-sectional shape -- D07B 2201/2016 pertains to ropes or cables whose defining feature is the shape of their cross-section, such as round, oval, flat or other geometric profiles. The classification covers designs where the cross-sectional form is deliberately selected to provide particular mechanical or functional characteristics.
[D07B 2201/2017] triangular -- D07B 2201/2017 covers ropes or cables having a triangular cross-section, where the three sides form the overall shape of the strand. Such triangular ropes are used when a flat or V-shaped profile provides advantages in handling, winding or strength distribution. The classification applies to any material or construction that results in a triangular geometry of the rope or cable.
[D07B 2201/2018] oval -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2018 titled oval designates ropes or cables that have an overall oval cross-section. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES - ROPES - ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL, covering general rope constructions where the shape is elongated rather than circular.
[D07B 2201/2019] pressed to shape -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2019 concerns methods of shaping ropes or cables by pressing them into a desired form or cross-section, such as compressing strands, flattening, or profiled shaping to achieve specific mechanical or functional characteristics. It includes the use of dies, rollers or other pressing tools applied during or after the rope-making process. This subclass falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL.
[D07B 2201/202] characterised by a value or range of the dimension given -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/202 covers ropes or cables whose invention is defined by specifying a particular dimension or a range of dimensions, such as diameter, length, or cross-sectional size. It is used when the value or interval of a dimensional parameter is a key distinguishing feature of the product.
[D07B 2201/2021] characterised by their longitudinal shape -- This CPC subclass covers ropes or cables that are identified primarily by the shape of their longitudinal profile, such as round, flat, triangular or other defined cross-sectional forms. It groups inventions where the distinguishing feature of the rope is its longitudinal shape rather than material or construction.
[D07B 2201/2022] coreless -- Coreless refers to ropes or cables that lack a central core fiber or wire, consisting only of outer strands that are directly intertwined. This construction gives uniform strength and flexibility, often chosen when a core would add weight or reduce pliability.
[D07B 2201/2023] with core -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers ropes or cables that include a distinct core element, distinguishing them from solid or core-less variants. It falls under the textile section, specifically within the hierarchy of ropes in general, and is identified by the code D07B 2201/2023. Use of a core may affect the rope's strength, flexibility, or intended application.
[D07B 2201/2024] twisted -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2024 pertains to ropes or cables that are produced by twisting fibers, yarns or strands together, covering the twisted construction and related manufacturing methods for textile ropes.
[D07B 2201/2025] characterised by a value or range of the pitch parameter given -- The subclass D07B 2201/2025 covers ropes or cables that are defined by a specified value or range for the pitch parameter, which is the distance between successive turns or elements of the rope. It includes inventions that explicitly state or limit the pitch to achieve particular mechanical or functional characteristics. This entry falls within the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL.
[D07B 2201/2026] Pitch changing over length -- The entry D07B 2201/2026 covers ropes or cables whose pitch - the spacing or helical angle of the strands - varies along the length of the product. This classification is used for designs where the pitch is intentionally altered to modify flexibility, strength, or handling characteristics of the rope.
[D07B 2201/2027] Compact winding -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/2027 "Compact winding" covers techniques or devices for tightly winding ropes or cables into a compact, space-saving configuration, often to facilitate handling, storage or transport. It includes methods that coil or bundle the rope without damaging its structure, and apparatus that guide the material into a dense, orderly arrangement. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes and general rope or cable technology.
[D07B 2201/2028] having the same lay direction and lay pitch -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2028 covers ropes or cables whose constituent strands are twisted in the same lay direction with a uniform lay pitch. This uniform lay ensures consistent geometry and performance across the length of the rope, facilitating predictable handling, strength, and wear characteristics. It is applied to textile products classified under ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2029] Open winding -- Open winding describes a way of arranging rope or cable strands so that the fibers lie side-by-side without overlapping, forming a loosely assembled bundle. The method lacks a closed or tightly bound structure, making the rope easy to unravel or adjust.
[D07B 2201/203] Cylinder winding, i.e. S/Z or Z/S -- This CPC group covers the method of winding rope or cable onto a cylindrical core using an alternating S/Z or Z/S pattern, specifying how the strands are laid to achieve balanced lay and reduced torque during the winding process.
[D07B 2201/2031] Different twist pitch -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2031 pertains to ropes or cables whose strands are constructed with different twist pitches, meaning the distance between successive turns varies along the rope. It addresses the structural design of textile ropes where non-uniform twist pitch influences flexibility and strength.
[D07B 2201/2032] compared with the core -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2032 covers techniques for evaluating a rope or cable by comparing its overall properties with those of its core, such as strength, elasticity or wear. It includes methods, devices or procedures that assess the condition or performance of the outer strands relative to the central core material. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2033] Parallel wires -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2033 designates ropes or cables whose structure consists of parallel wires. It covers the composition, configuration and manufacturing of such wire-based ropes, typically used in textile applications where strength and flexibility are required. This subclass sits under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2034] comprising crossing wires or filaments in the same layer -- The subclass D07B 2201/2034 covers ropes or cables whose individual wires or filaments are interlaced so that they cross one another within the same layer, rather than running parallel. This arrangement is used to improve flexibility, strength or handling characteristics of the rope, and the classification applies to the design and manufacture of such crossed-wire structures.
[D07B 2201/2035] false twisted -- False twisted refers to a rope or cable construction where the strands are twisted in the opposite direction to the main lay, creating a balanced lay that reduces the tendency to unwind and improves flexibility while maintaining strength. This configuration is used in various textile rope applications where enhanced handling and reduced kinking are required.
[D07B 2201/2036] characterised by the use of different wires or filaments -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2036 covers ropes or cables that are made from, or incorporate, two or more distinct types of wire or filament, such as combining steel strands with synthetic fibers. It pertains to general rope and cable constructions where the differing materials are used together to achieve specific mechanical or functional properties.
[D07B 2201/2037] regarding the dimension of the wires or filaments -- D07B 2201/2037 covers technical features that define the size of the individual wires or filaments used in ropes or cables, such as diameter, thickness, cross-sectional shape or any dimensional parameters that affect the rope's mechanical properties. This subclass focuses on specifications that determine how the constituent elements are measured and controlled.
[D07B 2201/2038] characterised by the number of wires or filaments -- The CPC code D07B 2201/2038 falls under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general and is used for patents concerning ropes or cables that are characterised by the number of wires or filaments they contain, distinguishing them by their strand count.
[D07B 2201/2039] three to eight wires or filaments respectively forming a single layer -- A rope or cable composed of three to eight separate wires or filaments that are laid side-by-side in one single layer, rather than being twisted together or formed around a core. This configuration provides a simple, flat construction used where a uniform cross-section and easy handling are required.
[D07B 2201/204] nine or more wires or filaments respectively forming multiple layers -- D07B 2201/204 covers ropes or cables that consist of nine or more individual wires or filaments arranged in multiple layers, a type of textile product used where high strength or flexibility is required. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes, and general ropes or cables.
[D07B 2201/2041] characterised by the materials used -- D07B 2201/2041 identifies ropes or cables whose defining feature is the material from which they are made, encompassing natural fibers, synthetic polymers, metals and other substances. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL, and classifies inventions that are distinguished primarily by the material composition of the rope or cable.
[D07B 2201/2042] characterised by a coating -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2042 covers ropes or cables that are characterised by a coating applied to the strand or braid. The coating may serve protective, insulating, decorative or functional purposes, such as resistance to wear, moisture, chemicals or fire. It applies to all rope types irrespective of material.
[D07B 2201/2043] comprising metals -- This CPC subclass designates ropes or cables in which metal makes up all or part of the structure, including steel, iron, copper or alloy elements used as the sole material or as reinforcement within a textile rope. It falls under the broader categories of textiles and general ropes.
[D07B 2201/2044] comprising polymers -- The D07B 2201/2044 subclass covers ropes or cables whose construction incorporates polymeric materials, such as synthetic fibers, plastics or polymer-based coatings, either alone or blended with other substances. It includes all rope or cable types where polymers form an essential part of the strand, sheath, or protective layer.
[D07B 2201/2045] comprising multiple layers -- Ropes or cables built with more than one concentric layer, such as a core surrounded by one or several sheath layers that provide added strength, protection or flexibility. The multilayer construction may involve different materials or structures in each layer to achieve specific performance characteristics. This classification covers any rope or cable where multiple layers are integral to its design.
[D07B 2201/2046] comprising fillers -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2046 refers to ropes or cables that are made with one or more filler materials incorporated into the strand or core. These fillers, such as fibers, polymers, or other solid substances, are used to modify mechanical properties, improve handling, or reduce weight. The entry falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL.
[D07B 2201/2047] Cores -- The entry covers the core component of a rope or cable, the central part which may be made of fibres, wires, or synthetic material and provides structural strength, often surrounded by a sheath. It includes designs of core constructions, materials, and configurations.
[D07B 2201/2048] characterised by their cross-sectional shape -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2048 covers ropes or cables whose distinguishing feature is the shape of their cross-section. It includes any rope or cable where the profile - circular, rectangular, elliptical or other geometric form - influences performance or manufacturing. This subclass falls under textiles, ropes, general ropes or cables.
[D07B 2201/2049] having protrusions extending radially functioning as spacer between strands or wires -- This subgroup covers rope or cable constructions that incorporate radial protrusions on the strands or wires to act as spacers, keeping the individual elements separated and improving flexibility, ventilation and reducing friction, especially in textile, marine or industrial rope applications.
[D07B 2201/2051] characterised by a value or range of the dimension given -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2051 covers rope or cable products in which the claim specifies a particular dimensional value or a range for that dimension, such as diameter, length, or thickness. It applies when the rope or cable is distinguished from others by the numeric measurement or interval given, regardless of material or construction. This subclass falls under Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2052] characterised by their structure -- This CPC subclass groups patents on ropes or cables whose novelty lies in their physical structure, such as the arrangement of strands, braiding pattern, core-sheath configuration, or lay geometry. It covers any textile rope where the structural design, rather than material composition, defines the invention.
[D07B 2201/2053] being homogeneous -- The code D07B 2201/2053 classifies ropes or cables that are homogeneous, i.e., formed from a single material or uniform construction without distinct layers or reinforcing elements. It falls under the textiles hierarchy for general rope categories.
[D07B 2201/2054] comprising foam material -- A rope or cable that incorporates foam material, such as a foam core or foam-filled strands, is classified under D07B 2201/2054. This entry covers textile ropes in which the structural or protective component consists wholly or partially of a foamed polymer or rubber, providing cushioning, buoyancy or thermal insulation. It applies to any rope, cable or similar flexible element where foam is an integral part of the construction.
[D07B 2201/2055] comprising filaments or fibers -- The CPC code D07B 2201/2055 classifies ropes or cables that are constructed from filaments or fibers, distinguishing them from those made of other materials such as metal or synthetic cords. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL.
[D07B 2201/2056] arranged parallel to the axis -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2056 covers ropes or cables that are positioned so that their lengths run parallel to the main axis of the product or assembly. This arrangement is common in textile contexts where multiple strands are aligned side-by-side to form a uniform structure or to aid handling and processing. It includes methods and devices that maintain this parallel orientation.
[D07B 2201/2057] resulting in a twisted structure -- The D07B 2201/2057 subclass concerns manufacturing processes that produce a twisted structure in ropes or cables. It includes methods for twisting individual strands or filaments to form helical or plaited ropes, covering the steps that result in the final twisted geometry.
[D07B 2201/2058] comprising fillers -- A rope or cable that incorporates filler material-such as fibers, powders, granules or other substances mixed into the core or sheath-to modify its mechanical, physical or functional properties, for example to reduce weight, increase stiffness, improve durability or lower cost. This classification covers any rope or cable construction where such fillers are a defining element of the product.
[D07B 2201/2059] comprising wires -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2059 covers ropes or cables that are made up of wires. It falls under the textiles group, specifically the category for general ropes or cables, and denotes wire-based constructions rather than fibre or synthetic strands.
[D07B 2201/206] arranged parallel to the axis -- This subclass covers ropes or cables whose individual strands, yarns or elements are positioned parallel to the longitudinal axis of the rope, a configuration that influences tensile strength, flexibility and handling characteristics. It pertains to designs where the constituent components run essentially in the same direction along the rope's length.
[D07B 2201/2061] resulting in a twisted structure -- This subclass concerns ropes or cables that are formed by twisting strands together to create a helical or twisted structure. It includes processes, devices or features that result in a twisted configuration of the rope, such as methods of twisting fibres, yarns or wires during manufacture.
[D07B 2201/2062] comprising fillers -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2062 falls under Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general and covers ropes or cables that incorporate filler materials. It addresses designs where a filler, such as a textile strand, polymer, or other substance, is placed within the rope structure to enhance strength, stiffness, or other functional properties.
[D07B 2201/2063] being hollow -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2063 pertains to ropes or cables that are hollow, meaning they contain a longitudinal cavity or channel within the strand or assembly. Such hollow ropes are designed for applications where fluid passage, wiring, or weight reduction is required, and they fall under the broader categories of textiles, ropes, and general rope or cable constructions.
[D07B 2201/2064] being discontinuous in the longitudinal direction -- The CPC class D07B 2201/2064 pertains to ropes or cables whose structure is discontinuous along the longitudinal axis. It includes products made from separate elements, segments or joints rather than a continuous strand, such as sectional or link-type ropes. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Textiles - Ropes - Ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2065] comprising a coating -- The subclass D07B 2201/2065 covers ropes or cables that are provided with a coating, such as a protective, insulating, waterproof or friction-reducing layer applied to the strand or the whole assembly. It includes any type of coated textile rope or cable regardless of material, where the coating is an integral part of the product's function or durability.
[D07B 2201/2066] characterised by the materials used -- D07B 2201/2066 refers to ropes or cables that are classified according to the material from which they are made, such as natural fibers, plastics, metals or composites. The subclass groups inventions that achieve specific performance, durability or handling characteristics by selecting or modifying the constituent material, rather than by shape or construction.
[D07B 2201/2067] characterised by the elongation or tension behaviour -- The D07B 2201/2067 subclass groups rope and cable products whose technical features are defined by their elongation or tension behaviour, including the material composition, construction and treatment that affect stretch, load-bearing capacity and resilience. It is used to classify inventions that control or optimise how ropes or cables respond to pulling forces during use.
[D07B 2201/2068] having a load bearing function -- D07B 2201/2068 pertains to ropes or cables whose primary purpose is to bear loads, including lifting, supporting, or transmitting weight in structural or mechanical applications. The subclass groups items designed for load-bearing rather than decorative or signaling functions.
[D07B 2201/2069] being elastic -- D07B 2201/2069 covers ropes or cables that are elastic, meaning they can be stretched and recover their original length, including elastic cords, stretchable ropes and bungee-type cables used in textile or fastening applications.
[D07B 2201/207] being viscous -- The subclass D07B 2201/207 pertains to ropes or cables that are characterized by a viscous quality, such as those impregnated with or containing a thick, flowable coating, paste, or lubricant to improve handling, flexibility, or protection. It includes methods for producing or treating such ropes with viscous substances.
[D07B 2201/2071] Spacers -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/2071 titled Spacers covers components inserted between rope strands or cable elements to keep them separated, improve flexibility and control geometry, often preventing abrasion and allowing precise pitch adjustment in synthetic or metal ropes used for mooring, lifting or transmission applications.
[D07B 2201/2072] characterised by the materials used -- D07B 2201/2072 designates ropes or cables that are distinguished primarily by the material from which they are made, such as natural fibers, synthetic polymers, metal wires or composite blends, enabling classification of rope types within the broader textiles and rope categories.
[D07B 2201/2073] in circumferencial direction -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2073 pertains to ropes or cables where the defining feature relates to a circumferential direction, such as winding, braiding, or arranging strands around a central axis. It includes techniques that produce a circular or ring-shaped orientation of the material, affecting strength or flexibility. The subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of textiles - ropes - ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2074] in radial direction -- This CPC subclass covers aspects of ropes or cables that are defined, treated, or measured in the radial direction, such as radial reinforcement, radial load bearing, or radial deformation of the rope or cable structure. It applies to textile rope constructions where the radial axis, perpendicular to the length of the rope, is the focus of the invention or analysis.
[D07B 2201/2075] Fillers -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2075 covers the incorporation of filler materials into ropes or cables, such as fibers, yarns, strands or other substances placed in the core or sheath to modify properties like strength, stiffness, bulk or handling. It applies to any rope or cable design where such fillers are used.
[D07B 2201/2076] having a lubricant function -- The CPC entry D07B 2201/2076 covers ropes or cables that incorporate or are treated with a lubricant to reduce friction, improve handling, and protect against wear. It includes any material or coating applied for a lubricating function on the rope or cable.
[D07B 2201/2077] having an anti-corrosive function -- This subclass covers ropes or cables that include features to prevent or limit corrosion, such as protective coatings, anti-corrosive alloys, or additives that inhibit rust. It applies to any rope or cable whose primary design purpose is to provide an anti-corrosive function.
[D07B 2201/2078] having a load bearing function -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2078 designates ropes or cables that are intended to support or transmit loads. It includes devices whose primary purpose is to bear weight, resist tension, or provide structural support in applications such as lifting, suspension, or anchoring.
[D07B 2201/2079] characterised by the kind or amount of filling -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2079, within textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general, covers ropes or cables that are characterised by the kind or amount of filling material, such as sand, water, powders or other substances, which influences properties like flexibility, buoyancy or strength. It applies when the filling is a defining feature of the rope or cable design.
[D07B 2201/208] having an open structure -- The subclass D07B 2201/208 covers ropes or cables whose construction leaves an open structure, meaning the individual strands or filaments are arranged so that spaces exist between them, as in braided, plaited or similar configurations that permit air or fluid to pass through. This open-structured design is applied to textile ropes and related cable products.
[D07B 2201/2081] having maximum filling -- The subclass D07B 2201/2081 covers ropes or cables defined by the requirement that they contain the maximum possible filling of constituent materials, such as fibers, strands or filaments, within the rope's cross-section. It is used to classify textile ropes in which the filler density is a principal design feature.
[D07B 2201/2082] characterised by the materials used -- This CPC subgroup covers rope or cable products that are defined primarily by the type of material they are made from, such as natural fibers, synthetic polymers, metal or composite materials. It includes specifications on material composition that affect performance, durability and application.
[D07B 2201/2083] Jackets or coverings -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2083 covers jackets or coverings applied to ropes or cables, a sub-category of textiles used for protective or insulating purposes. It includes any material layer that encases a rope to guard against abrasion, moisture, chemicals or mechanical damage, thereby extending the rope's service life and improving safety in handling.
[D07B 2201/2084] characterised by their shape -- The D07B 2201/2084 subgroup covers ropes or cables whose primary distinguishing feature is their shape, including round, flat, triangular or other cross-sectional geometries that affect handling, strength and specific applications. It pertains to rope designs where the profile, rather than material or construction, is the key characteristic.
[D07B 2201/2085] concerning the internal shape -- D07B 2201/2085 classifies ropes or cables according to their internal shape, covering features such as core geometry, strand arrangement, or hollow sections that affect flexibility, strength, or load distribution. The code is used for patents describing how the interior configuration of a rope or cable is designed or modified.
[D07B 2201/2086] concerning the external shape -- D07B 2201/2086 defines the classification for features related to the external shape of ropes or cables, covering aspects such as profile, diameter, cross-section, and overall geometry of the finished product. It falls under the hierarchy Textile → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2087] being of the coated type -- The code D07B 2201/2087 covers ropes or cables that are manufactured with a coating applied to the strands or the whole cable. The coating may provide protection, insulation, improved grip or other functional benefits, and can be applied by dipping, spraying, extruding or similar processes. This subclass falls under the general category of ropes and cables in the textiles hierarchy.
[D07B 2201/2088] having multiple layers -- This CPC subclass D07B 2201/2088 covers ropes or cables that are constructed with more than one layer of material, such as an inner core and outer sheath, to enhance strength, flexibility or protection. It falls under textiles, ropes, ropes or cables in general.
[D07B 2201/2089] comprising wrapped structures -- The subclass D07B 2201/2089 covers ropes or cables that are formed by wrapping one or more strands, wires or filaments around a core or around each other to create a composite structure. It includes helically wrapped or otherwise enveloped constructions such as wrapped wire rope, stranded cable and similar wrapped rope types.
[D07B 2201/209] comprising braided structures -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/209 covers ropes and cables whose construction consists of braided structures, in which multiple strands are interlaced to form a braid for enhanced strength, flexibility, and durability. This category applies to any rope or cable where the primary form of assembly is a braid, regardless of the material or intended use.
[D07B 2201/20903] comprising woven structures -- This CPC subgroup covers ropes or cables whose core or any component is formed by woven structures, meaning the strands are interlaced in a fabric-like pattern rather than being twisted or braided. It applies to textile-based rope constructions.
[D07B 2201/20907] comprising knitted structures -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/20907 covers ropes or cables that are formed from knitted structures, meaning the individual fibers or strands are interlooped in a knit pattern rather than twisted or braided. It applies to any rope or cable where the primary load-bearing structure is a knit.
[D07B 2201/2091] being movable relative to the internal structure -- The code D07B 2201/2091 covers ropes or cables whose components can move relative to the internal structure, such as adjustable or draw-string ropes where sections slide or shift within the main body. This includes designs where a core, sheath or individual strands are intended to shift for length adjustment, tensioning or reconfiguration.
[D07B 2201/2092] characterised by the materials used -- D07B 2201/2092 covers ropes or cables that are defined primarily by the type of material from which they are made, such as natural fibers, synthetic polymers or metal components. The subclass falls under the textiles hierarchy and addresses material-based classification of general-purpose ropes.
[D07B 2201/2093] being translucent -- The CPC subclass D07B 2201/2093 covers ropes or cables that are at least partially translucent, meaning they permit light to pass through the material. It applies to any rope or cable design where translucency is a functional or aesthetic characteristic, regardless of the specific fiber or polymer used.
[D07B 2201/2094] being luminescent or reflective -- D07B 2201/2094 covers ropes or cables that incorporate luminescent or reflective elements to enhance visibility, such as safety lines with phosphorescent fibers or reflective strips. The code applies to any textile rope designed to emit or reflect light for identification or warning purposes.
[D07B 2201/2095] Auxiliary components, e.g. electric conductors or light guides -- This subclass deals with ropes or cables that incorporate auxiliary elements such as embedded electrical conductors, fiber-optic light guides, or similar devices that add power transmission, signaling or illumination functions while retaining the rope's structural role. It covers the design and integration of these components within the rope or cable body.
[D07B 2201/2096] Light guides -- This CPC subgroup D07B 2201/2096 covers ropes or cables that incorporate light-guiding elements such as optical fibers or light-conducting channels. The classification is used for textile-based rope structures designed to transmit light for illumination, signalling, or information transfer along the length of the rope.
[D07B 2201/2097] Binding wires -- Binding wires are metal strands or wire assemblies employed to secure, tie, or reinforce ropes and cables, often used in rope splicing, terminations, or as supplemental strength members within cable constructions. The CPC entry D07B 2201/2097 classifies these components alongside rope and cable products in the textiles domain. It covers various wire forms designed specifically for binding purposes rather than for standalone rope or cable functions.
