[D07B 2201/2098] characterized by special properties or the arrangements of the binding wire -- This CPC subclass D07B 2201/2098, within the hierarchy textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general, pertains to ropes or cables that are characterized by special properties or by the particular arrangement of the binding wire.
[D07B 2205/00] Rope or cable materials -- The code D07B 2205/00 covers materials used to make ropes or cables, such as fibers, metal strands, composites and any other substances that form the core or sheath of these products. It relates to the selection and processing of these materials for textile applications without specifying particular rope types or uses.
[D07B 2205/10] Natural organic materials -- D07B 2205/10 designates natural organic materials employed as raw fibers or substances for making ropes and cables. It includes plant-derived fibers such as hemp, jute, sisal, coir and other biodegradable polymers used in textile rope production. The code falls under the textiles-ropes hierarchy in the CPC system.
[D07B 2205/103] Animal and plant materials -- The D07B 2205/103 subclass covers rope or cable materials that are derived from animal and plant sources, such as hemp, jute, sisal, cotton, flax, coir or other natural fibers. It includes the preparation and processing of these fibers for use in making ropes, cables or similar textile products.
[D07B 2205/106] Manila, hemp or sisal -- D07B 2205/106 classifies rope or cable materials that are produced from natural fibers such as Manila, hemp or sisal. It falls under the textile hierarchy, specifically within ropes and cables in general, and is used to identify patents concerning these plant-based rope materials.
[D07B 2205/20] Organic high polymers -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/20 covers organic high-molecular-weight polymers employed as material for ropes or cables, including synthetic polymers such as polyesters, polyamides, polyurethanes and similar compounds that provide strength, durability and flexibility in textile rope applications.
[D07B 2205/2003] Thermoplastics -- D07B 2205/2003 covers rope and cable materials that are formed from thermoplastic polymers, specifying the use of heat-meltable plastics in the manufacture of textile ropes or cables. It addresses the composition, processing and properties of such thermoplastic rope elements within the broader textile and rope classifications.
[D07B 2205/2007] Duroplastics -- The subclass D07B 2205/2007 pertains to duroplastics used as materials for ropes or cables. It covers rigid or semi-rigid polymer compositions, such as phenolic or epoxy-based plastics, that are formulated to provide high strength, durability, and resistance to wear in rope or cable applications. This classification falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes, and general rope or cable materials.
[D07B 2205/201] Polyolefins -- D07B 2205/201 - Polyolefins classifies rope or cable materials that are composed primarily of polyolefin polymers such as polyethylene, polypropylene or their copolymers. It falls within the textiles hierarchy under ropes and rope or cable materials, covering the composition, processing and use of these thermoplastic fibres in rope or cable products.
[D07B 2205/2014] High performance polyolefins, e.g. Dyneema or Spectra -- The entry D07B 2205/2014 covers high-performance polyolefin materials such as ultra-high-molecular-weight polyethylene used for ropes and cables. These polymers, marketed under names like Dyneema or Spectra, provide exceptional tensile strength, low stretch and light weight, making them suitable for demanding rope or cable applications.
[D07B 2205/2017] Polystyrenes -- D07B 2205/2017 covers the use of polystyrene polymers as the material for ropes or cables. It falls within the textile subclass for rope and cable constructions, specifying polystyrene-based fibers, films or composites employed to form the rope structure.
[D07B 2205/2021] Polyvinyl halides -- D07B 2205/2021 falls under the CPC heading for rope or cable materials and specifically covers the use of polyvinyl halides-synthetic polymers such as PVC-in the manufacture of ropes and cables, detailing their composition, properties and typical applications.
[D07B 2205/2025] Polyvinyl acetates -- D07B 2205/2025 designates the use of polyvinyl acetate polymers as materials for ropes or cables within the textile sector. These resins provide flexibility, adhesion and resistance to moisture, making them suitable for manufacturing general-purpose or specialty rope constructions.
[D07B 2205/2028] Polyvinyl alcohols -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2028 designates polyvinyl alcohols as raw or processed materials for ropes or cables. It includes polymeric compounds based on vinyl alcohol units that may be formed into fibers, films or coatings used in the manufacture of textile ropes and cables.
[D07B 2205/2032] Polyacrylics -- The subclass D07B 2205/2032 designates polyacrylic polymers employed as material for ropes or cables, falling under the broader categories of textiles, ropes, and rope or cable materials. It includes polyacrylic fibers, films or compounds specifically formulated for use in general-purpose or specialized rope and cable applications.
[D07B 2205/2035] Polyacetals -- Polyacetals are thermoplastic polymers characterised by high strength, low friction and good chemical resistance, making them suitable for durable rope and cable applications. In the CPC they are classified under D07B 2205/2035, which covers rope or cable materials specifically formed from polyacetals.
[D07B 2205/2039] Polyesters -- D07B 2205/2039 classifies polyesters used as the material for ropes or cables, situated within the ropes or cables in general subgroup of the textiles hierarchy. It identifies polyester fibers or filaments that form the construction of rope or cable products.
[D07B 2205/2042] High performance polyesters, e.g. Vectran -- The CPC code D07B 2205/2042 covers high-performance polyester fibers such as Vectran that are used as rope or cable material. It belongs to the hierarchy textiles, ropes, ropes or cables in general, rope or cable materials, and refers to synthetic fibers engineered for exceptional strength and durability.
[D07B 2205/2046] Polyamides, e.g. nylons -- This CPC subgroup (D07B 2205/2046) falls under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general → rope or cable materials and specifies the use of polyamide polymers, commonly known as nylons, as the primary material for manufacturing ropes and cables. It covers inventions that employ nylon fibers or filaments to provide strength, durability, and flexibility in rope products.
[D07B 2205/205] Aramides -- D07B 2205/205 covers the use of aramides as material for ropes or cables. This subclass falls under the broader category of rope or cable materials within the textiles field.
[D07B 2205/2053] Polybenzimidazol [PBI] -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2053 designates the use of polybenzimidazole (PBI) in rope or cable materials within the broader textiles and ropes categories. PBI is a high-performance polymer known for exceptional thermal stability and tensile strength, making it suitable for advanced rope, cable and protective textile applications.
[D07B 2205/2057] Phenol resins -- D07B 2205/2057 classifies phenol resin materials employed in the construction of ropes and cables. The entry covers the use of phenolic polymers as binding or protective agents in textile rope manufacture, including treated fibers and composite structures.

[D07B 2205/2064] Polyurethane resins -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2064 covers polyurethane resins used in making rope and cable materials. [D07B 2205/2067] Viscose or regenerated cellulose, e.g. Rayon -- This CPC subclass D07B 2205/2067 falls under textiles-ropes-ropes or cables in general and relates to rope or cable materials made from viscose or regenerated cellulose such as rayon. It covers the use, processing or manufacture of these fibers for rope applications.
[D07B 2205/2071] Fluor resins -- A fluor resin is a polymer containing fluorine, such as polytetrafluoroethylene or fluorinated ethylene-propylene, used to coat or impregnate rope or cable materials for chemical resistance, low friction, and weatherability. This subcategory groups patents that apply these resins to improve the performance or durability of ropes and cables.
[D07B 2205/2075] Rubbers, i.e. elastomers -- D07B 2205/2075 classifies elastomeric materials such as natural or synthetic rubbers employed in the construction of ropes or cables. [D07B 2205/2078] being of natural origin -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2078 designates rope or cable materials that are obtained from natural sources, such as plant fibres, animal hair or other organically derived substances, distinguishing them from synthetic or man-made rope compositions.
[D07B 2205/2082] being of synthetic nature, e.g. chloroprene -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2082 classifies rope and cable materials that are of synthetic nature, for example those made from chloroprene or similar polymeric compounds. [D07B 2205/2085] having particular high polymer characteristics -- The CPC subclass D07B 2205/2085 concerns ropes or cables that are made from polymers with unusually high molecular weight or distinctive polymer structures, such as specialty polyamides, thermoplastic elastomers or other high-polymer materials. [D07B 2205/507] Solid lubricants -- The D07B 2205/507 class covers solid lubricants applied to rope or cable materials to reduce friction and wear. It includes substances such as graphite, PTFE, molybdenum disulfide and waxes that are incorporated into the fiber, coating or sheath of the rope. These additives improve handling, prolong service life and enable smoother movement over pulleys and drums.
[D07B 2207/00] Rope or cable making machines -- This CPC entry D07B 2207/00, under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general, relates to machines that manufacture ropes or cables, including braiding, twisting, and winding equipment used to produce various types of rope and cable.
[D07B 2207/20] Type of machine -- D07B 2207/20 designates machines used to manufacture ropes or cables, covering the specific type of equipment employed in the production process. It falls under the textile section for rope or cable making machinery and classifies the functional or structural category of the machine, such as twisting, braiding or winding devices.
[D07B 2207/201] Manually operated systems -- The code D07B 2207/201 designates manually operated rope or cable making machines, encompassing hand-powered devices that twist, braid, or otherwise form ropes and cables without the use of electric or hydraulic power. These systems are typically employed in textile workshops for small-scale production or maintenance tasks such as splicing and re-braiding.
[D07B 2207/202] Double twist unwinding -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/202 relates to rope-making equipment that separates a double-twisted rope or cable into its component strands by unwinding them. It is used in textile rope production to prepare the strands for subsequent processes such as braiding, laying or finishing.
[D07B 2207/203] comprising flyer -- The D07B 2207/203 subclass refers to rope-or-cable making machines that include a flyer, the rotating element that guides and twists the strands as they are formed into rope. It is used in textile equipment for producing ropes or cables by laying or twisting filaments and fibers.
[D07B 2207/204] Double twist winding -- The D07B 2207/204 class covers rope or cable manufacturing processes that employ a double-twist winding technique, where two strands are twisted together to form the final product. It includes machines and methods specifically designed to create this double-twist configuration for strengthened or specialized ropes and cables.
[D07B 2207/205] comprising flyer -- The subclass D07B 2207/205 relates to rope-or-cable making machines that incorporate a flyer, the rotating element that interlaces fibers or strands to form the rope or cable. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL → Rope or cable making machines, specifying equipment where the flyer is an essential component of the manufacturing process.
[D07B 2207/206] with means for providing less than double twist, e.g. counter rotating means -- Rope or cable making machines equipped with mechanisms that limit the lay to less than a full double twist, typically employing counter-rotating components to control strand rotation. This subclass covers devices that provide precise, reduced-twist production for improved rope performance.
[D07B 2207/207] Sequential double twisting devices -- Sequential double-twisting devices are mechanisms used in rope or cable manufacturing that twist the strands twice in succession to produce a tighter, more uniform construction. They are employed in rope-making machines to improve strength and reduce elongation, and are classified under CPC D07B 2207/207.
[D07B 2207/208] characterised by at least partially unwinding the twist of the upstream double twisting step -- The CPC entry D07B 2207/208 covers machines for making ropes or cables that incorporate a step in which the twist applied in an earlier double-twisting stage is partially unwound before the final lay-in. This feature is used to adjust the rope's construction, improve flexibility or reduce residual stresses during production.
[D07B 2207/209] Tubular strander -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) the code D07B 2207/209 designates a tubular strander, a machine used in rope or cable production to arrange strands into a tubular configuration before they are twisted or woven. It falls under the hierarchy Textiles → Ropes → Ropes or cables in general → Rope or cable making machines.
[D07B 2207/40] Machine components -- This CPC subclass covers the individual parts and assemblies incorporated into machines used to manufacture ropes or cables, such as feeding gears, tensioning devices, winding drums and cutting tools. It belongs to group 2207 for rope or cable making machines within the textiles-ropes hierarchy.
[D07B 2207/4004] Unwinding devices -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4004 defines devices that unwind rope or cable material during production. It includes mechanisms attached to rope-making machines that separate the strand from a supply reel and control its feed for subsequent processing such as twisting, braiding or laying. These tools ensure consistent tension and speed while the rope or cable is being formed.
[D07B 2207/4009] over the head -- The D07B 2207/4009 subclass covers rope-or-cable manufacturing equipment where the material is guided or processed over a head element that shapes, twists or braids the strand. It includes machines in which the rope passes over a rotating or stationary head to form the final product.
[D07B 2207/4013] comprising flyer -- The CPC entry D07B 2207/4013 falls under textiles, ropes, ropes or cables in general, and rope or cable making machines. It covers machines that include a flyer, the rotating component that guides and stretches yarn or filament as it is formed into rope or cable. This sub-category specifies equipment where the flyer is an integral part of the rope-making process.
[D07B 2207/4018] Rope twisting devices -- The CPC entry D07B 2207/4018 covers machines designed to twist rope or cable elements, providing the required helical formation of fibers or strands during rope production. It includes devices that apply controlled torsion to create uniform, strong ropes for textile and industrial use, falling under the hierarchy of textiles, ropes, and rope-making machinery.
[D07B 2207/4022] characterised by twisting die specifics -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4022 pertains to rope-or-cable making machines that are distinguished by the particular design or features of their twisting die. It covers equipment where the die's geometry, configuration or operating elements are specified to achieve a defined twist in the rope or cable during manufacture. This subclass falls under the broader categories of textiles, ropes and general rope-making machinery.
[D07B 2207/4027] including a coating die -- The D07B 2207/4027 subclass covers rope- or cable-making machinery that incorporates a coating die, i.e. a device that applies a protective or functional layer to the strand as it is produced. It is part of the broader category of rope and cable manufacturing equipment within the textiles field.
[D07B 2207/4031] Winding device -- The D07B 2207/4031 winding device class includes mechanisms that coil or unwind rope or cable onto drums, spools or other containers during manufacture or handling. Such devices are integral to rope-making and cable-making machines, controlling tension and layering to produce uniform lengths. The classification falls under textiles, specifically rope and cable production equipment.
[D07B 2207/4036] comprising traversing means -- The CPC entry D07B 2207/4036 covers rope or cable making machines that incorporate traversing means, i.e., mechanisms that move the strands or yarns laterally during production to form the rope or cable. This sub-class pertains specifically to equipment used in textile rope manufacturing where the traversing device controls strand placement for consistent geometry.
[D07B 2207/404] Heat treating devices -- A heat-treating device used in rope or cable making machines, classified under the textiles hierarchy for ropes, specifically for treating the material during production to improve strength, flexibility or durability. This entry falls within the Cooperative Patent Classification section D07B 2207/404.
[D07B 2207/4045] to change the crystal structure of the load bearing material -- This subclass relates to rope or cable making machines that incorporate processes to alter the crystal structure of the load-bearing material, such as heat treatment, mechanical working or annealing, thereby improving mechanical properties like strength, flexibility or wear resistance.
[D07B 2207/405] to heat towards the glass transition temperature of the load bearing material -- This CPC subclass covers rope or cable making machines that include a heating step raising the load-bearing material toward its glass transition temperature, a process used to improve pliability or assist subsequent forming. It falls under the textiles-rope hierarchy of the CPC.
[D07B 2207/4054] to soften the load bearing material -- The subclass D07B 2207/4054 covers methods and apparatus used in rope-or-cable making machines to soften the load-bearing material, such as fibers or strands, before or during the construction of the rope. Techniques may include heating, steaming, chemical treatment or mechanical conditioning that reduces stiffness and improves pliability.
[D07B 2207/4059] to soften the filler material -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4059 covers rope-or-cable manufacturing equipment that includes means for softening the filler material, such as heating, steaming or chemical treatment, to improve flexibility and performance before the strands are combined.
[D07B 2207/4063] for stress relief -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4063 covers features of rope- or cable-making machines that provide stress-relief treatment to the product during manufacture. It includes devices, adjustments or processes designed to reduce internal tension in textile ropes or cables, improving their flexibility, durability and handling characteristics. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of textiles, ropes, and general rope or cable manufacturing.
[D07B 2207/4068] for curing -- D07B 2207/4068 covers rope-or-cable making machines that incorporate a curing stage, typically using heat, steam or chemical treatment to set the fibers or strands after winding or braiding. The classification applies to equipment designed to improve tensile strength, elasticity or durability of textiles-based ropes through controlled post-formation processing.
[D07B 2207/4072] Means for mechanically reducing serpentining or mechanically killing of rope -- The code D07B 2207/4072 covers devices that mechanically straighten or "kill" rope during production, reducing unwanted serpentining and stabilising the lay. Such means are typically integrated into rope-making machines and may include rollers, tensioners, or other apparatus that apply controlled forces to align fibers and eliminate twists, improving rope uniformity and performance.
[D07B 2207/4077] Safety devices -- The CPC code D07B 2207/4077 covers safety devices for rope or cable making machines. It includes mechanisms that protect operators and prevent damage during the production of ropes and cables, such as emergency stops, guards, overload detectors and tension-release systems. These features fall under the textiles hierarchy.
[D07B 2207/4081] comprising means for stopping or shutting down the machine -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4081 refers to rope or cable making machines that are equipped with provisions for stopping or shutting down the equipment, such as emergency-stop devices, safety interlocks or automatic shutdown controls. It falls under the hierarchy TEXTILES → ROPES → ROPES OR CABLES IN GENERAL → Rope or cable making machines.
[D07B 2207/4086] providing warnings -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4086 falls under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general → rope or cable making machines and covers features that provide warnings to operators, such as alerts for excessive tension, entanglement risk or equipment malfunction. It is used to classify safety-related signaling or alert systems incorporated in rope-making machinery.
[D07B 2207/409] Drives -- The D07B 2207/409 subclass pertains to the drive systems employed in rope-or-cable making machines, covering the power transmission components such as motors, gearings, belts and other mechanisms that propel the manufacturing process. It includes the design, arrangement and control of these drives to achieve the required speed and tension for forming ropes or cables.
[D07B 2207/4095] Control means therefor -- The CPC subclass D07B 2207/4095 relates to control means used in rope or cable making machines. It covers devices that regulate tension, speed, feeding or other operating parameters of equipment that manufactures ropes or cables, situating it under textiles → ropes → ropes or cables in general.

[D07B 3/00] Manufacture of ropes or cables -- **Definition:** This place covers:  general purpose machines and methods for producing twisted ropes or cables from component strands of the same or different material.
[D07B 3/005] with alternating twist directions
[D07B 3/02] in which the supply reels rotate about the axis of the rope or cable
[D07B 3/022] with provision for imparting two or more twists to the filaments for each revolution of the guide member
[D07B 3/04] and are arranged in tandem along the axis of the machine
[D07B 3/045] with the reels axially aligned, their common axis coinciding with the axis of the machine
[D07B 3/06] and are spaced radially from the axis of the machine
[D07B 3/08] in which the take-up reel rotates about the axis of the rope or cable and the supply reels are fixed in position
[D07B 3/085] in which a guide member rotates about the axis of the rope or cable to guide the rope or cable on the take-up reel in fixed position
[D07B 3/10] with provision for imparting more than one complete twist to the ropes or cables for each revolution of the take-up reel
[D07B 3/103] characterised by the bow construction
[D07B 3/106] characterised by comprising two bows, both guiding the same bundle to impart a twist
[D07B 3/12] operating with rotating loops of filaments
[D07B 3/14] hand-operated

[D21] PAPER
[D21B] FIBROUS RAW MATERIALS OR THEIR MECHANICAL TREATMENT -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The preparation of fibrous raw materials, e.g. wood, rags, waste paper or pulp bales and their mechanical treatment to obtain mechanical pulp, in particular for paper-making and the production of fibreboard, comprises pretreating raw materials before pulp preparation, pulp preparation by dividing raw materials into small particles, e.g. by dry or wet methods of mechanical defibrating (cutting, grinding, kneading in mills or pulpers), conserving the obtained finely-divided cellulosic material (wood chips).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Splitting wood; Manufacture of veneer, wooden sticks, wood shavings, wood fibres or wood powder -> B27L - Obtaining fibres from plant material (mechanical separation) -> D01B1/00 - Hackling or heckling machines -> D01B5/00 - Pretreatment of the finely-divided materials before digesting -> D21C1/00 - Methods of beating or refining pulp -> D21D1/00 - Purification of the pulp suspension by mechanical means -> D21D5/00
[D21B 1/00] Fibrous raw materials or their mechanical treatment
[D21B 1/02] Pretreatment of the raw materials by chemical or physical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pretreatment of the raw materials by chemical or physical means, for example steaming or heating of wood chips.
[D21B 1/021] by chemical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Impregnation of wood chips with chemical or biological (enzymatic) substances.
[D21B 1/023] Cleaning wood chips or other raw materials
[D21B 1/025] Separating pith from fibrous vegetable materials
[D21B 1/026] Separating fibrous materials from waste
[D21B 1/028] by dry methods
[D21B 1/04] by dividing raw materials into small particles, e.g. fibres
[D21B 1/06] by dry methods
[D21B 1/061] using cutting devices

[D21C] PRODUCTION OF CELLULOSE BY REMOVING NON-CELLULOSE SUBSTANCES FROM CELLULOSE-CONTAINING MATERIALS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mechanical treatment of waste paper by dry methods -> D21B1/08 - Mechanical treatment of waste paper by wet methods, by the use of steam or other means -> D21B1/32  **Glossary:** - bagasse: fibre remaining after the extraction of the sugar-bearing juice from sugarcane - bleaching: treatment of lignocellulosic material to obtain a pulp/paper having an increased brightness - cellulose: structural component of the primary cell wall of green plants. It is an organic compound with the formula (C 6 H 10 O 5 ) n and is the major constituent of paper, paper-board and of textiles made from cotton, linen and other plant fibres. - cotton linters: fine, silky fibres which adhere to the seeds of the cotton plant after ginning and are traditionally used in the manufacture of paper and as a raw material in the manufacture of cellulose - delignification: treatment of lignocellulosic material to remove a part of the lignin - lignocellulosic material: cellulosic material which also comprises lignin - linings: material that covers the inner surface of something - pulp: a dispersion (e.g. aqueous suspension) comprising cellulosic fibres and optional additives; it is prepared by chemically or mechanically separating cellulose fibres from wood, fibre crops or waste paper
[D21C 1/00] Pretreatment of the finely-divided materials before digesting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Different pre-treatments (e.g. impregnation) of finely divided cellulosic containing material using e.g. water, steam, acids, alkaline compounds, oxygen generating compounds or physical methods for facilitating impregnation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Working up waste paper -> D21C5/02 - Treatment of wood -> B27K
[D21C 1/02] with water or steam
[D21C 1/04] with acid reacting compounds
[D21C 1/06] with alkaline reacting compounds
[D21C 1/08] with oxygen-generating compounds
[D21C 1/10] Physical methods for facilitating impregnation
[D21C 11/00] Regeneration of pulp liquors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Aspects concerning the production and the treatment of green and white liquors, e.g. causticizing green liquor  Combustion of pulp liquors  Concentration spent liquors by evaporation  Deodorisation or elimination of malodorous compounds, e.g. sulphur compounds such as hydrogen sulphide and mercaptans, for gas streams  Introduction of auxiliary substances into the regenerating system in order to improve the performance of certain steps of the latter, the presence of these substances being confined to the regeneration cycle  Recovery of by-products, i.e. compounds other than those necessary for pulping  Regeneration of alkali lye, of pulp liquors or effluent waste waters, of acid, neutral or alkaline sulphite lye  Treatment of pulp gases or of gases arising from various sources in pulp and paper mills  Recovery of the heat content in the gases  Regeneration of gaseous SO2, e.g. arising from liquors containing sulphur compounds  Wet combustion  Treatment of pulp liquor without previous evaporation, by oxidation of liquors remaining at least partially in the liquid phase, e.g. by application of pressure  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Water treatment -> C02F

[D21D] TREATMENT OF THE MATERIALS BEFORE PASSING TO THE PAPER-MAKING MACHINE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - beating: treatment of fibrous suspensions in Hollander type equipment in order to fibrillate, shorten or soften fibers - bed plate: stator of Hollander or Jordan devices - Hollander: traditional equipment for beating fibrous suspensions, being a device using at least one wheel like beating rotor (roll) equipped with beaters revolving in a trough having a beating stator (bed plate), also equipped with beaters, in which trough the suspension is circulated to pass multiple times through the beating zone formed between the rotor and the stator - Jordan: refiner having a frusto-conical rotor revolving inside a complimentary stator - paper stock: a term used to define pulp after mechanical (refining or beating) and/or chemical treatment (sizing, loading, dyeing etc.) in the paper making process, e.g. the pulp ready to make paper - refining: treatment of fibrous suspensions for the same purpose in all other types of equipment
[D21D 1/00] Methods of beating or refining -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of beating and refining of pulp fibers in general.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - knotter screens -> D21F
[D21D 1/002] Control devices
[D21D 1/004] Methods of beating or refining including disperging or deflaking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Disperging (i.e. separating rather small impurities sticking to the surface of a fiber) or deflaking (i.e. separating flakes or flocs formed by fibers into the individual fibers again) methods and equipments for fibrous materials.
[D21D 1/006] Disc mills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Disc mills only if particularly suitable for disperging or deflaking.
[D21D 1/008] Discs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Discs for disc mills only if particularly suitable for disperging or deflaking.
[D21D 1/02] Methods of beating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treatment in Hollander equipment, i.e. in a device using at least one wheel like beating rotor (roll) equipped with beating tools (knifes) revolving in a trough having a beating stator (bed plate), also equipped with beating tools (knifes), in which trough the suspension is circulated to pass multiple times through the beating zone formed between the rotor and the stator.

[D21D 5/00] Purification of the pulp suspension by mechanical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purification methods and equipments for pulp suspensions acting by mechanical means, i.e. straining, screening, centrifugal cleaning, sorting, de-aeration; tanks for storing or agitating pulp.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - centrifuges, cyclones -> B04

[D21D 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject-matter that fits within the scope of the subclass but is not covered by any of the other groups, such as for example fractioning methods, methods dividing the total suspension stream in portions, not for cleaning purposes, but for obtaining fibre fractions of different properties, e.g. separating longer from shorter fibres, finer from coarser fibres; and treating at least one of these portions differently from remaining portion(s) in subsequent process steps.
[D21F] PAPER-MAKING MACHINES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices in the wet end, press section and drying section of a papermaking machine, embossing paper, forming discontinuous sheets of paper.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Calendering paper webs -> D21G - Coating paper webs -> D21H  **Glossary:** - Section: portion of a paper production machine effecting a certain process step, for example forming, pressing, drying, calendering, coating, etc. - Yankee: steam heated drying cylinder of large diameter, mainly used for the production of creped tissue paper - TAD: through air drying process, drying paper webs by passing streams of air therethrough
[D21F 1/00] Wet end of machines for making continuous webs of paper -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wet end of papermaking machines, starting at the stable section, ending at the transfer/pick up to press section
[D21F 1/0009] Regulating the freeness of the pulp -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Controlling and regulating drainage rate, fiber length or degree of refining of a paper pulp suspension.
[D21F 1/0018] Devices for dispensing fibres in a fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices or altering the distribution of the fibers in the suspension, like for example by pressure pulse or ultrasound, the suspension flowing through the headbox of a papermaking machine.
[D21F 1/0027] Screen-cloths -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Screens or fabrics made from plastic materials and useable for forming paper webs in a paper machine by separating fibers from an aqueous suspension or also if usable for drying a paper web in the drying section of a paper machine.
[D21F 1/0036] Multi-layer screen-cloths
[D21F 1/0045] Triple layer fabrics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fabrics comprising two separate layers (paper side layer and machine side layer) and a system of binding yarns interconnecting said layers. The binding yarns can also contribute to the forming of the paper side layer (known as sheet support binding [SSB]).

[D21G] CALENDERS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Calenders, smoothing apparatus. [D21H] PULP COMPOSITIONS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pulp compositions and preparation thereof including after-treatment not covered by subclasses D21B , D21C , D21D : (a) from cellulose or lignocellulose fibres of natural origin, (b) from synthetic cellulose or non-cellulose fibres or web-forming material, or (c) non-fibrous material added to the pulp.  Coated paper characterised by: (a) coating material, (b) paper substrate, or (c) comprising more than one coating.  Processes or apparatus for adding material to the pulp or to the paper (processes for making continuous lengths of paper D21F11/00 ).  Treatment of finished paper not covered by class B31 or subclass D21G .  Special paper not otherwise provided for, i.e. paper with special properties or applications which are only partially or not at all provided for elsewhere in the classification, e.g. made by multi-step processes. Whenever possible, however, these papers are classified according to the criteria used in the other groups of this subclass.  Paper-impregnating material characterised by its constitution.  **Glossary:** - binder or binding agent: usually a macromolecular product used to bind the paper-making fibres together in order to give cohesion to the final paper sheet, e.g. starchy materials, latexes, etc. - coating: layer of material applied to the paper substrate. Although part of this material may penetrate through the substrate, a coated paper is characterised by the presence of said layer on its surface. - fibres: under paper-making fibres are meant short fibres (maximum length in the range of the mm). Papers also comprising long fibres or filaments are classified in D21H15/00 . - filler or pigment: usually designating fine particles added to the pulp (very often inorganic materials such as talc, kaolin, silica, calcium carbonate, etc.). - finished paper: paper after passing to the finishing process, creating a surface characteristic of a sheet created by either on-machine or off-machine papermaking processes. Popular text and cover finishes include smooth, vellum, felt, laid, and linen. - impregnation: application of a liquid material to a paper substrate wherein the liquid material penetrates substantially the substrate, e.g. by submerging the substrate into a bath containing the liquid material. - pulp: a dispersion e.g. an aqueous suspension comprising paper-making fibres and optional additives, which is to be processed, and covers the term "paper stock"; it also means dry paper-making fibres which are to be made into paper by either wet or dry processes. - sizing agent or hydrophobising agent: substances used during paper manufacture to reduce the paper's tendency when dry to absorb liquid, allowing inks and paints to remain on the surface of the paper and to dry there, rather than be absorbed into the paper or agent added in order to increase the hydrophobicity of the paper. - paper stock: term used to define pulp after mechanical (refining or beating) and/or chemical treatment (sizing, loading, dyeing, etc.) in the paper making process, e.g. the pulp ready to make paper.

[D21J] FIBREBOARD -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Manufacture of fibreboard and articles from cellulosic fibrous suspensions by wet methods, and fibreboard product obtained thereby.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Manufacture by dry processes of articles made from particles or fibres consisting of wood other lignocellulosic material -> B27N - Preparation of pulp compositions -> D21B - Addition of chemical agents -> D21C - Formation of fibrous webs -> D21F
[D21J 1/00] Fibreboard -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and devices for the production of wet-formed board from raw materials comprising fibrous materials and optionally all kind of other filler material.
[D21J 1/02] Cutting, e.g. using wet saws
[D21J 1/04] Pressing
[D21J 1/06] Drying
[D21J 1/08] Impregnated or coated fibreboard
[D21J 1/10] After-treatment
[D21J 1/12] Hardening
[D21J 1/14] Conditioning
[D21J 1/16] Special fibreboard -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibreboard specially adapted for a certain purpose.
[D21J 1/165] Roofing material
[D21J 1/18] Hardboard
[D21J 1/20] Insulating board -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. acoustic or thermal insulating board.
[D21J 3/00] Manufacture of articles by pressing wet fibre pulp, or papier-mâché, between moulds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibreboard manufacturing processes without the use of vacuum, i.e. only pressing the moulds together.
[D21J 3/02] of rings
[D21J 3/04] of tubes
[D21J 3/06] of stoppers
[D21J 3/08] of bobbins
[D21J 3/10] of hollow bodies
[D21J 3/12] of sheets
[D21J 5/00] Manufacture of hollow articles by transferring sheets, produced from fibres suspensions or papier-mâché by suction on wire-net moulds, to couch-moulds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibreboard manufacturing processes comprising sheet forming and subsequent moulding/pressing.
[D21J 7/00] Manufacture of hollow articles from fibre suspensions or papier-mâché by deposition of fibres in or on a wire-net mould -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fibreboard manufacturing processes comprising simultaneous forming and moulding.
